Rotherham.label: The Emphasised Bible
Rotherham.overlayPolicy: mismatch chapters stored under translationOverlays.Rotherham without altering target verse sequence
Rotherham.emptySourceRowsPolicy: empty Rotherham source rows are governed omissions and are not imported as blank text

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: Since many have undertaken to set down an orderly account of the events that have been fulfilled among us,
text.Rotherham: Seeing, indeed, that, many, had taken in hand to re-arrange for themselves a narrative, concerning the facts which have been fully confirmed amongst us,—
text.KJV: Forasmuch as many haue taken in hande to set foorth in order a declaration of those things which are most surely beleeued among vs,
text.NABRE: I. The Prologue Chapter 1 Since many have undertaken to compile a narrative of the events that have been fulfilled among us,
text.DRB: Because many have gone about to compile a narration of the things that have been accomplished among us;

text.NRSV: just as they were handed on to us by those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word,
text.Rotherham: according as they who from the beginning became eye-witnesses and attendants of the Word delivered them unto us,
text.KJV: Euen as they deliuered them vnto vs, which from the beginning were eye-witnesses, & ministers of the word:
text.NABRE: just as those who were eyewitnesses from the beginning and ministers of the word have handed them down to us,
text.DRB: according as they have delivered unto us, who from the beginning themselves saw and were ministers of the word;

text.NRSV: I too decided, after investigating everything carefully from the very first, to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,
text.Rotherham: it seemed good, even to me, having closely traced from the outset all things accurately, to write unto thee, in order, most excellent Theophilus:
text.KJV: It seemed good to me also, hauing had perfect vnderstanding of things from the very first, to write vnto thee in order, most excellent Theophilus,
text.NABRE: I too have decided, after investigating everything accurately anew, to write it down in an orderly sequence for you, most excellent Theophilus,
text.DRB: it seemed good also unto me having diligently attained to all things from the beginning, to write to thee in order, good Theophilus,

text.NRSV: so that you may know the truth concerning the things about which you have been instructed.
text.Rotherham: that, as touching the matters which thou hadst been taught by word of mouth, thou mightest obtain full knowledge, of the certainty.
text.KJV: That thou mightest know the certainetie of those things wherein thou hast bene instructed.
text.NABRE: so that you may realize the certainty of the teachings you have received.
text.DRB: that thou mayst know the verity of those words whereof thou hast been instructed.

text.NRSV: In the days of King Herod of Judea, there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the priestly order of Abijah. His wife was a descendant of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
text.Rotherham: It came to pass, in the days of Herod, king of Judaea, that there was a certain priest, by name Zachariah, of the daily course of Abia; and that he had a wife of the daughters of Aaron, and, her name, was Elizabeth.
text.KJV: ¶ There was in the dayes of Herode the king of Iudea, a certaine Priest, named Zacharias, of the course of Abia, and his wife was of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
text.NABRE: II. The Infancy Narrative Announcement of the Birth of John. In the days of Herod, King of Judea, there was a priest named Zechariah of the priestly division of Abijah; his wife was from the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
text.DRB: There was in the days of Herod the king of Jewry, a certain Priest named Zacharie, of the course of Abia; & his wife of the daughters of Aaron, and her name Elizabeth.

text.NRSV: Both of them were righteous before God, living blamelessly according to all the commandments and regulations of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Now they were both righteous before God, walking in all the commandments and righteous appointments of the Lord, blameless;
text.KJV: And they were both righteous before God, walking in all the Commandements and ordinances of the Lord, blamelesse.
text.NABRE: Both were righteous in the eyes of God, observing all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blamelessly.
text.DRB: And they were both just before God, walking in all the commandments and justifications of our Lord without blame,

text.NRSV: But they had no children, because Elizabeth was barren, and both were getting on in years.
text.Rotherham: and they had no child, inasmuch as Elizabeth was barren, and, both, had become, advanced in their days.
text.KJV: And they had no childe, because that Elizabeth was barren, and they both were now well striken in yeeres.
text.NABRE: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren and both were advanced in years.
text.DRB: and they had no son: for that Elizabeth was barren, and both were well striken in their days.

text.NRSV: Once when he was serving as priest before God and his section was on duty,
text.Rotherham: But it came to pass, as he was doing priestly service in the order of his daily course, before God,
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that while he executed the Priests office before God in the order of his course,
text.NABRE: Once when he was serving as priest in his division’s turn before God,
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when he executed the priestly function in the order of his course before God,

text.NRSV: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter the sanctuary of the Lord and offer incense.
text.Rotherham: according to the custom of his priesthood, it fell to his lot to offer incense, entering into the Temple of the Lord;
text.KJV: According to the custome of the Priests office, his lot was to burne incense when he went into the Temple of the Lord.
text.NABRE: according to the practice of the priestly service, he was chosen by lot to enter the sanctuary of the Lord to burn incense.
text.DRB: according to the custom of the Priestly function, he went forth by lot to offer incense, entering into the temple of our Lord;

text.NRSV: Now at the time of the incense offering, the whole assembly of the people was praying outside.
text.Rotherham: and, all the throng of the people, was praying outside, at the hour of the incense offering.
text.KJV: And the whole multitude of the people were praying without, at the time of incense.
text.NABRE: Then, when the whole assembly of the people was praying outside at the hour of the incense offering,
text.DRB: and all the multitude of the people was praying without at the hour of the incense.

text.NRSV: Then there appeared to him an angel of the Lord, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.
text.Rotherham: And there appeared unto him a messenger of the Lord, standing on the right hand of the altar of incense;
text.KJV: And there appeared vnto him an Angel of the Lord, standing on the right side of the Altar of incense.
text.NABRE: the angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right of the altar of incense.
text.DRB: And there appeared to him an Angel of our Lord, standing on the right hand of the Altar of incense.

text.NRSV: When Zechariah saw him, he was terrified; and fear overwhelmed him.
text.Rotherham: and Zachariah was troubled when he beheld, and, fear, fell upon him.
text.KJV: And when Zacharias sawe him, hee was troubled, and feare fell vpon him.
text.NABRE: Zechariah was troubled by what he saw, and fear came upon him.
text.DRB: And Zacharie was troubled, seeing him; and fear fell upon him.

text.NRSV: But the angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zechariah, for your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will name him John.
text.Rotherham: But the messenger said unto him—Do not fear, Zachariah! Inasmuch as thy supplication hath been hearkened to,—and, thy wife Elizabeth, shall bring forth a son to thee, and thou shalt call his name, John;
text.KJV: But the Angel said vnto him, Feare not, Zacharias, for thy prayer is heard, and thy wife Elizabeth shall beare thee a sonne, and thou shalt call his name Iohn.
text.NABRE: But the angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zechariah, because your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you shall name him John.
text.DRB: But the Angel said to him: Fear not Zacharie, for thy prayer is heard; and thy wife Elizabeth shall bear thee a son and thou shalt call his name John:

text.NRSV: You will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,
text.Rotherham: And there shall be joy to thee and exulting, and, many, over his birth, shall rejoice;
text.KJV: And thou shalt haue ioy and gladnesse, and many shall reioyce at his birth:
text.NABRE: And you will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,
text.DRB: and thou shalt have joy and exultation, and many shall rejoice in his nativity.

text.NRSV: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He must never drink wine or strong drink; even before his birth he will be filled with the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For he shall be great before the Lord, and, wine and strong drink, in nowise may he drink, and, with Holy Spirit, shall he be filled, already, from his mother’s womb;
text.KJV: For he shall be great in the sight of the Lord, and shal drinke neither wine, nor strong drinke, and he shall bee filled with the holy Ghost, euen from his mothers wombe.
text.NABRE: for he will be great in the sight of [the] Lord. He will drink neither wine nor strong drink. He will be filled with the holy Spirit even from his mother’s womb,
text.DRB: For he shall be great before our Lord; and wine and sicer he shall not drink; and he shall be replenished with the Holy Ghost even from his mother's womb.

text.NRSV: He will turn many of the people of Israel to the Lord their God.
text.Rotherham: And, many of the Sons of Israel, shall he turn towards the Lord their God;
text.KJV: And many of the children of Israel shall hee turne to the Lord their God.
text.NABRE: and he will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God.
text.DRB: And he shall convert many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God.

text.NRSV: With the spirit and power of Elijah he will go before him, to turn the hearts of parents to their children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous, to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, shall go before him, in the spirit and power of Elijah,—To turn the hearts of fathers unto children, and the unyielding, into the prudence of the righteous, and to prepare, for the Lord, a people made ready.
text.KJV: And hee shall goe before him in the spirit and power of Elias, to turne the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the disobedient to the wisedome of the iust, to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
text.NABRE: He will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah to turn the hearts of fathers toward children and the disobedient to the understanding of the righteous, to prepare a people fit for the Lord.”
text.DRB: And he shall go before him in the spirit and virtue of Elias, that he may convert the hearts of the fathers unto the children, and the incredulous to the wisdom of the just, to prepare unto the Lord a perfect people.

text.NRSV: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know that this is so? For I am an old man, and my wife is getting on in years.”
text.Rotherham: And Zachariah said unto the messenger—Whereby, shall I know this? for, I, am, aged, and, my wife, advanced in her days.
text.KJV: And Zacharias said vnto the Angel, Whereby shall I know this? For I am an old man, and my wife well striken in yeeres.
text.NABRE: Then Zechariah said to the angel, “How shall I know this? For I am an old man, and my wife is advanced in years.”
text.DRB: And Zacharie said to the Angel: Whereby shall I know this? For I am old; and my wife is well striken in her days.

text.NRSV: The angel replied, “I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger, answering, said unto him—I, am Gabriel,—he that standeth near before God; and have been sent forth to speak unto thee, and to deliver the joyful message unto thee, as touching these things.
text.KJV: And the Angel answering, said vnto him, I am Gabriel that stand in the presence of God, and am sent to speake vnto thee, and to shew thee these glad tidings.
text.NABRE: And the angel said to him in reply, “I am Gabriel, who stand before God. I was sent to speak to you and to announce to you this good news.
text.DRB: And the Angel answering said to him: I am Gabriel that assist before God; and am sent to speak to thee, and to evangelize these things to thee.

text.NRSV: But now, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their time, you will become mute, unable to speak, until the day these things occur.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! thou shalt be silent, and not able to speak until the day when these things shall come to pass; because thou didst not believe in my words,—the which shall be fulfilled for their season.
text.KJV: And behold, thou shalt be dumbe, and not able to speake, vntill the day that these things shall bee performed, because thou beleeuest not my words, which shall bee fulfilled in their season.
text.NABRE: But now you will be speechless and unable to talk until the day these things take place, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled at their proper time.”
text.DRB: And behold, thou shalt be dumb, and shalt not be able to speak until the day wherein these things shall be done; for-because thou hast not believed my words, which shall be fulfilled in their time.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile the people were waiting for Zechariah, and wondered at his delay in the sanctuary.
text.Rotherham: And the people were expecting Zachariah, and began to marvel that he should tarry in the Temple;
text.KJV: And the people waited for Zacharias, and maruelled that hee taried so long in the temple.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile the people were waiting for Zechariah and were amazed that he stayed so long in the sanctuary.
text.DRB: And the people was expecting Zacharie; and they marveled that he made tarriance in the temple.

text.NRSV: When he did come out, he could not speak to them, and they realized that he had seen a vision in the sanctuary. He kept motioning to them and remained unable to speak.
text.Rotherham: and when he came forth he was not able to speak unto them, and they perceived that, a vision, he had seen in the Temple,—and, he, continued making signs unto them, and remained dumb.
text.KJV: And when he came out, he could not speake vnto them: and they perceiued that he had seene a vision in the temple: for he beckened vnto them, and remained speechlesse.
text.NABRE: But when he came out, he was unable to speak to them, and they realized that he had seen a vision in the sanctuary. He was gesturing to them but remained mute.
text.DRB: And coming forth he could not speak to them, and they knew that he had seen a vision in the temple. And he made signs to them, and remained dumb.

text.NRSV: When his time of service was ended, he went to his home.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the days of his public ministration were fulfilled, that he departed unto his house.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that as soone as the dayes of his ministration were accomplished, he departed to his owne house.
text.NABRE: Then, when his days of ministry were completed, he went home.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, after the days of his office were expired, he departed into his house.

text.NRSV: After those days his wife Elizabeth conceived, and for five months she remained in seclusion. She said,
text.Rotherham: And, after these days, Elizabeth his wife conceived, and she disguised herself five months, saying—
text.KJV: And after those dayes his wife Elizabeth conceiued, and hid her selfe fiue moneths, saying,
text.NABRE: After this time his wife Elizabeth conceived, and she went into seclusion for five months, saying,
text.DRB: And after these days Elizabeth his wife conceived; and hid herself five months, saying:

text.NRSV: “This is what the Lord has done for me when he looked favorably on me and took away the disgrace I have endured among my people.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, for me, hath the Lord wrought,—in the days in which he looked upon me, to take away my reproach among men.
text.KJV: Thus hath the Lord dealt with me in the dayes wherein he looked on me, to take away my reproch among men.
text.NABRE: “So has the Lord done for me at a time when he has seen fit to take away my disgrace before others.” Announcement of the Birth of Jesus.
text.DRB: For thus hath our Lord done to me in the days wherein he had respect to take away my reproach among men.

text.NRSV: In the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent by God to a town in Galilee called Nazareth,
text.Rotherham: Now, in the sixth month, was the messenger Gabriel sent forth from God, into a city of Galilee, the name of which was Nazareth,—
text.KJV: And in the sixt moneth, the Angel Gabriel was sent from God, vnto a citie of Galilee, named Nazareth,
text.NABRE: In the sixth month, the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a town of Galilee called Nazareth,
text.DRB: And in the sixth month, the Angel Gabriel was sent of God into a city of Galilee, called Nazareth,

text.NRSV: to a virgin engaged to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David. The virgin’s name was Mary.
text.Rotherham: unto a virgin, betrothed to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and, the name of the virgin, was, Mary;
text.KJV: To a virgine espoused to a man whose name was Ioseph, of the house of Dauid, and the virgins name was Marie.
text.NABRE: to a virgin betrothed to a man named Joseph, of the house of David, and the virgin’s name was Mary.
text.DRB: to a virgin despoused to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and the virgin's name was Mary.

text.NRSV: And he came to her and said, “Greetings, favored one! The Lord is with you.”
text.Rotherham: and entering in unto her, he said—Joy to thee, favoured one! The Lord, be with thee!
text.KJV: And the Angel came in vnto her, and said, Haile thou that art highly fauoured, the Lord is with thee: Blessed art thou among women.
text.NABRE: And coming to her, he said, “Hail, favored one! The Lord is with you.”
text.DRB: And the Angel being entered in, said unto her: Hail full of grace, our Lord is with thee: blessed art thou among women.

text.NRSV: But she was much perplexed by his words and pondered what sort of greeting this might be.
text.Rotherham: And, she, at the word, was greatly troubled, and began to deliberate, of what kind, this salutation, might be.
text.KJV: And when she saw him, she was troubled at his saying, and cast in her minde what maner of salutation this should be.
text.NABRE: But she was greatly troubled at what was said and pondered what sort of greeting this might be.
text.DRB: Who having heard, was troubled at his saying, and thought what manner of salutation this should be.

text.NRSV: The angel said to her, “Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger said unto her—Do not fear, Mary, for thou hast found favour with God,—
text.KJV: And the Angel said vnto her, Feare not, Marie, for thou hast found fauour with God.
text.NABRE: Then the angel said to her, “Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.
text.DRB: And the Angel said to her: Fear not Mary, for thou hast found grace with God.

text.NRSV: And now, you will conceive in your womb and bear a son, and you will name him Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And lo! thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and bring forth a son, and shalt call his name, Jesus:
text.KJV: And behold, thou shalt conceiue in thy wombe, and bring forth a sonne, and shalt call his name Iesus.
text.NABRE: Behold, you will conceive in your womb and bear a son, and you shall name him Jesus.
text.DRB: Behold thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and shalt bear a Son; and thou shalt call his name Jesus.

text.NRSV: He will be great, and will be called the Son of the Most High, and the Lord God will give to him the throne of his ancestor David.
text.Rotherham: the same, shall be great, and, Son of the Most High, shall be called, and the Lord God, will give unto him, the throne of David his father,—
text.KJV: He shall be great, and shall be called the sonne of the Highest, and the Lord God shall giue vnto him the throne of his father Dauid.
text.NABRE: He will be great and will be called Son of the Most High, and the Lord God will give him the throne of David his father,
text.DRB: He shall be great, and shall be called the Son of the most High, and our Lord God shall give him the seat of David his father:

text.NRSV: He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and of his kingdom there will be no end.”
text.Rotherham: And he shall reign over the house of Jacob, unto the ages, and, of his kingdom, there shall be, no end.
text.KJV: And hee shall reigne ouer the house of Iacob for euer, and of his kingdome there shall be no end.
text.NABRE: and he will rule over the house of Jacob forever, and of his kingdom there will be no end.”
text.DRB: and he shall reign in the house of Jacob for ever, and of his Kingdom there shall be no end.

text.NRSV: Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, since I am a virgin?”
text.Rotherham: But Mary said unto the messenger—How, shall this thing be, seeing that, a man, I know not?
text.KJV: Then said Marie vnto the Angel, How shall this be, seeing I know not a man?
text.NABRE: But Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, since I have no relations with a man?”
text.DRB: And Mary said to the Angel: How shall this be done, because I know not man?

text.NRSV: The angel said to her, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you; therefore the child to be born will be holy; he will be called Son of God.
text.Rotherham: And answering, the messenger said unto her—The Holy Spirit, shall come upon thee, and, the power of the Most High, shall overshadow thee; wherefore, even that which is to be born, Holy, shall be called, Son of God.
text.KJV: And the Angel answered and said vnto her, The holy Ghost shall come vpon thee, and the power of the Highest shall ouershadow thee. Therefore also that holy thing which shall bee borne of thee, shall bee called the sonne of God.
text.NABRE: And the angel said to her in reply, “The holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore the child to be born will be called holy, the Son of God.
text.DRB: And the Angel answering, said to her: The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the power of the most High shall overshadow thee. And therefore also that which of thee shall be born Holy, shall be called the Son of God.

text.NRSV: And now, your relative Elizabeth in her old age has also conceived a son; and this is the sixth month for her who was said to be barren.
text.Rotherham: And lo! Elizabeth thy kinswoman, even she, hath conceived a son in, her old-age;—and, this month, is, the sixth, to her, the so-called barren one;
text.KJV: And behold, thy cousin Elizabeth, she hath also conceiued a sonne in her old age, and this is the sixt moneth with her, who was called barren.
text.NABRE: And behold, Elizabeth, your relative, has also conceived a son in her old age, and this is the sixth month for her who was called barren;
text.DRB: And behold Elizabeth thy cousin, she also hath conceived a Son in her old age; and this month, is the sixth to her that is called barren;

text.NRSV: For nothing will be impossible with God.”
text.Rotherham: Because no declaration from God, shall be void of power.
text.KJV: For with God no thing shall be vnpossible.
text.NABRE: for nothing will be impossible for God.”
text.DRB: because there shall not be impossible with God any word.

text.NRSV: Then Mary said, “Here am I, the servant of the Lord; let it be with me according to your word.” Then the angel departed from her.
text.Rotherham: And Mary said—Lo! the handmaid of the Lord! Might it come to pass unto me, according to thy declaration. And the messenger departed from her.
text.KJV: And Marie said, Behold the handmaide of the Lord, be it vnto me according to thy word: and the Angel departed from her.
text.NABRE: Mary said, “Behold, I am the handmaid of the Lord. May it be done to me according to your word.” Then the angel departed from her.
text.DRB: And Mary said, Behold the handmaid of our Lord, be it done to me according to thy word. And the Angel departed from her.

text.NRSV: In those days Mary set out and went with haste to a Judean town in the hill country,
text.Rotherham: And Mary, arising, in these days, journeyed into the hill country with haste, into a city of Judah,—
text.KJV: And Marie arose in those dayes, and went into the hill countrey with haste, into a citie of Iuda,
text.NABRE: Mary Visits Elizabeth. During those days Mary set out and traveled to the hill country in haste to a town of Judah,
text.DRB: And Mary rising up in those days, went unto the hill country with speed into a city of Juda.

text.NRSV: where she entered the house of Zechariah and greeted Elizabeth.
text.Rotherham: and entered into the house of Zachariah, and saluted Elizabeth.
text.KJV: And entred into the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elizabeth.
text.NABRE: where she entered the house of Zechariah and greeted Elizabeth.
text.DRB: And she entered into the house of Zacharie, and saluted Elizabeth.

text.NRSV: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the child leaped in her womb. And Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, as Elizabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leapt in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with Holy Spirit,—
text.KJV: And it came to passe that when Elizabeth heard the salutation of Marie, the babe leaped in her wombe, and Elizabeth was filled with the holy Ghost.
text.NABRE: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the infant leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth, filled with the holy Spirit,
text.DRB: And it came to pass; as Elizabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the infant did leap in her womb. And Elizabeth was replenished with the Holy Ghost:

text.NRSV: and exclaimed with a loud cry, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb.
text.Rotherham: and lifted up her voice with loud exclamation, and said—Blessed, art thou among women, and, blessed, is the fruit of thy womb;
text.KJV: And she spake out with a loud voyce, and saide, Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruite of thy wombe.
text.NABRE: cried out in a loud voice and said, “Most blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb.
text.DRB: and she cried out with a loud voice, and said, Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.

text.NRSV: And why has this happened to me, that the mother of my Lord comes to me?
text.Rotherham: And, whence, to me is, this, That the mother of my Lord should come, unto me?
text.KJV: And whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to mee?
text.NABRE: And how does this happen to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
text.DRB: And whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord doth come to me?

text.NRSV: For as soon as I heard the sound of your greeting, the child in my womb leaped for joy.
text.Rotherham: For lo! as the sound of thy salutation came into mine ears, the babe in my womb, leapt in exultation.
text.KJV: For loe, assoone as the voice of thy salutation sounded in mine eares, the babe leaped in my wombe for ioy.
text.NABRE: For at the moment the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the infant in my womb leaped for joy.
text.DRB: For behold as the voice of thy salutation sounded in mine ears, the infant in my womb did leap for joy.

text.NRSV: And blessed is she who believed that there would be a fulfillment of what was spoken to her by the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And, happy, is she who hath believed, that there shall be a perfecting of the things which have been spoken to her from the Lord!
text.KJV: And blessed is she that beleeued, for there shalbe a performance of those things, which were told her from the Lord.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you who believed that what was spoken to you by the Lord would be fulfilled.”
text.DRB: And Blessed is she that believed because those things shall be accomplished that were spoken to her by our Lord.

text.NRSV: And Mary said, “My soul magnifies the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And Mary said—My soul doth magnify the Lord,
text.KJV: And Marie said, My soule doth magnifie the Lord.
text.NABRE: The Canticle of Mary. And Mary said: “My soul proclaims the greatness of the Lord;
text.DRB: And Mary said: MY Soul doth magnify our Lord:

text.NRSV: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,
text.Rotherham: And my spirit hath exulted upon God my saviour;
text.KJV: And my spirit hath reioyced in God my sauiour.
text.NABRE: my spirit rejoices in God my savior.
text.DRB: And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.

text.NRSV: for he has looked with favor on the lowliness of his servant. Surely, from now on all generations will call me blessed;
text.Rotherham: Because he hath looked upon the humbling of his handmaid; for lo! from the present time, all the generations, will pronounce me happy.
text.KJV: For hee hath regarded the low estate of his handmaiden: for behold, from hencefoorth all generations shall call me blessed.
text.NABRE: For he has looked upon his handmaid’s lowliness; behold, from now on will all ages call me blessed.
text.DRB: Because he hath regarded the humility of his handmaid; for behold from hence forth all generations shall call me blessed.

text.NRSV: for the Mighty One has done great things for me, and holy is his name.
text.Rotherham: Because he that is mighty hath done for me great things, and, holy, is his name;
text.KJV: For he that is mighty hath done to mee great things, and holy is his Name.
text.NABRE: The Mighty One has done great things for me, and holy is his name.
text.DRB: Because he that is mighty hath done great things to me: and holy is his name.

text.NRSV: His mercy is for those who fear him from generation to generation.
text.Rotherham: And his mercy is unto generations and generations, to them who revere him;
text.KJV: And his mercy is on them that feare him, from generation to generation.
text.NABRE: His mercy is from age to age to those who fear him.
text.DRB: And his mercy from generation unto generations, to them that fear him.

text.NRSV: He has shown strength with his arm; he has scattered the proud in the thoughts of their hearts.
text.Rotherham: He hath wrought strength with his arm, He hath scattered men arrogant in the intention of their heart;
text.KJV: Hee hath shewed strength with his arme, he hath scattered the proud, in the imagination of their hearts.
text.NABRE: He has shown might with his arm, dispersed the arrogant of mind and heart.
text.DRB: He hath shewed might in his arm: he hath dispersed the proud in the conceit of their heart.

text.NRSV: He has brought down the powerful from their thrones, and lifted up the lowly;
text.Rotherham: He hath deposed potentates from thrones, and uplifted the lowly;
text.KJV: He hath put downe the mighty from their seates, and exalted them of low degree.
text.NABRE: He has thrown down the rulers from their thrones but lifted up the lowly.
text.DRB: He hath deposed the mighty from their seat, & hath exalted the humble.

text.NRSV: he has filled the hungry with good things, and sent the rich away empty.
text.Rotherham: The hungry, hath he filled with good things, and, the wealthy, hath he sent empty away;
text.KJV: Hee hath filled the hungry with good things, and the rich hee hath sent emptie away.
text.NABRE: The hungry he has filled with good things; the rich he has sent away empty.
text.DRB: The hungry he hath filled with good things: and the rich he hath sent away empty.

text.NRSV: He has helped his servant Israel, in remembrance of his mercy,
text.Rotherham: He hath laid hold of Israel his servant, to be mindful of mercies:
text.KJV: Hee hath holpen his seruant Israel, in remembrance of his mercy,
text.NABRE: He has helped Israel his servant, remembering his mercy,
text.DRB: He hath received Israel his child, being mindful of his mercy,

text.NRSV: according to the promise he made to our ancestors, to Abraham and to his descendants forever.”
text.Rotherham: According as he spake unto our fathers,—To Abraham, and to his seed,—Unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: As he spake to our fathers, to Abraham, and to his seed for euer.
text.NABRE: according to his promise to our fathers, to Abraham and to his descendants forever.”
text.DRB: As he spake to our fathers, to Abraham and his seed for ever.

text.NRSV: And Mary remained with her about three months and then returned to her home.
text.Rotherham: And Mary abode with her about three months, and returned unto her house.
text.KJV: And Mary abode with her about three moneths, and returned to her owne house.
text.NABRE: Mary remained with her about three months and then returned to her home. The Birth of John.
text.DRB: And Mary tarried with her about three months: and she returned into her house.

text.NRSV: Now the time came for Elizabeth to give birth, and she bore a son.
text.Rotherham: And, to Elizabeth, was the time fulfilled, that she should be bringing forth,—and she gave birth to a son.
text.KJV: Now Elizabeths full time came, that shee should be deliuered, and shee brought foorth a sonne.
text.NABRE: When the time arrived for Elizabeth to have her child she gave birth to a son.
text.DRB: And Elizabeth's full time was come to be delivered; and she bare a son.

text.NRSV: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown his great mercy to her, and they rejoiced with her.
text.Rotherham: And her neighbours and kinsfolk heard, that the Lord had magnified his mercy with her, and they were rejoicing with her.
text.KJV: And her neighbours and her cousins heard how the Lord had shewed great mercy vpon her, and they reioyced with her.
text.NABRE: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown his great mercy toward her, and they rejoiced with her.
text.DRB: And her neighbours and kinsfolk heard that our Lord did magnify his mercy with her, and they did congratulate her.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day they came to baptize the child, and they were going to name him Zechariah after his father.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the eighth day, that they came to circumcise the child, and were calling it, after the name of its father, Zachariah.
text.KJV: And it came to passe that on the eight day they came to circumcise the childe, and they called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.
text.NABRE: When they came on the eighth day to circumcise the child, they were going to call him Zechariah after his father,
text.DRB: And it came to pass; on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they called him by his father's name Zacharie.

text.NRSV: But his mother said, “No; he is to be called John.”
text.Rotherham: And his mother, answering, said—Nay! but he shall be called, John.
text.KJV: And his mother answered, and said, Not so, but he shalbe called Iohn.
text.NABRE: but his mother said in reply, “No. He will be called John.”
text.DRB: And his mother answering, said: Not so, but he shall be called John.

text.NRSV: They said to her, “None of your relatives has this name.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto her—There is, no one from among thy kindred, who is called by this name!
text.KJV: And they said vnto her, There is none of thy kinred that is called by this name.
text.NABRE: But they answered her, “There is no one among your relatives who has this name.”
text.DRB: And they said to her, That there is none in thy kindred that is called by this name.

text.NRSV: Then they began motioning to his father to find out what name he wanted to give him.
text.Rotherham: And they began making signs unto his father, as to what he might be wishing it to be called.
text.KJV: And they made signes to his father, how he would haue him called.
text.NABRE: So they made signs, asking his father what he wished him to be called.
text.DRB: And they made signs to his father, what he would have him called.

text.NRSV: He asked for a writing tablet and wrote, “His name is John.” And all of them were amazed.
text.Rotherham: And, asking for a small tablet, he wrote, saying—John, is his name! and they marvelled all.
text.KJV: And he asked for a writing table, and wrote, saying, His name is Iohn: and they marueiled all.
text.NABRE: He asked for a tablet and wrote, “John is his name,” and all were amazed.
text.DRB: And demanding a writing table, he wrote, saying: John is his name. And they all marveled.

text.NRSV: Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue freed, and he began to speak, praising God.
text.Rotherham: And his mouth was opened instantly, and his tongue [loosed], and he began to speak, blessing, God.
text.KJV: And his mouth was opened immediatly, and his tongue loosed, and hee spake, and praised God.
text.NABRE: Immediately his mouth was opened, his tongue freed, and he spoke blessing God.
text.DRB: And forthwith his mouth was opened, and his tongue, and he spake blessing God.

text.NRSV: Fear came over all their neighbors, and all these things were talked about throughout the entire hill country of Judea.
text.Rotherham: And fear came upon all the neighbours themselves; and, throughout all the hill-country of Judaea, were all these matters being much talked of;
text.KJV: And feare came on all that dwelt round about them, and all these sayings were noised abroad thorowout all the hill countrey of Iudea.
text.NABRE: Then fear came upon all their neighbors, and all these matters were discussed throughout the hill country of Judea.
text.DRB: And fear came upon all their neighbours; and all these things were bruited over all the hill-country of Jewry:

text.NRSV: All who heard them pondered them and said, “What then will this child become?” For, indeed, the hand of the Lord was with him.
text.Rotherham: and all who heard laid [them] up in their hearts, saying—What then shall his child be? for, even the hand of the Lord, was with him.
text.KJV: And all they that had heard them, layde them vp in their hearts, saying, What maner of childe shal this be? And the hand of the Lord was with him.
text.NABRE: All who heard these things took them to heart, saying, “What, then, will this child be?” For surely the hand of the Lord was with him.
text.DRB: and all that had heard, laid them up in their heart, saying: What an one, trow ye, shall this child be? For the hand of our Lord was with him.

text.NRSV: Then his father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke this prophecy:
text.Rotherham: And, Zachariah his father, was filled with Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying:—
text.KJV: And his father Zacharias was filled with the holy Ghost, and prophesied, saying,
text.NABRE: The Canticle of Zechariah. Then Zechariah his father, filled with the holy Spirit, prophesied, saying:
text.DRB: And Zacharie his father was replenished with the Holy Ghost; and he prophesied, saying:

text.NRSV: “Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he has looked favorably on his people and redeemed them.
text.Rotherham: Blessed, be the Lord, the God of Israel! Because he hath visited and wrought redemption for his people,
text.KJV: Blessed bee the Lord God of Israel, for hee hath visited and redeemed his people,
text.NABRE: “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, for he has visited and brought redemption to his people.
text.DRB: Blessed be Our Lord God of Israel: because he hath visited and wrought the redemption of his people:

text.NRSV: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in the house of his servant David,
text.Rotherham: And hath raised up a horn of salvation for us, In the house of David his servant:
text.KJV: And hath raised vp an horne of saluation for vs, in the house of his seruant Dauid,
text.NABRE: He has raised up a horn for our salvation within the house of David his servant,
text.DRB: And hath erected the horn of salvation to us, in the house of David his servant.

text.NRSV: as he spoke through the mouth of his holy prophets from of old,
text.Rotherham: According as he hath spoken by mouth of his holy ancient prophets,—
text.KJV: As he spake by the mouth of his holy Prophets, which haue bene since the world began:
text.NABRE: even as he promised through the mouth of his holy prophets from of old:
text.DRB: As he spake by the mouth of his holy Prophets, that are from the beginning:

text.NRSV: that we would be saved from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us.
text.Rotherham: Of salvation from among our foes, and out of the hand of all them that hate us:
text.KJV: That wee should be saued from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate vs,
text.NABRE: salvation from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us,
text.DRB: Salvation from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate us:

text.NRSV: Thus he has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and has remembered his holy covenant,
text.Rotherham: To perform mercy with our fathers, and to be mindful of his holy covenant,—
text.KJV: To performe the mercy promised to our fathers, and to remember his holy Couenant,
text.NABRE: to show mercy to our fathers and to be mindful of his holy covenant
text.DRB: To work mercy with our fathers; and to remember his holy testament,

text.NRSV: the oath that he swore to our ancestor Abraham, to grant us
text.Rotherham: The oath which he sware unto Abraham our father, To grant us,
text.KJV: The oath which he sware to our father Abraham,
text.NABRE: and of the oath he swore to Abraham our father, and to grant us that,
text.DRB: The oath which he sware to Abraham our father,

text.NRSV: that we, being rescued from the hands of our enemies, might serve him without fear,
text.Rotherham: without fear, from the hand of enemies rescued, to be rendering divine service unto him,
text.KJV: That hee would grant vnto vs, that wee beeing deliuered out of the hands of our enemies, might serue him without feare,
text.NABRE: rescued from the hand of enemies, without fear we might worship him
text.DRB: that he would give to us. That without fear being delivered from the hand of our enemies, we may serve him,

text.NRSV: in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.
text.Rotherham: in lovingkindness and righteousness before him, all our days.
text.KJV: In holinesse and righteousnesse before him, all the dayes of our life.
text.NABRE: in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.
text.DRB: In holiness and justice before him, all our days.

text.NRSV: And you, child, will be called the prophet of the Most High; for you will go before the Lord to prepare his ways,
text.Rotherham: And, even thou, child, prophet of the Most High, shalt be called,—for thou shall march on before the Lord, to prepare his ways,
text.KJV: And thou childe shalt bee called the Prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt goe before the face of the Lord to prepare his wayes.
text.NABRE: And you, child, will be called prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his ways,
text.DRB: And thou child, shalt be called the Prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt go before the face of our Lord to prepare his ways.

text.NRSV: to give knowledge of salvation to his people by the forgiveness of their sins.
text.Rotherham: Giving a knowledge of salvation unto his people, by a remission of their sins.
text.KJV: To giue knowledge of saluation vnto his people, by the remission of their sinnes,
text.NABRE: to give his people knowledge of salvation through the forgiveness of their sins,
text.DRB: To give knowledge of salvation to his people, unto remission of their sins,

text.NRSV: By the tender mercy of our God, the dawn from on high will break upon us,
text.Rotherham: Because of the yearning compassion of the mercy of our God, wherein shall visit us a day-dawn from on high,—
text.KJV: Through the tender mercy of our God, whereby the day-spring from on high hath visited vs,
text.NABRE: because of the tender mercy of our God by which the daybreak from on high will visit us
text.DRB: Through the bowels of the mercy of our God, in which the Orient, from on high, hath visited us.

text.NRSV: to give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.”
text.Rotherham: To shine on them who, in the darkness and shade of death, are sitting, to guide our feet into a way of peace.
text.KJV: To giue light to them that sit in darknes, and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.
text.NABRE: to shine on those who sit in darkness and death’s shadow, to guide our feet into the path of peace.”
text.DRB: To illuminate them that sit in darkness, and in the shadow of death: to direct our feet into the way of peace.

text.NRSV: The child grew and became strong in spirit, and he was in the wilderness until the day he appeared publicly to Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, the child, went on growing, and being strengthened in spirit, and was in the deserts, until the day he was pointed out unto Israel.
text.KJV: And the childe grew, and waxed strong in spirit, and was in the deserts, till the day of his shewing vnto Israel.
text.NABRE: The child grew and became strong in spirit, and he was in the desert until the day of his manifestation to Israel.
text.DRB: And the child grew, and was strengthened in spirit, and was in the deserts until the day of his manifestation to Israel.

text.NRSV: In those days a decree went out from Emperor Augustus that all the world should be registered.
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass, in those days, that there went forth a decree from Caesar Augustus, for all the inhabited earth to be enrolled:
text.KJV: And it came to passe in those dayes, that there went out a decree from Cesar Augustus, that all the world should be taxed.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 The Birth of Jesus. In those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that the whole world should be enrolled.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, in those days there came forth an Edict from Cæsar Augustus that the whole world should be enrolled.

text.NRSV: This was the first registration and was taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria.
text.Rotherham: this enrolment, first was made while Cyrenius was governor of Syria:
text.KJV: (And this taxing was first made whē Cyrenius was gouernor of Syria)
text.NABRE: This was the first enrollment, when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
text.DRB: This first enrolling was made by the President of Syria Cyrinus.

text.NRSV: All went to their own towns to be registered.
text.Rotherham: and all were journeying to be enrolled, each one unto his own city.
text.KJV: And all went to bee taxed, euery one into his owne citie.
text.NABRE: So all went to be enrolled, each to his own town.
text.DRB: And all went to be enrolled, every one into his own city.

text.NRSV: Joseph also went from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to the city of David called Bethlehem, because he was descended from the house and family of David.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of a city Nazareth, into Judaea, into the city of David which is called Bethlehem; because he was of the house and family of David:
text.KJV: And Ioseph also wēt vp frō Galilee, out of the citie of Nazareth, into Iudea, vnto the citie of Dauid, which is called Bethlehem, (because he was of the house and linage of Dauid,)
text.NABRE: And Joseph too went up from Galilee from the town of Nazareth to Judea, to the city of David that is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David,
text.DRB: And Joseph also went up from Galilee out of the city of Nazareth into Jewry, to the city of David that is called Beth-lehem: for because he was of the house and family of David,

text.NRSV: He went to be registered with Mary, to whom he was engaged and who was expecting a child.
text.Rotherham: to enrol himself with Mary who was betrothed to him, she being with child.
text.KJV: To be taxed with Mary his espoused wife, being great with child.
text.NABRE: to be enrolled with Mary, his betrothed, who was with child.
text.DRB: to be enrolled with Mary his despoused wife that was with child.

text.NRSV: While they were there, the time came for her to deliver her child.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while they were there, that the days were fulfilled for her to give birth;
text.KJV: And so it was, that while they were there, the dayes were accomplished that she should be deliuered.
text.NABRE: While they were there, the time came for her to have her child,
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when they were there, her days were fully come that she should be delivered.

text.NRSV: And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in bands of cloth, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the inn.
text.Rotherham: and she gave birth to her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.
text.KJV: And she brought foorth her first borne sonne, and wrapped him in swadling clothes, and laid him in a manger, because there was no roome for them in the Inne.
text.NABRE: and she gave birth to her firstborn son. She wrapped him in swaddling clothes and laid him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.
text.DRB: And she brought forth her first begotten son, and swaddled him in cloths, and laid him down in a manger; because there was not place for them in the inn.

text.NRSV: In that region there were shepherds living in the fields, keeping watch over their flock by night.
text.Rotherham: And there were, shepherds, in the same country, dwelling in the fields, and keeping the watches of the night over their flock.
text.KJV: And there were in the same countrey shepheards abiding in þ field, keeping watch ouer their flocke by night.
text.NABRE: Now there were shepherds in that region living in the fields and keeping the night watch over their flock.
text.DRB: And there were in the same country shepherds watching, and keeping the night watches over their flock.

text.NRSV: Then an angel of the Lord stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.
text.Rotherham: And, a messenger of the Lord, stood over them, and, a glory of the Lord, shone round about them; and they feared, a great fear.
text.KJV: And loe, the Angel of the Lord came vpon them, and the glory of the Lord shone round about them, and they were sore afraid.
text.NABRE: The angel of the Lord appeared to them and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were struck with great fear.
text.DRB: And behold, an Angel of our Lord stood beside them, and the brightness of God did shine round about them, and they feared with a great fear.

text.NRSV: But the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid; for see—I am bringing you good news of great joy for all the people:
text.Rotherham: And the messenger said unto them—Be not afraid! For lo! I bring you good tidings of a great joy, the which shall be for all the people:
text.KJV: And the Angel said vnto them, Feare not: For behold, I bring you good tidings of great ioy, which shall be to all people.
text.NABRE: The angel said to them, “Do not be afraid; for behold, I proclaim to you good news of great joy that will be for all the people.
text.DRB: And the Angel said to them: Fear not; for behold I evangelize to you great joy, that shall be to all the people:

text.NRSV: to you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, who is the Messiah, the Lord.
text.Rotherham: That there hath been born unto you, this day, a saviour,—Who is Christ the Lord—in the city of David!
text.KJV: For vnto you is borne this day, in the citie of Dauid, a Sauiour, which is Christ the Lord.
text.NABRE: For today in the city of David a savior has been born for you who is Messiah and Lord.
text.DRB: because this day is born to you a Saviour which is Christ our Lord, in the city of David.

text.NRSV: This will be a sign for you: you will find a child wrapped in bands of cloth and lying in a manger.”
text.Rotherham: And, this, to you is, a sign: Ye shall find a babe, wrapped in swaddling clothes, and lying in a manger.
text.KJV: And this shall be a signe vnto you; yee shall find the babe wrapped in swadling clothes lying in a manger.
text.NABRE: And this will be a sign for you: you will find an infant wrapped in swaddling clothes and lying in a manger.”
text.DRB: And this shall be a sign to you; You shall find the infant swaddled in cloths, and laid in a manger.

text.NRSV: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host, praising God and saying,
text.Rotherham: And, suddenly, there came to be, with the messenger, the throng of the heavenly host, praising God, and saying—
text.KJV: And suddenly there was with the Angel a multitude of the heauenly hoste praising God, and saying,
text.NABRE: And suddenly there was a multitude of the heavenly host with the angel, praising God and saying:
text.DRB: And suddenly there was with the Angel a multitude of the heavenly army, praising God, and saying,

text.NRSV: “Glory to God in the highest heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors!”
text.Rotherham: Glory, in the highest, unto God! And, on earth, peace, among men of goodwill.
text.KJV: Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good wil towards men.
text.NABRE: “Glory to God in the highest and on earth peace to those on whom his favor rests.”
text.DRB: Glory in the highest to God: and in earth peace to men of good will.

text.NRSV: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let us go now to Bethlehem and see this thing that has taken place, which the Lord has made known to us.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the messengers had departed from them into heaven, that, the shepherds, began to say one to another—Let us go through forthwith as far as to Bethlehem, and see this thing which hath come to pass, which the Lord hath made known to us.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, as the Angels were gone away from them into heauen, the shepheards said one to another, Let vs now goe euen vnto Bethlehem, and see this thing which is come to passe, which the Lord hath made knowen vnto vs.
text.NABRE: The Visit of the Shepherds. When the angels went away from them to heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let us go, then, to Bethlehem to see this thing that has taken place, which the Lord has made known to us.”
text.DRB: And it came to pass, after the Angels departed from them into Heaven, the shepherds spake one to another: Let us go over to Bethlehem, and let us see this word that is done, which our Lord hath shewed to us.

text.NRSV: So they went with haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the child lying in the manger.
text.Rotherham: And they came, with haste, and found both Mary and Joseph, and the babe lying in the manger.
text.KJV: And they came with haste, and found Mary and Ioseph, and the babe lying in a manger.
text.NABRE: So they went in haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the infant lying in the manger.
text.DRB: And they came with speed; and they found Mary and Joseph, and the infant laid in the manger.

text.NRSV: When they saw this, they made known what had been told them about this child;
text.Rotherham: Now, when they beheld, they made known concerning the thing which had been told them as to this child.
text.KJV: And when they had seene it, they made knowen abroad the saying, which was told them, concerning this child.
text.NABRE: When they saw this, they made known the message that had been told them about this child.
text.DRB: And seeing it, they understood of the word that had been spoken to them concerning this child.

text.NRSV: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds told them.
text.Rotherham: And, all who heard, marvelled concerning the things which had been told by the shepherds unto them;
text.KJV: And all they that heard it, wondered at those things, which were tolde them by the shepheards.
text.NABRE: All who heard it were amazed by what had been told them by the shepherds.
text.DRB: And all that heard, did marvel; and concerning those things that were reported to them by the shepherds.

text.NRSV: But Mary treasured all these words and pondered them in her heart.
text.Rotherham: but, Mary, was closely observing, all, these things, putting them together in her heart.
text.KJV: But Mary kept all these things, and pondered them in her heart.
text.NABRE: And Mary kept all these things, reflecting on them in her heart.
text.DRB: But Mary kept all these words, conferring them in her heart.

text.NRSV: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen, as it had been told them.
text.Rotherham: And the shepherds returned, giving glory and singing praise unto God, over all things which they had heard and seen, according as it had been told unto them.
text.KJV: And the shepheards returned, glorifying & praising God for all the things that they had heard and seene, as it was told vnto them.
text.NABRE: Then the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen, just as it had been told to them.
text.DRB: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God in all things that they had heard, and seen, as it was said to them.

text.NRSV: After eight days had passed, it was time to circumcise the child; and he was called Jesus, the name given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
text.Rotherham: And, when eight days were fulfilled for circumcising him, then was his name called, Jesus,—which it was called by the messenger, before he was conceived in the womb.
text.KJV: And when eight dayes were accomplished for the circumcising of the childe, his name was called Iesus, which was so named of the Angel before he was conceiued in the wombe.
text.NABRE: The Circumcision and Naming of Jesus. When eight days were completed for his circumcision, he was named Jesus, the name given him by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
text.DRB: And after eight days were expired, that the child should be circumcised; his name was called Jesus, which was called by the Angel, before that he was conceived in the womb.

text.NRSV: When the time came for their purification according to the law of Moses, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
text.Rotherham: And, when the days of their purification, according to the law of Moses, were fulfilled, they took him up into Jerusalem, to present [him] unto the Lord,—
text.KJV: And when the dayes of her purification according to the law of Moses, were accomplished, they brought him to Hierusalem, to present him to the Lord,
text.NABRE: The Presentation in the Temple. When the days were completed for their purification according to the law of Moses, they took him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord,
text.DRB: And after the days were fully ended of her purification according to the law of Moyses, they carried him into Jerusalem, to present him to our Lord

text.NRSV: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every firstborn male shall be designated as holy to the Lord”),
text.Rotherham: according as it is written in the law of the Lord—Every male that is a firstborn, Holy unto the Lord, shall be called,
text.KJV: (As it is written in the law of the Lord, Euery male that openeth the wombe, shalbe called holy to the Lord)
text.NABRE: just as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every male that opens the womb shall be consecrated to the Lord,”
text.DRB: (as it is written in the law of our Lord, That every male opening the matrice, shall be called holy to the Lord. )

text.NRSV: and they offered a sacrifice according to what is stated in the law of the Lord, “a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons.”
text.Rotherham: and to give a sacrifice, according to that which is said in the law of the Lord—A pair of turtle-doves, or two young pigeons.
text.KJV: And to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the Law of the Lord, a paire of turtle doues, or two yong pigeons.
text.NABRE: and to offer the sacrifice of “a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons,” in accordance with the dictate in the law of the Lord.
text.DRB: and to give a sacrifice according as it is written in the law of our Lord, a pair of turtles, or two young pigeons.

text.NRSV: Now there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; this man was righteous and devout, looking forward to the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
text.Rotherham: And lo! there was, a man, in Jerusalem, whose name, was Symeon; and, this man, was righteous and devout, awaiting the consolation of Israel, and Holy Spirit was upon him;
text.KJV: And behold, there was a man in Hierusalem, whose name was Simeon, and the same man was iust and deuout, waiting for the consolation of Israel: and the holy Ghost was vpon him.
text.NABRE: Now there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon. This man was righteous and devout, awaiting the consolation of Israel, and the holy Spirit was upon him.
text.DRB: And behold there was a man in Jerusalem named Simeon, and this man was just and religious, expecting the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was in him.

text.NRSV: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Messiah.
text.Rotherham: and it had been intimated to him by the Holy Spirit, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Christ of the Lord.
text.KJV: And it was reuealed vnto him by the holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seene the Lords Christ.
text.NABRE: It had been revealed to him by the holy Spirit that he should not see death before he had seen the Messiah of the Lord.
text.DRB: And he had received an answer of the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death unless he saw first the Christ of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Guided by the Spirit, Simeon came into the temple; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him what was customary under the law,
text.Rotherham: And he came, in the Spirit, into the temple; and, when the parents brought in the child Jesus, that they might do according to that which was customary by the law concerning it,
text.KJV: And hee came by the spirit into the Temple: and when the parents brought in the child Iesus, to doe for him after the custome of the Lawe,
text.NABRE: He came in the Spirit into the temple; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus to perform the custom of the law in regard to him,
text.DRB: And he came in Spirit into the temple. And when his parents brought in the child Jesus, to do according to the custom of the Law for him,

text.NRSV: Simeon took him in his arms and praised God, saying,
text.Rotherham: even he, welcomed it into his arms, and blessed God, and said—
text.KJV: Then tooke hee him vp in his armes, and blessed God, and said,
text.NABRE: he took him into his arms and blessed God, saying:
text.DRB: he also took him into his arms, and blessed God, and said:

text.NRSV: “Master, now you are dismissing your servant in peace, according to your word;
text.Rotherham: Now, dost thou dismiss thy servant, O Sovereign, according to thy declaration—in peace;
text.KJV: Lord now lettest thou thy seruant depart in peace, according to thy word.
text.NABRE: “Now, Master, you may let your servant go in peace, according to your word,
text.DRB: Now Thou doest dismiss thy servant O Lord, according to thy word in peace.

text.NRSV: for my eyes have seen your salvation,
text.Rotherham: Because mine eyes have seen thy salvation,
text.KJV: For mine eyes haue seene thy saluation.
text.NABRE: for my eyes have seen your salvation,
text.DRB: Because mine eyes have seen thy Salvation,

text.NRSV: which you have prepared in the presence of all peoples,
text.Rotherham: Which thou hast prepared in face of all the peoples:
text.KJV: Which thou hast prepared before the face of all people.
text.NABRE: which you prepared in sight of all the peoples,
text.DRB: Which thou hast prepared before the face of all peoples:

text.NRSV: a light for revelation to the Gentiles and for glory to your people Israel.”
text.Rotherham: A light for the unveiling of nations, and the glory of thy people Israel.
text.KJV: A light to lighten the Gentiles, and the glory of thy people Israel.
text.NABRE: a light for revelation to the Gentiles, and glory for your people Israel.”
text.DRB: A light to the revelation of the Gentiles, & the glory of thy people Israel.

text.NRSV: And the child’s father and mother were amazed at what was being said about him.
text.Rotherham: And his father and mother were marvelling over the things which were being spoken concerning him.
text.KJV: And Ioseph and his mother marueiled at those things which were spoken of him.
text.NABRE: The child’s father and mother were amazed at what was said about him;
text.DRB: And his father and mother were marveling upon those things which were spoken concerning him.

text.NRSV: Then Simeon blessed them and said to his mother Mary, “This child is destined for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be opposed
text.Rotherham: And Symeon blessed them, and said unto Mary his mother—Lo! this one, is being set for the falling and rising of many in Israel, and for a sign to be spoken against;
text.KJV: And Simeon blessed them, and said vnto Marie his mother, Behold, this child is set for the fall and rising againe of many in Israel: and for a signe which shall be spoken against,
text.NABRE: and Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, “Behold, this child is destined for the fall and rise of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be contradicted
text.DRB: And Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary his mother: Behold this is set unto the ruin, and unto the resurrection of many in Israel, & for a sign which shall be contradicted,

text.NRSV: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed—and a sword will pierce your own soul too.”
text.Rotherham: And, thou! through thy very soul, shall pass a sword, that, reasonings, may be revealed, out of many hearts.
text.KJV: (Yea a sword shall pearce thorow thy owne soule also) that the thoughts of many hearts may be reuealed.
text.NABRE: (and you yourself a sword will pierce) so that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.”
text.DRB: and thine own soul shall a sword pierce, that out of many hearts cogitations may be revealed.

text.NRSV: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was of a great age, having lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
text.Rotherham: And there was one Anna, a prophetess, daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher;—the same, advanced in many days; having lived with a husband seven years from her virginity,
text.KJV: And there was one Anna a Prophetesse, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Aser; she was of a great age, and had liued with an husband seuen yeeres from her virginitie.
text.NABRE: There was also a prophetess, Anna, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was advanced in years, having lived seven years with her husband after her marriage,
text.DRB: And there was Anne a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Aser: she was far striken in days, and had lived with her husband seven years from her virginity.

text.NRSV: then as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the temple but worshiped there with fasting and prayer night and day.
text.Rotherham: and, she, had been a widow for as long as eighty-four years,—who left not the temple, with fastings and supplications, rendering divine service night and day;—
text.KJV: And she was a widow of about fourescore and foure yeeres, which departed not from the Temple, but serued God with fastings and prayers night and day.
text.NABRE: and then as a widow until she was eighty-four. She never left the temple, but worshiped night and day with fasting and prayer.
text.DRB: And she was a widow until eighty and four years; who departed not from the temple, by fastings and prayers serving night and day.

text.NRSV: At that moment she came, and began to praise God and to speak about the child to all who were looking for the redemption of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and, in that very hour, coming near, she began to give thanks unto God, and to speak concerning him unto all who were waiting for the redemption of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And she comming in that instant, gaue thankes likewise vnto the Lord, and spake of him to al them that looked for redemption in Hierusalem.
text.NABRE: And coming forward at that very time, she gave thanks to God and spoke about the child to all who were awaiting the redemption of Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And she at the same hour suddenly coming in, confessed to our Lord; and spake of him to all that expected the redemption of Israel.

text.NRSV: When they had finished everything required by the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had finished all things that were according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, into their own city, Nazareth.
text.KJV: And when they had performed all things according to the Lawe of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their owne citie Nazareth.
text.NABRE: The Return to Nazareth. When they had fulfilled all the prescriptions of the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.
text.DRB: And after they had wholly done all things according to the law of our Lord, they returned into Galilee, into their city Nazareth.

text.NRSV: The child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom; and the favor of God was upon him.
text.Rotherham: And, the child, went on growing, and waxing strong, becoming filled with wisdom; and, the favour of God, was upon it.
text.KJV: And the child grew, and waxed strong in spirit filled with wisedome, and the grace of God was vpon him.
text.NABRE: The child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom; and the favor of God was upon him. The Boy Jesus in the Temple.
text.DRB: And the child grew, and waxed strong: full of wisdom, and the grace of God was in him.

text.NRSV: Now every year his parents went to Jerusalem for the festival of the Passover.
text.Rotherham: And his parents used to journey yearly into Jerusalem, at the feast of the passover.
text.KJV: Now his parents went to Hierusalem euery yeere, at the feast of the Passeouer.
text.NABRE: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the feast of Passover,
text.DRB: And his parents went every year unto Jerusalem, at the solemn day of Pasch.

text.NRSV: And when he was twelve years old, they went up as usual for the festival.
text.Rotherham: And, when he became twelve years, and they went up, according to the custom of the feast,
text.KJV: And when he was twelue yeeres old, they went vp to Hierusalem, after the custome of the feast.
text.NABRE: and when he was twelve years old, they went up according to festival custom.
text.DRB: And when he was twelve years old, they going up into Jerusalem according to the custom of the festival day;

text.NRSV: When the festival was ended and they started to return, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but his parents did not know it.
text.Rotherham: and completed the days, and then were returning, the boy Jesus remained behind in Jerusalem,—and his parents noticed it not;
text.KJV: And when they had fulfilled the dayes, as they returned, the childe Iesus taried behind in Hierusalem, and Ioseph and his mother knew not of it.
text.NABRE: After they had completed its days, as they were returning, the boy Jesus remained behind in Jerusalem, but his parents did not know it.
text.DRB: and having ended the days, when they returned, the child Jesus remained in Jerusalem: and his parents knew it not.

text.NRSV: Assuming that he was in the group of travelers, they went a day’s journey. Then they started to look for him among their relatives and friends.
text.Rotherham: but, supposing him to be in the company, went a day’s journey, and then began to seek for him among their kinsfolk and acquaintances,
text.KJV: But they supposing him to haue bene in the company, went a daies iourney, and they sought him among their kinsefolke and acquaintance.
text.NABRE: Thinking that he was in the caravan, they journeyed for a day and looked for him among their relatives and acquaintances,
text.DRB: And thinking that he was in the company, they came a day's journey, and sought him among their kinsfolk and acquaintance.

text.NRSV: When they did not find him, they returned to Jerusalem to search for him.
text.Rotherham: and, not finding him, returned unto Jerusalem, seeking him.
text.KJV: And when they found him not, they turned backe againe to Hierusalem, seeking him.
text.NABRE: but not finding him, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
text.DRB: And not finding him, they returned into Jerusalem, seeking him.

text.NRSV: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that, after three days, they found him in the temple, sitting amidst the teachers,—both hearkening unto them, and questioning them.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that after three daies they found him in the Temple, sitting in the midst of the Doctours, both hearing them, and asking them questions.
text.NABRE: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions,
text.DRB: And it came to pass, after three days they found him in the temple sitting in the midst of the Doctors, hearing them and asking them.

text.NRSV: And all who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.
text.Rotherham: Now all who heard him were beside themselves, because of his understanding and his answers.
text.KJV: And all that heard him were astonished at his vnderstanding, and answeres.
text.NABRE: and all who heard him were astounded at his understanding and his answers.
text.DRB: And all were astonished that heard him, upon his wisdom & answers.

text.NRSV: When his parents saw him they were astonished; and his mother said to him, “Child, why have you treated us like this? Look, your father and I have been searching for you in great anxiety.”
text.Rotherham: And, when they behold him, they were astounded, and his mother said unto him—Child! why, hast thou dealt with us, thus? Lo! thy father and I, in anguish, were seeking thee.
text.KJV: And when they sawe him, they were amazed: and his mother said vnto him, Sonne, why hast thou thus dealt with vs? Behold, thy father and I haue sought thee sorrowing.
text.NABRE: When his parents saw him, they were astonished, and his mother said to him, “Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been looking for you with great anxiety.”
text.DRB: And seeing him, they wondered. And his mother said to him: Son, why hast thou so done to us? Behold thy father and I sorrowing did seek thee.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Why were you searching for me? Did you not know that I must be in my Father’s house?”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Why was it that ye were seeking me? Perceived ye not, that, in the courts of my Father, I must needs be?
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, How is it that ye sought me? Wist yee not that I must bee about my fathers businesse?
text.NABRE: And he said to them, “Why were you looking for me? Did you not know that I must be in my Father’s house?”
text.DRB: And he said to them: What is it that you sought me? Did you not know, that I must be about those things which are my Father's?

text.NRSV: But they did not understand what he said to them.
text.Rotherham: And, they, understood not the thing which he spake to them.
text.KJV: And they vnderstood not the saying which he spake vnto them.
text.NABRE: But they did not understand what he said to them.
text.DRB: And they understood not the word that he spake unto them.

text.NRSV: Then he went down with them and came to Nazareth, and was obedient to them. His mother treasured all these things in her heart.
text.Rotherham: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth, and was submitting himself unto them. And, his mother, was closely observing all these things in her heart.
text.KJV: And he went downe with them, and came to Nazareth, and was subiect vnto them: But his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.
text.NABRE: He went down with them and came to Nazareth, and was obedient to them; and his mother kept all these things in her heart.
text.DRB: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth; and was subject to them. And his mother kept all these words in her heart.

text.NRSV: And Jesus increased in wisdom and in years, and in divine and human favor.
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, went on advancing in wisdom, and in stature, and in favour with God and men.
text.KJV: And Iesus increased in wisedom and stature, and in fauour with God and man.
text.NABRE: And Jesus advanced [in] wisdom and age and favor before God and man.
text.DRB: And Jesus proceeded in wisdom and age, and grace with God and men.

text.NRSV: In the fifteenth year of the reign of Emperor Tiberius, when Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, and Herod was ruler of Galilee, and his brother Philip ruler of the region of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias ruler of Abilene,
text.Rotherham: Now, in the fifteenth year of the supremacy of Tiberius Caesar,—Pontius Pilate being governor of Judaea, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and, Philip his brother, tetrarch of Ituraea and the country of Trachonitis, and Lysanius, tetrarch of Abylene,—
text.KJV: Now in the fifteenth yeere of the reigne of Tiberius Cesar, Pontius Pilate being Gouernour of Iudea, & Herode being Tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip Tetrarch of Iturea, and of the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanias the Tetrarch of Abilene,
text.NABRE: III. The Preparation for the Public Ministry Chapter 3 The Preaching of John the Baptist. In the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, when Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, and Herod was tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of the region of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was tetrarch of Abilene,
text.DRB: And in the fifteenth year of the empire of Tiberius Cæsar, Pontius Pilate being Governor of Jewry, and Herod being Tetrarch of Galilee, and Philip his brother Tetrarch of Iturea and the country Trachonitis, and Lysanias Tetrarch of Abilina,

text.NRSV: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: in the High-priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, came a word of God unto John the son, of Zechariah, in rue desert;
text.KJV: Annas and Caiaphas being the high Priests, the word of God came vnto Iohn the sonne of Zacharias, in the wildernesse.
