text.NABRE: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John the son of Zechariah in the desert.
text.DRB: under the high Priests Annas and Caiphas: the word of our Lord was made upon John the son of Zacharie, in the desert.

text.NRSV: He went into all the region around the Jordan, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins,
text.Rotherham: and he came into all the country about the Jordan, proclaiming an immersion of repentance into remission of sins:
text.KJV: And he came into all the countrey about Iordane, preaching the baptisme of repentance, for the remissiō of sinnes,
text.NABRE: He went throughout [the] whole region of the Jordan, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins,
text.DRB: And he came into all the country of Jordan, preaching the baptism of penance unto remission of sins; as it is written in the book of the sayings of Esay the Prophet:

text.NRSV: as it is written in the book of the words of the prophet Isaiah, “The voice of one crying out in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
text.Rotherham: as it is written in the book of the discourses of Isaiah the prophet: A voice of one crying aloud! In the desert, prepare ye the way of the Lord, straight, be making his paths;
text.KJV: As it is written in the book of the words of Esaias the Prophet, saying, The voyce of one crying in the wildernesse, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
text.NABRE: as it is written in the book of the words of the prophet Isaiah: “A voice of one crying out in the desert: ‘Prepare the way of the Lord, make straight his paths.
text.DRB: A voice of one crying in the desert; prepare the way of our Lord, make straight his paths.

text.NRSV: Every valley shall be filled, and every mountain and hill shall be made low, and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough ways made smooth;
text.Rotherham: Every chasm, shall be filled up, and, every mountain and hill, be made low; and the, crooked, places shall become, straight, and the, rugged, places, smooth ways;—
text.KJV: Euery valley shall be filled, and euery mountaine and hill shalbe brought low, and the crooked shall bee made straight, and the rough wayes shall be made smooth.
text.NABRE: Every valley shall be filled and every mountain and hill shall be made low. The winding roads shall be made straight, and the rough ways made smooth,
text.DRB: Every valley shall be filled; and every mountain and hill shall be made low, and crooked things shall become straight; and rough ways, plain:

text.NRSV: and all flesh shall see the salvation of God.’”
text.Rotherham: And all flesh shall see the salvation of God.
text.KJV: And all flesh shal see the saluation of God.
text.NABRE: and all flesh shall see the salvation of God.’”
text.DRB: And all flesh shall see the Salvation of God.

text.NRSV: John said to the crowds that came out to be baptized by him, “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
text.Rotherham: He was saying, therefore, unto the multitudes who were going forth to be immersed by him—Broods of vipers! who suggested to you, to flee from the coming wrath?
text.KJV: Then said hee to the multitude that came forth to bee baptized of him, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
text.NABRE: He said to the crowds who came out to be baptized by him, “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?
text.DRB: He said therefore to the multitudes that went forth to be baptized of him: Ye vipers' broods, who hath shewed you to flee from the wrath to come?

text.NRSV: Bear fruits worthy of repentance. Do not begin to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our ancestor’; for I tell you, God is able from these stones to raise up children to Abraham.
text.Rotherham: Bring forth, therefore, fruits worthy of repentance, and do not begin to be saying within yourselves, As our father, we have, Abraham; for, I say unto you—God is able, out of these stones, to raise up children unto Abraham.
text.KJV: Bring forth therfore fruits worthy of repentance, and begin not to say within your selues, We haue Abraham to our father: For I say vnto you, that God is able of these stones to raise vp children vnto Abraham.
text.NABRE: Produce good fruits as evidence of your repentance; and do not begin to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father,’ for I tell you, God can raise up children to Abraham from these stones.
text.DRB: Yield therefore fruits worthy of penance; and do ye not begin to say, we have Abraham, to our father. For I tell you, that God is able of these stones to raise up children to Abraham.

text.NRSV: Even now the ax is lying at the root of the trees; every tree therefore that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.”
text.Rotherham: And, already, even the axe, unto the root of the trees is being laid; every tree, therefore, not bringing forth [good] fruit, is to be hewn down, and, into fire, to be cast.
text.KJV: And now also the axe is laid vnto the root of the trees: Euery tree therefore which bringeth not foorth good fruit, is hewen downe, and cast into the fire.
text.NABRE: Even now the ax lies at the root of the trees. Therefore every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.”
text.DRB: And now the axe is put to the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that yieldeth not good fruit, shall be cut down, and cast into fire.

text.NRSV: And the crowds asked him, “What then should we do?”
text.Rotherham: And the multitude began to question him saying—What, then, shall we do?
text.KJV: And the people asked him, saying, What shall we doe then?
text.NABRE: And the crowds asked him, “What then should we do?”
text.DRB: And the multitudes asked him, saying: What shall we do then?

text.NRSV: In reply he said to them, “Whoever has two coats must share with anyone who has none; and whoever has food must do likewise.”
text.Rotherham: And, answering, he said unto them—He that hath two tunics, let him share with him that hath none, and, he that hath food, in like manner, let him be doing.
text.KJV: He answereth, and saith vnto them, He that hath two coats, let him impart to him that hath none, and he that hath meat, let him doe likewise.
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “Whoever has two tunics should share with the person who has none. And whoever has food should do likewise.”
text.DRB: And he answering, said unto them: He that hath two coats, let him give to him that hath not; and he that hath meat, let him do likewise.

text.NRSV: Even tax collectors came to be baptized, and they asked him, “Teacher, what should we do?”
text.Rotherham: And there came, even tax-collectors, to be immersed; and they said unto him—Teacher! what, shall we, do?
text.KJV: Then came also Publicanes to be baptized, and said vnto him, Master, what shall we doe?
text.NABRE: Even tax collectors came to be baptized and they said to him, “Teacher, what should we do?”
text.DRB: And the Publicans also came to be baptized, and said to him: Master, what shall we do?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Collect no more than the amount prescribed for you.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them, Nothing more than what is appointed you, exact ye.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Exact no more then that which is appointed you.
text.NABRE: He answered them, “Stop collecting more than what is prescribed.”
text.DRB: But he said to them: Do nothing more than that which is appointed you.

text.NRSV: Soldiers also asked him, “And we, what should we do?” He said to them, “Do not extort money from anyone by threats or false accusation, and be satisfied with your wages.”
text.Rotherham: Then were questioning him, soldiers also, saying—What shall, even we, do? And he said unto them—Molest ye, no one, neither accuse falsely; and be content with your supplies.
text.KJV: And the souldiers likewise demanded of him, saying, And what shall we doe? And he said vnto them, Doe violence to no man, neither accuse any falsely, & be content with your wages.
text.NABRE: Soldiers also asked him, “And what is it that we should do?” He told them, “Do not practice extortion, do not falsely accuse anyone, and be satisfied with your wages.”
text.DRB: And the soldiers also asked him, saying: What shall we also do? And he said to them: Vex not, neither calumniate any man; and be content with your stipends.

text.NRSV: As the people were filled with expectation, and all were questioning in their hearts concerning John, whether he might be the Messiah,
text.Rotherham: Now, as the people were in expectation, and all were deliberating in their hearts, concerning John,—whether by any means, he, might be, the Christ,
text.KJV: And as the people were in expectation, and all men mused in their hearts of Iohn, whether he were the Christ or not:
text.NABRE: Now the people were filled with expectation, and all were asking in their hearts whether John might be the Messiah.
text.DRB: And the people imagining, and all men thinking in their hearts of John, lest perhaps he were Christ:

text.NRSV: John answered all of them by saying, “I baptize you with water; but one who is more powerful than I is coming; I am not worthy to untie the thong of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
text.Rotherham: John answered, saying unto all—I, indeed, in water, am immersing you, but he that is mightier than I, cometh, the thong of whose sandals I am not worthy to unloose,—he, will immerse you in Holy Spirit and fire:
text.KJV: Iohn answered, saying vnto them all, I indeede baptize you with water, but one mightier then I commeth, the latchet of whose shooes I am not worthy to vnloose, he shall baptize you with the holy Ghost, and with fire.
text.NABRE: John answered them all, saying, “I am baptizing you with water, but one mightier than I is coming. I am not worthy to loosen the thongs of his sandals. He will baptize you with the holy Spirit and fire.
text.DRB: John answered, saying unto all: I indeed baptize you with water, but there shall come a mightier than I, whose latchet of his shoes I am not worthy to loose; he shall baptize you in the Holy Ghost and fire:

text.NRSV: His winnowing fork is in his hand, to clear his threshing floor and to gather the wheat into his granary; but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.”
text.Rotherham: whose fan is in his hand, to clear out his threshing-floor, and to gather the wheat into his granary; but, the chaff, will he burn up with fire unquenchable.
text.KJV: Whose fanne is in his hand, and he will thorowly purge his floore, and will gather the wheat into his garner, but the chaffe he will burne with fire vnquencheable.
text.NABRE: His winnowing fan is in his hand to clear his threshing floor and to gather the wheat into his barn, but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.”
text.DRB: whose fan is in his hand, and he will purge his floor; and will gather the wheat into his barn, but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.

text.NRSV: So, with many other exhortations, he proclaimed the good news to the people.
text.Rotherham: So then indeed, as to many things and various, he exhorted, and continued telling his glad-message unto the people.
text.KJV: And many other things in his exhortation preached he vnto the people.
text.NABRE: Exhorting them in many other ways, he preached good news to the people.
text.DRB: Many other things also exhorting did he evangelize to the people.

text.NRSV: But Herod the ruler, who had been rebuked by him because of Herodias, his brother’s wife, and because of all the evil things that Herod had done,
text.Rotherham: But, Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him concerning Herodias, the wife of his brother, and concerning all the wicked things Herod had done,
text.KJV: But Herode the Tetrarch being reprooued by him for Herodias his brother Philips wife, and for all the euils which Herode had done,
text.NABRE: Now Herod the tetrarch, who had been censured by him because of Herodias, his brother’s wife, and because of all the evil deeds Herod had committed,
text.DRB: And Herod the Tetrarch, when he was rebuked of him for Herodias his brother's wife, and for all the evils which Herod did:

text.NRSV: added to them all by shutting up John in prison.
text.Rotherham: added this also unto all,—he locked up John in prison.
text.KJV: Added yet this aboue all, that he shut vp Iohn in prison.
text.NABRE: added still another to these by [also] putting John in prison. The Baptism of Jesus.
text.DRB: He added this also above all, and shut up John into prison.

text.NRSV: Now when all the people were baptized, and when Jesus also had been baptized and was praying, the heaven was opened,
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass, when one and all the people were immersed, Jesus also, having been immersed, and being at prayer, heaven was opened;
text.KJV: Now when all the people were baptized, and it came to passe that Iesus also being baptized, and praying, the heauen was opened:
text.NABRE: After all the people had been baptized and Jesus also had been baptized and was praying, heaven was opened
text.DRB: And it came to pass when all the people was baptized, Jesus also being baptized and praying, Heaven was opened:

text.NRSV: and the Holy Spirit descended upon him in bodily form like a dove. And a voice came from heaven, “You are my Son, the Beloved; with you I am well pleased.”
text.Rotherham: and the Holy Spirit descended, in bodily appearance, as a dove, upon him,—and, a voice out of heaven, came—Thou, art my Son, the Beloved, in thee, I delight.
text.KJV: And the holy Ghost descended in a bodily shape like a Doue vpon him, and a voice came from heauen, which said, Thou art my beloued sonne, in thee I am well pleased.
text.NABRE: and the holy Spirit descended upon him in bodily form like a dove. And a voice came from heaven, “You are my beloved Son; with you I am well pleased.” The Genealogy of Jesus.
text.DRB: And the Holy Ghost descended in corporal shape as a dove upon him: and a voice from Heaven was made: Thou art my beloved Son, in thee I am well pleased.

text.NRSV: Jesus was about thirty years old when he began his work. He was the son (as was thought) of Joseph son of Heli,
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus himself, was, when he began, about thirty years of age, being the son, as was supposed—of Joseph, of Heli:
text.KJV: And Iesus himselfe began to be about thirty yeeres of age, being (as was supposed) the sonne of Ioseph, which was the sonne of Heli,
text.NABRE: When Jesus began his ministry he was about thirty years of age. He was the son, as was thought, of Joseph, the son of Heli,
text.DRB: And Jesus himself was beginning to be about thirty years old: as it was thought, the son of Joseph, who was of Heli,

text.NRSV: son of Matthat, son of Levi, son of Melchi, son of Jannai, son of Joseph,
text.Rotherham: of Matthat, of Levi, of Melchi,—of Jannai, of Joseph:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Matthat, which was the sonne of Leui, which was the sonne of Melchi, which was the sonne of Ianna, which was the sonne of Ioseph,
text.NABRE: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,
text.DRB: who was of Matthat, who was of Levi, who was of Melchi, who was of Ianne, who was of Joseph,

text.NRSV: son of Mattathias, son of Amos, son of Nahum, son of Esli, son of Naggai,
text.Rotherham: of Mattathias, of Amos, of Nahum,—of Esli, of Naggai:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Matthathias, which was the sonne of Amos, which was the sonne of Naum, which was the sonne of Eli, which was the sonne of Nagge,
text.NABRE: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,
text.DRB: who was of Matthathias, who was of Amos, who was of Naum, who was of Hesli, who was of Nagge,

text.NRSV: son of Maath, son of Mattathias, son of Semein, son of Josech, son of Joda,
text.Rotherham: of Maath, of Mattathias, of Semein,—of Josech, of Joda:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Maath, which was the sonne of Matthathias, which was the sonne of Semei, which was the sonne of Ioseph, which was the sonne of Iuda,
text.NABRE: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Josech, the son of Joda,
text.DRB: who was of Mahath, who was of Matthathias, who was of Semei, who was of Joseph, who was of Juda,

text.NRSV: son of Joanan, son of Rhesa, son of Zerubbabel, son of Shealtiel, son of Neri,
text.Rotherham: of Joanan, of Rhesa, of Zerubbabel,—of Salathiel, of Neri:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Ioanna, which was the sonne of Rhesa, which was the sonne of Zorobabel, which was the sonne of Salathiel, which was the sonne of Neri,
text.NABRE: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,
text.DRB: who was of Johanna, who was of Resa, who was of Zorobabel, who was of Salathiel, who was of Neri,

text.NRSV: son of Melchi, son of Addi, son of Cosam, son of Elmadam, son of Er,
text.Rotherham: of Melchi, of Addi, of Cosam,—of Elmadam, of Er:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Melchi, which was the sonne of Addi, which was the sonne of Cosam, which was the sonne of Elmodam, which was the sonne of Er,
text.NABRE: the son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmadam, the son of Er,
text.DRB: who was of Melchi, who was of Addi, who was of Cosam, who was of Elmadan, who was of Her,

text.NRSV: son of Joshua, son of Eliezer, son of Jorim, son of Matthat, son of Levi,
text.Rotherham: of Jesus, of Eliezer, of Jorim,—of Matthat, of Levi:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Iose, which was the sonne of Eliezer, which was the sonne of Iorim, which was the sonne of Matthat, which was the sonne of Leui,
text.NABRE: the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,
text.DRB: who was of Jesus, who was of Eliezer, who was of Iorim, who was of Matthat, who was of Levi,

text.NRSV: son of Simeon, son of Judah, son of Joseph, son of Jonam, son of Eliakim,
text.Rotherham: of Symeon, of Judas, of Joseph,—of Jonam, of Eliakim:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Simeon, which was the sonne of Iuda, which was the sonne of Ioseph, which was the sonne of Ionan, which was the sonne of Eliakim,
text.NABRE: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim,
text.DRB: who was of Simeon, who was of Judas, who was of Joseph, who was of Jona, who was of Eliacim.

text.NRSV: son of Melea, son of Menna, son of Mattatha, son of Nathan, son of David,
text.Rotherham: of Melea, of Menna, of Mattatha,—of Natham, of David:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Melea, which was the sonne of Menam, which was the sonne of Mattatha, which was the sonne of Nathan, which was the sonne of Dauid,
text.NABRE: the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,
text.DRB: who was of Melcha, who was of Menna, who was of Matthatha, who was of Nathan, who was of David,

text.NRSV: son of Jesse, son of Obed, son of Boaz, son of Sala, son of Nahshon,
text.Rotherham: of Jesse, of Obed, of Boaz,—of Salmon, of Nashon:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Iesse, which was the sonne of Obed, which was the sonne of Booz, which was the sonne of Salmon, which was the sonne of Nasson,
text.NABRE: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Sala, the son of Nahshon,
text.DRB: who was of Jesse, who was of Obed, who was of Booz, who was of Salmon, who was of Naasson,

text.NRSV: son of Amminadab, son of Admin, son of Arni, son of Hezron, son of Perez, son of Judah,
text.Rotherham: of Amminadab, of Arni, of Hezron,—of Perez, of Judah:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Aminadab, which was the sonne of Aram, which was the sonne of Esrom, which was the sonne of Phares, which was the sonne of Iuda,
text.NABRE: the son of Amminadab, the son of Admin, the son of Arni, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,
text.DRB: who was of Aminadab, who was of Aram, who was of Esron, who was of Phares, who was of Judas,

text.NRSV: son of Jacob, son of Isaac, son of Abraham, son of Terah, son of Nahor,
text.Rotherham: of Jacob, of Isaac, of Abraham,—of Terah, of Nahor:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Iacob, which was the sonne of Isaac, which was the sonne of Abraham, which was the sonne of Thara, which was the sonne of Nachor,
text.NABRE: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,
text.DRB: who was of Jacob, who was of Isaac, who was of Abraham, who was of Thare, who was of Nachor,

text.NRSV: son of Serug, son of Reu, son of Peleg, son of Eber, son of Shelah,
text.Rotherham: of Serug, of Reu, of Peleg,—of Eber, of Shelah:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Saruch, which was the sonne of Ragau, which was the sonne of Phaleg, which was the sonne of Heber, which was the sonne of Sala,
text.NABRE: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah,
text.DRB: who was of Sarug, who was of Ragau, who was of Phaleg, who was of Heber, who was of Sale,

text.NRSV: son of Cainan, son of Arphaxad, son of Shem, son of Noah, son of Lamech,
text.Rotherham: of Cainan, of Arphaxad, of Shem,—of Noah, of Lamech:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Cainan, which was the sonne of Arphaxad, which was the sonne of Sem, which was the sonne of Noe, which was the sonne of Lamech,
text.NABRE: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,
text.DRB: who was of Cainan, who was of Arphaxad, who was of Sem, who was of Noe, who was of Lamech,

text.NRSV: son of Methuselah, son of Enoch, son of Jared, son of Mahalaleel, son of Cainan,
text.Rotherham: of Methuselah, of Enoch, of Jared,—of Mahalaleel, of Cainan:
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Mathusala, which was the sonne of Enoch, which was the sonne of Iared, which was the sonne of Maleleel, which was the sonne of Cainan,
text.NABRE: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalaleel, the son of Cainan,
text.DRB: who was of Mathusale, who was of Henoch, who was of Jared, who was of Malaleel, who was of Cainan,

text.NRSV: son of Enos, son of Seth, son of Adam, son of God.
text.Rotherham: of Enos, of Seth,—of Adam; of God.
text.KJV: Which was the sonne of Enos, which was the sonne of Seth, which was the sonne of Adam, which was the sonne of God.
text.NABRE: the son of Enos, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.
text.DRB: who was of Henos, who was of Seth, who was of Adam, who was of God.

text.NRSV: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit in the wilderness,
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, full of Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and was led in the Spirit in the desert,
text.KJV: And Iesus being full of the holy Ghost, returned from Iordane, and was led by the spirit into the wildernesse,
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 The Temptation of Jesus. Filled with the holy Spirit, Jesus returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit into the desert
text.DRB: And Jesus full of the Holy Ghost, returned from Jordan, & was driven in the spirit into the desert.

text.NRSV: where for forty days he was tempted by the devil. He ate nothing at all during those days, and when they were over, he was famished.
text.Rotherham: forty days,—being tempted by the adversary; and he did eat nothing in those days,—and, when they were concluded, he hungered.
text.KJV: Being fourtie dayes tempted of the deuil, and in those dayes he did eat nothing: and when they were ended, he afterward hungred.
text.NABRE: for forty days, to be tempted by the devil. He ate nothing during those days, and when they were over he was hungry.
text.DRB: forty days, and was tempted of the devil. And he did eat nothing in those days; and when they were ended, he was an hungered.

text.NRSV: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.”
text.Rotherham: And the adversary said to him—If thou art God’s, Son, speak unto this stone; that it become bread.
text.KJV: And the deuil saide vnto him, If thou be the Sonne of God, command this stone that it be made bread.
text.NABRE: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.”
text.DRB: And the devil said to him: If thou be the Son of God, say to this stone that it be made bread.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “It is written, ‘One does not live by bread alone.’”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus made answer unto him—It is written: Not, on bread alone, shall, man, live.
text.KJV: And Iesus answered him, saying, It is written, that man shall not liue by bread alone, but by euery word of God.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered him, “It is written, ‘One does not live by bread alone.’”
text.DRB: And Jesus made answer unto him: It is written, That not in bread alone shall man live, but in every word of God.

text.NRSV: Then the devil led him up and showed him in an instant all the kingdoms of the world.
text.Rotherham: And, leading him up, he shewed him all the kingdoms of the inhabited earth, in a moment of time.
text.KJV: And the deuil taking him vp into an high mountaine, shewed vnto him all the kingdomes of the world in a moment of time.
text.NABRE: Then he took him up and showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a single instant.
text.DRB: And the devil brought him into an high mountain, and shewed him all the kingdoms of the whole world in a moment of time;

text.NRSV: And the devil said to him, “To you I will give their glory and all this authority; for it has been given over to me, and I give it to anyone I please.
text.Rotherham: And the adversary said to him—Unto thee, will I give this authority, all together, and their glory; because, unto me, hath it been delivered up, and, to whomsoever I please, I give it:
text.KJV: And the deuil said vnto him, All this power will I giue thee, and the glory of them; for that is deliuered vnto me, & to whomsoeuer I will, I giue it.
text.NABRE: The devil said to him, “I shall give to you all this power and their glory; for it has been handed over to me, and I may give it to whomever I wish.
text.DRB: and he said to him: To thee will I give this whole power, and the glory of them; for to me they are delivered, and to whom I will, I do give them.

text.NRSV: If you, then, will worship me, it will all be yours.”
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, if thou wilt worship before me, it shall all, be thine.
text.KJV: If thou therefore wilt worship me, all shalbe thine.
text.NABRE: All this will be yours, if you worship me.”
text.DRB: Thou therefore if thou wilt adore before me, they shall all be thine.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “It is written, ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve only him.’”
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus said to him—It is written: The Lord thy God, shalt thou worship, and, unto him alone, render divine service.
text.KJV: And Iesus answered and said vnto him, Get thee behinde me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him onely shalt thou serue.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him in reply, “It is written: ‘You shall worship the Lord, your God, and him alone shall you serve.’”
text.DRB: And Jesus answering said to him: It is written, Thou shalt adore the Lord thy God & him only shalt thou serve.

text.NRSV: Then the devil took him to Jerusalem, and placed him on the pinnacle of the temple, saying to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down from here,
text.Rotherham: And he led him into Jerusalem, and set him upon the pinnacle of the temple,—and said [to him]—If thou art God’s, Son, cast thyself, from hence, down;
text.KJV: And hee brought him to Hierusalem, and set him on a pinacle of the Temple, and said vnto him, If thou be the Sonne of God, cast thy selfe downe from hence.
text.NABRE: Then he led him to Jerusalem, made him stand on the parapet of the temple, and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down from here,
text.DRB: And he brought him into Jerusalem, & set him upon the pinnacle of the Temple; and he said to him: If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself from hence downward.

text.NRSV: for it is written, ‘He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you,’
text.Rotherham: for it is written—Unto his messengers, will he give command concerning thee, to keep vigilant watch over thee,—
text.KJV: For it is written, He shall giue his Angels charge ouer thee, to keepe thee.
text.NABRE: for it is written: ‘He will command his angels concerning you, to guard you,’
text.DRB: For it is written, that He hath given his Angels charge of thee, that they preserve thee:

text.NRSV: and ‘On their hands they will bear you up, so that you will not dash your foot against a stone.’”
text.Rotherham: And, On hands, will they take thee up, lest once thou strike, against a stone, thy foot.
text.KJV: And in their handes they shall beare thee vp, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.
text.NABRE: and: ‘With their hands they will support you, lest you dash your foot against a stone.’”
text.DRB: and that in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest perhaps thou knock thy foot against a stone.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered him, “It is said, ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, answering, said to him—It is said: Thou shalt not put to the test the Lord thy God.
text.KJV: And Iesus answering, said vnto him, It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him in reply, “It also says, ‘You shall not put the Lord, your God, to the test.’”
text.DRB: And Jesus answering said to him: It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.

text.NRSV: When the devil had finished every test, he departed from him until an opportune time.
text.Rotherham: And, having concluded every temptation, the adversary departed from him until a fitting season.
text.KJV: And when the deuil had ended all the temptation, hee departed from him for a season.
text.NABRE: When the devil had finished every temptation, he departed from him for a time.
text.DRB: And all the tentation being ended, the devil departed from him until a time.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus, filled with the power of the Spirit, returned to Galilee, and a report about him spread through all the surrounding country.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus returned, in the power of the Spirit, into Galilee; and, a report, went out along the whole of the region, concerning him;
text.KJV: ¶ And Iesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and there went out a fame of him through all the region round about.
text.NABRE: IV. The Ministry in Galilee The Beginning of the Galilean Ministry. Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news of him spread throughout the whole region.
text.DRB: And Jesus returned in the force of the spirit into Galilee, and the fame went forth through the whole country of him.

text.NRSV: He began to teach in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
text.Rotherham: and, he, began teaching in their synagogues, being glorified by all.
text.KJV: And hee taught in their Synagogues, being glorified of all.
text.NABRE: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by all. The Rejection at Nazareth.
text.DRB: And he taught in their Synagogues, and was magnified of all.

text.NRSV: When he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, he went to the synagogue on the sabbath day, as was his custom. He stood up to read,
text.Rotherham: And he came into Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and entered, according to his custom, on the sabbath day, into the synagogue,—and stood up to read.
text.KJV: ¶ And hee came to Nazareth, where he had bene brought vp, and as his custome was, he went into the Synagogue on the Sabbath day, and stood vp for to reade.
text.NABRE: He came to Nazareth, where he had grown up, and went according to his custom into the synagogue on the sabbath day. He stood up to read
text.DRB: And he came to Nazareth where he was brought up: and he entered according to his custom on the Sabboth day into the Synagogue; and he rose up to read.

text.NRSV: and the scroll of the prophet Isaiah was given to him. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written:
text.Rotherham: And there was handed to him a scroll of the prophet Isaiah; and unfolding the scroll, he found the place where it was written:
text.KJV: And there was deliuered vnto him the booke of the Prophet Esaias, and when he had opened the Booke, he found the place where it was written,
text.NABRE: and was handed a scroll of the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the passage where it was written:
text.DRB: And the book of Esay the Prophet was delivered unto him. And as he unfolded the book, he found the place where it was written:

text.NRSV: “The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to bring good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim release to the captives and recovery of sight to the blind, to let the oppressed go free,
text.Rotherham: The Spirit of the Lord, is upon me, because he hath anointed me—to tell glad tidings unto the destitute; He hath sent me forth,—To proclaim, to captives, a release, and, to the blind, a recovering of sight,—to send away the crushed, with a release;
text.KJV: The Spirit of the Lord is vpon mee, because hee hath anointed mee, to preach the Gospel to the poore, he hath sent mee to heale the broken hearted, to preach deliuerance to the captiues, and recouering of sight to the blinde, to set at libertie them that are bruised,
text.NABRE: “The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to bring glad tidings to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim liberty to captives and recovery of sight to the blind, to let the oppressed go free,
text.DRB: The Spirit of the Lord upon me, for which he anointed me, to evangelize unto the poor he sent me, to heal the contrite of heart,

text.NRSV: to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor.”
text.Rotherham: To proclaim the welcome year of the Lord.
text.KJV: To preach the acceptable yeere of the Lord.
text.NABRE: and to proclaim a year acceptable to the Lord.”
text.DRB: to preach to the captives remission, and sight to the blind, to dismiss the bruised unto remission, to preach the acceptable year of the Lord, and the day of retribution.

text.NRSV: And he rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed on him.
text.Rotherham: And, folding up the scroll, he handed it to the attendant, and sat down; and, the eyes of all, in the synagogue, were intently fixed upon him;
text.KJV: And he closed the booke, and hee gaue it againe to the minister, and sate downe: and the eyes of all them that were in the Synagogue were fastened on him.
text.NABRE: Rolling up the scroll, he handed it back to the attendant and sat down, and the eyes of all in the synagogue looked intently at him.
text.DRB: And when he had folded the book, he rendered it to the minister, and sat down. And the eyes of all in the Synagogue were bent upon him.

text.NRSV: Then he began to say to them, “Today this scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.”
text.Rotherham: and he began to be saying to them—This day, is fulfilled this scripture, in your ears.
text.KJV: And hee began to say vnto them, This day is this Scripture fulfilled in your eares.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Today this scripture passage is fulfilled in your hearing.”
text.DRB: And he began to say unto them: That this day is fulfilled this scripture in your ears.

text.NRSV: All spoke well of him and were amazed at the gracious words that came from his mouth. They said, “Is not this Joseph’s son?”
text.Rotherham: And, all, were bearing witness to him, and marvelling at the words of favour which were proceeding out of his mouth; and they were saying—Is not, this, the, son of Joseph?
text.KJV: And all bare him witnesse, and wondered at the gracious wordes, which proceeded out of his mouth. And they said, Is not this Iosephs sonne?
text.NABRE: And all spoke highly of him and were amazed at the gracious words that came from his mouth. They also asked, “Isn’t this the son of Joseph?”
text.DRB: And all gave testimony to him; and they marveled in the words of grace that proceeded from his mouth, and they said: Is not this Joseph's son?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Doubtless you will quote to me this proverb, ‘Doctor, cure yourself!’ And you will say, ‘Do here also in your hometown the things that we have heard you did at Capernaum.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—By all means, ye will speak to me this similitude: Physician! heal, thyself,—Whatsoever things we have heard of coming to pass in Capernaum, do here also, in thine own country.
text.KJV: And hee said vnto them, Yee will surely say vnto me this prouerbe, Physition, heale thy selfe: Whatsoeuer wee haue heard done in Capernaum, doe also here in thy countrey.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Surely you will quote me this proverb, ‘Physician, cure yourself,’ and say, ‘Do here in your native place the things that we heard were done in Capernaum.’”
text.DRB: And he said to them: Certes you will say to me this similitude, Physician, cure thyself: as great things as we have heard done in Capharnaum, do also here in thy country.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Truly I tell you, no prophet is accepted in the prophet’s hometown.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Verily, I say unto you, No prophet, is, welcome, in his own country,
text.KJV: And hee said, Verely I say vnto you, no Prophet is accepted in his owne countrey.
text.NABRE: And he said, “Amen, I say to you, no prophet is accepted in his own native place.
text.DRB: And he said: Amen I say to you, that no Prophet is accepted in his own country.

text.NRSV: But the truth is, there were many widows in Israel in the time of Elijah, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, and there was a severe famine over all the land;
text.Rotherham: And, of a truth, I say unto you—Many widows, were in the days of Elijah, in Israel, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, when there came a great famine upon all the land;
text.KJV: But I tell you of a trueth, many widowes were in Israel in the dayes of Elias, when the heauen was shut vp three yeres and sixe moneths: when great famine was throughout all the land:
text.NABRE: Indeed, I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah when the sky was closed for three and a half years and a severe famine spread over the entire land.
text.DRB: In truth I say to you, there were many widows in the days of Elias in Israel, when the Heaven was shut three years and six months, when there was a great famine made in the whole earth:

text.NRSV: yet Elijah was sent to none of them except to a widow at Zarephath in Sidon.
text.Rotherham: And, unto none of them, was Elijah sent, save unto Sarepta of Sidonia, unto a woman that was a widow.
text.KJV: But vnto none of them was Elias sent, saue vnto Sarepta a citie of Sidon, vnto a woman that was a widow.
text.NABRE: It was to none of these that Elijah was sent, but only to a widow in Zarephath in the land of Sidon.
text.DRB: And to none of them was Elias sent, but into Sarepta of Sidon, to a widow woman.

text.NRSV: There were also many lepers in Israel in the time of the prophet Elisha, and none of them was cleansed except Naaman the Syrian.”
text.Rotherham: And, many lepers, were in Israel, in the time of Elisha the prophet, and, not one of them, was cleansed, save Naaman the Syrian.
text.KJV: And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Elizeus the Prophet: and none of them was cleansed, sauing Naaman the Syrian.
text.NABRE: Again, there were many lepers in Israel during the time of Elisha the prophet; yet not one of them was cleansed, but only Naaman the Syrian.”
text.DRB: And there were many lepers in Israel under Elisæus the Prophet; and none of them was made clean but Naaman the Syrian.

text.NRSV: When they heard this, all in the synagogue were filled with rage.
text.Rotherham: And all were filled with wrath, in the synagogue, as they heard these things.
text.KJV: And all they in the Synagogue, when they heard these things, were filled with wrath,
text.NABRE: When the people in the synagogue heard this, they were all filled with fury.
text.DRB: And all in the Synagogue were filled with anger, hearing these things.

text.NRSV: They got up, drove him out of the town, and led him to the brow of the hill on which their town was built, so that they might hurl him off the cliff.
text.Rotherham: And, rising up, they thrust him forth outside the city, and led him as far as a brow of the hill on which their city was built,—so that they might throw him down headlong.
text.KJV: And rose vp, and thrust him out of the citie, & led him vnto the brow of the hill (whereon their city was built) that they might cast him downe headlong.
text.NABRE: They rose up, drove him out of the town, and led him to the brow of the hill on which their town had been built, to hurl him down headlong.
text.DRB: And they rose, and cast him out of the city: and they brought him to the edge of the hill, where upon their city was built, that they might throw him down headlong.

text.NRSV: But he passed through the midst of them and went on his way.
text.Rotherham: But, he, passing through the midst of them, went his way.
text.KJV: But he passing thorow the mids of them, went his way:
text.NABRE: But he passed through the midst of them and went away.
text.DRB: But he passing through the midst of them, went his way.

text.NRSV: He went down to Capernaum, a city in Galilee, and was teaching them on the sabbath.
text.Rotherham: And he came down into Capernaum, a city of Galilee. And he was teaching them on the sabbath;
text.KJV: And came downe to Capernaum, a citie of Galile, and taught them on the Sabbath dayes.
text.NABRE: The Cure of a Demoniac. Jesus then went down to Capernaum, a town of Galilee. He taught them on the sabbath,
text.DRB: And he went down into Capharnaum a city of Galilee; and there he taught them on the Sabboths.

text.NRSV: They were astounded at his teaching, because he spoke with authority.
text.Rotherham: and they were being struck with astonishment at his teaching, because, with authority, was his word.
text.KJV: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for his worde was with power.
text.NABRE: and they were astonished at his teaching because he spoke with authority.
text.DRB: And they were astonished at his doctrine; because his talk was in power.

text.NRSV: In the synagogue there was a man who had the spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,
text.Rotherham: And, in the synagogue, was a man having a spirit of an impure demon; and he cried out with a loud voice—
text.KJV: ¶ And in the Synagogue there was a man which had a spirit of an vncleane deuill, and cryed out with a loud voice,
text.NABRE: In the synagogue there was a man with the spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out in a loud voice,
text.DRB: And in the Synagogue there was a man having an unclean devil, and he cried out with a loud voice.

text.NRSV: “Let us alone! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are, the Holy One of God.”
text.Rotherham: Let be! What have we in common with thee, O Jesus, Nazarene! Hast thou come to destroy us? I know thee, who thou art,—The Holy One of God.
text.KJV: Saying, Let vs alone, what haue wee to doe with thee, thou Iesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy vs? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.
text.NABRE: “Ha! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!”
text.DRB: Saying: Let be, what to us and thee Jesus of Nazareth? Art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Saint of God.

text.NRSV: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!” When the demon had thrown him down before them, he came out of him without having done him any harm.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus rebuked it, saying—Hold thy peace! and go forth from him. And the demon, throwing him into the midst, went forth from him, doing him no hurt.
text.KJV: And Iesus rebuked him, saying, Holde thy peace, and come out of him. And when the deuill had throwen him in the middes, hee came out of him, and hurt him not.
text.NABRE: Jesus rebuked him and said, “Be quiet! Come out of him!” Then the demon threw the man down in front of them and came out of him without doing him any harm.
text.DRB: And Jesus rebuked him, saying: Hold thy peace, & go out of him. And when the devil had thrown him into the midst, he went out of him, and hurted him nothing.

text.NRSV: They were all amazed and kept saying to one another, “What kind of utterance is this? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!”
text.Rotherham: And amazement came upon all, and they began to converse one with another, saying—What is this word, that, with authority and power, he giveth orders unto the impure spirits, and they go forth?
text.KJV: And they were all amazed, and spake among themselues, saying, What a word is this? for with authoritie and power hee commaundeth the vncleane spirits, and they come out.
text.NABRE: They were all amazed and said to one another, “What is there about his word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out.”
text.DRB: And there came fear upon all, and they talked together one with another, saying: What word is this, that in power and virtue he commandeth the unclean spirits, and they go out?

text.NRSV: And a report about him began to spread into every place in the region.
text.Rotherham: And a noise concerning him began to go out into every place of the country around.
text.KJV: And the fame of him went out into euery place of the countrey round about.
text.NABRE: And news of him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
text.DRB: And the fame of him was published into every place of the country.

text.NRSV: After leaving the synagogue he entered Simon’s house. Now Simon’s mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked him about her.
text.Rotherham: And, rising up, from the synagogue, he went into the house of Simon. Now, the mother-in-law of Simon, was in distress with a great fever; and they made request to him concerning her.
text.KJV: ¶ And he arose out of the Synagogue, and entred into Simons house: and Simons wiues mother was taken with a great feuer, and they besought him for her.
text.NABRE: The Cure of Simon’s Mother-in-Law. After he left the synagogue, he entered the house of Simon. Simon’s mother-in-law was afflicted with a severe fever, and they interceded with him about her.
text.DRB: And Jesus rising up out of the Synagogue, entered into Simon's house. And Simon's wife's mother was holden with a great fever; and they besought him for her.

text.NRSV: Then he stood over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. Immediately she got up and began to serve them.
text.Rotherham: And, standing over her, he rebuked the fever, and it left her; and, instantly arising, she began to minister unto them.
text.KJV: And he stood ouer her, and rebuked the feuer, & it left her. And immediatly she arose, & ministred vnto them.
text.NABRE: He stood over her, rebuked the fever, and it left her. She got up immediately and waited on them. Other Healings.
text.DRB: And standing over her, he commanded the fever, and it left her. And incontinent rising, she ministered to them.

text.NRSV: As the sun was setting, all those who had any who were sick with various kind of diseases brought them to him; and he laid his hands on each of them and cured them.
text.Rotherham: But, as the sun was going in, they one and all, as many as had any sick with divers diseases, brought them unto him; and, he, upon each one of them laying, his hands, was curing them.
text.KJV: ¶ Now when the Sunne was setting, all they that had any sicke with diuers diseases, brought them vnto him: and hee laid his handes on euery one of them, and healed them.
text.NABRE: At sunset, all who had people sick with various diseases brought them to him. He laid his hands on each of them and cured them.
text.DRB: And when the sun was down, all that had diseased of sundry maladies, brought them to him. But he imposing hands upon every one, cured them.

text.NRSV: Demons also came out of many, shouting, “You are the Son of God!” But he rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Messiah.
text.Rotherham: And demons also were going forth from many; crying aloud, and saying—Thou, art the Son of God. And, rebuking them, he suffered them not be talking; because they knew him to be, The Christ.
text.KJV: And deuils also came out of many, crying out, and saying, Thou art Christ the Sonne of God. And hee rebuking them, suffered them not to speake: for they knewe that hee was Christ.
text.NABRE: And demons also came out from many, shouting, “You are the Son of God.” But he rebuked them and did not allow them to speak because they knew that he was the Messiah. Jesus Leaves Capernaum.
text.DRB: And devils went out from many, crying and saying: That thou art the Son of God. And rebuking them he suffered them not to speak, that they knew he was Christ.

text.NRSV: At daybreak he departed and went into a deserted place. And the crowds were looking for him; and when they reached him, they wanted to prevent him from leaving them.
text.Rotherham: And, when it was day, going forth, he journeyed into a desert place; and, the multitudes, were seeking after him, and they came unto him, and would have detained him, that he might not depart from them.
text.KJV: And when it was day, he departed, and went into a desert place: and the people sought him, and came vnto him, and stayed him, that he should not depart from them.
text.NABRE: At daybreak, Jesus left and went to a deserted place. The crowds went looking for him, and when they came to him, they tried to prevent him from leaving them.
text.DRB: And when it was day, going forth he went into a desert place; & the multitudes sought him, and came even unto him; and they held him that he should not depart from them.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “I must proclaim the good news of the kingdom of God to the other cities also; for I was sent for this purpose.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto them—To the other cities also, I must needs tell the good-news of the kingdom of God, because, hereunto, was I sent forth.
text.KJV: And hee said vnto them, I must preach the kingdome of God to other cities also: for therefore am I sent.
text.NABRE: But he said to them, “To the other towns also I must proclaim the good news of the kingdom of God, because for this purpose I have been sent.”
text.DRB: To whom he said: That to other cities also must I evangelize the Kingdom of God; because therefore I was sent.

text.NRSV: So he continued proclaiming the message in the synagogues of Judea.
text.Rotherham: And he was proclaiming in the cities of Judaea.
text.KJV: And hee preached in the Synagogues of Galile.
text.NABRE: And he was preaching in the synagogues of Judea.
text.DRB: And he was preaching in the Synagogues of Galilee.

text.NRSV: Once while Jesus was standing beside the lake of Gennesaret, and the crowd was pressing in on him to hear the word of God,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while the multitude was pressing upon him, and was hearing the word of God, that, he, was standing near the lake of Gennesaret;
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that as the people preassed vpon him to heare the word of God, hee stood by the lake of Genesareth,
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 The Call of Simon the Fisherman. While the crowd was pressing in on Jesus and listening to the word of God, he was standing by the Lake of Gennesaret.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when the multitudes pressed upon him to hear the word of God, and himself stood beside the lake of Genesareth.

text.NRSV: he saw two boats there at the shore of the lake; the fishermen had gone out of them and were washing their nets.
text.Rotherham: and he saw two boats placed near the lake, and, the fishers, having gone away, from them, were washing their nets.
text.KJV: And sawe two ships standing by the lake: but the fishermen were gone out of them, and were washing their nets.
text.NABRE: He saw two boats there alongside the lake; the fishermen had disembarked and were washing their nets.
text.DRB: And he saw two ships standing by the lake: and the fishers were gone down, and washed their nets.

text.NRSV: He got into one of the boats, the one belonging to Simon, and asked him to put out a little way from the shore. Then he sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
text.Rotherham: And, entering into one of the boats, which was Simon’s, he requested him, to put off from the land, a little; and, taking a seat, out of the boat, began he teaching the multitudes.
text.KJV: And he entred into one of the ships, which was Simons, and prayed him, that he would thrust out a little from the land: and he sate downe, and taught the people out of the ship.
text.NABRE: Getting into one of the boats, the one belonging to Simon, he asked him to put out a short distance from the shore. Then he sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
text.DRB: And he going up into one ship that was Simon's, desired him to bring it back a little from the land. And sitting, he taught the multitudes out of the ship.

text.NRSV: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into the deep water and let down your nets for a catch.”
text.Rotherham: And, when he ceased speaking, he said unto Simon—Put off into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.
text.KJV: Now when he had left speaking, he said vnto Simon, Lanch out into the deepe, and let downe your nets for a draught.
text.NABRE: After he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into deep water and lower your nets for a catch.”
text.DRB: And as he ceased to speak, he said to Simon: Launch forth into the deep, and let loose your nets to make a draught.

text.NRSV: Simon answered, “Master, we have worked all night long but have caught nothing. Yet if you say so, I will let down the nets.”
text.Rotherham: And Simon, answering, said—Master! although through the whole night we toiled, we took, nothing; howbeit, at thy bidding, I will let down the nets.
text.KJV: And Simon answering, said vnto him, Master, wee haue toiled all the night, and haue taken nothing: neuerthelesse at thy word I will let downe the net.
text.NABRE: Simon said in reply, “Master, we have worked hard all night and have caught nothing, but at your command I will lower the nets.”
text.DRB: And Simon answering, said to him: Master, labouring all the night, we have taken nothing; but in thy word I will let loose the net.

text.NRSV: When they had done this, they caught so many fish that their nets were beginning to break.
text.Rotherham: And, when this they had done, they enclosed a very large number of fishes, and their nets began to break.
text.KJV: And when they had this done, they inclosed a great multitude of fishes, and their net brake:
text.NABRE: When they had done this, they caught a great number of fish and their nets were tearing.
text.DRB: And when they had done this, they inclosed a very great multitude of fishes, and their net was broken.

text.NRSV: So they signaled their partners in the other boat to come and help them. And they came and filled both boats, so that they began to sink.
text.Rotherham: And they made signs to their partners in the other boat, to come and help them; and they came, and filled both the boats,—so that they began to sink.
text.KJV: And they beckened vnto their partners, which were in the other ship, that they should come and helpe them. And they came, & filled both the ships, so that they began to sinke.
text.NABRE: They signaled to their partners in the other boat to come to help them. They came and filled both boats so that they were in danger of sinking.
text.DRB: And they beckoned to their fellows that were in the other ship, that they should come and help them. And they came and filled both ships, so that they did sink.

text.NRSV: But when Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, “Go away from me, Lord, for I am a sinful man!”
text.Rotherham: And Simon, beholding, fell down at the knees of Jesus, saying—Depart from me! Because, a sinful man, am I, O Lord!
text.KJV: When Simon Peter saw it, he fell downe at Iesus knees, saying, Depart from me, for I am a sinfull man, O Lord.
text.NABRE: When Simon Peter saw this, he fell at the knees of Jesus and said, “Depart from me, Lord, for I am a sinful man.”
text.DRB: Which when Simon Peter did see, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying: Go forth from me, because I am a sinful man, O Lord.

text.NRSV: For he and all who were with him were amazed at the catch of fish that they had taken;
text.Rotherham: For, amazement, overcame him, and all them who were with him, on account of the draught of the fishes which they had taken;
text.KJV: For he was astonished, and al that were with him, at the draught of the fishes which they had taken.
text.NABRE: For astonishment at the catch of fish they had made seized him and all those with him,
text.DRB: For he was wholly astonished and all that were with him, at the draught of fishes which they had taken.

text.NRSV: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. Then Jesus said to Simon, “Do not be afraid; from now on you will be catching people.”
text.Rotherham: likewise also, both James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. And Jesus said unto Simon—Do not fear! henceforth, shall thou be taking, men, that they may, live.
text.KJV: And so was also Iames, and Iohn the sonnes of Zebedee, which were partners with Simon. And Iesus said vnto Simon, Feare not, from henceforth thou shalt catch men.
text.NABRE: and likewise James and John, the sons of Zebedee, who were partners of Simon. Jesus said to Simon, “Do not be afraid; from now on you will be catching men.”
text.DRB: In like manner also James and John the sons of Zebedee, who were Simon's fellows. And Jesus said to Simon: Fear not; from this time now, thou shalt be taking men.

text.NRSV: When they had brought their boats to shore, they left everything and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, bringing the boats down on to the land, they left all, and followed him.
text.KJV: And when they had brought their ships to land, they forsooke all, and followed him.
text.NABRE: When they brought their boats to the shore, they left everything and followed him. The Cleansing of a Leper.
text.DRB: And having brought their ships to land, leaving all things they followed him.

text.NRSV: Once, when he was in one of the cities, there was a man covered with leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he bowed with his face to the ground and begged him, “Lord, if you choose, you can make me clean.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while he was in one of the cities, that lo! there was a man full of leprosy; and, seeing Jesus, he fell on his face, and entreated him, saying—Lord! if thou be willing, thou canst cleanse me.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, when he was in a certaine citie, behold a man full of leprosie: who seeing Iesus, fell on his face, & besought him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me cleane.
text.NABRE: Now there was a man full of leprosy in one of the towns where he was; and when he saw Jesus, he fell prostrate, pleaded with him, and said, “Lord, if you wish, you can make me clean.”
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when he was in one of the cities, & behold a man full of leprosy, and seeing Jesus, and falling on his face, besought him saying: Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus stretched out his hand, touched him, and said, “I do choose. Be made clean.” Immediately the leprosy left him.
text.Rotherham: And, stretching forth the hand, he touched him, saying—I am willing: Be cleansed! And, straightway, the leprosy departed from him.
text.KJV: And he put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I wil: be thou cleane. And immediatly the leprosie departed from him.
text.NABRE: Jesus stretched out his hand, touched him, and said, “I do will it. Be made clean.” And the leprosy left him immediately.
text.DRB: And stretching forth the hand, he touched him, saying: I will. Be thou made clean. And immediately the leprosy departed from him.

text.NRSV: And he ordered him to tell no one. “Go,” he said, “and show yourself to the priest, and, as Moses commanded, make an offering for your cleansing, for a testimony to them.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, charged him to tell, no one,—but, departing, Show thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses enjoined, for a witness unto them.
text.KJV: And hee charged him to tell no man: but, Goe, and shewe thy selfe to the Priest, and offer for thy clensing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimonie vnto them.
text.NABRE: Then he ordered him not to tell anyone, but “Go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing what Moses prescribed; that will be proof for them.”
text.DRB: And he commanded him that he should tell nobody, but, Go, shew thyself to the Priest, and offer for thy cleansing as Moyses commanded, for a testimony to them.

text.NRSV: But now more than ever the word about Jesus spread abroad; many crowds would gather to hear him and to be cured of their diseases.
text.Rotherham: But the report concerning him the more went abroad, and many multitudes were coming together, to hear, and be getting cured from their infirmities;
text.KJV: But so much the more went there a fame abroad of him, and great multitudes came together to heare, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.
text.NABRE: The report about him spread all the more, and great crowds assembled to listen to him and to be cured of their ailments,
text.DRB: But the bruit of him went abroad the more, and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be cured of their infirmities.

text.NRSV: But he would withdraw to deserted places and pray.
text.Rotherham: howbeit, he, was retiring in the deserts, and engaging in prayer.
text.KJV: ¶ And he withdrew himselfe into the wildernesse, and prayed.
text.NABRE: but he would withdraw to deserted places to pray. The Healing of a Paralytic.
text.DRB: And he retired into the desert, and prayed.

text.NRSV: One day, while he was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting near by (they had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem); and the power of the Lord was with him to heal.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on one of the days, that, he, was teaching, and there were sitting Pharisees and Teachers of the law, who had come out of every village of Galilee and Judaea and Jerusalem; and, the power of the Lord, was there, that he might heal.
text.KJV: And it came to passe on a certaine day, as hee was teaching, that there were Pharisees and Doctours of the Law sitting by, which were come out of euery towne of Galilee, and Iudea, and Hierusalem: and the power of the Lord was present to heale them.
text.NABRE: One day as Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting there who had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and Jerusalem, and the power of the Lord was with him for healing.
text.DRB: And it came to pass one day, and he sat teaching. And there were Pharisees sitting and Doctors of Law that were come out of every town of Galilee and Jewry and Jerusalem; and the virtue of our Lord was to heal them.

text.NRSV: Just then some men came, carrying a paralyzed man on a bed. They were trying to bring him in and lay him before Jesus;
text.Rotherham: And lo! men bearing, upon a couch, one who was paralyzed, and they were seeking to bring him in, and lay him before him.
text.KJV: ¶ And behold, men brought in a bed a man which was taken with a palsie: and they sought meanes to bring him in, and to lay him before him.
text.NABRE: And some men brought on a stretcher a man who was paralyzed; they were trying to bring him in and set [him] in his presence.
text.DRB: And behold men carrying in a bed a man that had the palsy: and they sought to bring him in, and to lay him before him.

text.NRSV: but finding no way to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and let him down with his bed through the tiles into the middle of the crowd in front of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, not finding by what means they might bring him in, because of the multitude, going up on the house-top, through the tiling, let they him down, with the little-couch, into the midst before Jesus.
text.KJV: And when they could not find by what way they might bring him in, because of the multitude, they went vpon the house top, & let him downe through the tiling with his couch, into the midst before Iesus.
text.NABRE: But not finding a way to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and lowered him on the stretcher through the tiles into the middle in front of Jesus.
text.DRB: And not finding on which side they might bring him in for the multitude, they went up upon the roof, and through the tiles let him down with the bed into the midst, before Jesus.

text.NRSV: When he saw their faith, he said, “Friend, your sins are forgiven you.”
text.Rotherham: And, beholding their faith, he said—O man! thy sins are forgiven thee.
text.KJV: And when he saw their faith, hee said vnto him, Man, thy sinnes are forgiuen thee.
text.NABRE: When he saw their faith, he said, “As for you, your sins are forgiven.”
text.DRB: Whose faith when he saw, he said: Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.

text.NRSV: Then the scribes and the Pharisees began to question, “Who is this who is speaking blasphemies? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying—Who is this that speaketh profanities? Who can forgive, sins, save, God alone?
text.KJV: And the Scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, Who is this which speaketh blasphemies? Who can forgiue sinnes, but God alone?
text.NABRE: Then the scribes and Pharisees began to ask themselves, “Who is this who speaks blasphemies? Who but God alone can forgive sins?”
text.DRB: And the Scribes and Pharisees began to think, saying: Who is this that speaketh blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but only God?

text.NRSV: When Jesus perceived their questionings, he answered them, “Why do you raise such questions in your hearts?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, taking note of their reasonings, answering, said unto them—Why are ye reasoning in your hearts?
text.KJV: But when Iesus perceiued their thoughts, he answering, saide vnto them, What reason ye in your hearts?
text.NABRE: Jesus knew their thoughts and said to them in reply, “What are you thinking in your hearts?
text.DRB: And when Jesus knew their cogitations, answering he said to them: What do you think in your hearts?

text.NRSV: Which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you,’ or to say, ‘Stand up and walk’?
text.Rotherham: Which is easier, To say—Thy sins are forgiven thee; or to say—Arise and be walking?
text.KJV: Whether is easier to say, Thy sinnes be forgiuen thee: or to say, Rise vp and walke?
text.NABRE: Which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Rise and walk’?
text.DRB: Which is easier to say, Thy sins are forgiven thee, or to say, Arise, and walk?

text.NRSV: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the one who was paralyzed—“I say to you, stand up and take your bed and go to your home.”
text.Rotherham: But, that ye may know that, the Son of Man, hath, authority, upon the earth to forgive sins—he said to the paralyzed man—To thee, I say, Arise, and, taking up thy couch, be going thy way unto thy house.
text.KJV: But that ye may know that the Sonne of man hath power vpon earth to forgiue sinnes (he said vnto the sicke of the palsie,) I say vnto thee, Arise, and take vp thy couch, and go into thine house.
text.NABRE: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the man who was paralyzed, “I say to you, rise, pick up your stretcher, and go home.”
text.DRB: But that you may know that the Son of man hath power in earth to forgive sins (he said to the sick of the palsy) I say to thee, Arise, take up thy bed, and go into thy house.

text.NRSV: Immediately he stood up before them, took what he had been lying on, and went to his home, glorifying God.
text.Rotherham: And, instantly arising before them, he took up that whereon he had been lying, and departed unto his house, glorifying God.
text.KJV: And immediatly he rose vp before them, and tooke vp that whereon hee lay, and departed to his owne house, glorifying God.
text.NABRE: He stood up immediately before them, picked up what he had been lying on, and went home, glorifying God.
text.DRB: And forthwith rising up before them, he took that wherein he lay; and he went into his house, magnifying God.

text.NRSV: Amazement seized all of them, and they glorified God and were filled with awe, saying, “We have seen strange things today.”
text.Rotherham: And, astonishment, seized one and all, and they began glorifying God, and were filled with fear, saying—We have seen unaccountable things, to-day!
text.KJV: And they were all amazed, and they glorified God, and were filled with feare, saying, Wee haue seene strange things to day.
text.NABRE: Then astonishment seized them all and they glorified God, and, struck with awe, they said, “We have seen incredible things today.” The Call of Levi.
text.DRB: And all were astonished: and they magnified God. And they were replenished with fear, saying: That we have seen marvelous things today.

text.NRSV: After this he went out and saw a tax collector named Levi, sitting at the tax booth; and he said to him, “Follow me.”
text.Rotherham: And, after these things, he went forth, and looked upon a tax-collector, by name Levi,—presiding over the tax-office; and he said to him—Be following me!
text.KJV: ¶ And after these things hee went foorth, and sawe a Publicane, named Leui, sitting at the receit of custome: and hee said vnto him, Follow me.
text.NABRE: After this he went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the customs post. He said to him, “Follow me.”
text.DRB: And after these things he went forth, and saw a Publican called Levi, sitting at the Custom-house, and he said to him: Follow me.

text.NRSV: And he got up, left everything, and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, forsaking all, he arose, and was following him.
text.KJV: And he left all, rose vp, and followed him.
text.NABRE: And leaving everything behind, he got up and followed him.
text.DRB: And leaving all things, he rose and followed him.

text.NRSV: Then Levi gave a great banquet for him in his house; and there was a large crowd of tax collectors and others sitting at the table with them.
text.Rotherham: And Levi made a great reception for him, in his house, and there was a great multitude of tax-collectors, and others,—who were with them reclining.
text.KJV: And Leui made him a great feast in his owne house: and there was a great company of Publicanes, and of others that sate downe with them.
text.NABRE: Then Levi gave a great banquet for him in his house, and a large crowd of tax collectors and others were at table with them.
text.DRB: And Levi made him a great feast in his house; and there was a great multitude of Publicans; and of others that were sitting at the table with them.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees and their scribes were complaining to his disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.Rotherham: And the Pharisees and their Scribes began murmuring unto his disciples, saying—Wherefore, with the tax-collectors and sinners, are ye eating and drinking?
text.KJV: But their Scribes and Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, Why doe ye eate and drinke with Publicanes and sinners?
text.NABRE: The Pharisees and their scribes complained to his disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.DRB: And their Pharisees and Scribes murmured, saying to his disciples: Why do you eat and drink with Publicans and sinners?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick;
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus said unto them—No need, have, the whole, of a physician, but, they who are sick.
text.KJV: And Iesus answering, said vnto them, They that are whole need not a physician: but they that are sicke.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them in reply, “Those who are healthy do not need a physician, but the sick do.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering said to them: They that are whole, need not the Physician: but they that are ill at ease.

text.NRSV: I have come to call not the righteous but sinners to repentance.”
text.Rotherham: I have not come to call, righteous, men, but, sinners, unto repentance.
text.KJV: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
text.NABRE: I have not come to call the righteous to repentance but sinners.” The Question About Fasting.
text.DRB: I came not to call the just, but sinners to penance.

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “John’s disciples, like the disciples of the Pharisees, frequently fast and pray, but your disciples eat and drink.”
text.Rotherham: But, they, said unto him—The disciples of John, do fast much, and, supplications, do make,—likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees; but, thine, do eat and drink!
text.KJV: ¶ And they said vnto him, Why doe the disciples of Iohn fast often, and make prayers, and likewise the disciples of the Pharisees: but thine eat and drinke?
text.NABRE: And they said to him, “The disciples of John fast often and offer prayers, and the disciples of the Pharisees do the same; but yours eat and drink.”
text.DRB: But they said to him: Why do the disciples of John fast often, and make obsecrations, and of the Pharisees in like manner; but thine do eat and drink?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “You cannot make wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is with them, can you?
text.Rotherham: Jesus, however, said unto them—Can, ye make, the sons of the bridechamber, fast, while the bridegroom is with them?
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Can yee make the children of the Bride-chamber fast, while the Bridegrome is with them?
text.NABRE: Jesus answered them, “Can you make the wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is with them?
text.DRB: To whom he said: Why, can you make the children of the bridegroom fast whiles the bridegroom is with them?

text.NRSV: The days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast in those days.”
text.Rotherham: But there will come days, even when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, then, will they fast, in those days.
text.KJV: But the dayes will come, when the Bridegrome shall bee taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those dayes.
text.NABRE: But the days will come, and when the bridegroom is taken away from them, then they will fast in those days.”
text.DRB: But the days will come; and when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, then they shall fast in those days.

text.NRSV: He also told them a parable: “No one tears a piece from a new garment and sews it on an old garment; otherwise the new will be torn, and the piece from the new will not match the old.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, he went on to speak, a parable also, unto them—No one, rending a patch from a new mantle, patcheth it upon an old mantle; otherwise, at least, both, the new, he will rend, and, with the old, the patch which is from the new, will not agree.
text.KJV: ¶ And he spake also a parable vnto them, No man putteth a piece of a newe garment vpon an olde: if otherwise, then both the newe maketh a rent, and the piece that was taken out of the new, agreeth not with the olde.
text.NABRE: And he also told them a parable. “No one tears a piece from a new cloak to patch an old one. Otherwise, he will tear the new and the piece from it will not match the old cloak.
text.DRB: And he said a similitude also unto them: That no man putteth a piece from a new garment into an old garment; otherwise both he breaketh the new, and the piece from the new agreeth not with the old.

text.NRSV: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the new wine will burst the skins and will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.
text.Rotherham: And, no one, poureth new wine into old skins; otherwise, at least, the new wine, will burst the skins, and will, itself, be poured out, and the skins be destroyed.
text.KJV: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine will burst the bottles, and be spilled, and the bottles shall perish.
text.NABRE: Likewise, no one pours new wine into old wineskins. Otherwise, the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be spilled, and the skins will be ruined.
text.DRB: And nobody putteth new wine into old bottles; otherwise the new wine will break the bottles, and itself will be shed, and the bottles will be lost.

text.NRSV: But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins.
text.Rotherham: But, new wine, into unused skins, must be poured.
text.KJV: But newe wine must be put into newe bottles, and both are preserued.
text.NABRE: Rather, new wine must be poured into fresh wineskins.
text.DRB: But new wine is to be put into new bottles: and both are preserved together.

text.NRSV: And no one after drinking old wine desires new wine, but says, ‘The old is good.’”
text.Rotherham: [No one, having drunk old, desireth new; for he saith, The old, is, mellow.]
text.KJV: No man also hauing drunke olde wine, straightway desireth new: for he saith, The old is better.
text.NABRE: [And] no one who has been drinking old wine desires new, for he says, ‘The old is good.’”
text.DRB: And no man drinking old, will new by and by, for he saith, The old is better.

text.NRSV: One sabbath while Jesus was going through the grainfields, his disciples plucked some heads of grain, rubbed them in their hands, and ate them.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on a Sabbath, that he was passing along through cornfields, and his disciples were plucking and eating the ears of corn, rubbing them with their hands.
text.KJV: And it came to passe on the second Sabbath after the first, that he went thorow the corne fields: and his disciples plucked the eares of corne, and did eate, rubbing them in their hands.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Debates About the Sabbath. While he was going through a field of grain on a sabbath, his disciples were picking the heads of grain, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
text.DRB: And it came to pass on the Sabboth second-first, when he passed through the corn, his Disciples did pluck the ears, & did eat rubbing them with their hands.

text.NRSV: But some of the Pharisees said, “Why are you doing what is not lawful on the sabbath?”
text.Rotherham: But, certain of the Pharisees, said—Why are ye doing what is not allowed on the Sabbath?
text.KJV: And certaine of the Pharisees said vnto them, Why doe yee that which is not lawfull to doe on the Sabbath dayes?
text.NABRE: Some Pharisees said, “Why are you doing what is unlawful on the sabbath?”
text.DRB: And certain of the Pharisees said to them: Why do you that which is not lawful on the Sabboths?
