text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
text.Rotherham: And, making answer unto them, Jesus said—Have ye never read, even this, what David did when he hungered, he, and they who were with him—
text.KJV: And Iesus answering them, said, Haue yee not read so much as this what Dauid did, when himselfe was an hungred, and they which were with him:
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them in reply, “Have you not read what David did when he and those [who were] with him were hungry?
text.DRB: And Jesus answering them, said: Neither this have you read which David did, when himself was an hungered & they that were with him:

text.NRSV: He entered the house of God and took and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and gave some to his companions?”
text.Rotherham: How he entered into the house of God, and, the presence-bread, receiving, did eat, and gave to them who were with him, which it is not allowable to eat, save alone, to the priests?
text.KJV: How he went into the house of God, and did take and eate the Shew bread, and gaue also to them that were with him, which it is not lawful to eate but for the Priests alone?
text.NABRE: [How] he went into the house of God, took the bread of offering, which only the priests could lawfully eat, ate of it, and shared it with his companions.”
text.DRB: how he entered into the house of God, and took the loaves of Proposition, and did eat, and gave to them that were with him, which it is not lawful to eat but only for Priests?

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “The Son of Man is lord of the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: And he was saying to them—The Son of Man is, Lord of the Sabbath.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, That the sonne of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
text.NABRE: Then he said to them, “The Son of Man is lord of the sabbath.”
text.DRB: And he said to them: That the Son of man is Lord of the Sabboth also.

text.NRSV: On another sabbath he entered the synagogue and taught, and there was a man there whose right hand was withered.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on another Sabbath, that he entered into the synagogue, and was teaching, and there was a man there, and, his right hand, was withered.
text.KJV: And it came to passe also on another Sabbath, that he entred into the Synagogue, and taught: and there was a man whose right hand was withered.
text.NABRE: On another sabbath he went into the synagogue and taught, and there was a man there whose right hand was withered.
text.DRB: And it came to pass on another Sabboth also, that he entered into the Synagogue, and taught. And there was a man, and his right hand was withered.

text.NRSV: The scribes and the Pharisees watched him to see whether he would cure on the sabbath, so that they might find an accusation against him.
text.Rotherham: Now the Scribes and the Pharisees were narrowly watching him, whether, on the Sabbath, he healeth,—that they might find whereof to accuse him.
text.KJV: And the Scribes and Pharisees watched him, whether he would heale on the Sabbath day: that they might find an accusation against him.
text.NABRE: The scribes and the Pharisees watched him closely to see if he would cure on the sabbath so that they might discover a reason to accuse him.
text.DRB: And the Scribes and Pharisees watched if he would cure on the Sabboth; that they might find how to accuse him.

text.NRSV: Even though he knew what they were thinking, he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Come and stand here.” He got up and stood there.
text.Rotherham: But, he, knew their reasonings, and said to the man who had the, withered, hand—Arise, and stand forward in the midst! And, arising, he stood forward.
text.KJV: But he knew their thoughts, and said to the man which had the withered hand, Rise vp, and stand foorth in the mids. And he arose, and stood foorth.
text.NABRE: But he realized their intentions and said to the man with the withered hand, “Come up and stand before us.” And he rose and stood there.
text.DRB: But he knew their cogitations; and he said to the man that had the withered hand: Arise, and stand forth into the midst. And rising he stood.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “I ask you, is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the sabbath, to save life or to destroy it?”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto them—I ask you, whether it is allowed, on the Sabbath, to do good or to do harm,—life, to save, or, to destroy.
text.KJV: Then said Iesus vnto them, I will aske you one thing, Is it lawfull on the Sabbath dayes to doe good, or to doe euill? to saue life, or to destroy it?
text.NABRE: Then Jesus said to them, “I ask you, is it lawful to do good on the sabbath rather than to do evil, to save life rather than to destroy it?”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them: I ask you, if it be lawful on the Sabboths to do well or ill; to save a soul or to destroy?

text.NRSV: After looking around at all of them, he said to him, “Stretch out your hand.” He did so, and his hand was restored.
text.Rotherham: And, looking round upon them all, he said unto him—Stretch forth thy hand! and, he, did so, and his hand was restored.
text.KJV: And looking round about vpon them all, he said vnto the man, Stretch foorth thy hand. And he did so: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
text.NABRE: Looking around at them all, he then said to him, “Stretch out your hand.” He did so and his hand was restored.
text.DRB: And looking about upon them all, he said to the man: Stretch forth thy hand. And he stretched it forth; & his hand was restored.

text.NRSV: But they were filled with fury and discussed with one another what they might do to Jesus.
text.Rotherham: But, they, were filled with folly, and began conversing one with another, as to, what they might do with Jesus.
text.KJV: And they were filled with madnesse, and communed one with another what they might doe to Iesus.
text.NABRE: But they became enraged and discussed together what they might do to Jesus. The Mission of the Twelve.
text.DRB: And they were replenished with madness; & they communed one with another what they might do to Jesus.

text.NRSV: Now during those days he went out to the mountain to pray; and he spent the night in prayer to God.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in these days, that he went forth into the mountain to pray, and was spending the night in the prayer-house of God.
text.KJV: And it came to passe in those dayes, that hee went out into a mountaine to pray, and continued all night in prayer to God.
text.NABRE: In those days he departed to the mountain to pray, and he spent the night in prayer to God.
text.DRB: And it came to pass in those days, he went forth into the mountain to pray, and he passed the whole night in the prayer of God.

text.NRSV: And when day came, he called his disciples and chose twelve of them, whom he also named apostles:
text.Rotherham: And, when it became day, he called his disciples, and chose from them, twelve, whom also he named, Apostles,—
text.KJV: ¶ And when it was day, he called vnto him his disciples: and of them he chose twelue; whom also hee named Apostles:
text.NABRE: When day came, he called his disciples to himself, and from them he chose Twelve, whom he also named apostles:
text.DRB: And when day was come, he called his Disciples; and he chose twelve of them (whom also he named Apostles )

text.NRSV: Simon, whom he named Peter, and his brother Andrew, and James, and John, and Philip, and Bartholomew,
text.Rotherham: Simon, whom also he named Peter, and Andrew his brother; and James and John; and Philip and Bartholomew;
text.KJV: Simon, (whom he also named Peter,) and Andrew his brother: Iames and Iohn, Philip and Bartholomew,
text.NABRE: Simon, whom he named Peter, and his brother Andrew, James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
text.DRB: Simon whom he surnamed Peter, and Andrew his brother, James and John, Philippe and Bartholomew,

text.NRSV: and Matthew, and Thomas, and James son of Alphaeus, and Simon, who was called the Zealot,
text.Rotherham: and Matthew and Thomas; and James, son of Alphaeus, and Simon, who was called Zealot; and Judas, [son] of James,
text.KJV: Matthew and Thomas, Iames the sonne of Alpheus, and Simon, called Zelotes,
text.NABRE: Matthew, Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon who was called a Zealot,
text.DRB: Matthew and Thomas, James of Alphæus and Simon that is called Zelotes,

text.NRSV: and Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
text.Rotherham: and Judas Iscariot, who became betrayer;—
text.KJV: And Iudas the brother of Iames, and Iudas Iscariot, which also was the traitour.
text.NABRE: and Judas the son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor. Ministering to a Great Multitude.
text.DRB: and Jude of James, and Judas Iscariote which was the traitor.

text.NRSV: He came down with them and stood on a level place, with a great crowd of his disciples and a great multitude of people from all Judea, Jerusalem, and the coast of Tyre and Sidon.
text.Rotherham: and, coming down with them, he stood upon a level place, also a great multitude of his disciples,—and a great throng of the people, from all Judaea and Jerusalem and the sea-coast of Tyre and Zidon, who had come to hearken unto him and to be healed from their diseases;
text.KJV: ¶ And hee came downe with them, and stood in the plaine, and the company of his disciples, and a great multitude of people, out of all Iudea and Hierusalem, and from the Sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, which came to heare him, and to be healed of their diseases,
text.NABRE: And he came down with them and stood on a stretch of level ground. A great crowd of his disciples and a large number of the people from all Judea and Jerusalem and the coastal region of Tyre and Sidon
text.DRB: And descending with them he stood in a plain place, and the multitude of his Disciples, and a very great company of people from all Jewry and Jerusalem, and the sea coast both of Tyre & Sidon,

text.NRSV: They had come to hear him and to be healed of their diseases; and those who were troubled with unclean spirits were cured.
text.Rotherham: and, they who were molested by impure spirits, were being cured;
text.KJV: And they that were vexed with vncleane spirits: and they were healed.
text.NABRE: came to hear him and to be healed of their diseases; and even those who were tormented by unclean spirits were cured.
text.DRB: Which were come to hear him, and to be healed of their maladies. And they that were vexed of unclean spirits, were cured.

text.NRSV: And all in the crowd were trying to touch him, for power came out from him and healed all of them.
text.Rotherham: and, all the multitude, were seeking to touch him, because, power, from him, was coming forth, and healing all.
text.KJV: And the whole multitude sought to touch him: for there went vertue out of him, and healed them all.
text.NABRE: Everyone in the crowd sought to touch him because power came forth from him and healed them all. Sermon on the Plain.
text.DRB: And all the multitude sought to touch him, because virtue went forth from him, and healed all.

text.NRSV: Then he looked up at his disciples and said: “Blessed are you who are poor, for yours is the kingdom of God.
text.Rotherham: And, he, lifting up his eyes towards his disciples, was saying:—Happy, ye destitute, for, yours, is the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: ¶ And hee lifted vp his eyes on his disciples, and said, Blessed be yee poore: for yours is the kingdome of God.
text.NABRE: And raising his eyes toward his disciples he said: “Blessed are you who are poor, for the kingdom of God is yours.
text.DRB: And he lifting up his eyes upon his Disciples, said: Blessed are ye poor: for yours is the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you who are hungry now, for you will be filled. “Blessed are you who weep now, for you will laugh.
text.Rotherham: Happy, ye that hunger now, for ye shall be filled. Happy, ye that weep now, for ye shall laugh.
text.KJV: Blessed are yee that hunger now: for yee shall be filled. Blessed are yee that weepe now, for yee shall laugh.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you who are now hungry, for you will be satisfied. Blessed are you who are now weeping, for you will laugh.
text.DRB: Blessed are you that now are an hungered: because you shall be filled. Blessed are you that now do weep: because you shall laugh.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you when people hate you, and when they exclude you, revile you, and defame you on account of the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: Happy, are ye, whensoever men shall hate you, and whensoever they shall separate you, and reproach you, and cast out your name as evil, for the sake of the Son of Man:
text.KJV: Blessed are yee when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you from their company, and shal reproach you, and cast out your name as euill, for the Sonne of mans sake.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you when people hate you, and when they exclude and insult you, and denounce your name as evil on account of the Son of Man.
text.DRB: Blessed shall you be when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you, and upbraid you, and abandon your name as evil, for the Son of man's sake.

text.NRSV: Rejoice on that day and leap for joy, for surely your reward is great in heaven; for that is what their ancestors did to the prophets.
text.Rotherham: be rejoiced in that day, and leap, for lo! your reward, is great in heaven; for, according to the same things, were their fathers doing unto the prophets.
text.KJV: Reioice yee in that day, and leape for ioy: for behold, your reward is great in heauen for in the like maner did their fathers vnto the Prophets.
text.NABRE: Rejoice and leap for joy on that day! Behold, your reward will be great in heaven. For their ancestors treated the prophets in the same way.
text.DRB: Be glad in that day and rejoice: for behold, your reward is much in Heaven. For according to these things did their fathers to the Prophets.

text.NRSV: “But woe to you who are rich, for you have received your consolation.
text.Rotherham: But alas! for you, ye wealthy, for ye are duly receiving you consolation.
text.KJV: But woe vnto you that are rich: for yee haue receiued your consolation.
text.NABRE: But woe to you who are rich, for you have received your consolation.
text.DRB: But woe to you that are rich: because you have your consolation.

text.NRSV: “Woe to you who are full now, for you will be hungry. “Woe to you who are laughing now, for you will mourn and weep.
text.Rotherham: Alas! for you, ye who are filled full now, for ye shall hunger. Alas! ye that laugh now, for ye shall mourn and weep.
text.KJV: Woe vnto you that are full: for yee shall hunger. Woe vnto you that laugh now: for yee shall mourne and weepe.
text.NABRE: But woe to you who are filled now, for you will be hungry. Woe to you who laugh now, for you will grieve and weep.
text.DRB: Woe to you that are filled: because you shall be hungry. Woe to you that now do laugh: because you shall mourn and weep.

text.NRSV: “Woe to you when all speak well of you, for that is what their ancestors did to the false prophets.
text.Rotherham: Alas! whensoever all men shall, speak well of you, for, according to the same things, were their fathers doing unto the false prophets.
text.KJV: Woe vnto you when all men shall speake well of you: for so did their fathers to the false Prophets.
text.NABRE: Woe to you when all speak well of you, for their ancestors treated the false prophets in this way. Love of Enemies.
text.DRB: Woe, when all man shall bless you: For according to these things did their fathers to the false-Prophets.

text.NRSV: “But I say to you that listen, Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,
text.Rotherham: But, unto you, I say, who are hearkening: Be loving your enemies; be doing, good, unto them that hate you;
text.KJV: ¶ But I say vnto you which heare, Loue your enemies, doe good to them which hate you,
text.NABRE: “But to you who hear I say, love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,
text.DRB: But to you I say that do hear: Love your enemies, do good to them that hate you.

text.NRSV: bless those who curse you, pray for those who abuse you.
text.Rotherham: Be blessing them that curse you; be praying for them that wantonly insult you.
text.KJV: Blesse them that curse you, & pray for them which despitefully vse you.
text.NABRE: bless those who curse you, pray for those who mistreat you.
text.DRB: Bless them that curse you, and pray for them that calumniate you.

text.NRSV: If anyone strikes you on the cheek, offer the other also; and from anyone who takes away your coat do not withhold even your shirt.
text.Rotherham: To him who is smiting thee upon the one cheek, be offering, the other also; and, from him who is taking away thy mantle, thy tunic also, do not forbid:
text.KJV: And vnto him that smiteth thee on the one cheeke, offer also the other: and him that taketh away thy cloake, forbid not to take thy coat also.
text.NABRE: To the person who strikes you on one cheek, offer the other one as well, and from the person who takes your cloak, do not withhold even your tunic.
text.DRB: And he that striketh thee on the cheek, offer also the other. And from him that taketh away from thee thy robe, prohibit not thy coat also.

text.NRSV: Give to everyone who begs from you; and if anyone takes away your goods, do not ask for them again.
text.Rotherham: To every one asking thee, give, and, from him that taketh away thy possessions, ask them not back.
text.KJV: Giue to euery man that asketh of thee, and of him that taketh away thy goods, aske them not againe.
text.NABRE: Give to everyone who asks of you, and from the one who takes what is yours do not demand it back.
text.DRB: And to every one that asketh thee, give, and of him that taketh away the things that are thine, ask not again.

text.NRSV: Do to others as you would have them do to you.
text.Rotherham: And, according as ye desire that men be doing unto you, be ye doing unto them, in like manner.
text.KJV: And as yee would that men should doe to you, doe yee also to them likewise.
text.NABRE: Do to others as you would have them do to you.
text.DRB: And according as you will that men do to you, do you also to them in like manner.

text.NRSV: “If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.
text.Rotherham: And, if ye love them that love you, what sort of thanks are there for you? for, even sinners, love, such as love them.
text.KJV: For if yee loue them which loue you, what thanke haue ye? for sinners also loue those that loue them.
text.NABRE: For if you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners love those who love them.
text.DRB: And if you love them that love you, what thank is to you? For sinners also love those that love them.

text.NRSV: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.
text.Rotherham: [For], if ye even do good unto them that do good unto you, what sort of thanks are there for you? Even sinners, the same, are doing.
text.KJV: And if ye doe good to them which doe good to you, What thanke haue ye? for sinners also doe euen the same.
text.NABRE: And if you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners do the same.
text.DRB: And if ye do good to them that do you good: what thank is to you? For sinners also do this.

text.NRSV: If you lend to those from whom you hope to receive, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, to receive as much again.
text.Rotherham: And, if ye lend to them from whom ye are hoping to receive, what sort of thanks are there for you? Even sinners, unto sinners, do lend, that they may receive back, as much.
text.KJV: And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receiue, What thanke haue ye? for sinners also lend to sinners, to receiue as much againe.
text.NABRE: If you lend money to those from whom you expect repayment, what credit [is] that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, and get back the same amount.
text.DRB: And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive; what thank is to you? For sinners also lend unto sinners, for to receive as much.

text.NRSV: But love your enemies, do good, and lend, expecting nothing in return. Your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High; for he is kind to the ungrateful and the wicked.
text.Rotherham: But love your enemies, and do good and lend, hoping for, nothing, back; and your reward shall be, great, and ye shall be sons of the Most High,—for, he, is, gracious, unto the ungrateful and wicked.
text.KJV: But loue yee your enemies, and doe good, and lend, hoping for nothing againe: and your reward shall bee great, and ye shalbe the children of the Highest: for hee is kinde vnto the vnthankfull, and to the euill.
text.NABRE: But rather, love your enemies and do good to them, and lend expecting nothing back; then your reward will be great and you will be children of the Most High, for he himself is kind to the ungrateful and the wicked.
text.DRB: But love ye your enemies; do good and lend, hoping for nothing thereby: and your reward shall be much, and you shall be the Sons of the Highest, because himself is beneficial upon the unkind and the evil.

text.NRSV: Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.
text.Rotherham: Become ye compassionate, according as, your Father, is, compassionate;
text.KJV: Be ye therefore mercifull, as your Father also is mercifull.
text.NABRE: Be merciful, just as [also] your Father is merciful. Judging Others.
text.DRB: Be ye therefore merciful as also your Father is merciful.

text.NRSV: “Do not judge, and you will not be judged; do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven;
text.Rotherham: And do not judge, and in nowise shall ye be judged; and do not condemn, and in nowise shall ye be condemned; release, and ye shall be released;
text.KJV: Iudge not, and ye shall not bee iudged: condemne not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgiue, and ye shall be forgiuen.
text.NABRE: “Stop judging and you will not be judged. Stop condemning and you will not be condemned. Forgive and you will be forgiven.
text.DRB: Judge not, & you shall not be judged. Condemn not, & you shall not be condemned. Forgive, and you shall be forgiven.

text.NRSV: give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, will be put into your lap; for the measure you give will be the measure you get back.”
text.Rotherham: Give, and it shall be given unto you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, will they give into your lap; for, with what measure ye mete, shall it be measured back unto you.
text.KJV: Giue, and it shall bee giuen vnto you, good measure, preassed downe, and shaken together, and running ouer, shall men giue into your bosome: for with the same measure that ye mete withall, it shall bee measured to you againe.
text.NABRE: Give and gifts will be given to you; a good measure, packed together, shaken down, and overflowing, will be poured into your lap. For the measure with which you measure will in return be measured out to you.”
text.DRB: Give, and there shall be given to you. Good measure & pressed down and shaken together and running over shall they give into your bosom. For with the same measure that you do mete, it shall be measured to you again.

text.NRSV: He also told them a parable: “Can a blind person guide a blind person? Will not both fall into a pit?
text.Rotherham: He spake, moreover, a parable also, unto them: Can, the blind, guide, the blind? will not, both, fall into, a ditch?
text.KJV: And hee spake a parable vnto them, Can the blinde leade the blinde? Shall they not both fall into the ditch?
text.NABRE: And he told them a parable, “Can a blind person guide a blind person? Will not both fall into a pit?
text.DRB: And he said to them a similitude also: Can the blind lead the blind? Do not both fall into the ditch?

text.NRSV: A disciple is not above the teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like the teacher.
text.Rotherham: A disciple is not above the teacher; but, when trained, every one shall be as his teacher.
text.KJV: The disciple is not aboue his master: but euery one that is perfect shalbe as his master.
text.NABRE: No disciple is superior to the teacher; but when fully trained, every disciple will be like his teacher.
text.DRB: The Disciple is not above his Master: but every one shall be perfect, if he be as his Master.

text.NRSV: Why do you see the speck in your neighbor’s eye, but do not notice the log in your own eye?
text.Rotherham: But why beholdest thou the mote that is in the eye of thy brother, while, the beam that is in thine own eye, thou dost not consider?
text.KJV: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brothers eye, but perceiuest not the beame that is in thine owne eye?
text.NABRE: Why do you notice the splinter in your brother’s eye, but do not perceive the wooden beam in your own?
text.DRB: And why seest thou the mote in thy brother's eye: but the beam that is in thine own eye thou considerest not?

text.NRSV: Or how can you say to your neighbor, ‘Friend, let me take out the speck in your eye,’ when you yourself do not see the log in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your neighbor’s eye.
text.Rotherham: How canst thou say to thy brother—Brother! let me cast out the mote that is in thine eye,—thyself, the beam in thine own eye, not beholding? Hypocrite! cast out, first, the beam out of thine own eye, and, then, shalt thou see clearly, to cast out, the mote that is in the eye of thy brother.
text.KJV: Either how canst thou say to thy brother, Brother, let mee pull out the mote that is in thine eye: when thou thy selfe beholdest not the beame that is in thine owne eye? Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beame out of thine owne eye, and then shalt thou see clearly to pul out the mote that is in thy brothers eye.
text.NABRE: How can you say to your brother, ‘Brother, let me remove that splinter in your eye,’ when you do not even notice the wooden beam in your own eye? You hypocrite! Remove the wooden beam from your eye first; then you will see clearly to remove the splinter in your brother’s eye. A Tree Known by Its Fruit.
text.DRB: Or how canst thou say to thy brother: Brother, let me cast out the mote out of thine eye: thyself not seeing the beam in thine own eye? Hypocrite, cast first the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to take forth the mote out of thy brother's eye.

text.NRSV: “No good tree bears bad fruit, nor again does a bad tree bear good fruit;
text.Rotherham: For a good tree doth not produce worthless fruit, neither again doth, a worthless tree, produce good fruit.
text.KJV: For a good tree bringeth not foorth corrupt fruit: neither doeth a corrupt tree bring foorth good fruit.
text.NABRE: “A good tree does not bear rotten fruit, nor does a rotten tree bear good fruit.
text.DRB: For there is no good tree that yieldeth evil fruits; nor evil tree, that yieldeth good fruit.

text.NRSV: for each tree is known by its own fruit. Figs are not gathered from thorns, nor are grapes picked from a bramble bush.
text.Rotherham: For, every tree, by its own fruit, becometh known. For not, of thorns, do they gather figs, neither, of a bramble-bush, do they gather, a bunch of grapes.
text.KJV: For euery tree is knowen by his owne fruit: for of thornes men doe not gather figs, nor of a bramble bush gather they grapes.
text.NABRE: For every tree is known by its own fruit. For people do not pick figs from thornbushes, nor do they gather grapes from brambles.
text.DRB: For every tree is known by his fruit. For neither do they gather figs of thorns; neither of a bush do they gather the grape.

text.NRSV: The good person out of the good treasure of the heart produces good, and the evil person out of evil treasure produces evil; for it is out of the abundance of the heart that the mouth speaks.
text.Rotherham: The good man, out of the good treasure of the heart, bringeth forth that which is good; and, the wicked man, out of the wicked heart, bringeth forth that which is wicked; for, out of an overflowing of heart, speaketh, his mouth.
text.KJV: A good man out of the good treasure of his heart, bringeth foorth that which is good: and an euill man out of the euill treasure of his heart, bringeth foorth that which is euill: For of the abundance of the heart, his mouth speaketh.
text.NABRE: A good person out of the store of goodness in his heart produces good, but an evil person out of a store of evil produces evil; for from the fullness of the heart the mouth speaks.
text.DRB: The good man of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth good; and the evil man of the ill treasure bringeth forth evil. For of the aboundance of the heart the mouth speaketh,

text.NRSV: “Why do you call me ‘Lord, Lord,’ and do not do what I tell you?
text.Rotherham: And why call ye me, Lord! Lord! and not do the things that I say?
text.KJV: ¶ And why call ye mee Lord, Lord, and doe not the things which I say?
text.NABRE: The Two Foundations. “Why do you call me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ but not do what I command?
text.DRB: And why call you me, Lord, Lord: and do not the things which I say?

text.NRSV: I will show you what someone is like who comes to me, hears my words, and acts on them.
text.Rotherham: Every one coming unto me, and hearkening unto my words, and doing them, I will suggest to you, whom he is like:
text.KJV: Whosoeuer commeth to me, and heareth my sayings, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like.
text.NABRE: I will show you what someone is like who comes to me, listens to my words, and acts on them.
text.DRB: Every one that cometh to me, and heareth my words, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like.

text.NRSV: That person is like a man building a house, who dug deeply and laid the foundation on rock; when a flood arose, the river burst against that house but could not shake it, because it had been well built.
text.Rotherham: He is like unto a man building a house, who digged, and deepened, and laid a foundation upon the rock,—and, a flood, coming, the stream burst against that house, and was not strong enough to shake it, because it had been, well, built.
text.KJV: He is like a man which built an house, and digged deepe, and layd the foundation on a rocke. And when the flood arose, the streame beat vehemently vpon that house, and could not shake it: for it was founded vpon a rocke.
text.NABRE: That one is like a person building a house, who dug deeply and laid the foundation on rock; when the flood came, the river burst against that house but could not shake it because it had been well built.
text.DRB: He is like to a man building a house, that digged deep, and laid the foundation upon a rock. And when an inundation rose, the river beat against that house, and it could not move it; for it was founded upon a rock.

text.NRSV: But the one who hears and does not act is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. When the river burst against it, immediately it fell, and great was the ruin of that house.”
text.Rotherham: But, he that hath heard and not done, is like unto a man having built a house upon the earth, without a foundation,—against which the stream burst, and, straightway, it fell in; and it came to pass, that, the crash of that house, was, great.
text.KJV: But he that heareth, and doeth not, is like a man that without a foundation built an house vpon the earth: against which the streame did beate vehemently, and immediatly it fell, and the ruine of that house was great.
text.NABRE: But the one who listens and does not act is like a person who built a house on the ground without a foundation. When the river burst against it, it collapsed at once and was completely destroyed.”
text.DRB: But he that heareth, and doeth not; is like to a man building his house upon the earth without a foundation: against the which the river did beat; and incontinent it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.

text.NRSV: After Jesus had finished all his sayings in the hearing of the people, he entered Capernaum.
text.Rotherham: After he had completed all his sayings within the hearing of the people, he entered into Capernaum.
text.KJV: Now when hee had ended all his sayings in the audience of the people, hee entred into Capernaum.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 The Healing of a Centurion’s Slave. When he had finished all his words to the people, he entered Capernaum.
text.DRB: And when he had fully said all his words into the ears of the people, he entered into Capharnaum.

text.NRSV: A centurion there had a servant whom he valued highly, and who was ill and close to death.
text.Rotherham: And, a certain centurion’s servant, being sick, was on the point of dying,—who was, by him, highly prized.
text.KJV: And a certaine Centurions seruant, who was deare vnto him, was sicke and ready to die.
text.NABRE: A centurion there had a slave who was ill and about to die, and he was valuable to him.
text.DRB: And the servant of a certain Centurion being sick, was ready to die: who was dear unto him.

text.NRSV: When he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to him, asking him to come and heal his servant.
text.Rotherham: But, hearing about Jesus, he sent forth unto him elders of the Jews, requesting him that he would come and bring his servant safely through.
text.KJV: And when he heard of Iesus, he sent vnto him the Elders of the Iewes, beseeching him that he would come and heale his seruant.
text.NABRE: When he heard about Jesus, he sent elders of the Jews to him, asking him to come and save the life of his slave.
text.DRB: And when he had heard of Jesus, he sent unto him the Ancients of the Jews, desiring him to come and heal his servant.

text.NRSV: When they came to Jesus, they appealed to him earnestly, saying, “He is worthy of having you do this for him,
text.Rotherham: And, they, coming near unto Jesus, began beseeching him earnestly, saying—Worthy, is he for whom thou mayest do this,
text.KJV: And when they came to Iesus, they besought him instantly, saying, that hee was worthy for whome hee should doe this.
text.NABRE: They approached Jesus and strongly urged him to come, saying, “He deserves to have you do this for him,
text.DRB: But they being come to Jesus, besought him earnestly, saying to him, That he is worthy that thou shouldest do this for him.

text.NRSV: for he loves our people, and it is he who built our synagogue for us.”
text.Rotherham: for he loveth our nation, and, the synagogue, he, built for us.
text.KJV: For he loueth our nation, and hee hath built vs a Synagogue.
text.NABRE: for he loves our nation and he built the synagogue for us.”
text.DRB: For he loveth our Nation; and he hath built a Synagogue for us.

text.NRSV: And Jesus went with them, but when he was not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to say to him, “Lord, do not trouble yourself, for I am not worthy to have you come under my roof;
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, was journeying with them. But, he, by this time, being not far from the house, the centurion sent friends, saying unto him—Lord! do not trouble thyself, for I am of no consideration, that, under my roof, thou shouldst enter.
text.KJV: Then Iesus went with them. And when he was now not farre from the house, the Centurion sent friends to him, saying vnto him, Lord, trouble not thy selfe: for I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter vnder my roofe.
text.NABRE: And Jesus went with them, but when he was only a short distance from the house, the centurion sent friends to tell him, “Lord, do not trouble yourself, for I am not worthy to have you enter under my roof.
text.DRB: And Jesus went with them. And when he was now not far from the house, the Centurion sent his friends unto him, saying: Lord, trouble not thyself. For I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof.

text.NRSV: therefore I did not presume to come to you. But only speak the word, and let my servant be healed.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, neither deemed I, myself, worthy to come unto thee,—but speak with a word, and let my servant be healed.
text.KJV: Wherefore neither thought I my selfe worthy to come vnto thee: but say in a worde, and my seruant shall bee healed.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I did not consider myself worthy to come to you; but say the word and let my servant be healed.
text.DRB: For the which cause neither did I think myself worthy to come to thee; but say the word, and my servant shall be made whole.

text.NRSV: For I also am a man set under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes, and to my slave, ‘Do this,’ and the slave does it.”
text.Rotherham: For, I too, am a man ranked, under authority, having, under myself, soldiers; and I say to this one—Go! and he goeth,—and to another—Come! and he cometh,—and to my servant—Do this! and he doeth it.
text.KJV: For I also am a man set vnder authoritie, hauing vnder mee souldiers: and I say vnto one, Goe, and he goeth: and to another, Come, and hee commeth: and to my seruant, Doe this, and he doeth it.
text.NABRE: For I too am a person subject to authority, with soldiers subject to me. And I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come here,’ and he comes; and to my slave, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
text.DRB: For I also am a man subject to authority, having under me soldiers: and I say to this, go, and he goeth: and to another, come, and he cometh; and to my servant, do this, and he doeth it.

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard this he was amazed at him, and turning to the crowd that followed him, he said, “I tell you, not even in Israel have I found such faith.”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing these things, Jesus marvelled at him; and, turning to the multitude following him, said—I tell you, Not even in Israel, such faith as this, have I found!
text.KJV: When Iesus heard these things, hee marueiled at him, and turned him about, and saide vnto the people that followed him, I say vnto you, I haue not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.
text.NABRE: When Jesus heard this he was amazed at him and, turning, said to the crowd following him, “I tell you, not even in Israel have I found such faith.”
text.DRB: Which Jesus hearing, marveled; and turning to the multitudes that followed him he said: Amen I say to you, neither in Israel have I found so great faith.

text.NRSV: When those who had been sent returned to the house, they found the servant in good health.
text.Rotherham: And they who were sent, returning unto the house, found the servant, well.
text.KJV: And they that were sent, returning to the house, found the seruant whole that had bene sicke.
text.NABRE: When the messengers returned to the house, they found the slave in good health. Raising of the Widow’s Son.
text.DRB: And they that were sent, being returned home, found the servant that had been sick, whole.

text.NRSV: Soon afterwards he went to a town called Nain, and his disciples and a large crowd went with him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass thereafter, that he journeyed unto a city called Nain, and there were journeying with him, his disciples and a great multitude.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe the day after, that he went into a citie called Naim: and many of his disciples went with him, and much people.
text.NABRE: Soon afterward he journeyed to a city called Nain, and his disciples and a large crowd accompanied him.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, afterward he went into a city that is called Naim; and there went with him his Disciples and a very great multitude.

text.NRSV: As he approached the gate of the town, a man who had died was being carried out. He was his mother’s only son, and she was a widow; and with her was a large crowd from the town.
text.Rotherham: Now, as he drew near unto the gate of the city, then lo! there was being brought forth, one dead, the only-begotten son of his mother,—and, she, was a widow; and, a considerable multitude of the city, was with her.
text.KJV: Now when he came nigh to the gate of the citie, behold, there was a dead man caried out, the onely sonne of his mother, and shee was a widow: and much people of the citie was with her.
text.NABRE: As he drew near to the gate of the city, a man who had died was being carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
text.DRB: And when he came nigh to the gate of the city, behold a dead man was carried forth, the only son of his mother; and she was a widow: and a great multitude of the city with her.

text.NRSV: When the Lord saw her, he had compassion for her and said to her, “Do not weep.”
text.Rotherham: And, beholding her, the Lord, was moved with compassion over her, and said to her—Be not weeping!
text.KJV: And when the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and saide vnto her, Weepe not.
text.NABRE: When the Lord saw her, he was moved with pity for her and said to her, “Do not weep.”
text.DRB: Whom when our Lord had seen, being moved with mercy upon her, he said to her: Weep not.

text.NRSV: Then he came forward and touched the bier, and the bearers stood still. And he said, “Young man, I say to you, rise!”
text.Rotherham: And, going forward, he touched the coffin, and the bearers stood still; and he said—Young man! to thee, I say, Arise!
text.KJV: And hee came and touched the beere (and they that bare him, stood still.) And he said, Yong man, I say vnto thee, Arise.
text.NABRE: He stepped forward and touched the coffin; at this the bearers halted, and he said, “Young man, I tell you, arise!”
text.DRB: And he came near and touched the coffin. And they that carried it stood still; and he said: Young man, I say to thee, arise.

text.NRSV: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
text.Rotherham: And the dead man sat up, and began to speak; and he gave him to his mother.
text.KJV: And he that was dead, sate vp, and began to speake: and he deliuered him to his mother.
text.NABRE: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
text.DRB: And he that was dead, sat up, and began to speak. And he gave him to his mother.

text.NRSV: Fear seized all of them, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has risen among us!” and “God has looked favorably on his people!”
text.Rotherham: And fear seized them, all, and they began to glorify God, saying—A great prophet, hath been raised up amongst us! and—God hath visited his people!
text.KJV: And there came a feare on all, and they glorified God, saying, that a great Prophet is risen vp among vs, and that God hath visited his people.
text.NABRE: Fear seized them all, and they glorified God, exclaiming, “A great prophet has arisen in our midst,” and “God has visited his people.”
text.DRB: And fear took them all; and they magnified God, saying, That a great Prophet is risen among us: and, that God hath visited his people.

text.NRSV: This word about him spread throughout Judea and all the surrounding country.
text.Rotherham: And this report went forth throughout the whole of Judaea, and all the surrounding country.
text.KJV: And this rumour of him went foorth throughout all Iudea, and throughout all the region round about.
text.NABRE: This report about him spread through the whole of Judea and in all the surrounding region. The Messengers from John the Baptist.
text.DRB: And this saying went forth into all Jewry of him, & into all the country about.

text.NRSV: The disciples of John reported all these things to him. So John summoned two of his disciples
text.Rotherham: And John’s disciples carried tidings unto him concerning all these things.
text.KJV: And the disciples of Iohn shewed him of all these things.
text.NABRE: The disciples of John told him about all these things. John summoned two of his disciples
text.DRB: And John's disciples shewed him of all these things.

text.NRSV: and sent them to the Lord to ask, “Are you the one who is to come, or are we to wait for another?”
text.Rotherham: And, calling unto him certain two of his disciples, John sent unto the Lord, saying—Art, thou, the Coming One, or, a different one, are we to expect?
text.KJV: ¶ And Iohn calling vnto him two of his disciples, sent them to Iesus, saying, Art thou hee that should come, or looke we for another?
text.NABRE: and sent them to the Lord to ask, “Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another?”
text.DRB: And John called two of his disciples, and sent them to Jesus, saying: Art thou he that art to come; or expect we another?

text.NRSV: When the men had come to him, they said, “John the Baptist has sent us to you to ask, ‘Are you the one who is to come, or are we to wait for another?’”
text.Rotherham: And, coming near unto him, the men said—John the Immerser, hath sent us unto thee, saying, Art, thou, the Coming One, or, another, are we to expect?
text.KJV: When the men were come vnto him, they said, Iohn Baptist hath sent vs vnto thee, saying, Art thou hee that should come, or looke we for another?
text.NABRE: When the men came to him, they said, “John the Baptist has sent us to you to ask, ‘Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another?’”
text.DRB: And when the men were come unto him, they said: John the Baptist hath sent us to thee, saying: Art thou he that art to come; or expect we another?

text.NRSV: Jesus had just then cured many people of diseases, plagues, and evil spirits, and had given sight to many who were blind.
text.Rotherham: In that very hour, he cured many from diseases, and plagues, and wicked spirits; and, unto many blind, gave he the favour to see.
text.KJV: And in that same houre hee cured many of their infirmities and plagues, and of euill spirits, and vnto many that were blind, he gaue sight.
text.NABRE: At that time he cured many of their diseases, sufferings, and evil spirits; he also granted sight to many who were blind.
text.DRB: (And the self same hour, he cured many of maladies, and hurts, and evil spirits: and to many blind he gave sight.)

text.NRSV: And he answered them, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard: the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, the poor have good news brought to them.
text.Rotherham: And, answering, he said unto them—Go and bear tidings unto John, as to what ye have seen and heard: The blind, are receiving sight, the lame, walk, lepers, are cleansed, and, the deaf, hear, the dead, are raised, the destitute, are told the glad-message;
text.KJV: Then Iesus answering, said vnto them, Go your way, and tell Iohn what things ye haue seene and heard, how that the blind see, the lame walke, the lepers are clensed, the deafe heare, the dead are raised, to the poore the Gospel is preached.
text.NABRE: And he said to them in reply, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard: the blind regain their sight, the lame walk, lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, the poor have the good news proclaimed to them.
text.DRB: And answering, he said to them: Go & report to John what you have heard and seen: That the blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are made clean, the deaf hear, the dead rise again,

text.NRSV: And blessed is anyone who takes no offense at me.”
text.Rotherham: And, happy, is he, whosoever shall not find occasion of stumbling in me!
text.KJV: And blessed is he whosoeuer shall not be offended in me.
text.NABRE: And blessed is the one who takes no offense at me.”
text.DRB: the poor are evangelized: and blessed is he whosoever shall not be scandalized in me.

text.NRSV: When John’s messengers had gone, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to look at? A reed shaken by the wind?
text.Rotherham: And, the messengers of John having departed, he began to say unto the multitudes, concerning John—What went ye forth into the desert to gaze at? A reed, by a wind, shaken?
text.KJV: ¶ And when the messengers of Iohn were departed, hee beganne to speake vnto þ people concerning Iohn: What went ye out into the wildernesse for to see? A reede shaken with the winde?
text.NABRE: Jesus’ Testimony to John. When the messengers of John had left, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John. “What did you go out to the desert to see—a reed swayed by the wind?
text.DRB: And when John's messengers were departed, he began to say of John to the multitudes: What went you out into the desert to see? A reed moved with the wind?

text.NRSV: What then did you go out to see? Someone dressed in soft robes? Look, those who put on fine clothing and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
text.Rotherham: But what went ye forth to see? A man, in soft garments, arrayed? Lo! they who in splendid apparel and luxury are found, are, in the kingly courts.
text.KJV: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? Behold, they which are gorgeously apparelled, and liue delicately, are in kings courts.
text.NABRE: Then what did you go out to see? Someone dressed in fine garments? Those who dress luxuriously and live sumptuously are found in royal palaces.
text.DRB: But what went you forth to see? A man clothed in soft garments? Behold they that are in costly apparel and delicacies, are in the house of kings.

text.NRSV: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
text.Rotherham: But what went ye forth to see? A prophet? Yea! I say unto you,—and abundantly more than a prophet.
text.KJV: But what went ye out for to see? A Prophet? Yea, I say vnto you, and much more then a Prophet.
text.NABRE: Then what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
text.DRB: But what went you out for to see? A Prophet? Certes I say to you, and more than a Prophet.

text.NRSV: This is the one about whom it is written, ‘See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way before you.’
text.Rotherham: This, is he concerning whom it is written—Lo! I am sending forth my messenger before thy face, who shall prepare thy way before thee.
text.KJV: This is he of whome it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
text.NABRE: This is the one about whom scripture says: ‘Behold, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, he will prepare your way before you.’
text.DRB: This is he of whom it is written: Behold I send mine Angel before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.

text.NRSV: I tell you, among those born of women no one is greater than John; yet the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.”
text.Rotherham: I say unto you—A greater, among them that are born of women, than John, is there none; but, he that is least in the kingdom of God, is greater than he.
text.KJV: For I say vnto you, among those that are borne of women, there is not a greater Prophet then Iohn the Baptist: but he that is least in the kingdome of God, is greater then he.
text.NABRE: I tell you, among those born of women, no one is greater than John; yet the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.”
text.DRB: For I say to you: A greater Prophet among the children of women than John the Baptist, there is no man. But he that is the lesser in the Kingdom of God, is greater than he.

text.NRSV: (And all the people who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged the justice of God, because they had been baptized with John’s baptism;
text.Rotherham: And, all the people, when they heard, and the tax-collectors, justified God, having been immersed with the immersion of John;
text.KJV: And all the people that heard him, and the Publicanes, iustified God, being baptized with the baptisme of Iohn.
text.NABRE: (All the people who listened, including the tax collectors, and who were baptized with the baptism of John, acknowledged the righteousness of God;
text.DRB: And all the people hearing and the Publicans, justified God, being baptized with John's baptism.

text.NRSV: but by refusing to be baptized by him, the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected God’s purpose for themselves.)
text.Rotherham: but, the Pharisees and the lawyers, had set aside, the counsel of God against themselves, not being immersed by him.
text.KJV: But the Pharisees and Lawyers reiected the counsell of God against themselues, being not baptized of him.
text.NABRE: but the Pharisees and scholars of the law, who were not baptized by him, rejected the plan of God for themselves.)
text.DRB: But the Pharisees and the lawyers despised the counsel of God against themselves, being not baptized of him.

text.NRSV: “To what then will I compare the people of this generation, and what are they like?
text.Rotherham: Unto what, then, shall I liken the men of this generation? And unto what are they like?
text.KJV: ¶ And the Lord said, whereunto then shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like?
text.NABRE: “Then to what shall I compare the people of this generation? What are they like?
text.DRB: And our Lord said: Where unto then shall I liken the men of this generation, and whereunto are they like?

text.NRSV: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling to one another, ‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we wailed, and you did not weep.’
text.Rotherham: They are like unto the children sitting, in a market-place, and calling one to another, who say—We played the flute for you, and ye danced not, We wailed, and ye wept not.
text.KJV: They are like vnto children sitting in the market place, & calling one to another, and saying, We haue piped vnto you, and ye haue not danced: wee haue mourned to you, and yee haue not wept.
text.NABRE: They are like children who sit in the marketplace and call to one another, ‘We played the flute for you, but you did not dance. We sang a dirge, but you did not weep.’
text.DRB: They are like to children sitting in the market-place, and speaking one to another, and saying: We have piped to you, and you have not danced; we have lamented, and you have not wept.

text.NRSV: For John the Baptist has come eating no bread and drinking no wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon’;
text.Rotherham: For John the Immerser hath come, neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and ye say—A demon, he hath:
text.KJV: For Iohn the Baptist came, neither eating bread, nor drinking wine, and ye say, He hath a deuill.
text.NABRE: For John the Baptist came neither eating food nor drinking wine, and you said, ‘He is possessed by a demon.’
text.DRB: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and you say: He hath a devil.

text.NRSV: the Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Look, a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’
text.Rotherham: The Son of Man hath come, eating and drinking, and ye say—Lo! a man, gluttonous and a wine-drinker, a friend of tax-collectors and sinners.
text.KJV: The sonne of man is come, eating, and drinking, and ye say, Behold a gluttonous man, and a wine bibber, a friend of Publicanes and sinners.
text.NABRE: The Son of Man came eating and drinking and you said, ‘Look, he is a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.’
text.DRB: The Son of man came eating and drinking; and you say: Behold a man that is a gourmander and a drinker of wine, a friend of Publicans and sinners.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless, wisdom is vindicated by all her children.”
text.Rotherham: And yet wisdom hath been justified by all her children.
text.KJV: But wisedome is iustified of all her children.
text.NABRE: But wisdom is vindicated by all her children.” The Pardon of the Sinful Woman.
text.DRB: And wisdom is justified of all her children.

text.NRSV: One of the Pharisees asked Jesus to eat with him, and he went into the Pharisee’s house and took his place at the table.
text.Rotherham: And a certain one of the Pharisees was requesting him, that he would eat with him; and, entering into the house of the Pharisee, he reclined.
text.KJV: ¶ And one of the Pharisees desired him that he would eat with him. And he went into the Pharisees house, and sate downe to meat.
text.NABRE: A Pharisee invited him to dine with him, and he entered the Pharisee’s house and reclined at table.
text.DRB: And one of the Pharisees desired him to eat with him. And being entered into the house of the Pharisee, he sat down to meat.

text.NRSV: And a woman in the city, who was a sinner, having learned that he was eating in the Pharisee’s house, brought an alabaster jar of ointment.
text.Rotherham: And, lo! a woman, who indeed was in the city a sinner; and, when she found out that he was reclining in the house of the Pharisee, providing an alabaster-jar of perfume,
text.KJV: And behold, a woman in the citie which was a sinner, when shee knew that Iesus sate at meat in the Pharisees house, brought an Alabaster boxe of ointment,
text.NABRE: Now there was a sinful woman in the city who learned that he was at table in the house of the Pharisee. Bringing an alabaster flask of ointment,
text.DRB: And behold a woman that was in the city, a sinner, as she knew that he was set down in the Pharisee's house, she brought an alabaster box of ointment;

text.NRSV: She stood behind him at his feet, weeping, and began to bathe his feet with her tears and to dry them with her hair. Then she continued kissing his feet and anointing them with the ointment.
text.Rotherham: and standing behind, near his feet, weeping, with the tears, began she to be wetting his feet, and, with the hair of her head, was wiping off [the tears], and was tenderly kissing his feet, and anointing them with the perfume.
text.KJV: And stood at his feet behind him, weeping, and began to wash his feete with teares, and did wipe them with the haires of her head, and kissed his feet, and anointed them with the oyntment.
text.NABRE: she stood behind him at his feet weeping and began to bathe his feet with her tears. Then she wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and anointed them with the ointment.
text.DRB: and standing behind beside his feet, she began to water his feet with tears, & wiped them with the hairs of her head, and kissed his feet, & anointed them with the ointment.

text.NRSV: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, “If this man were a prophet, he would have known who and what kind of woman this is who is touching him—that she is a sinner.”
text.Rotherham: But the Pharisee who had invited him, seeing it, spake within himself, saying, This one, if he were the prophet, would have been taking note, who and of what sort, is the woman, who is even touching him, that she is, a sinner.
text.KJV: Now when the Pharisee which had bidden him, saw it, he spake within himselfe, saying, This man, if he were a Prophet, would haue knowen who, and what maner of woman this is that toucheth him: for she is a sinner.
text.NABRE: When the Pharisee who had invited him saw this he said to himself, “If this man were a prophet, he would know who and what sort of woman this is who is touching him, that she is a sinner.”
text.DRB: And the Pharisee that had bid him, seeing it, spake within himself, saying: This man if he were a Prophet, would know certes who and what manner of woman she is which toucheth him, that she is a sinner.

text.NRSV: Jesus spoke up and said to him, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Teacher,” he replied, “Speak.”
text.Rotherham: And, making answer, Jesus said unto him—Simon! I have, unto thee, something to say. He, then—Teacher, speak!—saith he.
text.KJV: And Iesus answering, said vnto him, Simon, I haue somewhat to say vnto thee. And he saith, Master, say on.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him in reply, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Tell me, teacher,” he said.
text.DRB: And Jesus answering said to him: Simon, I have somewhat to say unto thee. But he said: Master, say.

text.NRSV: “A certain creditor had two debtors; one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
text.Rotherham: Two debtors, there were, to a certain creditor,—the one, owed five hundred denaries, and, the other, fifty.
text.KJV: There was a certaine creditour, which had two debtors: the one ought fiue hundred pence, and the other fiftie.
text.NABRE: “Two people were in debt to a certain creditor; one owed five hundred days’ wages and the other owed fifty.
text.DRB: Acertain creditor had two debters; one did owe five hundred pence, & the other fifty.

text.NRSV: When they could not pay, he canceled the debts for both of them. Now which of them will love him more?”
text.Rotherham: they not having wherewith to pay, he forgave, both. Which of them, therefore, will love him, more?
text.KJV: And when they had nothing to pay, he frankly forgaue them both. Tell me therefore, which of them will loue him most?
text.NABRE: Since they were unable to repay the debt, he forgave it for both. Which of them will love him more?”
text.DRB: They having not wherewith to pay, he forgave both. Whether therefore doth love him more?

text.NRSV: Simon answered, “I suppose the one for whom he canceled the greater debt.” And Jesus said to him, “You have judged rightly.”
text.Rotherham: Making answer, Simon said—I suppose, that he to whom, the more, he forgave. And, he, said unto him—Rightly, hast thou judged.
text.KJV: Simon answered, and saide, I suppose, that hee to whome he forgaue most. And he said vnto him, Thou hast rightly iudged.
text.NABRE: Simon said in reply, “The one, I suppose, whose larger debt was forgiven.” He said to him, “You have judged rightly.”
text.DRB: Simon answering said: I suppose that he to whom he forgave more. But he said to him: Thou hast judged rightly.

text.NRSV: Then turning toward the woman, he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered your house; you gave me no water for my feet, but she has bathed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.
text.Rotherham: And, turning towards the woman, unto Simon, he said—Seest thou this woman? I entered into thy house: water to me, on my feet, thou didst not give,—but, she, with her tears, hath wetted my feet, and, with her hair, wiped off [the tears] .
text.KJV: And hee turned to the woman, and said vnto Simon, Seest thou this woman? I entred into thine house, thou gauest me no water for my feete: but shee hath washed my feete with teares, and wiped them with the haires of her head.
text.NABRE: Then he turned to the woman and said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? When I entered your house, you did not give me water for my feet, but she has bathed them with her tears and wiped them with her hair.
text.DRB: And turning to the woman, he said unto Simon: Doest thou see this woman? I entered into thy house, water to my feet thou didst not give; but she with tears hath watered my feet, and with her hairs hath wiped them.

text.NRSV: You gave me no kiss, but from the time I came in she has not stopped kissing my feet.
text.Rotherham: A kiss, to me, thou didst not give,—but, she, from the time I came in, hath not ceased tenderly kissing my feet.
text.KJV: Thou gauest me no kisse: but this woman, since the time I came in, hath not ceased to kisse my feet.
text.NABRE: You did not give me a kiss, but she has not ceased kissing my feet since the time I entered.
text.DRB: Kiss thou gavest me not; but she since I came in, hath not ceased to kiss my feet.

text.NRSV: You did not anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.
text.Rotherham: With oil, my head, thou didst not anoint,—but, she, with perfume, hath anointed, my feet.
text.KJV: Mine head with oile thou didst not anoint: but this woman anointed my feet with oyntment.
text.NABRE: You did not anoint my head with oil, but she anointed my feet with ointment.
text.DRB: With oil thou didst not anoint my head; but she with ointment hath anointed my feet.

text.NRSV: Therefore, I tell you, her sins, which were many, have been forgiven; hence she has shown great love. But the one to whom little is forgiven, loves little.”
text.Rotherham: For which cause, I say unto thee—Her many sins, have been forgiven, because she hath loved, much: but, he to whom little is forgiven, little, loveth.
text.KJV: Wherefore, I say vnto thee, her sinnes, which are many, are forgiuen, for she loued much: but to whom litle is forgiuen, the same loueth litle.
text.NABRE: So I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven; hence, she has shown great love. But the one to whom little is forgiven, loves little.”
text.DRB: For the which I say to thee: Many sins are forgiven her, because she hath loved much. But to whom less is forgiven, he loveth less.

text.NRSV: Then he said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto her—Thy sins have been forgiven.
text.KJV: And he said vnto her, Thy sinnes are forgiuen.
text.NABRE: He said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”
text.DRB: And he said to her: Thy sins are forgiven thee.

text.NRSV: But those who were at the table with him began to say among themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”
text.Rotherham: And they who were reclining together, began to be saying within themselves—Who is, this, that, even forgiveth sins?
text.KJV: And they that sate at meat with him, began to say within themselues, Who is this that forgiueth sinnes also?
text.NABRE: The others at table said to themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”
text.DRB: And they that sat together at the table began to say within themselves. Who is this that also forgiveth sins?

text.NRSV: And he said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you; go in peace.”
text.Rotherham: But he said unto the woman—Thy faith, hath saved thee,—Go thy way into peace.
text.KJV: And he said to the woman, Thy faith hath saued thee, goe in peace.
text.NABRE: But he said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you; go in peace.”
text.DRB: And he said to the woman: Thy faith hath made thee safe, go in peace.

text.NRSV: Soon afterwards he went on through cities and villages, proclaiming and bringing the good news of the kingdom of God. The twelve were with him,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in due course, that he was passing through, city by city and village by village, proclaiming, and delivering the glad-message of the kingdom of God,—and, the twelve, were with him;
text.KJV: And it came to passe afterward, þ he went throughout euery citie and village preaching, and shewing the glad tidings of the kingdome of God: and the twelue were with him,
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 Galilean Women Follow Jesus. Afterward he journeyed from one town and village to another, preaching and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom of God. Accompanying him were the Twelve
text.DRB: And it came to pass afterward, and he made his journey by cities and towns preaching and evangelizing the Kingdom of God; and the Twelve with him,

text.NRSV: as well as some women who had been cured of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary, called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,
text.Rotherham: and certain women, who had been cured from evil spirits and infirmities,—Mary, the one called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone forth,
text.KJV: And certaine women which had bene healed of euill spirits and infirmities, Mary called Magdalene out of whom went seuen deuils,
text.NABRE: and some women who had been cured of evil spirits and infirmities, Mary, called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,
text.DRB: and some women that had been cured of wicked spirits and infirmities; Mary which is called Magdalene, out of whom seven devils were gone forth,

text.NRSV: and Joanna, the wife of Herod’s steward Chuza, and Susanna, and many others, who provided for them out of their resources.
text.Rotherham: and Joana, wife of Chuza steward of Herod, and Susanna, and many others,—who indeed were ministering unto them out of their possessions.
text.KJV: And Ioanna the wife of Chuza, Herods steward, and Susanna, and many others which ministred vnto him of their substance.
text.NABRE: Joanna, the wife of Herod’s steward Chuza, Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources. The Parable of the Sower.
text.DRB: And Joane the wife of Chusa Herod's procurator, and Susan, and many others that did minister unto him of their substance.

text.NRSV: When a great crowd gathered and people from town after town came to him, he said in a parable:
text.Rotherham: And, seeing that a great multitude were coming together, and they who from every city were journeying forth unto him, he spake through means of a parable:—
text.KJV: ¶ And when much people were gathered together, and were come to him out of euery citie, he spake by a parable:
text.NABRE: When a large crowd gathered, with people from one town after another journeying to him, he spoke in a parable.
text.DRB: And when a very great multitude assembled and hastened out of the cities unto him, he said by a similitude.

text.NRSV: “A sower went out to sow his seed; and as he sowed, some fell on the path and was trampled on, and the birds of the air ate it up.
text.Rotherham: The sower went forth to sow his seed,—and, as he sowed, some, indeed, fell beside the pathway, and was trodden down; and, the birds of heaven, devoured it.
text.KJV: A Sower went out to sowe his seed: and as he sowed, some fell by the wayes side, and it was troden downe, and the foules of the aire deuoured it.
text.NABRE: “A sower went out to sow his seed. And as he sowed, some seed fell on the path and was trampled, and the birds of the sky ate it up.
text.DRB: The sower went forth to sow his seed. And whiles he soweth, some fell by the way side, and was trodden upon, and the fowls of the air did eat it.

text.NRSV: Some fell on the rock; and as it grew up, it withered for lack of moisture.
text.Rotherham: And, other, fell down upon the rock; and, growing, was withered, because it had not moisture.
text.KJV: And some fell vpon a rocke, and assoone as it was sprung vp, it withered away, because it lacked moisture.
text.NABRE: Some seed fell on rocky ground, and when it grew, it withered for lack of moisture.
text.DRB: And other some fell upon the rock; and being shot up, it withered, because it had not moisture.

text.NRSV: Some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew with it and choked it.
text.Rotherham: And, other, fell and thorns; and, growing together, the thorns, choked it.
text.KJV: And some fell among thornes, and the thornes sprang vp with it, and choked it.
text.NABRE: Some seed fell among thorns, and the thorns grew with it and choked it.
text.DRB: And othersome fell among thorns, and the thorns growing up withal, choked it.

text.NRSV: Some fell into good soil, and when it grew, it produced a hundredfold.” As he said this, he called out, “Let anyone with ears to hear listen!”
text.Rotherham: And, other, fell into good ground; and, growing, brought forth fruit, a hundredfold. These things, saying, he went on to cry aloud—He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
text.KJV: And other fell on good ground, and sprang vp, and bare fruite an hundred fold. And when hee saide these things, he cryed, He that hath eares to heare, let him heare.
text.NABRE: And some seed fell on good soil, and when it grew, it produced fruit a hundredfold.” After saying this, he called out, “Whoever has ears to hear ought to hear.” The Purpose of the Parables.
text.DRB: And other some fell upon good ground; and being shot up, yielded fruit an hundred fold. Saying these things he cried: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: Then his disciples asked him what this parable meant.
text.Rotherham: But his disciples began to question him—What might, this very, parable be?
text.KJV: And his disciples asked him, saying, What might this parable be?
text.NABRE: Then his disciples asked him what the meaning of this parable might be.
text.DRB: And his Disciples asked him what this parable was.

text.NRSV: He said, “To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of God; but to others I speak in parables, so that ‘looking they may not perceive, and listening they may not understand.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Unto you, hath it been given, to get to know the sacred secrets of the kingdom of God; but, unto the rest, in parables,—in order that, seeing, they may not see, and, hearing, they may not understand.
text.KJV: And he said, Unto you it is giuen to know the mysteries of the kingdome of God: but to others in parables, that seeing, they might not see, and hearing, they might not vnderstand.
text.NABRE: He answered, “Knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of God has been granted to you; but to the rest, they are made known through parables so that ‘they may look but not see, and hear but not understand.’ The Parable of the Sower Explained.
text.DRB: To whom he said: To you it is given to know the mystery of the Kingdom of God; but to the rest in parables, that seeing they may not see, and hearing may not understand.

text.NRSV: “Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
text.Rotherham: Now the parable, is this—The seed, is, the word of God.
text.KJV: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
text.NABRE: “This is the meaning of the parable. The seed is the word of God.
text.DRB: And the parable is this: The seed, is the word of God.

text.NRSV: The ones on the path are those who have heard; then the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved.
text.Rotherham: And, those beside the pathway, are they who have heard; afterwards, cometh the adversary, and catcheth away the word from their heart, lest, believing, they should, be saved.
text.KJV: Those by the way side, are they that heare: then commeth the deuil, and taketh away the word out of their hearts, least they should beleeue, and be saued.
text.NABRE: Those on the path are the ones who have heard, but the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts that they may not believe and be saved.
text.DRB: And they besides the way, are those that hear, then the devil cometh, and taketh the word out of their heart, lest believing they be saved.

text.NRSV: The ones on the rock are those who, when they hear the word, receive it with joy. But these have no root; they believe only for a while and in a time of testing fall away.
text.Rotherham: And, those upon the rock, are they who, as soon as they hear, with joy, welcome the word; and, these, not having, root, are they who, for a season, believe,—and, in a season of testing, draw back.
text.KJV: They on the rocke, are they which when they heare, receiue the word with ioy; and these haue no roote, which for a while beleeue, and in time of temptation fall away.
text.NABRE: Those on rocky ground are the ones who, when they hear, receive the word with joy, but they have no root; they believe only for a time and fall away in time of trial.
text.DRB: For they upon the rock; such as when they hear, with joy receive the word: and these have no roots; because for a time they believe, and in time of tentation they revolt.

text.NRSV: As for what fell among the thorns, these are the ones who hear; but as they go on their way they are choked by the cares and riches and pleasures of life, and their fruit does not mature.
text.Rotherham: And, that which in among the thorns fell, these, are they who have heard; and, by anxieties and wealth and pleasures of life being borne along, are choked up, and bear not to perfection.
text.KJV: And that which fell among thornes, are they, which when they haue heard, goe forth, and are choked with cares and riches, and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruite to perfection.
text.NABRE: As for the seed that fell among thorns, they are the ones who have heard, but as they go along, they are choked by the anxieties and riches and pleasures of life, and they fail to produce mature fruit.
text.DRB: And that which fell into thorns, are they that have heard, and going their ways, are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and render not fruit.

text.NRSV: But as for that in the good soil, these are the ones who, when they hear the word, hold it fast in an honest and good heart, and bear fruit with patient endurance.
text.Rotherham: But, that in the good ground, these, are they who, indeed, in a noble and good heart having heard the word, hold fast, and bear fruit with endurance.
text.KJV: But that on the good ground, are they, which in an honest and good heart hauing heard the word, keepe it, and bring foorth fruite with patience.
text.NABRE: But as for the seed that fell on rich soil, they are the ones who, when they have heard the word, embrace it with a generous and good heart, and bear fruit through perseverance. The Parable of the Lamp.
text.DRB: And that upon good ground, are they which in a good and very good heart, hearing the word, do retain it, and yield fruit in patience.

text.NRSV: “No one after lighting a lamp covers it with a vessel, or puts it under a bed, but puts it on a lampstand, so that those who enter may see the light.
text.Rotherham: And, no one having lit a lamp, covereth it up with a vessel, or, beneath a couch, putteth it; but, upon a lamp-stand, putteth it, that, they who come in, may see the light.
text.KJV: ¶ No man when he hath lighted a candle, couereth it with a vessell, or putteth it vnder a bed: but setteth it on a candlesticke, that they which enter in, may see the light.
text.NABRE: “No one who lights a lamp conceals it with a vessel or sets it under a bed; rather, he places it on a lampstand so that those who enter may see the light.
text.DRB: And no man lighting a candle doth cover it with a vessel, or put it under a bed; but setteth it upon a candlestick, that they that enter in, may see the light.

text.NRSV: For nothing is hidden that will not be disclosed, nor is anything secret that will not become known and come to light.
text.Rotherham: For there is no secret, which shall not be made, manifest; neither a hidden thing, which shall in anywise not be made known, or not come, where it can be seen.
text.KJV: For nothing is secret, that shall not be made manifest: neither any thing hid, that shall not be knowen, and come abroad.
text.NABRE: For there is nothing hidden that will not become visible, and nothing secret that will not be known and come to light.
text.DRB: For there is not any thing secret, that shall not be made manifest; nor hid, that shall not be known, & come abroad.

text.NRSV: Then pay attention to how you listen; for to those who have, more will be given; and from those who have nothing, even what they seem to have will be taken away.”
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed therefore how ye hear; for, whosoever shall have, it shall be given unto him; and, whosoever shall not have, even what he seemeth to have, shall be taken from him.
text.KJV: Take heede therefore how yee heare: for whosoeuer hath, to him shall bee giuen; and whosoeuer hath not, from him shall be taken, euen that which he seemeth to haue.
text.NABRE: Take care, then, how you hear. To anyone who has, more will be given, and from the one who has not, even what he seems to have will be taken away.” Jesus and His Family.
text.DRB: See therefore how you hear. For he that hath, to him shall be given; and whosoever hath not, that also which he thinketh he hath, shall be taken away from him.

text.NRSV: Then his mother and his brothers came to him, but they could not reach him because of the crowd.
