text.Rotherham: And his mother and brethren came near unto him, and were unable to reach him, because of the multitude.
text.KJV: ¶ Then came to him his mother and his brethren, and could not come at him for the prease.
text.NABRE: Then his mother and his brothers came to him but were unable to join him because of the crowd.
text.DRB: And his mother and brethren came unto him; and they could not come at him for the multitude.

text.NRSV: And he was told, “Your mother and your brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.”
text.Rotherham: And it was reported to him—Thy mother and thy brethren, are standing outside, desiring, to see, thee.
text.KJV: And it was told him by certaine which saide, Thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to see thee.
text.NABRE: He was told, “Your mother and your brothers are standing outside and they wish to see you.”
text.DRB: And it was told him: Thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desirous to see thee.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “My mother and my brothers are those who hear the word of God and do it.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them—My mother and my brethren, are, these—they who, the word of God, are hearing and doing.
text.KJV: And hee answered and said vnto them, My mother and my brethren are these which heare the word of God, and doe it.
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “My mother and my brothers are those who hear the word of God and act on it.” The Calming of a Storm at Sea.
text.DRB: Who answering said to them: My mother and my brethren, are they that hear the word of God and do it.

text.NRSV: One day he got into a boat with his disciples, and he said to them, “Let us go across to the other side of the lake.” So they put out,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on one of the days, that, he, entered into a boat, and his disciples; and he said unto them—Let us pass over unto the other side of the lake. And they set sail.
text.KJV: ¶ Now it came to passe on a certaine day, that he went into a ship, with his disciples: and hee said vnto them, Let vs goe ouer vnto the other side of the lake, and they lanched foorth.
text.NABRE: One day he got into a boat with his disciples and said to them, “Let us cross to the other side of the lake.” So they set sail,
text.DRB: And it came to pass one day; and he went up into a boat, and his Disciples, and he said to them: Let us strike over the lake. And they launched forth.

text.NRSV: and while they were sailing he fell asleep. A gale swept down on the lake, and the boat was filling with water, and they were in danger.
text.Rotherham: Now, as they were sailing, he fell asleep. And there came down a hurricane of wind upon the lake, and they began to be filled, and to be in peril.
text.KJV: But as they sailed, he fell asleepe, and there came downe a storme of wind on the lake, and they were filled with water, and were in ieopardie.
text.NABRE: and while they were sailing he fell asleep. A squall blew over the lake, and they were taking in water and were in danger.
text.DRB: And when they were sailing, he slept; and there fell a storm of wind into the lake, and they were filled, and were in danger.

text.NRSV: They went to him and woke him up, shouting, “Master, Master, we are perishing!” And he woke up and rebuked the wind and the raging waves; they ceased, and there was a calm.
text.Rotherham: And, coming near, they roused him up, saying—Master! Master! we perish! And, he, roused up, rebuked the wind, and the surging of the water; and they ceased, and it became a calm.
text.KJV: And they came to him, and awoke him, saying, Master, master, we perish. Then he rose, and rebuked the wind, and the raging of the water: and they ceased, and there was a calme.
text.NABRE: They came and woke him saying, “Master, master, we are perishing!” He awakened, rebuked the wind and the waves, and they subsided and there was a calm.
text.DRB: And they came and raised him, saying: Master, we perish. But he rising, rebuked the wind and the tempest of water; and it ceased, and there was made a calm.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Where is your faith?” They were afraid and amazed, and said to one another, “Who then is this, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Where was your faith? But, struck with fear, they marvelled, saying one to another—Who then is, this,—that, even unto the winds, he giveth orders, and unto the water, and they hearken unto him.
text.KJV: And he saide vnto them, Where is your faith? And they being afraide wondred, saying one to another, What maner of man is this? For he commandeth euen the winds and water, and they obey him.
text.NABRE: Then he asked them, “Where is your faith?” But they were filled with awe and amazed and said to one another, “Who then is this, who commands even the winds and the sea, and they obey him?” The Healing of the Gerasene Demoniac.
text.DRB: And he said to them: Where is your faith? Who fearing, marveled one to another, saying: Who is this (trow ye) that he commandeth both the winds and the sea, and they obey him?

text.NRSV: Then they arrived at the country of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And they sailed down into the country of the Gerasenes, the which is over against Galilee.
text.KJV: ¶ And they arriued at the countrey of the Gadarenes, which is ouer against Galilee.
text.NABRE: Then they sailed to the territory of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.
text.DRB: And they sailed to the country of the Gerasens which is over against Galilee.

text.NRSV: As he stepped out on land, a man of the city who had demons met him. For a long time he had worn no clothes, and he did not live in a house but in the tombs.
text.Rotherham: And, when he went forth upon the land, there met him a certain man, out of the city, having demons; and, for a considerable time, he had put on no garment, and, in a house, would not abide, but among the tombs.
text.KJV: And when he went forth to land, there met him out of the citie a certaine man which had deuils long time, and ware no clothes, neither abode in any house, but in the tombes.
text.NABRE: When he came ashore a man from the town who was possessed by demons met him. For a long time he had not worn clothes; he did not live in a house, but lived among the tombs.
text.DRB: And when he was come forth to the land, there met him a certain man that had a devil now a very long time, and he did wear no cloths, neither did he tarry in house, but in the monuments.

text.NRSV: When he saw Jesus, he fell down before him and shouted at the top of his voice, “What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, do not torment me” —
text.Rotherham: But, seeing Jesus, he cried out aloud, and fell down before him, and, with a loud voice, said—What have I in common with thee, O Jesus, Son of [God] the Most High? I beg of thee—Do not torment me!—
text.KJV: When he saw Iesus, he cried out, and fell downe before him, and with a loud voyce said, What haue I to doe with thee, Iesus, thou sonne of God most high? I beseech thee torment me not.
text.NABRE: When he saw Jesus, he cried out and fell down before him; in a loud voice he shouted, “What have you to do with me, Jesus, son of the Most High God? I beg you, do not torment me!”
text.DRB: And as he saw Jesus, he fell down before him; and crying out with a great voice, he said: What is to me & thee Jesus Son of God most high? I beseech thee do not torment me.

text.NRSV: for Jesus had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. (For many times it had seized him; he was kept under guard and bound with chains and shackles, but he would break the bonds and be driven by the demon into the wilds.)
text.Rotherham: for he was about to charge the impure spirit to come out from the man. For, many times, had it snatched him away; and he used to be bound with chains and fetters, for a safe-guard, and was wont to break in pieces the bonds, and to be driven by the demon into the deserts.
text.KJV: (For he had commanded the vncleane spirit to come out of the man: For oftentimes it had caught him, and he was kept bound with chaines, and in fetters: and he brake the bands, and was driuen of the deuil into the wildernesse.)
text.NABRE: For he had ordered the unclean spirit to come out of the man. (It had taken hold of him many times, and he used to be bound with chains and shackles as a restraint, but he would break his bonds and be driven by the demon into deserted places.)
text.DRB: For he commanded the unclean spirit to go forth out of the man. For many times he caught him, and he was bound with chains, and kept with fetters; and breaking the bonds was driven of the devil into the deserts.

text.NRSV: Jesus then asked him, “What is your name?” He said, “Legion”; for many demons had entered him.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus questioned him—What name hast thou? And, he, said—Legion! because many demons had entered into him.
text.KJV: And Iesus asked him, saying, What is thy name? And he said, Legion: because many deuils were entred into him.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” He replied, “Legion,” because many demons had entered him.
text.DRB: And Jesus asked him saying: What is thy name? But he said: Legion, because many devils were entered into him.

text.NRSV: They begged him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
text.Rotherham: And they were beseeching him that he would not order them, into the abyss, to depart.
text.KJV: And they besought him, that he would not command them to goe out into the deepe.
text.NABRE: And they pleaded with him not to order them to depart to the abyss.
text.DRB: And they besought him that he would not command them to go into the depth.

text.NRSV: Now there on the hillside a large herd of swine was feeding; and the demons begged Jesus to let them enter these. So he gave them permission.
text.Rotherham: Now there was there, a herd of a good many swine, feeding in the mountain; and they besought him, that he would suffer them, into those, to enter; and he suffered them.
text.KJV: And there was there an herd of many swine feeding on the mountaine: and they besought him that he would suffer them to enter into them: and he suffered them.
text.NABRE: A herd of many swine was feeding there on the hillside, and they pleaded with him to allow them to enter those swine; and he let them.
text.DRB: And there was there a herd of many swine feeding on the mountain; & they desired him that he would permit them to enter into them. And he permitted them.

text.NRSV: Then the demons came out of the man and entered the swine, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and was drowned.
text.Rotherham: And the demons, going forth from the man entered into the swine; and the herd rushed down the cliff, into the lake, and were choked.
text.KJV: Then went the deuils out of the man, and entred into the swine: and the herd ran violently downe a steepe place into the lake, and were choked.
text.NABRE: The demons came out of the man and entered the swine, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and was drowned.
text.DRB: The devils therefore went forth out of the man, and entered into the swine; and the herd with violence went headlong into the lake, and was stifled.

text.NRSV: When the herdsmen saw what had happened, they ran off and told it in the city and in the country.
text.Rotherham: And they who had been feeding them, seeing what had happened, fled, and carried tidings into the city, and into the country-places.
text.KJV: When they that fed them saw what was done, they fled, and went, and tolde it in the citie, and in the countrey.
text.NABRE: When the swineherds saw what had happened, they ran away and reported the incident in the town and throughout the countryside.
text.DRB: Which when the swineherds saw done, they fled, and told into the city and into the towns.

text.NRSV: Then people came out to see what had happened, and when they came to Jesus, they found the man from whom the demons had gone sitting at the feet of Jesus, clothed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.
text.Rotherham: And they went forth to see what had happened; and came unto Jesus, and found the man from whom the demons had gone forth, sitting, clothed, and of sound mind, near the feet of Jesus,—and they were struck with fear.
text.KJV: Then they went out to see what was done, and came to Iesus, and found the man, out of whom the deuils were departed, sitting at the feete of Iesus, clothed, and in his right minde: and they were afraid.
text.NABRE: People came out to see what had happened and, when they approached Jesus, they discovered the man from whom the demons had come out sitting at his feet. He was clothed and in his right mind, and they were seized with fear.
text.DRB: And they went forth to see that which was done; and they came to Jesus, and found the man, out of whom the devils were gone forth, sitting at his feet, clothed, and well in his wits, and they were afraid.

text.NRSV: Those who had seen it told them how the one who had been possessed by demons had been healed.
text.Rotherham: And they who had seen it, reported unto them, how the demonized man was saved.
text.KJV: They also which saw it, told them by what meanes he that was possessed of the deuils, was healed.
text.NABRE: Those who witnessed it told them how the possessed man had been saved.
text.DRB: And they also that had seen, told them how he had been made whole from the legion.

text.NRSV: Then all the people of the surrounding country of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave them; for they were seized with great fear. So he got into the boat and returned.
text.Rotherham: And one and all of the throng of the surrounding country of the Gerasenes, requested him, to depart from them; because, with a great fear, were they oppressed. And, he, entering into a boat, returned.
text.KJV: ¶ Then the whole multitude of the countrey of the Gadarenes round about, besought him to depart from them, for they were taken with great feare: and he went vp into the ship, and returned back againe.
text.NABRE: The entire population of the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave them because they were seized with great fear. So he got into a boat and returned.
text.DRB: And all the multitude of the country of the Gerasens besought him to depart from them; for they were taken with great fear. And he going up into the boat, returned.

text.NRSV: The man from whom the demons had gone begged that he might be with him; but Jesus sent him away, saying,
text.Rotherham: And the man from whom the demons had gone forth, began to beg of him, that he might be with him; but he dismissed him, saying—
text.KJV: Now the man, out of whom the deuils were departed, besought him that he might be with him: but Iesus sent him away, saying,
text.NABRE: The man from whom the demons had come out begged to remain with him, but he sent him away, saying,
text.DRB: And the man out of whom the devils were departed, desired him that he might be with him. But Jesus dismissed him, saying:

text.NRSV: “Return to your home, and declare how much God has done for you.” So he went away, proclaiming throughout the city how much Jesus had done for him.
text.Rotherham: Be returning unto thy house, and telling forth what great things, God, for thee, hath done. And he departed, through all the city, proclaiming what great things, Jesus, had done for him.
text.KJV: Returne to thine owne house, and shew how great things God hath done vnto thee. And he went his way, and published throughout the whole citie how great things Iesus had done vnto him.
text.NABRE: “Return home and recount what God has done for you.” The man went off and proclaimed throughout the whole town what Jesus had done for him. Jairus’s Daughter and the Woman with a Hemorrhage.
text.DRB: Return into thy house, and tell how great things God hath done to thee. And he went through the whole city, preaching how great things Jesus had done to him.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.
text.Rotherham: Now, when Jesus returned, the multitude welcomed him back, for they were all expecting him.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that when Iesus was returned, the people gladly receiued him: for they were all waiting for him.
text.NABRE: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.
text.DRB: And it came to pass; when Jesus was returned, the multitude received him. And all were expecting him.

text.NRSV: Just then there came a man named Jairus, a leader of the synagogue. He fell at Jesus’ feet and begged him to come to his house,
text.Rotherham: And lo! there came a man, whose name was Jairus, and, the same, was, a ruler of the synagogue,—and, falling down near the feet of Jesus, he began beseeching him to enter into his house;
text.KJV: ¶ And behold, there came a man named Iairus, and hee was a ruler of the Synagogue, and hee fell downe at Iesus feete, and besought him that hee would come into his house:
text.NABRE: And a man named Jairus, an official of the synagogue, came forward. He fell at the feet of Jesus and begged him to come to his house,
text.DRB: And behold there came a man whose name was Jairus, and he was Prince of the Synagogue: and he fell at the feet of Jesus, desiring him that he would enter into his house,

text.NRSV: for he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, who was dying. As he went, the crowds pressed in on him.
text.Rotherham: because he had, an only-begotten daughter, about twelve years old, and, she, was dying. Now, as he withdrew, the multitudes, were hemming him in.
text.KJV: For hee had one onely daughter about twelue yeeres of age, and she lay a dying. (But as hee went the people thronged him.
text.NABRE: because he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, and she was dying. As he went, the crowds almost crushed him.
text.DRB: because he had an only daughter almost twelve years old, and she was a dying. And it chanced, whiles he went, he was thronged of the multitudes.

text.NRSV: Now there was a woman who had been suffering from hemorrhages for twelve years, and though she had spent all she had on physicians, no one could cure her.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman, with a flow of blood of twelve years standing, who indeed could, from no one, be cured,
text.KJV: ¶ And a woman hauing an issue of blood twelue yeres, which had spent all her liuing vpon Phisitions, neither could be healed of any,
text.NABRE: And a woman afflicted with hemorrhages for twelve years, who [had spent her whole livelihood on doctors and] was unable to be cured by anyone,
text.DRB: And there was a certain woman in a flux of blood from twelve years past, which had bestowed all her substance upon Physicians, neither could she be cured of any:

text.NRSV: She came up behind him and touched the fringe of his clothes, and immediately her hemorrhage stopped.
text.Rotherham: coming near behind, touched the fringe of his mantle; and, instantly, was stayed the flow of her blood.
text.KJV: Came behinde him, and touched the border of his garment: and immediatly her issue of blood stanched.
text.NABRE: came up behind him and touched the tassel on his cloak. Immediately her bleeding stopped.
text.DRB: she came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment; and forthwith the flux of her blood stinted,

text.NRSV: Then Jesus asked, “Who touched me?” When all denied it, Peter said, “Master, the crowds surround you and press in on you.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said—Who, is it that touched me? And, when all were denying, Peter said—Master! the multitudes, are hemming thee in, and pressing along.
text.KJV: And Iesus saide, Who touched mee? When all denied, Peter and they that were with him, said, Master, the multitude throng thee, and preasse thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?
text.NABRE: Jesus then asked, “Who touched me?” While all were denying it, Peter said, “Master, the crowds are pushing and pressing in upon you.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said: Who is it that touched me? And all denying, Peter said, and they that were with him: Master, the multitudes throng and press thee, and doest thou say, Who touched me?

text.NRSV: But Jesus said, “Someone touched me; for I noticed that power had gone out from me.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said—Some one touched me, for, I, took note of power, gone out from me.
text.KJV: And Iesus saide, Some body hath touched mee: for I perceiue that vertue is gone out of me.
text.NABRE: But Jesus said, “Someone has touched me; for I know that power has gone out from me.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said: Somebody hath touched me; for I know that there is virtue proceeded from me.

text.NRSV: When the woman saw that she could not remain hidden, she came trembling, and falling down before him, she declared in the presence of all the people why she had touched him, and how she had been immediately healed.
text.Rotherham: And, the woman seeing that she had not escaped notice, trembling, came, and, falling down before him, reported before all the people, for what cause she had touched him, and how she was healed instantly!
text.KJV: And when the woman saw that she was not hid, shee came trembling, and falling downe before him, shee declared vnto him before all the people, for what cause shee had touched him, and how she was healed immediatly.
text.NABRE: When the woman realized that she had not escaped notice, she came forward trembling. Falling down before him, she explained in the presence of all the people why she had touched him and how she had been healed immediately.
text.DRB: And the woman seeing, that she was not hid, came trembling, and fell down before his feet; and for what cause she had touched him, she shewed before all the people, and how forthwith she was made whole.

text.NRSV: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said to her—Daughter! thy faith, hath saved thee: Go thy way into peace.
text.KJV: And he said vnto her, Daughter, be of good comfort, thy faith hath made thee whole, goe in peace.)
text.NABRE: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has saved you; go in peace.”
text.DRB: But he said to her: Daughter, thy faith hath made thee safe, go thy way in peace.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking, someone came from the leader’s house to say, “Your daughter is dead; do not trouble the teacher any longer.”
text.Rotherham: While yet he is speaking, there cometh one from the synagogue ruler’s, saying—Thy daughter, is dead: No further, be troubling the teacher.
text.KJV: ¶ While hee yet spake, there commeth one from the ruler of the Synagogues house, saying to him, Thy daughter is dead, trouble not the Master.
text.NABRE: While he was still speaking, someone from the synagogue official’s house arrived and said, “Your daughter is dead; do not trouble the teacher any longer.”
text.DRB: As he was speaking, there cometh one to the Prince of the Synagogue, saying to him, That thy daughter is dead, trouble him not.

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard this, he replied, “Do not be afraid. Only believe, and she will be saved.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, hearing, answered him—Do not fear! Only believe, and she shall be saved.
text.KJV: But when Iesus heard it, he answered him, saying, Feare not, beleeue onely, and she shalbe made whole.
text.NABRE: On hearing this, Jesus answered him, “Do not be afraid; just have faith and she will be saved.”
text.DRB: And Jesus hearing this word, answered the father of the maid: Fear not; believe only, and she shall be safe.

text.NRSV: When he came to the house, he did not allow anyone to enter with him, except Peter, John, and James, and the child’s father and mother.
text.Rotherham: And, when he came into the house, he suffered no one to enter with him, save Peter and John and James, and the father of the girl, and the mother.
text.KJV: And when hee came into the house, hee suffered no man to goe in, saue Peter, and Iames, and Iohn, and the father and the mother of the mayden.
text.NABRE: When he arrived at the house he allowed no one to enter with him except Peter and John and James, and the child’s father and mother.
text.DRB: And when he was come to the house, he permitted not any man to enter in with him, but Peter, and James, and John, and the father and mother of the maid.

text.NRSV: Everyone was weeping and wailing for her; but he said, “Do not weep; for she is not dead but sleeping.”
text.Rotherham: And they were all weeping, and beating themselves, for her. And he said—Be not weeping; for she died not, but is sleeping.
text.KJV: And all wept, and bewailed her: but he said, Weepe not, she is not dead, but sleepeth.
text.NABRE: All were weeping and mourning for her, when he said, “Do not weep any longer, for she is not dead, but sleeping.”
text.DRB: And all wept, and mourned for her. But he said: Weep not, the maid is not dead, but sleepeth.

text.NRSV: And they laughed at him, knowing that she was dead.
text.Rotherham: And they were deriding him, knowing that she died.
text.KJV: And they laughed him to scorne, knowing that she was dead.
text.NABRE: And they ridiculed him, because they knew that she was dead.
text.DRB: And they derided him, knowing that she was dead.

text.NRSV: But he took her by the hand and called out, “Child, get up!”
text.Rotherham: But, he, grasping her hand, called aloud, saying—O girl! arise!
text.KJV: And hee put them all out, and tooke her by the hand, and called, saying, Mayd, arise.
text.NABRE: But he took her by the hand and called to her, “Child, arise!”
text.DRB: But he holding her hand cried saying: Maid arise.

text.NRSV: Her spirit returned, and she got up at once. Then he directed them to give her something to eat.
text.Rotherham: And her spirit returned, and she rose up instantly, and he ordered that something should be given her to eat.
text.KJV: And her spirit came againe, and shee arose straightway: and hee commanded to giue her meat.
text.NABRE: Her breath returned and she immediately arose. He then directed that she should be given something to eat.
text.DRB: And her spirit returned, and she rose incontinent. And he bade them give her to eat.

text.NRSV: Her parents were astounded; but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
text.Rotherham: And her parents were beside themselves. But, he, charged them to tell, no one, what had happened.
text.KJV: And her parents were astonished: but hee charged them that they should tell no man what was done.
text.NABRE: Her parents were astounded, and he instructed them to tell no one what had happened.
text.DRB: And her parents were astonished, whom he commanded to tell no man that which was done.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus called the twelve together and gave them power and authority over all demons and to cure diseases,
text.Rotherham: And, calling together the twelve, he gave them power and authority over all the demons, and to be curing, diseases;
text.KJV: Then he called his twelue disciples together, and gaue them power and authority ouer all deuils, and to cure diseases.
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 The Mission of the Twelve. He summoned the Twelve and gave them power and authority over all demons and to cure diseases,
text.DRB: And calling together the twelve Apostles, he gave them virtue and power over all devils, and to cure maladies.

text.NRSV: and he sent them out to proclaim the kingdom of God and to heal.
text.Rotherham: and sent them forth, to be proclaiming the kingdom of God, and to be healing;
text.KJV: And hee sent them to preach the Kingdome of God, and to heale the sicke.
text.NABRE: and he sent them to proclaim the kingdom of God and to heal [the sick].
text.DRB: And he sent them to preach the Kingdom of God; and to heal the sick.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Take nothing for your journey, no staff, nor bag, nor bread, nor money — not even an extra tunic.
text.Rotherham: and said unto them—Nothing, take ye for the journey,—neither staff, nor satchel, nor bread, nor silver, nor to have, two tunics.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Take nothing for your iourney, neither staues, nor scrip, neither bread, neither money, neither haue two coates apeece.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Take nothing for the journey, neither walking stick, nor sack, nor food, nor money, and let no one take a second tunic.
text.DRB: And he said to them: Take nothing for the way, neither rod, nor scrip, nor bread, nor money, neither have two coats.

text.NRSV: Whatever house you enter, stay there, and leave from there.
text.Rotherham: And, into whatsoever house ye enter, there, abide, and, thence, be going forth.
text.KJV: And whatsoeuer house yee enter into, there abide, and thence depart.
text.NABRE: Whatever house you enter, stay there and leave from there.
text.DRB: And into whatsoever house you enter, tarry there, and thence do not depart.

text.NRSV: Wherever they do not welcome you, as you are leaving that town shake the dust off your feet as a testimony against them.”
text.Rotherham: And, as many soever as shall not welcome you, in going forth from that city, the dust of your feet, shake ye off, for a witness against them.
text.KJV: And whosoeuer will not receiue you, when ye goe out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feete, for a testimonie against them.
text.NABRE: And as for those who do not welcome you, when you leave that town, shake the dust from your feet in testimony against them.”
text.DRB: And whosoever shall not receive you, going forth out of that city, shake off the dust also of your feet for a testimony upon them.

text.NRSV: They departed and went through the villages, bringing the good news and curing diseases everywhere.
text.Rotherham: And they went forth, and were passing through, along the villages,—delivering the glad-message, and effecting cures in every direction.
text.KJV: And they departed, and went through the townes, preaching the Gospel, and healing euery where.
text.NABRE: Then they set out and went from village to village proclaiming the good news and curing diseases everywhere. Herod’s Opinion of Jesus.
text.DRB: And going forth they went a circuit from town to town evangelizing and curing every-where.

text.NRSV: Now Herod the ruler heard about all that had taken place, and he was perplexed, because it was said by some that John had been raised from the dead,
text.Rotherham: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all the things which were coming to pass, and was utterly at a loss, because of its being said, by some, that, John, had been raised from the dead;
text.KJV: ¶ Now Herode the Tetrarch heard of all that was done by him: and hee was perplexed, because that it was said of some, that Iohn was risen from the dead:
text.NABRE: Herod the tetrarch heard about all that was happening, and he was greatly perplexed because some were saying, “John has been raised from the dead”;
text.DRB: And Herod the Tetrarch heard all things that were done by him; and he staggered because it was said of some, That John was risen from the dead.

text.NRSV: by some that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the ancient prophets had arisen.
text.Rotherham: and, by some, that, Elijah, had appeared; and, by others, that, some prophet of the ancients, had arisen.
text.KJV: And of some, that Elias had appeared: and of others, that one of the olde Prophets was risen againe.
text.NABRE: others were saying, “Elijah has appeared”; still others, “One of the ancient prophets has arisen.”
text.DRB: But of other some, That Elias hath appeared; and of others, that a Prophet one of the old ones was risen.

text.NRSV: Herod said, “John I beheaded; but who is this about whom I hear such things?” And he tried to see him.
text.Rotherham: But Herod said—John, I myself, beheaded; but, who is this, concerning whom I am hearing such things as these? And he was seeking to see him.
text.KJV: And Herode said, Iohn haue I beheaded: but who is this of whom I heare such things? And hee desired to see him.
text.NABRE: But Herod said, “John I beheaded. Who then is this about whom I hear such things?” And he kept trying to see him. The Return of the Twelve and the Feeding of the Five Thousand.
text.DRB: And Herod said: John I have beheaded; but who is this of whom I hear such things? And he sought for to see him.

text.NRSV: On their return the apostles told Jesus all they had done. He took them with him and withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
text.Rotherham: And the Apostles, returning, related to him what great things they had done. And, taking them aside, he retired privately, into a city called Bethsaida.
text.KJV: ¶ And the Apostles when they were returned, tolde him all that they had done. And hee tooke them, and went aside priuately into a desert place, belonging to the citie called Bethsaida.
text.NABRE: When the apostles returned, they explained to him what they had done. He took them and withdrew in private to a town called Bethsaida.
text.DRB: And the Apostles being returned, reported to him whatsoever they did: and taking them he retired apart into a desert place, which belongeth to Beth-saida.

text.NRSV: When the crowds found out about it, they followed him; and he welcomed them, and spoke to them about the kingdom of God, and healed those who needed to be cured.
text.Rotherham: But, the multitudes, getting to know, followed him; and, giving them welcome, he began speaking unto them concerning the kingdom of God,—and, them that had need of cure, he healed,
text.KJV: And the people when they knew it, followed him, and he receiued them, and spake vnto them of the kingdome of God, and healed them that had need of healing.
text.NABRE: The crowds, meanwhile, learned of this and followed him. He received them and spoke to them about the kingdom of God, and he healed those who needed to be cured.
text.DRB: Which the multitudes understanding, followed him & he received them, and spake to them of the Kingdom of God, and them that had need of cure he healed.

text.NRSV: The day was drawing to a close, and the twelve came to him and said, “Send the crowd away, so that they may go into the surrounding villages and countryside, to lodge and get provisions; for we are here in a deserted place.”
text.Rotherham: And, the day, began to decline; and the twelve, coming near, said to him—Dismiss the multitude, that, going into the surrounding villages and hamlets, they may lodge, and find provisions; because, here, in a desert place, are we.
text.KJV: And when the day beganne to weare away, then came the twelue, and said vnto him, Send the multitude away, that they may go into the townes and countrey round about, and lodge, and get victuals: for we are here in a desert place.
text.NABRE: As the day was drawing to a close, the Twelve approached him and said, “Dismiss the crowd so that they can go to the surrounding villages and farms and find lodging and provisions; for we are in a deserted place here.”
text.DRB: And the day began to draw towards an end. And the Twelve coming near, said to him: Dismiss the multitudes, that going into towns and villages here about, they may have lodging, and find meats; because here we are in a desert place.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “You give them something to eat.” They said, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish — unless we are to go and buy food for all these people.”
text.Rotherham: But he said unto them—Give, ye, them to eat. They, however, said—We have not more than five loaves, and two fishes,—unless perhaps, we, should go, and buy food, for all this people.
text.KJV: But he said vnto them, Giue yee them to eate. And they said, Wee haue no more but fiue loaues and two fishes, except we should goe and buy meate for all this people.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Give them some food yourselves.” They replied, “Five loaves and two fish are all we have, unless we ourselves go and buy food for all these people.”
text.DRB: And he said to them: Give you them to eat. But they said: We have no more but five loaves and two fishes; unless perhaps we should go & buy meats for all this multitude.

text.NRSV: For there were about five thousand men. And he said to his disciples, “Make them sit down in groups of about fifty each.”
text.Rotherham: For there were about five thousand men. And he said unto his disciples—Make them recline, in companies of about fifty each.
text.KJV: For they were about fiue thousand men. And he said to his disciples, Make them sit downe by fifties in a company.
text.NABRE: Now the men there numbered about five thousand. Then he said to his disciples, “Have them sit down in groups of [about] fifty.”
text.DRB: And there were men almost five thousand. And he said to his Disciples: Make them sit down by companies fifty and fifty.

text.NRSV: They did so and made them all sit down.
text.Rotherham: And they did so,—and made, one and all, recline.
text.KJV: And they did so, and made them all sit downe.
text.NABRE: They did so and made them all sit down.
text.DRB: And so they did. And they made all sit down.

text.NRSV: And taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven, and blessed and broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
text.Rotherham: And, taking the five loaves, and the two fishes, looking up into heaven, he blessed them, and brake [them] up, and went on giving to the disciples, to set before the multitude;
text.KJV: Then he tooke the fiue loaues and the two fishes, and looking vp to heauen, hee blessed them, and brake, and gaue to the disciples to set before the multitude.
text.NABRE: Then taking the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he said the blessing over them, broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
text.DRB: And taking the five loaves and the two fishes, he looked up unto Heaven, and blessed them; and he brake, and distributed to his Disciples, for to set before the multitudes.

text.NRSV: And all ate and were filled. What was left over was gathered up, twelve baskets of broken pieces.
text.Rotherham: and they did all eat and were filled. And that which remained over to them, was taken up,—of broken pieces, twelve baskets.
text.KJV: And they did eate, and were all filled. And there was taken vp of fragments that remained to them, twelue baskets.
text.NABRE: They all ate and were satisfied. And when the leftover fragments were picked up, they filled twelve wicker baskets. Peter’s Confession About Jesus.
text.DRB: And they did all eat, and had their fill. And there was taken up that which remained to them, twelve baskets of fragments.

text.NRSV: Once when Jesus was praying alone, with only the disciples near him, he asked them, “Who do the crowds say that I am?”
text.Rotherham: And, it came to pass, when he was praying in solitude, the disciples, were with him. And he questioned them, saying—Who do the multitudes say that I am?
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, as he was alone praying, his disciples were with him: and he asked them, saying, Whom say the people that I am?
text.NABRE: Once when Jesus was praying in solitude, and the disciples were with him, he asked them, “Who do the crowds say that I am?”
text.DRB: And it came to pass; when he was alone praying, his Disciples also were with him; and he asked them saying: Whom do the multitudes say that I am?

text.NRSV: They answered, “John the Baptist; but others, Elijah; and still others, that one of the ancient prophets has arisen.”
text.Rotherham: And they, answering, said John the Immerser; But some, Elijah, and others, that, some prophet of the ancients, hath arisen.
text.KJV: They answering, said, Iohn the Baptist: but some say, Elias: and others say, that one of the old Prophets is risen againe.
text.NABRE: They said in reply, “John the Baptist; others, Elijah; still others, ‘One of the ancient prophets has arisen.’”
text.DRB: But they answered, and said: John the Baptist; and some, Elias; but some, that one of the Prophets before time, is risen.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered, “The Messiah of God.”
text.Rotherham: And he said to them—But who say, ye, that I am? And, Peter, answering, said—The Christ of God!
text.KJV: He said vnto them, But whom say yee that I am? Peter answering, said, The Christ of God.
text.NABRE: Then he said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter said in reply, “The Messiah of God.”
text.DRB: And he said to them: But whom say ye that I am? Simon Peter answering, said: The Christ of God.

text.NRSV: He sternly ordered and commanded them not to tell anyone,
text.Rotherham: He, however, sternly admonishing them, gave charge that, unto no one, should they be telling this;
text.KJV: And he straitly charged them, and commanded them to tell no man that thing,
text.NABRE: He rebuked them and directed them not to tell this to anyone.
text.DRB: But he rebuking them, commanded that they should tell this to no man,

text.NRSV: saying, “The Son of Man must undergo great suffering, and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and on the third day be raised.”
text.Rotherham: saying—The Son of Man must needs suffer, many things, and be rejected by the Elders and High priests and Scribes, and be slain,—and, on the third day, arise.
text.KJV: Saying, The Sonne of man must suffer many things, and be reiected of the Elders, and chiefe Priests, and Scribes, and be slaine, and be raised the third day.
text.NABRE: The First Prediction of the Passion. He said, “The Son of Man must suffer greatly and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed and on the third day be raised.” The Conditions of Discipleship.
text.DRB: saying: That the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the Ancients and chief Priests and Scribes, and be killed, and the third day rise again.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them all, “If any want to become my followers, let them deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying, unto all—If any one intendeth after me to come, Let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily,—and be following me.
text.KJV: ¶ And he said to them all, If any man will come after me, let him denie himselfe, and take vp his crosse daily, and follow me.
text.NABRE: Then he said to all, “If anyone wishes to come after me, he must deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow me.
text.DRB: And he said to all: If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow me.

text.NRSV: For those who want to save their life will lose it, and those who lose their life for my sake will save it.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever intendeth, his life, to save, shall lose it; but, whosoever shall lose his life for my sake, the same, shall save it?
text.KJV: For whosoeuer will saue his life, shall lose it: but whosoeuer will lose his life for my sake, the same shall saue it.
text.NABRE: For whoever wishes to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake will save it.
text.DRB: For he that will save his life, shall lose it; for he that shall lose his life for my sake, shall save it.

text.NRSV: What does it profit them if they gain the whole world, but lose or forfeit themselves?
text.Rotherham: For what doth a man profit, who hath gained the whole world, but hath lost, or been made to forfeit, himself?
text.KJV: For what is a man aduantaged, if hee gaine the whole world, and lose himselfe, or be cast away?
text.NABRE: What profit is there for one to gain the whole world yet lose or forfeit himself?
text.DRB: For what profit hath a man if he gain the whole world, and lose himself, and cast away himself?

text.NRSV: Those who are ashamed of me and of my words, of them the Son of Man will be ashamed when he comes in his glory and the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever shall be ashamed of me, and of my words, of him, the Son of Man, will be ashamed, whensoever he shall come in his glory, and that of the Father, and of the holy messengers.
text.KJV: For whosoeuer shall bee ashamed of me, and of my wordes, of him shall the Sonne of man be ashamed, when he shall come in his owne glory, and in his Fathers, and of the holy Angels.
text.NABRE: Whoever is ashamed of me and of my words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
text.DRB: For he that shall be ashamed of me and of my words, him the Son of man shall be ashamed of, when he shall come in his majesty, and his Father's, and of the holy Angels.

text.NRSV: But truly I tell you, there are some standing here who will not taste death before they see the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: But I tell you of a truth—There, are, some of those, here, standing, who shall in nowise taste of death, until they see the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: But I tell you of a trueth, there be some standing here, which shall not taste of death, till they see the kingdome of God.
text.NABRE: Truly I say to you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God.” The Transfiguration of Jesus.
text.DRB: And I say to you assuredly: There be some standing here that shall not taste death, till they see the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Now about eight days after these sayings Jesus took with him Peter and John and James, and went up on the mountain to pray.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after these words, about eight days, taking with him Peter and John and James, he went up into the mountain to pray.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, about an eight dayes after these sayings, hee tooke Peter, and Iohn, and Iames, and went vp into a mountaine to pray:
text.NABRE: About eight days after he said this, he took Peter, John, and James and went up the mountain to pray.
text.DRB: And it came to pass after these words almost eight days, and he took Peter and James and John, and went into a mountain to pray.

text.NRSV: And while he was praying, the appearance of his face changed, and his clothes became dazzling white.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while he was praying, that, the appearance of his face, was changed, and, his clothing, became white, effulgent.
text.KJV: And as hee prayed, the fashion of his countenance was altered, and his raiment was white and glistering.
text.NABRE: While he was praying his face changed in appearance and his clothing became dazzling white.
text.DRB: And whiles he prayed, the shape of his countenance was altered: and his raiment white and glistering.

text.NRSV: Suddenly they saw two men, Moses and Elijah, talking to him.
text.Rotherham: And lo! two men, were conversing with him, who, indeed, were Moses and Elijah,—
text.KJV: And behold, there talked with him two men, which were Moses and Elias,
text.NABRE: And behold, two men were conversing with him, Moses and Elijah,
text.DRB: And behold two men talked with him. And they were Moyses and Elias,

text.NRSV: They appeared in glory and were speaking of his departure, which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: who, appearing in glory, were speaking as to his departure, which he was about to fulfil in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: Who appeared in glory, and spake of his decease, which he should accomplish at Hierusalem.
text.NABRE: who appeared in glory and spoke of his exodus that he was going to accomplish in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: appearing in majesty. And they told his decease that he should accomplish in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Now Peter and his companions were weighed down with sleep; but since they had stayed awake, they saw his glory and the two men who stood with him.
text.Rotherham: Now, Peter, and they who were with him, had become heavy with sleep; but, waking up, they saw his glory, and the two men who were standing with him.
text.KJV: But Peter, and they that were with him, were heauie with sleepe: and when they were awake, they saw his glory, and the two men that stood with him.
text.NABRE: Peter and his companions had been overcome by sleep, but becoming fully awake, they saw his glory and the two men standing with him.
text.DRB: But Peter and they that were with him, were heavy with sleep. And awaking, they saw his majesty, and the two men that stood with him.

text.NRSV: Just as they were leaving him, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good for us to be here; let us make three dwellings, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah” — not knowing what he said.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when they were being parted from him, Peter said unto Jesus—Master! it is, delightful, for us to be, here: Let us, therefore, make three tents, one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elijah:—not knowing what he said.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, as they departed from him, Peter said vnto Iesus, Master, it is good for vs to be here, and let vs make three tabernacles, one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias: not knowing what he said.
text.NABRE: As they were about to part from him, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good that we are here; let us make three tents, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.” But he did not know what he was saying.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when they departed from him, Peter said to Jesus: Master, it is good for us to be here; and let us make three tabernacles, one for thee, and one for Moyses, and one for Elias: not knowing what he said.

text.NRSV: While he was saying this, a cloud came and overshadowed them; and they were terrified as they entered the cloud.
text.Rotherham: Now, while he was saying these things, there came a cloud, and it began to overshadow them,—and they were overcome with fear as they entered into the cloud.
text.KJV: While he thus spake, there came a cloud, and ouershadowed them, & they feared, as they entred into the cloude.
text.NABRE: While he was still speaking, a cloud came and cast a shadow over them, and they became frightened when they entered the cloud.
text.DRB: And as he spake these things, there came a cloud, and overshadowed them: and they feared, when they entered into the cloud.

text.NRSV: Then from the cloud came a voice that said, “This is my Son, my Chosen; listen to him!”
text.Rotherham: And, a voice, came out of the cloud, saying—This, is my Son, the Chosen One: Unto him, be hearkening.
text.KJV: And there came a voice out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloued Son, heare him.
text.NABRE: Then from the cloud came a voice that said, “This is my chosen Son; listen to him.”
text.DRB: And a voice was made out of the cloud, saying: This is my beloved Son, hear him.

text.NRSV: When the voice had spoken, Jesus was found alone. And they kept silent and in those days told no one any of the things they had seen.
text.Rotherham: And, when the voice came, there was found, Jesus alone. And they held their peace, and, unto no one, reported they, in those days, any of the things which they had seen.
text.KJV: And when the voyce was past, Iesus was found alone, and they kept it close, & told no man in those dayes any of those things which they had seene.
text.NABRE: After the voice had spoken, Jesus was found alone. They fell silent and did not at that time tell anyone what they had seen. The Healing of a Boy with a Demon.
text.DRB: And whiles the voice was made, Jesus was found alone. And they held their peace, and told no man in those days any of these things which they had seen.

text.NRSV: On the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, a great crowd met him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the next day, when they came down from the mountain, that there met him a great multitude.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, that on the next day, when they were come downe from the hill, much people met him.
text.NABRE: On the next day, when they came down from the mountain, a large crowd met him.
text.DRB: And it came to pass the day following, when they came down from the mountain, there met him a great multitude.

text.NRSV: Just then a man from the crowd shouted, “Teacher, I beg you to look at my son; he is my only child.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a man from the multitude, uttered a cry, saying—Teacher! I beg of thee, to look upon my son, because, my only begotten, he is.
text.KJV: And behold, a man of the companie cried out, saying, Master, I beseech thee looke vpon my sonne, for he is mine onely child.
text.NABRE: There was a man in the crowd who cried out, “Teacher, I beg you, look at my son; he is my only child.
text.DRB: And behold a man of the multitude cried out, saying: Master, I beseech thee, look upon my son because he is mine only one.

text.NRSV: Suddenly a spirit seizes him, and all at once he shrieks; it convulses him until he foams at the mouth; it mauls him and will scarcely leave him.
text.Rotherham: And lo! a spirit, taketh him, and, suddenly, he crieth out,—and it convulseth him—with foaming, and with difficulty departeth from him, sorely bruising him.
text.KJV: And loe, a spirit taketh him, and hee suddenly crieth out, and it teareth him that he fometh againe, and bruising him, hardly departeth from him.
text.NABRE: For a spirit seizes him and he suddenly screams and it convulses him until he foams at the mouth; it releases him only with difficulty, wearing him out.
text.DRB: And lo, the spirit taketh him, and he suddenly cryeth, and he dasheth him, and teareth him that he foameth, and with much ado departeth renting him.

text.NRSV: I begged your disciples to cast it out, but they could not.”
text.Rotherham: And I begged of thy disciples, that they would cast it out, and they could not.
text.KJV: And I besought thy disciples to cast him out, and they could not.
text.NABRE: I begged your disciples to cast it out but they could not.”
text.DRB: And I desired thy Disciples to cast him out, and they could not.

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “You faithless and perverse generation, how much longer must I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here.”
text.Rotherham: And, making answer, Jesus said—O faithless and perverted generation! how long shall I be with you, and bear with you? Bring, hither, thy son.
text.KJV: And Iesus answering, said, O faithlesse, and peruerse generation, how long shal I be with you, and suffer you? bring thy sonne hither.
text.NABRE: Jesus said in reply, “O faithless and perverse generation, how long will I be with you and endure you? Bring your son here.”
text.DRB: And Jesus answering said: O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you and suffer you? Bring hither thy son.

text.NRSV: While he was coming, the demon dashed him to the ground in convulsions. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.
text.Rotherham: And, while yet he was coming, the demon tare him, and mangled him. But Jesus rebuked the impure spirit, and healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.
text.KJV: And as he was yet a comming, the deuill threw him downe, and tare him: and Iesus rebuked the vncleane spirit, and healed the child, and deliuered him againe to his father.
text.NABRE: As he was coming forward, the demon threw him to the ground in a convulsion; but Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the boy, and returned him to his father.
text.DRB: And when he came to him, the devil dashed, and tore him. And Jesus rebuked the unclean Spirit, and healed the lad, and rendered him to his father.

text.NRSV: And all were astounded at the greatness of God. While everyone was amazed at all that he was doing, he said to his disciples,
text.Rotherham: And they were all being struck with astonishment, at the majesty of God. And, while all were marvelling at all things which he was doing, he said unto his disciples:
text.KJV: ¶ And they were al amazed at the mightie power of God: But while they wondred euery one at all things which Iesus did, he said vnto his disciples,
text.NABRE: And all were astonished by the majesty of God. The Second Prediction of the Passion. While they were all amazed at his every deed, he said to his disciples,
text.DRB: And all were astonished at the might of God; and all marveling at all things that he did, he said to his Disciples:

text.NRSV: “Let these words sink into your ears: The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into human hands.”
text.Rotherham: Lay, ye, up in your ears, these words; for, the Son of Man, is about to be delivered up, into the hands of men.
text.KJV: Let these sayings sinke downe into your eares: for the Sonne of man shall bee deliuered into the handes of men.
text.NABRE: “Pay attention to what I am telling you. The Son of Man is to be handed over to men.”
text.DRB: Lay you in your hearts these words, for it shall come to pass that the Son of man shall be delivered into the hands of men.

text.NRSV: But they did not understand this saying; its meaning was concealed from them, so that they could not perceive it. And they were afraid to ask him about this saying.
text.Rotherham: But, they, understood not this saying, and it had become veiled from them, that they might not grasp it,—and they were afraid to question him, concerning this saying.
text.KJV: But they vnderstood not this saying, and it was hid from them, that they perceiued it not: and they feared to aske him of that saying.
text.NABRE: But they did not understand this saying; its meaning was hidden from them so that they should not understand it, and they were afraid to ask him about this saying. The Greatest in the Kingdom.
text.DRB: But they did not know this word, and it was covered before them, that they perceived it not. And they were afraid to ask him of this word.

text.NRSV: An argument arose among them as to which one of them was the greatest.
text.Rotherham: But there entered a reasoning among them, as to who of them should be greatest.
text.KJV: ¶ Then there arose a reasoning among them, which of them should be greatest.
text.NABRE: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
text.DRB: And there entered a cogitation into them, which of them should be greater.

text.NRSV: But Jesus, aware of their inner thoughts, took a little child and put it by his side,
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, perceiving the reasoning of their heart, taking a child, placed it near himself,—
text.KJV: And Iesus perceiuing þ thought of their heart, tooke a child, and set him by him,
text.NABRE: Jesus realized the intention of their hearts and took a child and placed it by his side
text.DRB: But Jesus seeing the cogitations of their heart, took a child and set him by him,

text.NRSV: and said to them, “Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes the one who sent me; for the least among all of you is the greatest.”
text.Rotherham: and said unto them—Whosoever shall give welcome unto this child, on my name, unto me, giveth welcome; and, whosoever, unto me, giveth welcome, giveth welcome unto him that sent me forth. For, he who is least among you all, the same, is, great.
text.KJV: And said vnto them, Whosoeuer shall receiue this child in my Name, receiueth me: and whosoeuer shal receiue me, receiueth him that sent me: For hee that is least among you all, the same shalbe great.
text.NABRE: and said to them, “Whoever receives this child in my name receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me. For the one who is least among all of you is the one who is the greatest.” Another Exorcist.
text.DRB: and said to them, whosoever receiveth this child in my name, receiveth me, and whosoever receiveth me, receiveth him that sent me. For he that is the lesser among you all, he is the greater.

text.NRSV: John answered, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him, because he does not follow with us.”
text.Rotherham: But John, answering, said—Master! we saw some one, in thy name, casting out demons,—and we forbade him, because he followeth not with us.
text.KJV: ¶ And Iohn answered, and said, Master, we saw one casting out deuils in thy Name, and we forbade him, because he followeth not with vs.
text.NABRE: Then John said in reply, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name and we tried to prevent him because he does not follow in our company.”
text.DRB: And John answering said: Master, we saw a certain man casting out devils in thy name, and we prohibited him, because he followeth not with us.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to him, “Do not stop him; for whoever is not against you is for you.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto him—Do not forbid; for, whosoever is not against you, is, for you.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto him, Forbid him not: for he that is not against vs, is for vs.
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “Do not prevent him, for whoever is not against you is for you.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: Prohibit not. For he that is not against you, is for you.

text.NRSV: When the days drew near for him to be taken up, he set his face to go to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the days for taking him up were on the point of being fulfilled, even he himself, set, his face, to be journeying unto Jerusalem;
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, when the time was come that he should bee receiued vp, he stedfastly set his face to goe to Hierusalem,
text.NABRE: V. The Journey to Jerusalem: Luke’s Travel Narrative Departure for Jerusalem; Samaritan Inhospitality. When the days for his being taken up were fulfilled, he resolutely determined to journey to Jerusalem,
text.DRB: And it came to pass, whiles the days of his assumption were accomplishing, and he fixed his face to go into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: And he sent messengers ahead of him. On their way they entered a village of the Samaritans to make ready for him;
text.Rotherham: and he sent messengers before his face,—and, taking their journey, they entered into a village of Samaritans, so as to prepare for him.
text.KJV: And sent messengers before his face, and they went and entred into a village of the Samaritanes to make ready for him.
text.NABRE: and he sent messengers ahead of him. On the way they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his reception there,
text.DRB: And he sent messengers before his face; and going they entered into a city of the Samaritans to prepare for him.

text.NRSV: but they did not receive him, because his face was set toward Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And they welcomed him not, because, his face, was for journeying unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And they did not receiue him, because his face was as though he would goe to Hierusalem.
text.NABRE: but they would not welcome him because the destination of his journey was Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And they received him not, because his face was to go to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: When his disciples James and John saw it, they said, “Lord, do you want us to command fire to come down from heaven and consume them?”
text.Rotherham: And the disciples James and John, seeing it, said—Lord! wilt thou, that we bid fire come down from heaven, and destroy them?
text.KJV: And when his disciples, Iames and Iohn sawe this, they said, Lord, wilt thou that wee command fire to come downe from heauen, and consume them, euen as Elias did?
text.NABRE: When the disciples James and John saw this they asked, “Lord, do you want us to call down fire from heaven to consume them?”
text.DRB: And when his Disciples James and John had seen it, they said: Lord wilt thou we say that fire come down from Heaven and consume them?

text.NRSV: But he turned and rebuked them.
text.Rotherham: But, turning, he rebuked them.
text.KJV: But he turned, and rebuked them, and said, Ye know not what maner spirit ye are of.
text.NABRE: Jesus turned and rebuked them,
text.DRB: And turning, he rebuked them, saying: You know not of what spirit you are.

text.NRSV: Then they went on to another village.
text.Rotherham: And they journeyed into a different village.
text.KJV: For the Sonne of man is not come to destroy mens liues, but to saue them. And they went to another village.
text.NABRE: and they journeyed to another village. The Would-be Followers of Jesus.
text.DRB: The Son of man came not to destroy souls, but to save. And they went into another town.

text.NRSV: As they were going along the road, someone said to him, “I will follow you wherever you go.”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were journeying on the road, one said unto him—I will follow thee, whithersoever thou shalt depart.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe that as they went in the way, a certaine man said vnto him, Lord, I wil follow thee whithersoeuer thou goest.
text.NABRE: As they were proceeding on their journey someone said to him, “I will follow you wherever you go.”
text.DRB: And it came to pass as they walked in the way, a certain man said to him: I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest.

text.NRSV: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto him—the foxes, have, dens, and, the birds of heaven, nests; but, the Son of Man, hath not where, his head, he may recline.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto him, Foxes haue holes, and birds of the aire haue nests, but the Sonne of man hath not where to lay his head.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered him, “Foxes have dens and birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to rest his head.”
text.DRB: Jesus said to him: The foxes have holes, and the fowls of the air nests; but the Son of man hath not where to repose his head.

text.NRSV: To another he said, “Follow me.” But he said, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto another—Be following me! But, he, said—Suffer me, first, to depart, and bury my father.
text.KJV: And he said vnto another, Follow me: But he said, Lord, suffer mee first to goe and bury my father.
text.NABRE: And to another he said, “Follow me.” But he replied, “[Lord,] let me go first and bury my father.”
text.DRB: But he said to another: Follow me. And he said: Lord, permit me first to go, and to bury my father.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said to him, “Let the dead bury their own dead; but as for you, go and proclaim the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him—Leave, the dead, to bury their own dead; but, thou, departing, be declaring the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: Iesus said vnto him, Let the dead bury their dead: but go thou and preach the kingdome of God.
text.NABRE: But he answered him, “Let the dead bury their dead. But you, go and proclaim the kingdom of God.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: Let the dead bury their dead; but go thou, set forth the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Another said, “I will follow you, Lord; but let me first say farewell to those at my home.”
text.Rotherham: And, yet another, said—I will follow thee, Lord; But, first, suffer me to bid adieu to them that are in my house.
text.KJV: And another also said, Lord, I will follow thee: but let me first goe bid them farewel, which are at home at my house.
text.NABRE: And another said, “I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say farewell to my family at home.”
text.DRB: And another said: I will follow thee Lord, but permit me first to take my leave of them that are at home.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “No one who puts a hand to the plow and looks back is fit for the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: But Jesus said [unto him] —No one, laying the hand on a plough and looking unto the things behind, is, fit, for the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto him, No man hauing put his hand to the plough, and looking backe, is fit for the kingdome of God.
text.NABRE: [To him] Jesus said, “No one who sets a hand to the plow and looks to what was left behind is fit for the kingdom of God.”
text.DRB: Jesus said to him: No man putting his hand to the plough, and looking back, is apt for the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: After this the Lord appointed seventy others and sent them on ahead of him in pairs to every town and place where he himself intended to go.
text.Rotherham: And, after these things, the Lord appointed seventy two others, and sent them forth, two and two before his face, into every city and place whither, he himself, was about to come.
text.KJV: After these things, the Lord appointed other seuenty also, and sent them two and two before his face, into euery citie and place, whither hee himselfe would come.
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 The Mission of the Seventy-two. After this the Lord appointed seventy[-two] others whom he sent ahead of him in pairs to every town and place he intended to visit.
text.DRB: And after this our Lord designed also other seventy two: and he sent them two and two before his face into every city and place whither himself would come.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few; therefore ask the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.
text.Rotherham: And he was saying unto them—The harvest, indeed, is, great, but, the labourers, few; beg ye, therefore, of the Lord of the harvest, that he would thrust forth, labourers, into his harvest.
text.KJV: Therefore said hee vnto them, The haruest truly is great, but the labourers are few; pray ye therefore the Lord of the haruest, that he would send foorth labourers into his haruest.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “The harvest is abundant but the laborers are few; so ask the master of the harvest to send out laborers for his harvest.
text.DRB: And he said to them: The harvest truly is much; but the workmen few. Desire therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he send workmen into his harvest.

text.NRSV: Go on your way. See, I am sending you out like lambs into the midst of wolves.
text.Rotherham: Withdraw! Lo! I send you forth as lambs amid wolves.
text.KJV: Go your wayes: Behold, I send you forth as lambes among wolues.
text.NABRE: Go on your way; behold, I am sending you like lambs among wolves.
text.DRB: Go: Behold I send you as lambs among wolves.

text.NRSV: Carry no purse, no bag, no sandals; and greet no one on the road.
text.Rotherham: Be not carrying purse, or satchel, or sandals, and, no one along the road, salute ye;
text.KJV: Cary neither purse nor scrip, nor shoes, and salute no man by the way.
text.NABRE: Carry no money bag, no sack, no sandals; and greet no one along the way.
text.DRB: Carry not purse nor scrip, nor shoes; and salute nobody by the way.

text.NRSV: Whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace to this house!’
text.Rotherham: And, into whatsoever house ye enter, First, say, Peace to this house!
text.KJV: And into whatsoeuer house yee enter, first say, Peace bee to this house.
text.NABRE: Into whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace to this household.’
text.DRB: Into whatsoever house you enter, first say: Peace to this house.

text.NRSV: And if anyone is there who shares in peace, your peace will rest on that person; but if not, it will return to you.
text.Rotherham: And, if the son of peace be, there, your peace, shall rest upon it; but, otherwise, at least, unto you, shall it return.
text.KJV: And if the sonne of peace be there, your peace shall rest vpon it: if not, it shall turne to you againe.
text.NABRE: If a peaceful person lives there, your peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.
text.DRB: And if the son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon him: but if not, it shall return to you.

text.NRSV: Stay in the same house, eating and drinking whatever they provide, for the laborer deserves to be paid. Do not move about from house to house.
text.Rotherham: And, in the self-same house, abide ye, eating and drinking such things as they have; for, worthy, is the labourer, of his hire: be not removing from house to house.
text.KJV: And in the same house remaine, eating and drinking such things as they giue: For the labourer is worthy of his hire. Goe not from house to house.
text.NABRE: Stay in the same house and eat and drink what is offered to you, for the laborer deserves his payment. Do not move about from one house to another.
text.DRB: And in the same house tarry you, eating and drinking such things as they have. For the workman is worthy of his hire. Remove not from house to house.

text.NRSV: Whenever you enter a town and its people welcome you, eat what is set before you;
text.Rotherham: And, into whatsoever city ye shall enter, and they bid you welcome, be eating such things as are set before you;
text.KJV: And into whatsoeuer citie yee enter, and they receiue you, eate such things as are set before you:
text.NABRE: Whatever town you enter and they welcome you, eat what is set before you,
text.DRB: And into what city soever you enter, and they receive you, eat such things as are set before you;

text.NRSV: cure the sick who are there, and say to them, ‘The kingdom of God has come near to you.’
text.Rotherham: And be curing the sick that are, therein, and be saying unto them—The kingdom of God hath drawn nigh upon you.
text.KJV: And heale the sicke that are therein, and say vnto them, The kingdome of God is come nigh vnto you.
text.NABRE: cure the sick in it and say to them, ‘The kingdom of God is at hand for you.’
text.DRB: and cure the sick that are in it, and say to them: The Kingdom of God is come nigh upon you.

text.NRSV: But whenever you enter a town and they do not welcome you, go out into its streets and say,
text.Rotherham: But, into whatsoever city ye shall enter, and they do not welcome you, going forth into the broadways thereof, say ye:
text.KJV: But into whatsoeuer citie yee enter, and they receiue you not, goe your waies out into the streetes of the same, and say,
text.NABRE: Whatever town you enter and they do not receive you, go out into the streets and say,
text.DRB: And into whatsoever city you enter, and they receive you not, going forth into the streets thereof, say:

text.NRSV: ‘Even the dust of your town that clings to our feet, we wipe off in protest against you. Yet know this: the kingdom of God has come near.’
text.Rotherham: Even the dust that cleaveth unto us, out of your city, unto our feet, do we wipe off against you; nevertheless, of this, be taking notice—The kingdom of God hath drawn near.
text.KJV: Euen the very dust of your citie which cleaueth on vs, we doe wipe off against you: notwithstanding, be yee sure of this, that the kingdome of God is come nigh vnto you.
text.NABRE: ‘The dust of your town that clings to our feet, even that we shake off against you.’ Yet know this: the kingdom of God is at hand.
text.DRB: The dust also of your city that cleaveth to us, we do wipe off against you. Yet this know ye that the Kingdom of God is at hand.

text.NRSV: I tell you, on that day it will be more tolerable for Sodom than for that town.
text.Rotherham: I tell you, for them of Sodom, in that day, more tolerable, will it be, than for that city.
text.KJV: But I say vnto you, That it shall be more tolerable in that day for Sodome, then for that citie.
text.NABRE: I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Sodom on that day than for that town. Reproaches to Unrepentant Towns.
text.DRB: Isay to you, it shall be more tolerable for Sodom in that day, than for that city.

text.NRSV: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the deeds of power done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
text.Rotherham: Alas for thee, Chorazin! Alas for thee, Bethsaida! Because, if, in Tyre and Zidon, had been done the works of power which have been down in you, of old, in sackcloth and ashes sitting, they would have repented.
text.KJV: Woe vnto thee Chorazin, wo vnto thee Bethsaida: For if the mighty workes had beene done in Tyre and Sidon, which haue beene done in you, they had a great while agoe repented, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
text.NABRE: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty deeds done in your midst had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would long ago have repented, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
text.DRB: Woe to thee Corozaim, woe to thee Beth-saida: for if in Tyre and Sidon had been wrought the miracles that have been wrought in you, they had done penance sitting in sack cloth and ashes long ago.

text.NRSV: But at the judgment it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, for Tyre and Zidon, more tolerable, will it be, in the judgment, than, for you.
text.KJV: But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the iudgment, then for you.
text.NABRE: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.
text.DRB: But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgement, than for you.

text.NRSV: And you, Capernaum, will you be exalted to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades.
text.Rotherham: And, thou, Capernaum,—Unto heaven, shalt thou be uplifted? …Unto hades, thou shalt be brought down!
text.KJV: And thou Capernaum, which art exalted to heauen, shalt be thrust downe to hell.
text.NABRE: And as for you, Capernaum, ‘Will you be exalted to heaven? You will go down to the netherworld.’
text.DRB: And thou Capharnaum that art exalted unto Heaven: thou shalt be thrust down even unto hell.

text.NRSV: “Whoever listens to you listens to me, and whoever rejects you rejects me, and whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.”
text.Rotherham: He that hearkeneth unto you, unto me, doth hearken, and, he that setteth you aside, doth set, me, aside; and, he that setteth, me, aside, doth set aside, him that sent me.
text.KJV: Hee that heareth you, heareth me: and he that despiseth you, despiseth me: and he that despiseth me, despiseth him that sent me.
text.NABRE: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. And whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.”
text.DRB: He that heareth you, heareth me; and he that despiseth you, despiseth me. And he that despiseth me, despiseth him that sent me.

text.NRSV: The seventy returned with joy, saying, “Lord, in your name even the demons submit to us!”
text.Rotherham: And the seventy [-two] returned, with joy, saying—Lord! even the demons, submit themselves unto us, in thy name!
text.KJV: ¶ And the seuenty returned againe with ioy, saying, Lord, euen the deuils are subiect vnto vs through thy name.
text.NABRE: Return of the Seventy-two. The seventy[-two] returned rejoicing, and said, “Lord, even the demons are subject to us because of your name.”
text.DRB: And the Seventy-two returned with joy, saying: Lord, the devils also are subject to us in thy name.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “I watched Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—I was beholding Satan, when, like lightning, out of heaven, he fell!
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heauen.
text.NABRE: Jesus said, “I have observed Satan fall like lightning from the sky.
text.DRB: And he said to them: I saw Satan as a lightning fall from Heaven.

text.NRSV: See, I have given you authority to tread on snakes and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy; and nothing will hurt you.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I have given you the authority—to be treading upon serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy, and, nothing, unto you, shall in anywise do harm;
text.KJV: Behold, I giue vnto you power to tread on serpents and scorpions, and ouer all the power of the enemie: and nothing shall by any meanes hurt you.
text.NABRE: Behold, I have given you the power ‘to tread upon serpents’ and scorpions and upon the full force of the enemy and nothing will harm you.
