text.DRB: Behold, I have given you power to tread upon serpents, and scorpions, and upon all the power of the enemy, and nothing shall hurt you.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless, do not rejoice at this, that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”
text.Rotherham: Notwithstanding, in this, be not rejoicing—that, the spirits, unto you submit themselves; but be rejoicing—that, your names, are inscribed in the heavens!
text.KJV: Notwithstanding in this reioyce not, that the spirits are subiect vnto you: but rather reioyce, because your names are written in heauen.
text.NABRE: Nevertheless, do not rejoice because the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice because your names are written in heaven.” Praise of the Father.
text.DRB: But yet rejoice not in this, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rejoice in this, that your names are written in Heaven.

text.NRSV: At that same hour Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and the intelligent and have revealed them to infants; yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.
text.Rotherham: In the self-same hour, exulted he in the Holy Spirit, and said—I openly give praise unto thee, Father! Lord of heaven and earth! in that thou hast hid these things from the wise and discerning, and hast revealed them unto babes,—Yea, O Father! that, so, hath it become, a delight, before thee.
text.KJV: ¶ In that houre Iesus reioyced in spirit, and said, I thanke thee, O father, Lord of heauen and earth, that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast reuealed them vnto babes: euen so father, for so it seemed good in thy sight.
text.NABRE: At that very moment he rejoiced [in] the holy Spirit and said, “I give you praise, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, for although you have hidden these things from the wise and the learned you have revealed them to the childlike. Yes, Father, such has been your gracious will.
text.DRB: In that very hour he rejoiced in spirit, and said: I confess to thee O Father, Lord of Heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them to little ones. Yea Father, for so hath it well pleased thee.

text.NRSV: All things have been handed over to me by my Father; and no one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.”
text.Rotherham: All things, unto me, have been delivered up by my Father; and, no one, knoweth, who the Son is, save the Father,—and who the Father is, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son may be minded to reveal him.
text.KJV: All things are deliuered to me of my father: and no man knoweth who the sonne is, but the father: and who the father is, but the sonne, and he to whom the sonne will reueale him.
text.NABRE: All things have been handed over to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wishes to reveal him.” The Privileges of Discipleship.
text.DRB: All things are delivered to me of my Father. And no man knoweth who the Son is, but the Father; and who the Father is, but the Son, and to whom the Son will reveal.

text.NRSV: Then turning to the disciples, Jesus said to them privately, “Blessed are the eyes that see what you see!
text.Rotherham: And, turning unto his disciples, privately, he said—Happy, the eyes, that see what ye see!
text.KJV: ¶ And he turned him vnto his disciples, and said priuately, Blessed are the eyes which see the things that yee see.
text.NABRE: Turning to the disciples in private he said, “Blessed are the eyes that see what you see.
text.DRB: And turning to his Disciples, he said: Blessed are the eyes that see the things that you see.

text.NRSV: For I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see what you see, but did not see it, and to hear what you hear, but did not hear it.”
text.Rotherham: For I tell you, Many prophets and kings, have desired to see what, ye, see, and they saw not, and to hear what ye hear, and they heard not.
text.KJV: For I tell you, that many Prophets, and kings haue desired to see those things which yee see, and haue not seene them: & to heare those things which yee heare, and haue not heard them.
text.NABRE: For I say to you, many prophets and kings desired to see what you see, but did not see it, and to hear what you hear, but did not hear it.” The Greatest Commandment.
text.DRB: For I say to you, that many Prophets and kings desired to see the things that you see, and saw them not; and to hear the things that you hear, and heard them not.

text.NRSV: Just then a lawyer stood up to test Jesus. “Teacher,” he said, “what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
text.Rotherham: And lo! a certain lawyer, arose, putting him to the test, saying—Teacher! by doing what, shall I inherit, life age-abiding?
text.KJV: ¶ And behold, a certaine Lawyer stood vp, and tempted him, saying, Master, what shall I doe to inherite eternall life? He said vnto him,
text.NABRE: There was a scholar of the law who stood up to test him and said, “Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
text.DRB: And behold a certain lawyer stood up, tempting him and saying: Master, by doing of what thing shall I possess life everlasting?

text.NRSV: He said to him, “What is written in the law? What do you read there?”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto him—In the law, what is written? how dost thou read?
text.KJV: What is written in the law? how readest thou?
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “What is written in the law? How do you read it?”
text.DRB: But he said to him: In the law what is written? How readest thou?

text.NRSV: He answered, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your strength, and with all your mind; and your neighbor as yourself.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, answering, said—Thou shalt love the Lord thy God, out of all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might, and with all thine intention; and thy neighbour, as thyself?
text.KJV: And he answering, said, Thou shalt loue the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soule, and with all thy strength, and with all thy minde, and thy neighbour as thy selfe.
text.NABRE: He said in reply, “You shall love the Lord, your God, with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and your neighbor as yourself.”
text.DRB: He answering said: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with thy whole heart, and with thy whole soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind: and thy neighbour as thyself.

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “You have given the right answer; do this, and you will live.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him—Rightly, hast thou answered: This, do, and thou shalt live.
text.KJV: And he said vnto him, Thou hast answered right: this do, and thou shalt liue.
text.NABRE: He replied to him, “You have answered correctly; do this and you will live.”
text.DRB: And he said to him: Thou hast answered right, this do and thou shalt live.

text.NRSV: But wanting to justify himself, he asked Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, wishing to justify himself, said unto Jesus—And, who is, my, neighbour?
text.KJV: But he willing to iustifie himselfe, said vnto Iesus, And who is my neighbour?
text.NABRE: The Parable of the Good Samaritan. But because he wished to justify himself, he said to Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”
text.DRB: But he desirous to justify himself, said to Jesus: And who is my neighbour?

text.NRSV: Jesus replied, “A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell into the hands of robbers, who stripped him, beat him, and went away, leaving him half dead.
text.Rotherham: And taking up [the question], Jesus said—A certain man, was going down from Jerusalem unto Jericho, and, with robbers, fell in,—who, stripping him, and inflicting, wounds, upon him, departed, leaving him, half dead.
text.KJV: And Iesus answering, said, A certaine man went downe from Hierusalem to Iericho, and fel among theeues, which stripped him of his raiment, and wounded him, and departed, leauing him halfe dead.
text.NABRE: Jesus replied, “A man fell victim to robbers as he went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. They stripped and beat him and went off leaving him half-dead.
text.DRB: And Jesus taking it, said: A certain man went down from Jerusalem into Jericho, and fell among thieves, who also spoiled him, and giving him wounds went away leaving him half-dead.

text.NRSV: Now by chance a priest was going down that road; and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
text.Rotherham: And, by chance, a certain priest, was coming down by that road, and, seeing him, passed by, on the opposite side.
text.KJV: And by chaunce there came downe a certaine Priest that way, and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
text.NABRE: A priest happened to be going down that road, but when he saw him, he passed by on the opposite side.
text.DRB: And it chanced that a certain Priest went down the same way; and seeing him, passed by.

text.NRSV: So likewise a Levite, when he came to the place and saw him, passed by on the other side.
text.Rotherham: And, in like manner, a Levite also, coming down to the place, and seeing him, passed by, on the opposite side.
text.KJV: And likewise a Leuite, when hee was at the place, came and looked on him, and passed by on the other side.
text.NABRE: Likewise a Levite came to the place, and when he saw him, he passed by on the opposite side.
text.DRB: In like manner also a Levite, when he was near the place, and saw him, passed by.

text.NRSV: But a Samaritan while traveling came near him; and when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
text.Rotherham: But, a certain Samaritan, going on his journey, came down to him, and, seeing him, was moved with compassion;
text.KJV: But a certaine Samaritane as he iourneyed, came where he was; and when hee saw him, hee had compassion on him,
text.NABRE: But a Samaritan traveler who came upon him was moved with compassion at the sight.
text.DRB: But a certain Samaritane going his journey, came near him; and seeing him, was moved with mercy.

text.NRSV: He went to him and bandaged his wounds, having poured oil and wine on them. Then he put him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
text.Rotherham: and, coming near, bound up his bruises, pouring thereon oil and wine,—and, setting him on his own beast, brought him into an inn, and took care of him.
text.KJV: And went to him, and bound vp his wounds, powring in oile and wine, and set him on his owne beast, and brought him to an Inne, and tooke care of him.
text.NABRE: He approached the victim, poured oil and wine over his wounds and bandaged them. Then he lifted him up on his own animal, took him to an inn and cared for him.
text.DRB: And going unto him, bound his wounds, pouring in oil and wine: and setting him upon his own beast, brought him into an inn, and took care of him.

text.NRSV: The next day he took out two denarii, gave them to the innkeeper, and said, ‘Take care of him; and when I come back, I will repay you whatever more you spend.’
text.Rotherham: And, on the morrow, throwing out two denaries, he gave them to the inn-keeper, and said—Take care of him, and, whatsoever thou shall further spend, I, when on my way back, will duly pay thee.
text.KJV: And on the morrow when he departed, hee tooke out two pence, and gaue them to the hoste, and saide vnto him, Take care of him, and whatsoeuer thou spendest more, when I come againe I will repay thee.
text.NABRE: The next day he took out two silver coins and gave them to the innkeeper with the instruction, ‘Take care of him. If you spend more than what I have given you, I shall repay you on my way back.’
text.DRB: And the next day he took forth two pence, and gave to the host, and said: Have care of him; and whatsoever thou shalt supererogate, I at my return will repay thee.

text.NRSV: Which of these three, do you think, was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of the robbers?”
text.Rotherham: Which of these three, seemeth unto thee to have become, neighbour, unto him who fell among the robbers?
text.KJV: Which now of these three, thinkest thou, was neighbour vnto him that fell among the theeues?
text.NABRE: Which of these three, in your opinion, was neighbor to the robbers’ victim?”
text.DRB: Which of these three in thy opinion was neighbour to him that fell among thieves?

text.NRSV: He said, “The one who showed him mercy.” Jesus said to him, “Go and do likewise.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—He who dealt mercifully with him. And Jesus said unto him—Be taking thy journey, and, thou, be doing, in like manner.
text.KJV: And he said, He that shewed mercie on him. Then said Iesus vnto him, Goe, and doe thou likewise.
text.NABRE: He answered, “The one who treated him with mercy.” Jesus said to him, “Go and do likewise.” Martha and Mary.
text.DRB: But he said: He that did mercy upon him. And Jesus said to him: Go, and do thou in like manner.

text.NRSV: Now as they went on their way, he entered a certain village, where a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
text.Rotherham: And, as they were journeying, he, entered into a certain village; and, a certain woman, named Martha, welcomed him into her house.
text.KJV: ¶ Now it came to passe, as they went, that he entred into a certaine village: and a certaine woman named Martha, receiued him into her house.
text.NABRE: As they continued their journey he entered a village where a woman whose name was Martha welcomed him.
text.DRB: And it came to pass as they went, and he entered into a certain town; and a certain woman named Martha, received him into her house,

text.NRSV: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lord’s feet and listened to what he was saying.
text.Rotherham: And, she, had a sister, called Mary, who also, seating herself at the feet of the Lord, was hearing his word.
text.KJV: And shee had a sister called Mary, which also sate at Iesus feet, and heard his word:
text.NABRE: She had a sister named Mary [who] sat beside the Lord at his feet listening to him speak.
text.DRB: and she had a sister called Mary. Who sitting also at our Lord's feet, heard his word.

text.NRSV: But Martha was distracted by her many tasks; so she came to him and asked, “Lord, do you not care that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her then to help me.”
text.Rotherham: But, Martha, was distracted about much ministering, and, coming near, said—Lord! carest thou not that, my sister, hath left me to be ministering, alone? Speak to her, then, that she help me.
text.KJV: But Martha was cumbred about much seruing, and came to him, and said, Lord, doest thou not care that my sister hath left mee to serue alone? Bid her therefore that she helpe me.
text.NABRE: Martha, burdened with much serving, came to him and said, “Lord, do you not care that my sister has left me by myself to do the serving? Tell her to help me.”
text.DRB: But Martha was busy about much service. Who stood and said: Lord, hast thou no care that my sister hath left me alone to serve? Speak to her therefore, that she help me.

text.NRSV: But the Lord answered her, “Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things;
text.Rotherham: But the Lord, answering, said to her—Martha! Martha! thou art anxious and troubled about many things:
text.KJV: And Iesus answered, and saide vnto her, Martha, Martha, thou art carefull, and troubled about many things:
text.NABRE: The Lord said to her in reply, “Martha, Martha, you are anxious and worried about many things.
text.DRB: And our Lord answering said to her: Martha, Martha, thou art careful, and art troubled about many things.

text.NRSV: there is need of only one thing. Mary has chosen the better part, which will not be taken away from her.”
text.Rotherham: Of few things, is there need, or, of one; Mary, in fact, hath chosen, the good part,—one which shall not be taken away from her.
text.KJV: But one thing is needefull, and Mary hath chosen that good part, which shall not bee taken away from her.
text.NABRE: There is need of only one thing. Mary has chosen the better part and it will not be taken from her.”
text.DRB: But one thing is necessary, Mary hath chosen the best part which shall not be taken away from her.

text.NRSV: He was praying in a certain place, and after he had finished, one of his disciples said to him, “Lord, teach us to pray, as John taught his disciples.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when he was in a certain place praying, as he ceased, one of his disciples said unto him—Lord! teach us to pray, as, John also, taught his disciples.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that as he was praying in a certaine place, when hee ceased, one of his disciples said vnto him, Lord, teach vs to pray, as Iohn also taught his disciples.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 The Lord’s Prayer. He was praying in a certain place, and when he had finished, one of his disciples said to him, “Lord, teach us to pray just as John taught his disciples.”
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when he was in a certain place praying, as he ceased, one of his Disciples said to him: Lord teach us to pray, as John also taught his Disciples.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “When you pray, say: Father, hallowed be your name. Your kingdom come.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Whensoever ye are praying, say—Father! Hallowed be thy name, Come may thy kingdom,
text.KJV: And hee said vnto them, When ye pray, say, Our Father which art in heauen, Halowed be thy Name, Thy kingdome come, Thy will be done as in heauen, so in earth.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “When you pray, say: Father, hallowed be your name, your kingdom come.
text.DRB: And he said to them: When you pray, say, Father, sanctified be thy name. Thy Kingdom come,

text.NRSV: Give us each day our daily bread.
text.Rotherham: Our needful bread, be giving us, day by day;
text.KJV: Giue vs day by day our dayly bread.
text.NABRE: Give us each day our daily bread
text.DRB: Our daily bread give us this day,

text.NRSV: And forgive us our sins, for we ourselves forgive everyone indebted to us. And do not bring us to the time of trial.”
text.Rotherham: And forgive us our sins, for, even we ourselves, forgive every one indebted to us; And bring us not into temptation.
text.KJV: And forgiue vs our sinnes: for we also forgiue euery one that is indebted to vs. And lead vs not into temptation, but deliuer vs from euill.
text.NABRE: and forgive us our sins for we ourselves forgive everyone in debt to us, and do not subject us to the final test.” Further Teachings on Prayer.
text.DRB: and forgive us our sins, for because ourselves also do forgive every one that is in debt to us. And lead us not into temptation.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Suppose one of you has a friend, and you go to him at midnight and say to him, ‘Friend, lend me three loaves of bread;
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Who from among you, shall have a friend, and shall go unto him at midnight, and say unto him—Friend! supply me with three loaves,
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Which of you shall haue a friend, and shall goe vnto him at midnight, and say vnto him, Friend, lend me three loaues.
text.NABRE: And he said to them, “Suppose one of you has a friend to whom he goes at midnight and says, ‘Friend, lend me three loaves of bread,
text.DRB: And he said to them: Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go to him at midnight, and shall say to him, Friend, lend me three loaves,

text.NRSV: for a friend of mine has arrived, and I have nothing to set before him.’
text.Rotherham: forasmuch as, a friend of mine, hath come off a journey unto me, and I have not what I can set before him;
text.KJV: For a friend of mine in his iourney is come to me, and I haue nothing to set before him,
text.NABRE: for a friend of mine has arrived at my house from a journey and I have nothing to offer him,’
text.DRB: because a friend of mine is come out of his way to me, and I have not what to set before him:

text.NRSV: And he answers from within, ‘Do not bother me; the door has already been locked, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot get up and give you anything.’
text.Rotherham: and, he, from within, shall answer, and say—Be not disturbing me,—already, the door hath been fastened, and, my children, with me, are, in bed: I cannot rise and give thee?
text.KJV: And he from within shal answere and say, Trouble mee not, the doore is now shut, and my children are with me in bed: I cannot rise and giue thee.
text.NABRE: and he says in reply from within, ‘Do not bother me; the door has already been locked and my children and I are already in bed. I cannot get up to give you anything.’
text.DRB: and he from within answering saith: Trouble me not, now the door is shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise and give thee.

text.NRSV: I tell you, even though he will not get up and give him anything because he is his friend, at least because of his persistence he will get up and give him whatever he needs.
text.Rotherham: I say unto you—Even though he will not give him, rising because of his being a friend of his, because, at least, of his importunity, he will rouse himself, and give him as many as he needeth.
text.KJV: I say vnto you, Though he will not rise, and giue him, because he is his friend: yet because of his importunitie, hee will rise and giue him as many as he needeth.
text.NABRE: I tell you, if he does not get up to give him the loaves because of their friendship, he will get up to give him whatever he needs because of his persistence. The Answer to Prayer.
text.DRB: And if he shall persevere knocking, I say to you, although he will not rise and give him because he is his friend, yet for his importunity he will rise, and give him as many as he needeth.

text.NRSV: “So I say to you, Ask, and it will be given you; search, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, unto you, say—Be asking, and it shall be given to you, Be seeking, and ye shall find,—Be knocking, and it shall be opened unto you.
text.KJV: And I say vnto you, Aske, and it shalbe giuen you: seeke, and ye shal find: knocke, and it shalbe opened vnto you.
text.NABRE: “And I tell you, ask and you will receive; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.
text.DRB: And I say to you, Ask, and it shall be given you: seek, and you shall find: knock, and it shall be opened to you.

text.NRSV: For everyone who asks receives, and everyone who searches finds, and for everyone who knocks, the door will be opened.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever asketh, receiveth, and, he that seeketh, findeth,—and, to him that knocketh, shall it be opened.
text.KJV: For euery one that asketh, receiueth: and he that seeketh, findeth: and to him that knocketh, it shalbe opened.
text.NABRE: For everyone who asks, receives; and the one who seeks, finds; and to the one who knocks, the door will be opened.
text.DRB: For every one that asketh, receiveth: and he that seeketh, findeth: and to him that knocketh, it shall be opened.

text.NRSV: Is there anyone among you who, if your child asks for a fish, will give a snake instead of a fish?
text.Rotherham: But which is the father, from among yourselves, whom the son will ask for, a fish, who, instead of a fish, will give him, a serpent?
text.KJV: If a sonne shall aske bread of any of you that is a father, will hee giue him a stone? Or if he aske a fish, will he for a fish giue him a serpent?
text.NABRE: What father among you would hand his son a snake when he asks for a fish?
text.DRB: And which of you if he ask his father bread, will he give him a stone? Or a fish, will he for a fish give him a serpent?

text.NRSV: Or if the child asks for an egg, will give a scorpion?
text.Rotherham: Or shall also ask, an egg, who will give him, a scorpion?
text.KJV: Or if he shall aske an egge, will he offer him a scorpion?
text.NABRE: Or hand him a scorpion when he asks for an egg?
text.DRB: Or if he ask an egg, will he reach him a scorpion?

text.NRSV: If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him!”
text.Rotherham: If therefore, ye, being, evil, know how to be giving, good gifts, unto your children, how much rather, will, the heavenly Father, give Holy Spirit unto them that ask him!
text.KJV: If ye then, being euill, know how to giue good gifts vnto your children: how much more shall your heauenly Father giue the holy Spirit to them that aske him?
text.NABRE: If you then, who are wicked, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the Father in heaven give the holy Spirit to those who ask him?” Jesus and Beelzebul.
text.DRB: If you then being naught, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father from Heaven give the good spirit to them that ask him?

text.NRSV: Now he was casting out a demon that was mute; when the demon had gone out, the one who had been mute spoke, and the crowds were amazed.
text.Rotherham: And he was casting out a demon that was dumb; and it came to pass, when the demon went out, the dumb began speaking. And the multitudes marvelled;
text.KJV: ¶ And he was casting out a deuil, and it was dumbe. And it came to passe, when the deuill was gone out, the dumbe spake: and the people wondred.
text.NABRE: He was driving out a demon [that was] mute, and when the demon had gone out, the mute person spoke and the crowds were amazed.
text.DRB: And he was casting out a devil, and that was dumb. And when he had cast out the devil, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marveled.

text.NRSV: But some of them said, “He casts out demons by Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.”
text.Rotherham: but, some from among them, said—In Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons, is he casting out the demons;
text.KJV: But some of them said, Hee casteth out deuils through Beelzebub the chiefe of the deuils.
text.NABRE: Some of them said, “By the power of Beelzebul, the prince of demons, he drives out demons.”
text.DRB: And certain of them said: In Beel-zebub the prince of devils he casteth out devils.

text.NRSV: Others, to test him, kept demanding from him a sign from heaven.
text.Rotherham: and, others, putting him to the test, a sign out of heaven, were seeking from him.
text.KJV: And other tempting him, sought of him a signe from heauen.
text.NABRE: Others, to test him, asked him for a sign from heaven.
text.DRB: And other tempting, asked of him a sign from Heaven.

text.NRSV: But he knew what they were thinking and said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself becomes a desert, and house falls on house.
text.Rotherham: But, he, knowing their thoughts, said unto them—Every kingdom, against itself divided, is laid waste, and, a house, against a house, [divided], falleth;
text.KJV: But he knowing their thoughts, said vnto them, Euery kingdome diuided against it selfe, is brought to desolation: and a house diuided against a house, falleth.
text.NABRE: But he knew their thoughts and said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste and house will fall against house.
text.DRB: But he seeing their cogitations, said to them: Every kingdom divided against itself, shall be made desolate, and house upon house, shall fall.

text.NRSV: If Satan also is divided against himself, how will his kingdom stand? — for you say that I cast out demons by Beelzebul.
text.Rotherham: And, if, even Satan, against himself hath become divided, how shall his kingdom stand? because ye are saying that, in Beelzebul, am I casting out the demons.
text.KJV: If Satan also be diuided against himselfe, how shall his kingdom stand? Because yee say that I cast out deuils through Beelzebub.
text.NABRE: And if Satan is divided against himself, how will his kingdom stand? For you say that it is by Beelzebul that I drive out demons.
text.DRB: And if Satan also be divided against himself, how shall his kingdom stand? Because you say that in Beel-zebub I do cast out devils.

text.NRSV: Now if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your exorcists cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
text.Rotherham: But, if, I, in Beelzebul, am casting out the demons, In whom are, your sons, casting them out? Wherefore, they, shall be, your judges.
text.KJV: And if I by Beelzebub cast out deuils, by whom doe your sonnes cast them out? therefore shall they be your iudges.
text.NABRE: If I, then, drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your own people drive them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
text.DRB: And if I in Beel-zebub cast out devils: your children, in whom do they cast out? Therefore they shall be your judges.

text.NRSV: But if it is by the finger of God that I cast out demons, then the kingdom of God has come to you.
text.Rotherham: But, if, with the finger of God, I, am casting out the demons, then doubtless unawares hath come upon you, the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: But if I with the finger of God cast out deuils, no doubt the kingdome of God is come vpon you.
text.NABRE: But if it is by the finger of God that [I] drive out demons, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.
text.DRB: But if I in the finger of God do cast out devils; surely the Kingdom of God is come upon you.

text.NRSV: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his castle, his property is safe.
text.Rotherham: Whensoever, the mighty one, armed, may be guarding his own dwelling, in peace, are his goods;
text.KJV: When a strong man armed keepeth his palace, his goods are in peace:
text.NABRE: When a strong man fully armed guards his palace, his possessions are safe.
text.DRB: When the strong armed keepeth his court, those things are in peace that he possesseth.

text.NRSV: But when one stronger than he attacks him and overpowers him, he takes away his armor in which he trusted and divides his plunder.
text.Rotherham: But, whensoever, a mightier than he, shall come upon and vanquish him, his panoply, he taketh away, wherein he was trusting, and, his spoils, he distributeth.
text.KJV: But when a stronger then he shal come vpon him, and ouercome him, hee taketh from him all his armour wherein he trusted, and diuideth his spoiles.
text.NABRE: But when one stronger than he attacks and overcomes him, he takes away the armor on which he relied and distributes the spoils.
text.DRB: But if a stronger than he come upon him and overcome him; he will take away his whole armour wherein he trusted, and will distribute his spoils.

text.NRSV: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters.
text.Rotherham: He that is not with me, is, against me; and, he that gathereth not with me, scattereth.
text.KJV: He that is not with me, is against me: and hee that gathereth not with me, scattereth.
text.NABRE: Whoever is not with me is against me, and whoever does not gather with me scatters. The Return of the Unclean Spirit.
text.DRB: He that is not with me, is against me: and he that gathereth not with me, scattereth.

text.NRSV: “When the unclean spirit has gone out of a person, it wanders through waterless regions looking for a resting place, but not finding any, it says, ‘I will return to my house from which I came.’
text.Rotherham: Whensoever, the impure spirit, goeth out from the man, it passeth through waterless places, seeking rest; and, not finding it, [then,] it saith, I will return unto my house, whence I came out;—
text.KJV: When the vncleane spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through drie places, seeking rest: and finding none, he sayth, I will returne vnto my house whence I came out.
text.NABRE: “When an unclean spirit goes out of someone, it roams through arid regions searching for rest but, finding none, it says, ‘I shall return to my home from which I came.’
text.DRB: When the unclean spirit shall depart out of a man, he wandereth through places without water, seeking rest. And not finding, he saith, I will return into my house whence I departed.

text.NRSV: When it comes, it finds it swept and put in order.
text.Rotherham: and, coming, findeth it, [empty,] swept, and adorned.
text.KJV: And when hee commeth, hee findeth it swept and garnished.
text.NABRE: But upon returning, it finds it swept clean and put in order.
text.DRB: And when he is come, he findeth it swept with a besom, and trimmed.

text.NRSV: Then it goes and brings seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they enter and live there; and the last state of that person is worse than the first.”
text.Rotherham: Then, goeth it, and taketh along with itself, other spirits, more wicked than itself—seven, and, entering in, fixeth its dwelling there; and, the last state of that man, becometh, worse than the first.
text.KJV: Then goeth he, and taketh to him seuen other spirits more wicked then himselfe, and they enter in, and dwell there, and the last state of that man is worse then the first.
text.NABRE: Then it goes and brings back seven other spirits more wicked than itself who move in and dwell there, and the last condition of that person is worse than the first.” True Blessedness.
text.DRB: Then he goeth and taketh seven other spirits worse than himself, and entering in they dwell there. And the last of that man be made worse than the first.

text.NRSV: While he was saying this, a woman in the crowd raised her voice and said to him, “Blessed is the womb that bore you and the breasts that nursed you!”
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass, while he was saying these things, that a certain woman out of the multitude, lifting up her voice, said unto him—Happy the womb that bare thee! And the breasts which thou didst suck!
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe as hee spake these things, a certaine woman of the company lift vp her voice, and said vnto him, Blessed is the wombe that bare thee, and the pappes which thou hast sucked.
text.NABRE: While he was speaking, a woman from the crowd called out and said to him, “Blessed is the womb that carried you and the breasts at which you nursed.”
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when he said these things, a certain woman lifting up her voice out of the multitude said to him: Blessed is the womb that bare thee, and the paps that thou didst suck.

text.NRSV: But he said, “Blessed rather are those who hear the word of God and obey it!”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said—Yea rather!—Happy they who hear the word of God, and observe it!
text.KJV: But hee said, Yea, rather blessed are they that heare the word of God, and keepe it.
text.NABRE: He replied, “Rather, blessed are those who hear the word of God and observe it.” The Demand for a Sign.
text.DRB: But he said: Yea rather, blessed are they that hear the word of God, and keep it.

text.NRSV: When the crowds were increasing, he began to say, “This generation is an evil generation; it asks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah.
text.Rotherham: And, as the multitudes were thronging together, he began to be saying—This generation, is, a wicked generation: A sign, it is seeking, and, a sign, shall not be given it, save the sign of Jonah.
text.KJV: ¶ And when the people were gathered thicke together, hee began to say, This is an euill generation, they seeke a signe, and there shall no signe be giuen it, but the signe of Ionas the Prophet:
text.NABRE: While still more people gathered in the crowd, he said to them, “This generation is an evil generation; it seeks a sign, but no sign will be given it, except the sign of Jonah.
text.DRB: And the multitudes running together, he began to say: This generation, is a wicked generation: it asketh a sign, and a sign shall not be given it but the sign of Jonas the Prophet.

text.NRSV: For just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Son of Man will be to this generation.
text.Rotherham: For, according as, Jonah, became, unto the Ninevites, a sign, so, shall be, the Son of Man also, unto this generation.
text.KJV: For as Ionas was a signe vnto the Nineuites, so shall also the Sonne of man be to this generation.
text.NABRE: Just as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so will the Son of Man be to this generation.
text.DRB: For as Jonas was a sign to the Ninivites; so shall the Son of man also be to this generation.

text.NRSV: The queen of the South will rise at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from the ends of the earth to listen to the wisdom of Solomon, and see, something greater than Solomon is here!
text.Rotherham: The queen of the south, will rise up, in the judgment, with the men of this generation, and will condemn them; because she came out of the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon,—and lo! something more than Solomon, here.
text.KJV: The Queene of the South shall rise vp in the iudgement with the men of this generation, & condemne them: for shee came from the vtmost parts of the earth, to heare the wisedome of Solomon: and behold, a greater then Solomon is here.
text.NABRE: At the judgment the queen of the south will rise with the men of this generation and she will condemn them, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and there is something greater than Solomon here.
text.DRB: The queen of the South shall rise in the judgement with the men of this generation, and shall condemn them: because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Salomon. And behold, more than Salomon here.

text.NRSV: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they repented at the proclamation of Jonah, and see, something greater than Jonah is here!
text.Rotherham: Men of Nineveh, will rise up, in the judgment, with this generation, and will condemn it; because they repented into the proclamation of Jonah,—and lo! something more than Jonah, here.
text.KJV: The men of Nineue shall rise vp in the iudgement with this generation, and shall condemne it: for they repented at the preaching of Ionas, and behold, a greater then Ionas is here.
text.NABRE: At the judgment the men of Nineveh will arise with this generation and condemn it, because at the preaching of Jonah they repented, and there is something greater than Jonah here.
text.DRB: The men of Ninivee shall rise in the judgement with this generation, and shall condemn it, because they did penance at the preaching of Jonas. And behold, more than Jonas here.

text.NRSV: “No one after lighting a lamp puts it in a cellar or under a bushel basket, but on a lampstand, so that those who enter may see the light.
text.Rotherham: No one, having lighted, a lamp, into a covered place, a putteth it, nor, under the measure; but upon the lampstand, that they who enter may see, the light.
text.KJV: No man when he hath lighted a candle, putteth it in a secret place, neither vnder a bushell, but on a candlesticke, that they which come in may see the light.
text.NABRE: The Simile of Light. “No one who lights a lamp hides it away or places it [under a bushel basket], but on a lampstand so that those who enter might see the light.
text.DRB: No man lighteth a candle, and putteth it in secret, neither under a bushel: but upon a candlestick, that they that go in may see the light.

text.NRSV: Your eye is the lamp of your body. If your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light; but if it is not healthy, your body is full of darkness.
text.Rotherham: The lamp of thy body, is thine eye: Whensoever, thine eye, may be, single, even the whole of thy body, is, lighted up; but, whensoever it may be, useless, even thy body, is darkened.
text.KJV: The light of the body is the eye: therefore when thine eye is single, thy whole body also is full of light: but when thine eye is euill, thy body also is full of darkenesse.
text.NABRE: The lamp of the body is your eye. When your eye is sound, then your whole body is filled with light, but when it is bad, then your body is in darkness.
text.DRB: The candle of thy body is thine eye. If thine eye be simple, thy whole body shall be lightsome: but if it be naught, thy body also shall be darksome.

text.NRSV: Therefore consider whether the light in you is not darkness.
text.Rotherham: Be looking to it, therefore, lest, the light that is in thee, be, darkness.
text.KJV: Take heede therefore, that the light which is in thee, be not darknesse.
text.NABRE: Take care, then, that the light in you not become darkness.
text.DRB: See therefore that the light which is in thee, be not darkness.

text.NRSV: If then your whole body is full of light, with no part of it in darkness, it will be as full of light as when a lamp gives you light with its rays.”
text.Rotherham: If, therefore, thy whole body, is lighted up, not having any part darkened, the whole, shall be lighted up, as whensoever, the lamp, with its radiance, may be giving thee light.
text.KJV: If thy whole body therefore be full of light, hauing no part darke, the whole shalbe full of light, as when the bright shining of a candle doeth giue thee light.
text.NABRE: If your whole body is full of light, and no part of it is in darkness, then it will be as full of light as a lamp illuminating you with its brightness.” Denunciation of the Pharisees and Scholars of the Law.
text.DRB: If then thy whole body be lightsome, having no part of darkness: it shall be lightsome wholly, and as a bright candle it shall lighten thee.

text.NRSV: While he was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to dine with him; so he went in and took his place at the table.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had spoken, a Pharisee was requesting him that he would dine with him; and, entering, he reclined.
text.KJV: ¶ And as he spake, a certaine Pharise besought him to dine with him: and he went in, and sate downe to meate.
text.NABRE: After he had spoken, a Pharisee invited him to dine at his home. He entered and reclined at table to eat.
text.DRB: And when he was speaking, a certain Pharisee desired him that he would dine with him. And he going in sat down to eat.

text.NRSV: The Pharisee was amazed to see that he did not first wash before dinner.
text.Rotherham: And, the Pharisee, beholding, marvelled that he was not, first, immersed, before the dinner.
text.KJV: And when the Pharise saw it, he marueiled that he had not first washed before dinner.
text.NABRE: The Pharisee was amazed to see that he did not observe the prescribed washing before the meal.
text.DRB: And the Pharisee began to think within himself and to say: Why he was not washed before dinner.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to him, “Now you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and of the dish, but inside you are full of greed and wickedness.
text.Rotherham: And the Lord said unto him: Now, ye, the Pharisees, the outside of the cup and of the tray, do make pure; but, your inward part, is full of plunder and wickedness.
text.KJV: And the Lord said vnto him, Now doe ye Pharises make cleane the outside of the cup and the platter: but your inward part is full of rauening and wickednesse.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to him, “Oh you Pharisees! Although you cleanse the outside of the cup and the dish, inside you are filled with plunder and evil.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: Now you Pharisees do make clean that on the out side of the cup and of the platter; but that of yours which is within, is full of rapine and iniquity.

text.NRSV: You fools! Did not the one who made the outside make the inside also?
text.Rotherham: Simple ones! Did not, he who made the outside, the inside also, make?
text.KJV: Yee fooles, did not he that made that which is without, make that which is within also?
text.NABRE: You fools! Did not the maker of the outside also make the inside?
text.DRB: Fools, did not he that made that on the out side, make that also that is on the inside?

text.NRSV: So give for alms those things that are within; and see, everything will be clean for you.
text.Rotherham: Notwithstanding, as to the things within, give alms, and lo! everything, is, pure unto you.
text.KJV: But rather giue almes of such things as you haue: and behold, all things are cleane vnto you.
text.NABRE: But as to what is within, give alms, and behold, everything will be clean for you.
text.DRB: But yet that that remaineth, give alms, & behold all things are clean unto you.

text.NRSV: “But woe to you Pharisees! For you tithe mint and rue and herbs of all kinds, and neglect justice and the love of God; it is these you ought to have practiced, without neglecting the others.
text.Rotherham: But alas for you, the Pharisees! because ye tithe the mint, and the rue, and every garden herb, and pass by justice, and the love of God. But, these things, it was binding to do, and, those, not to pass by.
text.KJV: But woe vnto you Pharises: for ye tythe Mint and Rue, and all maner of herbes, and passe ouer iudgement, and the loue of God: these ought yee to haue done, and not to leaue the other vndone.
text.NABRE: Woe to you Pharisees! You pay tithes of mint and of rue and of every garden herb, but you pay no attention to judgment and to love for God. These you should have done, without overlooking the others.
text.DRB: But woe to you Pharisees, because you tithe mint and rew and every herb: and pass over judgement and the charity of God. But these things you ought to have done, and not to omit those.

text.NRSV: Woe to you Pharisees! For you love to have the seat of honor in the synagogues and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces.
text.Rotherham: Alas for you, the Pharisees! because ye love the first seats in the synagogues, and the salutations in the market-places!
text.KJV: Woe vnto you Pharisees: for ye loue the vppermost seats in the Synagogues, and greetings in the markets.
text.NABRE: Woe to you Pharisees! You love the seat of honor in synagogues and greetings in marketplaces.
text.DRB: Woe to you Pharisees, because you love the first chairs in the Synagogues, and salutations in the market place.

text.NRSV: Woe to you! For you are like unmarked graves, and people walk over them without knowing it.”
text.Rotherham: Alas for you! because ye are as the secret tombs: even the men that are walking above them, know it not.
text.KJV: Woe vnto you Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites: for ye are as graues which appeare not, and the men that walk ouer them, are not aware of them.
text.NABRE: Woe to you! You are like unseen graves over which people unknowingly walk.”
text.DRB: Woe to you, because you are as monuments that appear not, and men walking over, are not ware.

text.NRSV: One of the lawyers answered him, “Teacher, when you say these things, you insult us too.”
text.Rotherham: And, making answer, one of the Lawyers saith unto him, Teacher! these things, saying, us also, dost thou insult!
text.KJV: ¶ Then answered one of the Lawyers, and said vnto him, Master, thus saying, thou reprochest vs also.
text.NABRE: Then one of the scholars of the law said to him in reply, “Teacher, by saying this you are insulting us too.”
text.DRB: And one of the Lawyers answering saith to him: Master, in saying these things, thou speakest to our reproach also.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Woe also to you lawyers! For you load people with burdens hard to bear, and you yourselves do not lift a finger to ease them.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—And, for you, the lawyers, alas! because ye lade men with burdens hard to be borne, and, yourselves, with one of your fingers, touch not the burdens.
text.KJV: And he said, Woe vnto you also ye lawyers: for ye lade men with burdens grieuous to be borne, and ye your selues touch not the burdens with one of your fingers.
text.NABRE: And he said, “Woe also to you scholars of the law! You impose on people burdens hard to carry, but you yourselves do not lift one finger to touch them.
text.DRB: But he said: Woe to you Lawyers also: because you load men with burdens which they cannot bear, and yourselves touch not the packs with one of your fingers.

text.NRSV: Woe to you! For you build the tombs of the prophets whom your ancestors killed.
text.Rotherham: Alas for you! because ye build the tombs of the prophets, and your fathers slew them!
text.KJV: Woe vnto you: for ye build the sepulchres of the Prophets, and your fathers killed them.
text.NABRE: Woe to you! You build the memorials of the prophets whom your ancestors killed.
text.DRB: Woe to you that build the monuments of the Prophets: and your fathers did kill them.

text.NRSV: So you are witnesses and approve of the deeds of your ancestors; for they killed them, and you build their tombs.
text.Rotherham: Hence, witnesses, are ye, and find consenting pleasure in the works of your fathers: because, they, indeed, slew them, and, ye, are building, [their tombs] .
text.KJV: Truely ye beare witnesse that ye allowe the deeds of your fathers: for they indeed killed them, and yee build their sepulchres.
text.NABRE: Consequently, you bear witness and give consent to the deeds of your ancestors, for they killed them and you do the building.
text.DRB: Surely you do testify that you consent to the works of your fathers: because they indeed did kill them, and you build their sepulchres.

text.NRSV: Therefore also the Wisdom of God said, ‘I will send them prophets and apostles, some of whom they will kill and persecute,’
text.Rotherham: For this cause, the Wisdom of God hath said—I will send forth, unto them, prophets and apostles,—and, some from among them, will they slay, and, [some] persecute:
text.KJV: Therefore also said the wisedome of God, I wil send them Prophets and Apostles, and some of them they shal slay and persecute:
text.NABRE: Therefore, the wisdom of God said, ‘I will send to them prophets and apostles; some of them they will kill and persecute’
text.DRB: For this cause the wisdom also of God said, I will send to them Prophets and Apostles, and of them they will kill and persecute.

text.NRSV: so that this generation may be charged with the blood of all the prophets shed since the foundation of the world,
text.Rotherham: That the blood of all the prophets which hath been shed from the foundation of the world, may be sought out, from this generation,—
text.KJV: That the blood of all the Prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation,
text.NABRE: in order that this generation might be charged with the blood of all the prophets shed since the foundation of the world,
text.DRB: That the blood of all the Prophets that was shed from the making of the world, may be required of this generation,

text.NRSV: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who perished between the altar and the sanctuary. Yes, I tell you, it will be charged against this generation.
text.Rotherham: From the blood of Abel, unto the blood of Zachariah who was destroyed betwixt the altar and the house; Yea! I say unto you—It shall be sought out from this generation.
text.KJV: From the blood of Abel vnto the blood of Zacharias, which perished betweene the Altar and the Temple: Uerely I say vnto you, it shall be required of this generation.
text.NABRE: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah who died between the altar and the temple building. Yes, I tell you, this generation will be charged with their blood!
text.DRB: from the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharie that was slain between the altar and the temple. Yea I say to you, it shall be required of this generation.

text.NRSV: Woe to you lawyers! For you have taken away the key of knowledge; you did not enter yourselves, and you hindered those who were entering.”
text.Rotherham: Alas for you, the lawyers! because ye took away the key of knowledge: yourselves, entered not, and, them who were entering, ye hindered.
text.KJV: Woe vnto you Lawyers: for ye haue taken away the key of knowledge: ye entred not in your selues, and them that were entring in, ye hindred.
text.NABRE: Woe to you, scholars of the law! You have taken away the key of knowledge. You yourselves did not enter and you stopped those trying to enter.”
text.DRB: Woe to you Lawyers, because you have taken away the key of knowledge: yourselves have not entered, & those that did enter you have prohibited.

text.NRSV: When he went outside, the scribes and the Pharisees began to be very hostile toward him and to cross-examine him about many things,
text.Rotherham: And, when, from thence, he came out, the Scribes and the Pharisees began, with vehemence, to be hemming him in, and trying to make him speak off-hand concerning many things,—
text.KJV: And as he said these things vnto them, the Scribes and the Pharisees began to vrge him vehemently, and to prouoke him to speake of many things:
text.NABRE: When he left, the scribes and Pharisees began to act with hostility toward him and to interrogate him about many things,
text.DRB: And when he said these things to them, the Pharisees & the Lawyers began vehemently to urge him, & to stop his mouth about many things,

text.NRSV: lying in wait for him, to catch him in something he might say.
text.Rotherham: lying in wait for him, to catch something out of his mouth.
text.KJV: Laying wait for him, and seeking to catch something out of his mouth, that they might accuse him.
text.NABRE: for they were plotting to catch him at something he might say.
text.DRB: lying in wait for him, and seeking to catch something of his mouth, that they might accuse him.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile, when the crowd gathered by the thousands, so that they trampled on one another, he began to speak first to his disciples, “Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees, that is, their hypocrisy.
text.Rotherham: Amongst which things, when the ten thousands of the multitude were gathered together, so that they were treading one upon another, he began to be saying, unto his disciples, first—Be keeping yourselves free from the leaven of the Pharisees, the which is, hypocrisy.
text.KJV: In the meane time, when there were gathered together an innumerable multitude of people, insomuch that they trode one vpon another, he began to say vnto his disciples first of all, Beware yee of the leauen of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisie.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 The Leaven of the Pharisees. Meanwhile, so many people were crowding together that they were trampling one another underfoot. He began to speak, first to his disciples, “Beware of the leaven—that is, the hypocrisy—of the Pharisees. Courage Under Persecution.
text.DRB: And when great multitudes stood about him, so that they trod one another, he began to say to his Disciples: Take good heed of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

text.NRSV: Nothing is covered up that will not be uncovered, and nothing secret that will not become known.
text.Rotherham: But, nothing, hath been, covered up, which shall not be uncovered, and hidden, which shall not be made known.
text.KJV: For there is nothing couered, that shall not be reuealed, neither hid, that shall not be knowen.
text.NABRE: “There is nothing concealed that will not be revealed, nor secret that will not be known.
text.DRB: For nothing is hid, that shall not be revealed: nor secret, that shall not be known.

text.NRSV: Therefore whatever you have said in the dark will be heard in the light, and what you have whispered in private rooms will be proclaimed from the housetops.
text.Rotherham: Because, as many things as, in the darkness, ye have said, in the light, shall be heard; and, what to the ear ye spake, in the chambers, shall be proclaimed on the housetops.
text.KJV: Therefore, whatsoeuer yee haue spoken in darkenesse, shall bee heard in the light: and that which yee haue spoken in the eare, in closets, shal be proclaimed vpon the house tops.
text.NABRE: Therefore whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and what you have whispered behind closed doors will be proclaimed on the housetops.
text.DRB: For the things that you have said in darkness, shall be said in the light: and that which you have spoken into the ear in the chambers, shall be preached in the house-tops.

text.NRSV: “I tell you, my friends, do not fear those who kill the body, and after that can do nothing more.
text.Rotherham: And I say unto you, my friends—Do not be put in fear of them who kill the body, and, after these things, have nothing more uncommon which they can do.
text.KJV: And I say vnto you my friends, Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after that, haue no more that they can doe.
text.NABRE: I tell you, my friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body but after that can do no more.
text.DRB: And I say to you my friends, Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after this have no more to do.

text.NRSV: But I will warn you whom to fear: fear him who, after he has killed, has authority to cast into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear him!
text.Rotherham: But I will suggest to you, whom ye should fear—Fear him who, after killing, hath authority to cast into gehenna,—Yea, I say unto you—Him, fear ye.
text.KJV: But I will forewarne you whom you shall feare: Feare him, which after he hath killed, hath power to cast into hell, yea, I say vnto you, Feare him.
text.NABRE: I shall show you whom to fear. Be afraid of the one who after killing has the power to cast into Gehenna; yes, I tell you, be afraid of that one.
text.DRB: But I will shew you whom ye shall fear: fear him who after he hath killed, hath power to cast into hell. Yea I say to you, fear him.

text.NRSV: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten in God’s sight.
text.Rotherham: Are not, five sparrows, sold for two farthings? and, not one from among them, hath been forgotten before God.
text.KJV: Are not fiue sparrowes solde for two farthings, and not one of them is forgotten before God?
text.NABRE: Are not five sparrows sold for two small coins? Yet not one of them has escaped the notice of God.
text.DRB: Are not five sparrows sold for two farthings, and one of them is not forgotten before God?

text.NRSV: But even the hairs of your head are all counted. Do not be afraid; you are of more value than many sparrows.
text.Rotherham: But, even the hairs of your head, have all been numbered: Be not afraid: many sparrows, ye excel.
text.KJV: But euen the very haires of your head are all numbred: Feare not therefore, ye are of more value then many sparrowes.
text.NABRE: Even the hairs of your head have all been counted. Do not be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
text.DRB: Yea the hairs also of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore: you are more worth than many sparrows.

text.NRSV: “And I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before others, the Son of Man also will acknowledge before the angels of God;
text.Rotherham: And I say unto you—Whosoever shall confess me before men, even the Son of Man, will confess him, before the messengers of God;
text.KJV: Also I say vnto you, Whosoeuer shall confesse me before men, him shall the Sonne of man also confesse before the Angels of God.
text.NABRE: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before others the Son of Man will acknowledge before the angels of God.
text.DRB: And I say to you, Every one that confesseth me before men, the Son of man also will confess him before the Angels of God.

text.NRSV: but whoever denies me before others will be denied before the angels of God.
text.Rotherham: But, he who denied me before men, shall be denied before the messengers of God.
text.KJV: But he that denieth me before men, shalbe denied before the Angels of God.
text.NABRE: But whoever denies me before others will be denied before the angels of God. Sayings About the Holy Spirit.
text.DRB: But he that denieth me before men, shall be denied before the Angels of God.

text.NRSV: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven; but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever shall say a word against the Son of Man, it shall be forgiven him; but, unto him who, against the Holy Spirit, speaketh profanely, it shall not be forgiven.
text.KJV: And whosoeuer shall speake a word against the Sonne of man, it shall be forgiuen him: but vnto him that blasphemeth against the holy Ghost, it shal not be forgiuen.
text.NABRE: “Everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but the one who blasphemes against the holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
text.DRB: And every one that speaketh a word against the Son of man it shall be forgiven him: but he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost, to him it shall not be forgiven.

text.NRSV: When they bring you before the synagogues, the rulers, and the authorities, do not worry about how you are to defend yourselves or what you are to say;
text.Rotherham: But, whensoever they shall be bringing you in before the synagogues, and the rulers, and the authorities, do not be anxious how, [or what] ye shall answer, or what ye shall say;
text.KJV: And when they bring you vnto the Synagogues, and vnto Magistrates, & powers, take yee no thought how or what thing ye shall answere, or what ye shall say:
text.NABRE: When they take you before synagogues and before rulers and authorities, do not worry about how or what your defense will be or about what you are to say.
text.DRB: And when they shall bring you into the Synagogues and to Magistrates and Potestates, be not careful in what manner, or what you shall answer, or what you shall say.

text.NRSV: for the Holy Spirit will teach you at that very hour what you ought to say.”
text.Rotherham: For, the Holy Spirit, shall teach you, in that very hour, what ye ought to say.
text.KJV: For the holy Ghost shal teach you in the same houre, what ye ought to say.
text.NABRE: For the holy Spirit will teach you at that moment what you should say.”
text.DRB: For the Holy Ghost shall teach you in the very hour what you must say.

text.NRSV: Someone in the crowd said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the family inheritance with me.”
text.Rotherham: And one from amongst the multitude said unto him—Teacher! bid my brother divide with me the inheritance.
text.KJV: ¶ And one of the company saide vnto him, Master, speake to my brother, that he diuide the inheritance with me.
text.NABRE: Saying Against Greed. Someone in the crowd said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to share the inheritance with me.”
text.DRB: And one of the multitude said to him: Master, speak to my brother that he divide the inheritance with me.

text.NRSV: But he said to him, “Friend, who set me to be a judge or arbitrator over you?”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto him—Man! who hath appointed me a judge or divider over you?
text.KJV: And he said vnto him, Man, who made mee a iudge, or a diuider ouer you?
text.NABRE: He replied to him, “Friend, who appointed me as your judge and arbitrator?”
text.DRB: But he said to him: Man, who hath appointed me judge or divider over you?

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Take care! Be on your guard against all kinds of greed; for one’s life does not consist in the abundance of possessions.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Mind and be guarding yourselves from all covetousness; for not, in one’s abundance, doth his life spring out of his possessions.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Take heed and beware of couetousnes: for a mans life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.
text.NABRE: Then he said to the crowd, “Take care to guard against all greed, for though one may be rich, one’s life does not consist of possessions.”
text.DRB: And he said to them: See and beware of all avarice: for not in any man's aboundance doth his life consist, of those things which he possesseth.

text.NRSV: Then he told them a parable: “The land of a rich man produced abundantly.
text.Rotherham: And he spake a parable unto them, saying—A certain rich man’s estate, bare well.
text.KJV: And he spake a parable vnto them, saying, The ground of a certaine rich man brought foorth plentifully.
text.NABRE: Parable of the Rich Fool. Then he told them a parable. “There was a rich man whose land produced a bountiful harvest.
text.DRB: And he spake a similitude to them, saying: A certain rich man's field yielded plenty of fruits.

text.NRSV: And he thought to himself, ‘What should I do, for I have no place to store my crops?’
text.Rotherham: And he began to deliberate within himself, saying—What shall I do? because I have not where I can gather my fruits.
text.KJV: And he thought within himselfe, saying, What shall I doe, because I haue no roome where to bestow my fruits?
text.NABRE: He asked himself, ‘What shall I do, for I do not have space to store my harvest?’
text.DRB: And he thought within himself, saying, What shall I do, because I have not whither to gather my fruits?

text.NRSV: Then he said, ‘I will do this: I will pull down my barns and build larger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.
text.Rotherham: And he said—This, will I do,—I will pull down my barns, and, greater ones, build, and gather, there, all my wheat and good things;
text.KJV: And he said, This will I doe, I will pull downe my barnes, and build greater, and there will I bestow all my fruits, and my goods.
text.NABRE: And he said, ‘This is what I shall do: I shall tear down my barns and build larger ones. There I shall store all my grain and other goods
text.DRB: And he said: This will I do, I will destroy my barns, and will make greater, and thither will I gather all things that are grown to me, and my goods.

text.NRSV: And I will say to my soul, Soul, you have ample goods laid up for many years; relax, eat, drink, be merry.’
text.Rotherham: and will say to my soul—Soul! thou hast many good things [lying by for many years: be taking thy rest, eat, drink,] be making merry!
text.KJV: And I will say to my soule, Soule, thou hast much goods layd vp for many yeeres, take thine ease, eate, drinke, and be merry.
text.NABRE: and I shall say to myself, “Now as for you, you have so many good things stored up for many years, rest, eat, drink, be merry!”’
text.DRB: And I will say to my soul; Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years, take thy rest, eat, drink, make good cheer.

text.NRSV: But God said to him, ‘You fool! This very night your life is being demanded of you. And the things you have prepared, whose will they be?’
text.Rotherham: But God said unto him—Simple one! on this very night, they are asking, thy soul, from thee; The things, then, which thou hast prepared, whose shall they be?
text.KJV: But God said vnto him, Thou foole, this night thy soule shal be required of thee: then whose shal those things be which thou hast prouided?
text.NABRE: But God said to him, ‘You fool, this night your life will be demanded of you; and the things you have prepared, to whom will they belong?’
text.DRB: But God said to him, Thou fool, this night they require thy soul of thee; and the things that thou hast provided, whose shall they be?

text.NRSV: So it is with those who store up treasures for themselves but are not rich toward God.”
text.Rotherham: [So, is he that is laying up treasure for himself, and is not rich, towards God.]
text.KJV: So is he that laieth vp treasure for himselfe, and is not rich towards God.
text.NABRE: Thus will it be for the one who stores up treasure for himself but is not rich in what matters to God.”
text.DRB: So is he that layeth up treasure to himself, and is not rich to God-ward.

text.NRSV: He said to his disciples, “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto his disciples—For this cause, I say unto you,—Be not anxious for the life, what ye shall eat, nor yet for [your] body, what ye shall put on;
text.KJV: ¶ And he said vnto his disciples, Therefore I say vnto you, Take no thought for your life what yee shall eate, neither for the body what yee shall put on.
text.NABRE: Dependence on God. He said to [his] disciples, “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life and what you will eat, or about your body and what you will wear.
text.DRB: And he said to his Disciples: Therefore I say to you, Be not careful for your life, what you shall eat; nor for your body, what you shall do on.

text.NRSV: For life is more than food, and the body more than clothing.
text.Rotherham: For, the life, is more, than the food, and, the body, than, the clothing.
text.KJV: The life is more then meate, and the body is more then raiment.
text.NABRE: For life is more than food and the body more than clothing.
text.DRB: The life is more than the meat, and the body is more than the raiment.

text.NRSV: Consider the ravens: they neither sow nor reap, they have neither storehouse nor barn, and yet God feeds them. Of how much more value are you than the birds!
text.Rotherham: Consider well the ravens—That they sow nor, neither do they reap, which have neither chamber nor barn,—and, God, feedeth, them! By how much do, ye, excel, the birds?
text.KJV: Consider the rauens, for they neither sow nor reape, which neither haue storehouse nor barne, and God feedeth them: How much more are yee better then the foules?
text.NABRE: Notice the ravens: they do not sow or reap; they have neither storehouse nor barn, yet God feeds them. How much more important are you than birds!
text.DRB: Consider the ravens, for they sow not, neither do they reap, which neither have storehouse nor barn, and God feedeth them. How much more are you of greater price than they?

text.NRSV: And can any of you by worrying add a single hour to your span of life?
text.Rotherham: And, who from among you, though anxious, can, unto his stature, add a cubit?
text.KJV: And which of you with taking thought can adde to his stature one cubite?
text.NABRE: Can any of you by worrying add a moment to your life-span?
text.DRB: And which of you by caring can add to his stature one cubit?

text.NRSV: If then you are not able to do so small a thing as that, why do you worry about the rest?
text.Rotherham: If then, not even the least thing, ye can do, why, concerning the rest, are ye anxious?
text.KJV: If yee then bee not able to doe that thing which is least, why take yee thought for the rest?
text.NABRE: If even the smallest things are beyond your control, why are you anxious about the rest?
text.DRB: If then you be not able to do so much as the least thing, for the rest why are you careful?

text.NRSV: Consider the lilies, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin; yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not clothed like one of these.
text.Rotherham: Consider well the lilies, how they grow: they toil not, neither do they spin, and yet I say unto you—Not even Solomon, in all his glory, was arrayed like one of these.
text.KJV: Consider the Lillies how they growe, they toile not; they spinne not: and yet I say vnto you, that Solomon in all his glory, was not arayed like one of these.
text.NABRE: Notice how the flowers grow. They do not toil or spin. But I tell you, not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of them.
text.DRB: Consider the lilies how they grow: they labour not, neither do they spin. But I say to you, Neither Salomon in all his glory was arrayed as one of these.

text.NRSV: But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which is alive today and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, how much more will he clothe you — you of little faith!
text.Rotherham: But, if God thus adorneth, the grass, which is, in a field, to-day, and, to-morrow, into an oven, is cast, how much rather you, O little-of-faith?
text.KJV: If then God so clothe the grasse, which is to day in the field, and to morrow is cast into the ouen: how much more will he clothe you, O ye of litle faith?
text.NABRE: If God so clothes the grass in the field that grows today and is thrown into the oven tomorrow, will he not much more provide for you, O you of little faith?
text.DRB: And if the grass that today is in the field, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, God so clotheth; how much more you, O ye of little faith?

text.NRSV: And do not keep striving for what you are to eat and what you are to drink, and do not keep worrying.
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, be not seeking what ye shall eat and what ye shall drink, and be not held in suspense;
text.KJV: And seeke not yee what yee shall eate, or what ye shall drinke, neither be ye of doubtfull minde.
text.NABRE: As for you, do not seek what you are to eat and what you are to drink, and do not worry anymore.
text.DRB: And you, do not seek what you shall eat, or what you shall drink: and be not lifted up on high.

text.NRSV: For it is the nations of the world that strive after all these things, and your Father knows that you need them.
text.Rotherham: For, after all these things, do, the nations of the world, seek,—but, your Father, knoweth that ye need these things.
text.KJV: For all these things doe the nations of the world seeke after: and your father knoweth that yee haue neede of these things.
text.NABRE: All the nations of the world seek for these things, and your Father knows that you need them.
text.DRB: For all these things the Nations of the world do seek. But your Father knoweth that you have need of these things.

text.NRSV: Instead, strive for his kingdom, and these things will be given to you as well.
text.Rotherham: Notwithstanding, be seeking his kingdom,—and, these things, shall be added unto you.
text.KJV: ¶ But rather seeke yee the kingdome of God, and all these things shall be added vnto you.
text.NABRE: Instead, seek his kingdom, and these other things will be given you besides.
text.DRB: But seek first the Kingdom of God, and all these things shall be given you besides.

text.NRSV: “Do not be afraid, little flock, for it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the kingdom.
text.Rotherham: Be not afraid, the dear little flock! for your Father delighteth to give you, the kingdom.
text.KJV: Feare not, litle flocke, for it is your fathers good pleasure to giue you the kingdome.
text.NABRE: Do not be afraid any longer, little flock, for your Father is pleased to give you the kingdom.
text.DRB: Fear not little flock, for it hath pleased your Father to give you a Kingdom.

text.NRSV: Sell your possessions, and give alms. Make purses for yourselves that do not wear out, an unfailing treasure in heaven, where no thief comes near and no moth destroys.
text.Rotherham: Sell your possessions, and give alms, make for yourselves purses that wax not old,—treasure unfailing, in the heavens, where, thief, doth not draw near, and, moth, doth not spoil.
text.KJV: Sell that yee haue, and giue almes: prouide your selues bagges which waxe not old, a treasure in the heauens that faileth not, where no theefe approcheth, neither moth corrupteth.
text.NABRE: Sell your belongings and give alms. Provide money bags for yourselves that do not wear out, an inexhaustible treasure in heaven that no thief can reach nor moth destroy.
text.DRB: Sell the things that you possess, and give alms. Make to you purses that wear not, treasure that wasteth not, in Heaven: whither the thief approacheth not, neither doth the moth corrupt.

text.NRSV: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
text.Rotherham: For, where your treasure is, there, will your heart he also.
text.KJV: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
text.NABRE: For where your treasure is, there also will your heart be. Vigilant and Faithful Servants.
text.DRB: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.

text.NRSV: “Be dressed for action and have your lamps lit;
text.Rotherham: Let your loins be girded, and your lamps burning,
text.KJV: Let your loines be girded about, and your lights burning,
text.NABRE: “Gird your loins and light your lamps
text.DRB: Let your loins be girded, and candles burning in your hands,

text.NRSV: be like those who are waiting for their master to return from the wedding banquet, so that they may open the door for him as soon as he comes and knocks.
text.Rotherham: And, ye yourselves, like unto men awaiting their own lord, once he may break up out of the marriage-feast,—that, when he cometh and knocketh, straightway, they may open unto him.
text.KJV: And ye your selues like vnto men that waite for their Lord, when he will returne from the wedding, that when he commeth and knocketh, they may open vnto him immediately.
text.NABRE: and be like servants who await their master’s return from a wedding, ready to open immediately when he comes and knocks.
text.DRB: and you like to men expecting their Lord, when he shall return from the marriage: that when he doth come and knock, forthwith they may open unto him.

text.NRSV: Blessed are those slaves whom the master finds alert when he comes; truly I tell you, he will fasten his belt and have them sit down to eat, and he will come and serve them.
text.Rotherham: Happy those servants, whom the lord, when he cometh, shall find watching! Verily, I say unto you—he will gird himself, and make them recline, and, coming near, will minister unto them.
text.KJV: Blessed are those seruants, whom the Lord when he commeth, shall find watching: Uerily, I say vnto you, That he shall girde himselfe, and make them to sit downe to meate, and will come foorth and serue them.
text.NABRE: Blessed are those servants whom the master finds vigilant on his arrival. Amen, I say to you, he will gird himself, have them recline at table, and proceed to wait on them.
text.DRB: Blessed are those servants, whom when the Lord cometh, he shall find watching. Amen I say to you, that he will gird himself, and make them sit down, and passing will minister unto them.

text.NRSV: If he comes during the middle of the night, or near dawn, and finds them so, blessed are those slaves.
text.Rotherham: And, if, in the second, or if, in the third, watch, he come and find, thus, happy, are, they!
text.KJV: And if he shall come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those seruants.
text.NABRE: And should he come in the second or third watch and find them prepared in this way, blessed are those servants.
text.DRB: And if he come in the second watch, and if in the third watch he come, and so find, blessed are those servants.

text.NRSV: “But know this: if the owner of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.
text.Rotherham: But, of this, be taking note—Had the householder known, in what hour the thief was coming, he would have watched, and not suffered his house, to be digged through.
text.KJV: And this know, that if the good man of the house had knowen what houre the theefe would come, he would haue watched, and not haue suffred his house to be broken thorow.
text.NABRE: Be sure of this: if the master of the house had known the hour when the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.
text.DRB: And this know ye, that if the householder did know what hour the thief would come, he would watch verily, and would not suffer his house to be broken up.

text.NRSV: You also must be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an unexpected hour.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, be getting ready, because, in what hour ye are not thinking, The Son of Man cometh!
text.KJV: Be yee therefore ready also: for the sonne of man commeth at an houre when yee thinke not.
text.NABRE: You also must be prepared, for at an hour you do not expect, the Son of Man will come.”
