text.NRSV: But he answered his father, ‘Listen! For all these years I have been working like a slave for you, and I have never disobeyed your command; yet you have never given me even a young goat so that I might celebrate with my friends.
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering said unto his father—Lo! so many years as these, do I serve thee, and, at no time, a commandment of thine, have I transgressed,—And, unto me, at no time, hast thou given a kid, that, with my friends, I might make merry;
text.KJV: And he answering said to his father, Loe, these many yeeres doe I serue thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandement, and yet thou neuer gauest mee a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:
text.NABRE: He said to his father in reply, ‘Look, all these years I served you and not once did I disobey your orders; yet you never gave me even a young goat to feast on with my friends.
text.DRB: But he answering said to his father: Behold, so many years do I serve thee, and I never transgressed thy commandment, and thou didst never give me a kid to make merry with my friends:

text.NRSV: But when this son of yours came back, who has devoured your property with prostitutes, you killed the fatted calf for him!’
text.Rotherham: But, when, this thy son, who had devoured thy living with harlots, came, thou didst sacrifice, for him, the fatted calf;
text.KJV: But as soone as this thy sonne was come, which hath deuoured thy liuing with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calfe.
text.NABRE: But when your son returns who swallowed up your property with prostitutes, for him you slaughter the fattened calf.’
text.DRB: but after that this thy son, that hath devoured his substance with whores, is come, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.

text.NRSV: Then the father said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto him—Child! thou, always, art, with me, and, all that is mine, is, thine;
text.KJV: And he said vnto him, Sonne, thou art euer with me, and all that I haue is thine.
text.NABRE: He said to him, ‘My son, you are here with me always; everything I have is yours.
text.DRB: But he said to him: Son, thou art always with me, and all my things are thine.

text.NRSV: But we had to celebrate and rejoice, because this brother of yours was dead and has come to life; he was lost and has been found.’”
text.Rotherham: But, to make merry and rejoice, there was need, because, this thy brother—was, dead, and hath come to life again, and was lost, and is found.
text.KJV: It was meete that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is aliue againe: and was lost, and is found.
text.NABRE: But now we must celebrate and rejoice, because your brother was dead and has come to life again; he was lost and has been found.’”
text.DRB: But it behoved us to make merry and be glad because this thy brother was dead, and is revived, was lost, and is found.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to the disciples, “There was a rich man who had a manager, and charges were brought to him that this man was squandering his property.
text.Rotherham: And he went on to say, unto his disciples also,—There was, a certain rich man, who had a steward, and, the same, was accused to him as squandering his goods.
text.KJV: And hee said also vnto his disciples, There was a certaine rich man which had a Steward, and the same was accused vnto him that he had wasted his goods.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 The Parable of the Dishonest Steward. Then he also said to his disciples, “A rich man had a steward who was reported to him for squandering his property.
text.DRB: And he said also to his Disciples: There was a certain rich man that had a bailiff: & he was ill reported of unto him, as he that had wasted his goods.

text.NRSV: So he summoned him and said to him, ‘What is this that I hear about you? Give me an accounting of your management, because you cannot be my manager any longer.’
text.Rotherham: And, accosting him, he said unto him—What is this I hear of thee? Render the account of thy stewardship, for thou canst no longer be steward.
text.KJV: And hee called him, and said vnto him, How is it that I heare this of thee? Giue an accompt of thy stewardship: for thou mayest bee no longer Steward.
text.NABRE: He summoned him and said, ‘What is this I hear about you? Prepare a full account of your stewardship, because you can no longer be my steward.’
text.DRB: And he called him, and said to him: What hear I this of thee? Render account of thy bailiffship: for now thou canst no more be bailiff.

text.NRSV: Then the manager said to himself, ‘What will I do, now that my master is taking the position away from me? I am not strong enough to dig, and I am ashamed to beg.
text.Rotherham: And the steward said within himself—What shall I do, because my lord taketh away the stewardship from me? Dig, I cannot: to beg, I am ashamed.
text.KJV: Then the Steward said within himselfe, What shall I doe, for my lord taketh away from mee the Stewardship? I cannot digge, to begge I am ashamed.
text.NABRE: The steward said to himself, ‘What shall I do, now that my master is taking the position of steward away from me? I am not strong enough to dig and I am ashamed to beg.
text.DRB: And the bailiff said within himself: What shall I do, because my lord taketh away from me the bailiffship? Dig I am not able, to beg I am ashamed.

text.NRSV: I have decided what to do so that, when I am dismissed as manager, people may welcome me into their homes.’
text.Rotherham: I know what I will do, that, when I am removed out of the stewardship, they may welcome me into their own houses.
text.KJV: I am resolued what to doe, that when I am put out of the stewardship, they may receiue me into their houses.
text.NABRE: I know what I shall do so that, when I am removed from the stewardship, they may welcome me into their homes.’
text.DRB: I know what I will do, that when I shall be removed from the bailiffship, they may receive me into their houses.

text.NRSV: So, summoning his master’s debtors one by one, he asked the first, ‘How much do you owe my master?’
text.Rotherham: And, calling unto him each one of the debtors of his own lord, he was saying unto the first,—How much owest thou my lord?
text.KJV: So hee called euery one of his lords detters vnto him, and said vnto the first, How much owest thou vnto my lord?
text.NABRE: He called in his master’s debtors one by one. To the first he said, ‘How much do you owe my master?’
text.DRB: Therefore calling together every one of his lord's debters, he said to the first: How much doest thou owe my lord?

text.NRSV: He answered, ‘A hundred jugs of olive oil.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, sit down quickly, and make it fifty.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—A hundred baths of oil. And, he, said unto him—Kindly take thine accounts, and, sitting down, make haste and write—Fifty!
text.KJV: And hee said, An hundred measures of oyle. And hee saide vnto him, Take thy bill, and sit downe quickly, and write fiftie.
text.NABRE: He replied, ‘One hundred measures of olive oil.’ He said to him, ‘Here is your promissory note. Sit down and quickly write one for fifty.’
text.DRB: But he saith: An hundred pipes of oil. And he said to him: Take thy bill and sit down, quickly write fifty.

text.NRSV: Then he asked another, ‘And how much do you owe?’ He replied, ‘A hundred containers of wheat.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill and make it eighty.’
text.Rotherham: After that, unto another, he said—And how much owest, thou? And, he, said—A hundred homers of wheat. He saith unto him—Kindly take thine accounts, and write—Eighty!
text.KJV: Then said hee to another, And how much owest thou? And hee said, An hundred measures of wheat. And hee saide vnto him, Take thy bill and write fourescore.
text.NABRE: Then to another he said, ‘And you, how much do you owe?’ He replied, ‘One hundred kors of wheat.’ He said to him, ‘Here is your promissory note; write one for eighty.’
text.DRB: After that he said to another: But thou, how much doest thou owe? Who said: An hundreth quarters of wheat. He said to him: Take thy bill, and write eighty.

text.NRSV: And his master commended the dishonest manager because he had acted shrewdly; for the children of this age are more shrewd in dealing with their own generation than are the children of light.
text.Rotherham: And the lord praised the unrighteous steward, in that with forethought he acted:—Because, the sons of this age, have more forethought than the sons of light, respecting their own generation.
text.KJV: And the lord commended the vniust Steward, because he had done wisely: for the children of this world are in their generation wiser then the children of light.
text.NABRE: And the master commended that dishonest steward for acting prudently. Application of the Parable. “For the children of this world are more prudent in dealing with their own generation than are the children of light.
text.DRB: And the lord praised the bailiff of iniquity, because he had done wisely: for the children of this world, are wiser than the children of light in their generation.

text.NRSV: And I tell you, make friends for yourselves by means of dishonest wealth so that when it is gone, they may welcome you into the eternal homes.
text.Rotherham: And, I, unto you, say—For yourselves, make ye friends, with the unjust Riches, in order that, as soon as it shall fail, they may welcome you into the age-abiding tents.
text.KJV: And I say vnto you, Make to your selues friends of the Mammon of vnrighteousnesse, that when ye faile, they may receiue you into euerlasting habitations.
text.NABRE: I tell you, make friends for yourselves with dishonest wealth, so that when it fails, you will be welcomed into eternal dwellings.
text.DRB: And I say to you: Make unto you friends of the mammon of iniquity that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tabernacles.

text.NRSV: “Whoever is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much; and whoever is dishonest in a very little is dishonest also in much.
text.Rotherham: The faithful in least, in much also, is, faithful, and, he that in least is unrighteous, in much also, is, unrighteous.
text.KJV: Hee that is faithfull in that which is least, is faithfull also in much: and he that is vniust in the least, is vniust also in much.
text.NABRE: The person who is trustworthy in very small matters is also trustworthy in great ones; and the person who is dishonest in very small matters is also dishonest in great ones.
text.DRB: He that is faithful in the least, is faithful in the greater also: and he that is unjust in little, is unjust in the greater also.

text.NRSV: If then you have not been faithful with the dishonest wealth, who will entrust to you the true riches?
text.Rotherham: If therefore, in the unjust Riches ye proved unfaithful, the true, who, unto you, will entrust?
text.KJV: If therefore yee haue not bene faithfull in the vnrighteous Mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?
text.NABRE: If, therefore, you are not trustworthy with dishonest wealth, who will trust you with true wealth?
text.DRB: If then you have not been faithful in the unjust mammon, with that which is the true who may credit you?

text.NRSV: And if you have not been faithful with what belongs to another, who will give you what is your own?
text.Rotherham: And, if, in what was another’s, ye proved unfaithful, your own, who will give unto you?
text.KJV: And if ye haue not bene faithful in that which is another mans, who shall giue you that which is your owne?
text.NABRE: If you are not trustworthy with what belongs to another, who will give you what is yours?
text.DRB: And if you have not been faithful in other men's, that which is yours, who will give you?

text.NRSV: No slave can serve two masters; for a slave will either hate the one and love the other, or be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.”
text.Rotherham: No domestic, can, unto two masters, be in service; for either, the one, he will hate, and, the other, love, or, unto the one, he will hold, and, the other, despise: Ye cannot, unto God, be in service, and unto Riches.
text.KJV: ¶ No seruant can serue two masters, for either he will hate the one, and loue the other: or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other: yee cannot serue God and Mammon.
text.NABRE: No servant can serve two masters. He will either hate one and love the other, or be devoted to one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and mammon.”
text.DRB: No servant can serve two masters; for either he shall hate the one, and love the other; or cleave to one, and contemn the other. You cannot serve God and mammon.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, heard all this, and they ridiculed him.
text.Rotherham: Now the Pharisees, who were, lovers of money, were hearing all these things, and were openly sneering at him.
text.KJV: And the Pharisees also who were couetous, heard all these things: and they derided him.
text.NABRE: A Saying Against the Pharisees. The Pharisees, who loved money, heard all these things and sneered at him.
text.DRB: And the Pharisees which were covetous, heard all these things: and they derided him.

text.NRSV: So he said to them, “You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of others; but God knows your hearts; for what is prized by human beings is an abomination in the sight of God.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Ye, are they who justify themselves before men, but, God, knoweth your hearts; because, that which amongst men is lofty, is an abomination before God.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Ye are they which iustifie your selues before men, but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed amongst men, is abomination in the sight of God.
text.NABRE: And he said to them, “You justify yourselves in the sight of others, but God knows your hearts; for what is of human esteem is an abomination in the sight of God.
text.DRB: And he said to them: You are they that justify yourselves before men, but God knoweth your hearts, because that which is high to men, is abomination before God.

text.NRSV: “The law and the prophets were until John came; since then the good news of the kingdom of God is proclaimed, and everyone tries to enter it by force.
text.Rotherham: The law and the prophets, were until John,—from that time, the good news of the kingdom of God, is being proclaimed, and, everyone, thereinto, is forcing his way.
text.KJV: The law and the Prophets were vntill Iohn: since that time the kingdome of God is preached, and euery man preasseth into it.
text.NABRE: Sayings About the Law. “The law and the prophets lasted until John; but from then on the kingdom of God is proclaimed, and everyone who enters does so with violence.
text.DRB: The Law and the Prophets, unto John. From that time the Kingdom of God is evangelized, and every one doth force toward it.

text.NRSV: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away, than for one stroke of a letter in the law to be struck out.
text.Rotherham: But it is, easier, for heaven and earth to pass away, than that, of the law, one little point, should fail.
text.KJV: And it is easier for heauen and earth to passe, then one title of the law to faile.
text.NABRE: It is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest part of a letter of the law to become invalid.
text.DRB: And it is easier for Heaven and earth to pass, than one tittle of the law to fall.

text.NRSV: “Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and whoever marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
text.Rotherham: Every one divorcing his wife and marrying another, committeth adultery; and, he that marrieth a woman divorced from a husband, committeth adultery.
text.KJV: Whosoeuer putteth away his wife, & marrieth another, committeth adultery: and whosoeuer marrieth her that is put away from her husband, committeth adultery.
text.NABRE: Sayings About Divorce. “Everyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and the one who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery. The Parable of the Rich Man and Lazarus.
text.DRB: Every one that dismisseth his wife, and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and he that marrieth her that is dismissed from her husband, committeth adultery.

text.NRSV: “There was a rich man who was dressed in purple and fine linen and who feasted sumptuously every day.
text.Rotherham: Now, a certain man, was rich, and he used to clothe himself with purple and fine linen, making merry day by day, brilliantly.
text.KJV: ¶ There was a certaine rich man, which was clothed in purple and fine linnen, and fared sumptuously euery day.
text.NABRE: “There was a rich man who dressed in purple garments and fine linen and dined sumptuously each day.
text.DRB: There was a certain rich man, & he was clothed with purple and silk: and he fared every day magnifically.

text.NRSV: And at his gate lay a poor man named Lazarus, covered with sores,
text.Rotherham: And, a certain beggar, by name Lazarus, used to be cast near his gate, full of sores,
text.KJV: And there was a certaine begger named Lazarus, which was layde at his gate full of sores,
text.NABRE: And lying at his door was a poor man named Lazarus, covered with sores,
text.DRB: And there was a certain beggar called Lazarus, that lay at his gate, full of sores:

text.NRSV: who longed to satisfy his hunger with what fell from the rich man’s table; even the dogs would come and lick his sores.
text.Rotherham: and to long to be fed from the crumbs that fell from the table of the rich man: nay! even, the dogs, used to come and lick his sores.
text.KJV: And desiring to bee fed with the crummes which fel from the rich mans table: moreouer the dogges came and licked his sores.
text.NABRE: who would gladly have eaten his fill of the scraps that fell from the rich man’s table. Dogs even used to come and lick his sores.
text.DRB: desiring to be filled of the crumbs, that fell from the rich man's table, but the dogs also came, and licked his sores.

text.NRSV: The poor man died and was carried away by the angels to be with Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that the beggar died, and was carried away by the messengers, into the bosom of Abraham. And, the rich man also, died, and was buried.
text.KJV: And it came to passe that the begger died, and was caried by the Angels into Abrahams bosome: the rich man also died, and was buried.
text.NABRE: When the poor man died, he was carried away by angels to the bosom of Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried,
text.DRB: And it came to pass that the beggar died, and was carried of the Angels into Abraham's bosom. And the rich man also died: and he was buried in hell.

text.NRSV: In Hades, where he was being tormented, he looked up and saw Abraham far away with Lazarus by his side.
text.Rotherham: And, in hades, lifting up his eyes, being in torments, he seeth Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.
text.KJV: And in hell he lift vp his eyes being in torments, and seeth Abraham afarre off, and Lazarus in his bosome:
text.NABRE: and from the netherworld, where he was in torment, he raised his eyes and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side.
text.DRB: And lifting up his eyes, when he was in torments, he saw Abraham a far off, and Lazarus in his bosom:

text.NRSV: He called out, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue; for I am in agony in these flames.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, calling out, said—Father Abraham! have mercy upon me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue,—because I am in anguish in this flame.
text.KJV: And he cried, and said, Father Abraham, haue mercy on mee, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and coole my tongue, for I am tormented in this flame.
text.NABRE: And he cried out, ‘Father Abraham, have pity on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, for I am suffering torment in these flames.’
text.DRB: and he crying said: Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus that he may dip the tip of his finger into water for to cool my tongue, because I am tormented in this flame.

text.NRSV: But Abraham said, ‘Child, remember that during your lifetime you received your good things, and Lazarus in like manner evil things; but now he is comforted here, and you are in agony.
text.Rotherham: But Abraham said—Child! remember—That thou didst duly received thy good things in thy life, and, Lazarus, in like manner, the evil things; but, now, here, he is comforted, and, thou, art in anguish.
text.KJV: But Abraham saide, Sonne, remember that thou in thy life-time receiuedst thy good things, and likewise Lazarus euill things, but now he is comforted, and thou art tormented.
text.NABRE: Abraham replied, ‘My child, remember that you received what was good during your lifetime while Lazarus likewise received what was bad; but now he is comforted here, whereas you are tormented.
text.DRB: And Abraham said to him: Son, remember that thou didst receive good things in thy life time, and Lazarus likewise evil: but now he is comforted, and thou art tormented.

text.NRSV: Besides all this, between us and you a great chasm has been fixed, so that those who might want to pass from here to you cannot do so, and no one can cross from there to us.’
text.Rotherham: And, besides all these things, betwixt us and you, a great chasm, hath been fixed,—so that, they who might wish to cross over from hence unto you, should not be able, nor any, from thence unto us, be crossing over.
text.KJV: And besides all this, betweene vs and you there is a great gulfe fixed, so that they which would passe from hence to you, cannot, neither can they passe to vs, that would come from thence.
text.NABRE: Moreover, between us and you a great chasm is established to prevent anyone from crossing who might wish to go from our side to yours or from your side to ours.’
text.DRB: And beside all these things, between us and you there is fixed a great chaos: that they which will pass from hence to you, may not, neither go from thence hither.

text.NRSV: He said, ‘Then, father, I beg you to send him to my father’s house—
text.Rotherham: But he said—I request thee then, father, that thou wouldst send him unto my father’s house,—
text.KJV: Then he said, I pray thee therefore father, that thou wouldest send him to my fathers house:
text.NABRE: He said, ‘Then I beg you, father, send him to my father’s house,
text.DRB: And he said: Then, father, I beseech thee that thou wouldest send him unto my father's house, for I have five brethren,

text.NRSV: for I have five brothers—that he may warn them, so that they will not also come into this place of torment.’
text.Rotherham: for I have five brethren;—that he may solemnly testify unto them, lest, they also, come into this place of torment.
text.KJV: For I haue fiue brethren, that he may testifie vnto them, lest they also come into this place of torment.
text.NABRE: for I have five brothers, so that he may warn them, lest they too come to this place of torment.’
text.DRB: for to testify unto them, lest they also come into this place of torments.

text.NRSV: Abraham replied, ‘They have Moses and the prophets; they should listen to them.’
text.Rotherham: But Abraham saith—They have Moses and the Prophets: Let them hearken unto them.
text.KJV: Abraham saith vnto him, They haue Moses and the Prophets, let them heare them.
text.NABRE: But Abraham replied, ‘They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.’
text.DRB: And Abraham said to him: They have Moyses and the Prophets: let them hear them.

text.NRSV: He said, ‘No, father Abraham; but if someone goes to them from the dead, they will repent.’
text.Rotherham: But, he, said—Nay! father Abraham, but, if one, from the dead, should go unto them, they would repent.
text.KJV: And hee said, Nay, father Abraham: but if one went vnto them from the dead, they will repent.
text.NABRE: He said, ‘Oh no, father Abraham, but if someone from the dead goes to them, they will repent.’
text.DRB: But he said: No, father Abraham, but if some man shall go from the dead to them, they will do penance.

text.NRSV: He said to him, ‘If they do not listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be convinced even if someone rises from the dead.’”
text.Rotherham: But he said unto him—If, unto Moses and the Prophets, they do not hearken, neither, if one, from among the dead, should arise, would they be persuaded.
text.KJV: And hee said vnto him, If they heare not Moses and the Prophets, neither will they be perswaded, though one rose from the dead.
text.NABRE: Then Abraham said, ‘If they will not listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded if someone should rise from the dead.’”
text.DRB: And he said to him: If they hear not Moyses and the Prophets, neither if one shall rise again from the dead, will they believe.

text.KJV: Then said he vnto the disciples, It is impossible but that offences will come, but wo vnto him through whom they come.
text.NABRE: Chapter 17 Temptations to Sin. He said to his disciples, “Things that cause sin will inevitably occur, but woe to the person through whom they occur.
text.NRSV: Jesus said to his disciples, “Occasions for stumbling are bound to come, but woe to anyone by whom they come!
text.DRB: And he said to his Disciples: It is impossible that scandals should not come: but woe to him by whom they come.

text.KJV: It were better for him that a milstone were hanged about his necke, and he cast into the Sea, then that he should offend one of these little ones.
text.NABRE: It would be better for him if a millstone were put around his neck and he be thrown into the sea than for him to cause one of these little ones to sin.
text.NRSV: It would be better for you if a millstone were hung around your neck and you were thrown into the sea than for you to cause one of these little ones to stumble.
text.DRB: It is more profitable for him, if a mill-stone be put about his neck, and he be cast into the sea, than that he scandalize one of these little ones.

text.KJV: ¶ Take heed to your selues: If thy brother trespasse against thee, rebuke him, and if he repent, forgiue him.
text.NABRE: Be on your guard! If your brother sins, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.
text.NRSV: Be on your guard! If another disciple sins, you must rebuke the offender, and if there is repentance, you must forgive.
text.DRB: Look well to yourselves. If thy brother sin against thee, rebuke him: and if he do penance, forgive him.

text.KJV: And if hee trespasse against thee seuen times in a day, and seuen times in a day turne againe to thee, saying, I repent, thou shalt forgiue him.
text.NABRE: And if he wrongs you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times saying, ‘I am sorry,’ you should forgive him.”
text.NRSV: And if the same person sins against you seven times a day, and turns back to you seven times and says, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive.”
text.DRB: And if he sin against thee seven times in a day, and seven times in a day be converted unto thee, saying, It repenteth me, forgive him.

text.KJV: And the Apostles said vnto the Lord, Increase our faith.
text.NABRE: Saying of Faith. And the apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.”
text.NRSV: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”
text.DRB: And the Apostles said to our Lord: Increase faith in us.

text.KJV: And the Lord said, If yee had faith as a graine of mustard seede, yee might say vnto this Sycamine tree, Be thou plucked vp by the root, and be thou planted in the sea, & it should obey you.
text.NABRE: The Lord replied, “If you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you would say to [this] mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you. Attitude of a Servant.
text.NRSV: The Lord replied, “If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
text.DRB: And our Lord said: If you had faith like to a mustard-seed, you might say to this mulberry tree, be thou rooted up, and be transplanted into the sea: and it would obey you.

text.KJV: But which of you hauing a seruant plowing, or feeding cattell, will say vnto him by & by when he is come from the field, Goe and sit downe to meate?
text.NABRE: “Who among you would say to your servant who has just come in from plowing or tending sheep in the field, ‘Come here immediately and take your place at table’?
text.NRSV: “Who among you would say to your slave who has just come in from plowing or tending sheep in the field, ‘Come here at once and take your place at the table’?
text.DRB: And which of you having a servant plowing or keeping cattle, that will say to him returning out of the field: Pass quickly, sit down:

text.KJV: And will not rather say vnto him, Make ready wherewith I may suppe, and gird thy selfe, and serue me, till I haue eaten and drunken: and afterward thou shalt eate and drinke.
text.NABRE: Would he not rather say to him, ‘Prepare something for me to eat. Put on your apron and wait on me while I eat and drink. You may eat and drink when I am finished’?
text.NRSV: Would you not rather say to him, ‘Prepare supper for me, put on your apron and serve me while I eat and drink; later you may eat and drink’?
text.DRB: and saith not to him: Make ready supper, and gird thyself, and serve me whiles I eat and drink, and afterward thou shalt eat and drink?

text.KJV: Doeth he thanke that seruant, because hee did the things that were commanded him? I trow not.
text.NABRE: Is he grateful to that servant because he did what was commanded?
text.NRSV: Do you thank the slave for doing what was commanded?
text.DRB: Doth he give that servant thanks, for doing the things which he commanded him?

text.KJV: So likewise ye, when ye shal haue done all those things which are commanded you, say, Wee are vnprofitable seruants: Wee haue done that which was our duety to doe.
text.NABRE: So should it be with you. When you have done all you have been commanded, say, ‘We are unprofitable servants; we have done what we were obliged to do.’” The Cleansing of Ten Lepers.
text.NRSV: So you also, when you have done all that you were ordered to do, say, ‘We are worthless slaves; we have done only what we ought to have done!’”
text.DRB: Itrow not: So you also, when you shall have done all things that are commanded you, say: We are unprofitable servants; we have done that which we ought to do.

text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, as he went to Hierusalem, that hee passed thorow the mids of Samaria and Galile.
text.NABRE: As he continued his journey to Jerusalem, he traveled through Samaria and Galilee.
text.NRSV: On the way to Jerusalem Jesus was going through the region between Samaria and Galilee.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, as he went unto Jerusalem, he passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.

text.KJV: And as he entred into a certaine village, there met him tenne men that were lepers, which stood afarre off.
text.NABRE: As he was entering a village, ten lepers met [him]. They stood at a distance from him
text.NRSV: As he entered a village, ten lepers approached him. Keeping their distance,
text.DRB: And when he entered into a certain town, there met him ten men that were lepers, who stood a far off;

text.KJV: And they lifted vp their voices, and said, Iesus master haue mercy on vs.
text.NABRE: and raised their voice, saying, “Jesus, Master! Have pity on us!”
text.NRSV: they called out, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”
text.DRB: and they lifted up their voice, saying: Jesus Master, have mercy on us.

text.KJV: And when he saw them, hee said vnto them, Goe shew your selues vnto the Priests. And it came to passe, that as they went, they were cleansed.
text.NABRE: And when he saw them, he said, “Go show yourselves to the priests.” As they were going they were cleansed.
text.NRSV: When he saw them, he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” And as they went, they were cleansed.
text.DRB: Whom as he saw, he said: Go, shew yourselves to the Priests. And it came to pass, as they went, they were made clean.

text.KJV: And one of them when hee sawe that he was healed, turned backe, and with a loud voice glorified God,
text.NABRE: And one of them, realizing he had been healed, returned, glorifying God in a loud voice;
text.NRSV: Then one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, praising God with a loud voice.
text.DRB: And one of them as he saw that he was made clean, went back with a loud voice magnifying God,

text.KJV: And fell downe on his face at his feet, giuing him thanks: and he was a Samaritane.
text.NABRE: and he fell at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
text.NRSV: He prostrated himself at Jesus’ feet and thanked him. And he was a Samaritan.
text.DRB: and he fell on his face before his feet, giving thanks: and this was a Samaritane.

text.KJV: And Iesus answering, said, Were there not ten cleansed, but where are the nine?
text.NABRE: Jesus said in reply, “Ten were cleansed, were they not? Where are the other nine?
text.NRSV: Then Jesus asked, “Were not ten made clean? But the other nine, where are they?
text.DRB: And Jesus answering said: Were not ten made clean? and where are the nine?

text.KJV: There are not found that returned to giue glory to God, saue this stranger.
text.NABRE: Has none but this foreigner returned to give thanks to God?”
text.NRSV: Was none of them found to return and give praise to God except this foreigner?”
text.DRB: There was not found that returned, and gave glory to God, but this stranger.

text.KJV: And he said vnto him, Arise, go thy way, thy faith hath made thee whole.
text.NABRE: Then he said to him, “Stand up and go; your faith has saved you.”
text.NRSV: Then he said to him, “Get up and go on your way; your faith has made you well.”
text.DRB: And he said to him: Arise, go thy ways; because thy faith hath made thee safe.

text.KJV: ¶ And when hee was demanded of the Pharises, when the kingdome of God should come, hee answered them, and said, The kingdome of God commeth not with obseruation.
text.NABRE: The Coming of the Kingdom of God. Asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, he said in reply, “The coming of the kingdom of God cannot be observed,
text.NRSV: Once Jesus was asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God was coming, and he answered, “The kingdom of God is not coming with things that can be observed;
text.DRB: And being asked of the Pharisees: when cometh the Kingdom of God? he answered them and said: The Kingdom of God cometh not with observation:

text.KJV: Neither shall they say, Loe here, or loe there: for behold, the kingdome of God is within you.
text.NABRE: and no one will announce, ‘Look, here it is,’ or, ‘There it is.’ For behold, the kingdom of God is among you.”
text.NRSV: nor will they say, ‘Look, here it is!’ or ‘There it is!’ For, in fact, the kingdom of God is among you.”
text.DRB: neither shall they say: Lo here, or lo there. For lo the Kingdom of God is within you.

text.KJV: And hee said vnto the disciples, The dayes will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the dayes of the Sonne of man, and ye shall not see it.
text.NABRE: The Day of the Son of Man. Then he said to his disciples, “The days will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it.
text.NRSV: Then he said to the disciples, “The days are coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
text.DRB: And he said to his Disciples: The days will come when you shall desire to see one day of the Son of man; and you shall not see.

text.KJV: And they shall say to you, See here, or see there: Goe not after them, nor follow them.
text.NABRE: There will be those who will say to you, ‘Look, there he is,’ [or] ‘Look, here he is.’ Do not go off, do not run in pursuit.
text.NRSV: They will say to you, ‘Look there!’ or ‘Look here!’ Do not go, do not set off in pursuit.
text.DRB: And they will say to you: Lo here and lo there. Go not, neither do ye follow after.

text.KJV: For as the lightning that lighteneth out of the one part vnder heauen, shineth vnto the other part vnder heauen: so shall also the Sonne of man be in his day.
text.NABRE: For just as lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one side to the other, so will the Son of Man be [in his day].
text.NRSV: For as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one side to the other, so will the Son of Man be in his day.
text.DRB: For even as the lightning that lighteneth from under Heaven, unto those parts that are under Heaven, shineth: so shall the Son of man be in his day.

text.KJV: But first must hee suffer many things, & be reiected of this generation.
text.NABRE: But first he must suffer greatly and be rejected by this generation.
text.NRSV: But first he must endure much suffering and be rejected by this generation.
text.DRB: But first he must suffer many things and be rejected of this generation.

text.KJV: And as it was in the dayes of Noe: so shal it be also in the dayes of the Sonne of man.
text.NABRE: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be in the days of the Son of Man;
text.NRSV: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so too it will be in the days of the Son of Man.
text.DRB: And as it came to pass in the days of Noe, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of man.

text.KJV: They did eate, they dranke, they married wiues, they were giuen in mariage, vntill the day that Noe entred into the arke: and the flood came, and destroyed them all.
text.NABRE: they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage up to the day that Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed them all.
text.NRSV: They were eating and drinking, and marrying and being given in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed all of them.
text.DRB: They did eat and drink, they did marry wives and were given to marriage even until the day that Noe entered into the ark: and the flood came, and destroyed them all.

text.KJV: Likewise also as it was in the dayes of Lot, they did eat, they dranke, they bought, they sold, they planted, they builded:
text.NABRE: Similarly, as it was in the days of Lot: they were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, building;
text.NRSV: Likewise, just as it was in the days of Lot: they were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building,
text.DRB: Likewise as it came to pass in the days of Lot: They did eat and drink, bought and sold, planted, and builded:

text.KJV: But the same day that Lot went out of Sodome, it rained fire and brimstone from heauen, & destroyed them all:
text.NABRE: on the day when Lot left Sodom, fire and brimstone rained from the sky to destroy them all.
text.NRSV: but on the day that Lot left Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from heaven and destroyed all of them.
text.DRB: and in the day that Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire & brimstone from Heaven, & destroyed them all:

text.KJV: Euen thus shall it bee in the day when the Sonne of man is reuealed.
text.NABRE: So it will be on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
text.NRSV: —it will be like that on the day that the Son of Man is revealed.
text.DRB: according to these things it shall be in the day that the Son of man shall be revealed.

text.KJV: In that day he which shall be vpon the house top, and his stuffe in the house, let him not come downe to take it away: and he that is in the field, let him likewise not returne backe.
text.NABRE: On that day, a person who is on the housetop and whose belongings are in the house must not go down to get them, and likewise a person in the field must not return to what was left behind.
text.NRSV: On that day, anyone on the housetop who has belongings in the house must not come down to take them away; and likewise anyone in the field must not turn back.
text.DRB: In that hour he that shall be in the house-top, and his vessels in the house, let him not go down to take them up: & he that is in the field, in like manner let him not return back.

text.KJV: Remember Lots wife.
text.NABRE: Remember the wife of Lot.
text.NRSV: Remember Lot’s wife.
text.DRB: Be mindful of Lot's wife.

text.KJV: Whosoeuer shall seeke to saue his life, shall lose it, and whosoeuer shall lose his life, shall preserue it.
text.NABRE: Whoever seeks to preserve his life will lose it, but whoever loses it will save it.
text.NRSV: Those who try to make their life secure will lose it, but those who lose their life will keep it.
text.DRB: Whosoever seeketh to save his life, shall lose it: and Whosoever doth lose the same, shall quicken it.

text.KJV: I tell you, in that night there shall be two men in one bed; the one shal be taken, the other shall be left.
text.NABRE: I tell you, on that night there will be two people in one bed; one will be taken, the other left.
text.NRSV: I tell you, on that night there will be two in one bed; one will be taken and the other left.
text.DRB: Isay to you, in that night there shall be two in one bed: the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left:

text.KJV: Two women shall bee grinding together; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
text.NABRE: And there will be two women grinding meal together; one will be taken, the other left.”
text.NRSV: There will be two women grinding meal together; one will be taken and the other left.”
text.DRB: two women shall be grinding together: the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left. two in the field: the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left.

text.KJV: Two men shall be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
text.DRB: They answering say to him: Where Lord?

text.KJV: And they answered, and said vnto him, Where, Lord? And he said vnto them, Wheresoeuer the body is, thither will the Eagles be gathered together.
text.NABRE: They said to him in reply, “Where, Lord?” He said to them, “Where the body is, there also the vultures will gather.”
text.NRSV: Then they asked him, “Where, Lord?” He said to them, “Where the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.”
text.DRB: Who said to them: Wheresoever the body shall be, thither will the eagles also be gathered together.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus told them a parable about their need to pray always and not to lose heart.
text.Rotherham: And he was speaking a parable unto them, as to its being needful for them always to pray, and not be faint-hearted;
text.KJV: And he spake a parable vnto them, to this ende, that men ought alwayes to pray, and not to faint,
text.NABRE: Chapter 18 The Parable of the Persistent Widow. Then he told them a parable about the necessity for them to pray always without becoming weary. He said,
text.DRB: And he spake also a parable to them that it behoveth always to pray, & not to be weary,

text.NRSV: He said, “In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor had respect for people.
text.Rotherham: saying—A certain judge, there was in a certain city,—having, for God, no reverence, and, for man, no respect.
text.KJV: Saying, There was in a city a Iudge, which feared not God neither regarded man.
text.NABRE: “There was a judge in a certain town who neither feared God nor respected any human being.
text.DRB: saying: There was a certain judge in a certain city, which feared not God, and of man made no account.

text.NRSV: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him and saying, ‘Grant me justice against my opponent.’
text.Rotherham: And, a widow, there was, in that city; and she kept coming unto him, saying—Vindicate me from mine adversary!
text.KJV: And there was a widowe in that citie, and she came vnto him, saying, Auenge me of mine aduersarie:
text.NABRE: And a widow in that town used to come to him and say, ‘Render a just decision for me against my adversary.’
text.DRB: And there was a certain widow in that city, and she came to him, saying: Revenge me of mine adversary.

text.NRSV: For a while he refused; but later he said to himself, ‘Though I have no fear of God and no respect for anyone,
text.Rotherham: And he was unwilling for a time; but, after these things, he said within himself—Although, neither God, I reverence, nor man, I respect,
text.KJV: And hee would not for a while. But afterward he said within himselfe, Though I feare not God, nor regard man,
text.NABRE: For a long time the judge was unwilling, but eventually he thought, ‘While it is true that I neither fear God nor respect any human being,
text.DRB: And he would not of a long time. But afterward he said within himself: Although I fear not God, nor make account of man,

text.NRSV: yet because this widow keeps bothering me, I will grant her justice, so that she may not finally wear me out by her continual coming.’”
text.Rotherham: Yet, if only because this widow annoyeth me, I will vindicate her,—lest, persistently coming, she wholly wear me out.
text.KJV: Yet because this widow troubleth me, I will auenge her, lest by her continuall comming, she wearie me.
text.NABRE: because this widow keeps bothering me I shall deliver a just decision for her lest she finally come and strike me.’”
text.DRB: yet because this widow is importune upon me, I will revenge her, lest at the last she come and defame me.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, “Listen to what the unjust judge says.
text.Rotherham: And the Lord said—Hear ye what, the unrighteous judge saith;—
text.KJV: And the Lord said, Heare what the vniust iudge saith.
text.NABRE: The Lord said, “Pay attention to what the dishonest judge says.
text.DRB: And our Lord said: Hear what the judge of iniquity sayeth.

text.NRSV: And will not God grant justice to his chosen ones who cry to him day and night? Will he delay long in helping them?
text.Rotherham: And shall, God, in any wise not execute the vindication of his chosen ones, who are crying out to him day and night, although he beareth long with regard to them?
text.KJV: And shall not God auenge his owne elect, which crie day and night vnto him, thogh he beare long with them?
text.NABRE: Will not God then secure the rights of his chosen ones who call out to him day and night? Will he be slow to answer them?
text.DRB: And will not God revenge his elect that cry to him day and night: and will he have patience in them?

text.NRSV: I tell you, he will quickly grant justice to them. And yet, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?”
text.Rotherham: I tell you—He will execute their vindication, quickly! nevertheless, though, the Son of Man, do come, will he, after all, find the faith on the earth?
text.KJV: I tell you that he wil auenge them speedily. Neuerthelesse, when the Son of man commeth, shall hee find faith on the earth?
text.NABRE: I tell you, he will see to it that justice is done for them speedily. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?”
text.DRB: Isay to you that he will quickly revenge them. But yet the Son of man coming, shall he find trow you, faith in the earth?

text.NRSV: He also told this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous and regarded others with contempt:
text.Rotherham: And he spake, even unto certain who were confident in themselves that they were righteous, and were despising the rest, this parable:—
text.KJV: And he spake this parable vnto certaine which trusted in themselues that they were righteous, & despised other:
text.NABRE: The Parable of the Pharisee and the Tax Collector. He then addressed this parable to those who were convinced of their own righteousness and despised everyone else.
text.DRB: And he said also to certain that trusted in themselves as just, and despised others, this parable:

text.NRSV: “Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
text.Rotherham: Two men, went up into the temple to pray, one, a Pharisee, and, the other, a tax-collector.
text.KJV: Two men went vp into the Temple to pray, the one a Pharisee, and the other a Publicane.
text.NABRE: “Two people went up to the temple area to pray; one was a Pharisee and the other was a tax collector.
text.DRB: Two men went up into the Temple to pray: the one a Pharisee, and the other a Publican.

text.NRSV: The Pharisee, standing by himself, was praying thus, ‘God, I thank you that I am not like other people: thieves, rogues, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.
text.Rotherham: The Pharisee, taking his stand, these things unto himself was praying: O God! I thank thee, that I am not like the rest of men,—extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or, even as this tax-collector;
text.KJV: The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himselfe, God, I thank thee, that I am not as other men are, extortioners, vniust, adulterers, or euen as this Publicane.
text.NABRE: The Pharisee took up his position and spoke this prayer to himself, ‘O God, I thank you that I am not like the rest of humanity—greedy, dishonest, adulterous—or even like this tax collector.
text.DRB: The Pharisee standing, prayed thus with himself: God, I give thee thanks that I am not as the rest of men, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, as also this Publican.

text.NRSV: I fast twice a week; I give a tenth of all my income.’
text.Rotherham: I fast twice in the week, I give a tenth of whatsoever things I gain!
text.KJV: I fast twise in the weeke, I giue tithes of all that I possesse.
text.NABRE: I fast twice a week, and I pay tithes on my whole income.’
text.DRB: I fast twice in a week: I give tithes of all that I possess.

text.NRSV: But the tax collector, standing far off, would not even look up to heaven, but was beating his breast and saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, a sinner!’
text.Rotherham: But, the tax-collector, afar off, standing,—would not so much as lift up, his eyes, unto heaven, but kept smiting his own breast saying—O God! be propitiated unto me, the sinner!
text.KJV: And the Publicane standing afarre off, would not lift vp so much as his eyes vnto heauen: but smote vpon his breast, saying, God me mercifull to mee a sinner.
text.NABRE: But the tax collector stood off at a distance and would not even raise his eyes to heaven but beat his breast and prayed, ‘O God, be merciful to me a sinner.’
text.DRB: And the Publican standing a far off would not so much as lift up his eyes toward Heaven; but he knocked his breast, saying: God be merciful to me a sinner.

text.NRSV: I tell you, this man went down to his home justified rather than the other; for all who exalt themselves will be humbled, but all who humble themselves will be exalted.”
text.Rotherham: I tell you—This one went down justified, unto his house, rather than that one; because, every one who exalteth himself, shall be abased, but, he that abaseth himself, shall be exalted?
text.KJV: I tell you, this man went downe to his house iustified rather then the other: For euery one that exalteth himselfe, shall be abased: and hee that humbleth himselfe, shall be exalted.
text.NABRE: I tell you, the latter went home justified, not the former; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and the one who humbles himself will be exalted.”
text.DRB: Isay to you, this man went down into his house justified more than he: because every one that exalteth himself, shall be humbled; and he that humbleth himself, shall be exalted.

text.NRSV: People were bringing even infants to him that he might touch them; and when the disciples saw it, they sternly ordered them not to do it.
text.Rotherham: And they were bringing unto him, even the babes, that he might touch, them; but the disciples, seeing it, began to rebuke them.
text.KJV: And they brought vnto him also infants, that he would touch them: but when his disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
text.NABRE: Saying on Children and the Kingdom. People were bringing even infants to him that he might touch them, and when the disciples saw this, they rebuked them.
text.DRB: And they brought unto him infants also, that he might touch them. Which thing when the Disciples saw, they rebuked them.

text.NRSV: But Jesus called for them and said, “Let the little children come to me, and do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of God belongs.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, called them near, saying—Suffer, the children, to be coming unto me, and do not hinder them; for, of such, is the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: But Iesus called them vnto him, and said, Suffer litle children to come vnto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdome of God.
text.NABRE: Jesus, however, called the children to himself and said, “Let the children come to me and do not prevent them; for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
text.DRB: But Jesus calling them together, said: Suffer children to come unto me, and forbid them not, for the Kingdom of Heaven is for such.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will never enter it.”
text.Rotherham: Verily I say unto you—Whosoever shall not welcome the kingdom of God, as a child, in nowise shall enter thereinto.
text.KJV: Uerely I say vnto you, whosoeuer shall not receiue the kingdome of God as a litle child, shal in no wise enter therein.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, whoever does not accept the kingdom of God like a child will not enter it.”
text.DRB: Amen I say to you: Whosoever receiveth not the Kingdom of God as a child, shall not enter into it.

text.NRSV: A certain ruler asked him, “Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
text.Rotherham: And a certain, ruler, questioned him, saying—Good Teacher! by doing what, shall I inherit life age-abiding?
text.KJV: And a certaine ruler asked him, saying, Good master, what shall I doe to inherit eternall life?
text.NABRE: The Rich Official. An official asked him this question, “Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
text.DRB: And a certain Prince asked him, saying: Good Master, by doing what, shall I possess everlasting life?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good but God alone.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus said to him—Why, callest thou me, good? None, is good, save one—God.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto him, Why callest thou mee good? None is good saue one, that is God.
text.NABRE: Jesus answered him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good but God alone.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: Why doest thou call me good? None is good but only God.

text.NRSV: You know the commandments: ‘You shall not commit adultery; You shall not murder; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness; Honor your father and mother.’”
text.Rotherham: The commandments, thou knowest:—Do not commit adultery, Do not commit murder, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness,—Honour thy father and mother,
text.KJV: Thou knowest the commaundements, Doe not commit adulterie, Doe not kill, Doe not steale, Doe not beare false witnesse, Honour thy father and thy mother.
text.NABRE: You know the commandments, ‘You shall not commit adultery; you shall not kill; you shall not steal; you shall not bear false witness; honor your father and your mother.’”
text.DRB: Thou knowest the commandments: Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness, Honour thy father & mother.

text.NRSV: He replied, “I have kept all these since my youth.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—All these things, have I kept from my youth.
text.KJV: And he said, All these haue I kept from my youth vp.
text.NABRE: And he replied, “All of these I have observed from my youth.”
text.DRB: Who said: All these things have I kept from my youth.

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, “There is still one thing lacking. Sell all that you own and distribute the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, hearing, said unto him—Yet one thing, unto thee, is lacking: Whatsoever thou hast, sell, and distribute unto the destitute,—and thou shalt have treasure in [the] heavens; and come! be following me.
text.KJV: Now when Iesus heard these things, hee said vnto him, Yet lackest thou one thing: Sell all that thou hast, and distribute vnto the poore, and thou shalt haue treasure in heauen, and come, follow me.
text.NABRE: When Jesus heard this he said to him, “There is still one thing left for you: sell all that you have and distribute it to the poor, and you will have a treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
text.DRB: Which Jesus hearing, said to him: Yet one thing thou lackest: Sell all that ever thou hast, & give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in Heaven: and come follow me.

text.NRSV: But when he heard this, he became sad; for he was very rich.
text.Rotherham: But, he, hearing these things, became, encompassed with grief; for he was rich exceedingly.
text.KJV: And when he heard this, he was very sorowfull, for he was very rich.
text.NABRE: But when he heard this he became quite sad, for he was very rich.
text.DRB: He hearing these things, was stroken sad: because he was very rich.

text.NRSV: Jesus looked at him and said, “How hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, beholding him, said—With what difficulty, shall, they who have money, enter, into the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: And when Iesus saw that hee was very sorrowfull, he said, How hardly shal they that haue riches, enter into the kingdome of God?
text.NABRE: On Riches and Renunciation. Jesus looked at him [now sad] and said, “How hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!
text.DRB: And Jesus seeing him stroken sad, said: How hardly shall they that have money enter into the Kingdom of God?

text.NRSV: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: For it is, easier, for a camel, to enter, through the eye of a needle, than for, a rich man, to enter, into the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: For it is easier for a camel to goe thorow a needles eye, then for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
text.NABRE: For it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.DRB: For it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?”
text.Rotherham: And they who heard said—Who, then, can be saved?
text.KJV: And they that heard it, said, Who then can be saued?
text.NABRE: Those who heard this said, “Then who can be saved?”
text.DRB: And they that heard, said: And who can be saved?

text.NRSV: He replied, “What is impossible for mortals is possible for God.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—The things impossible with men, are, possible with God.
text.KJV: And he said, The things which are vnpossible with men, are possible with God.
text.NABRE: And he said, “What is impossible for human beings is possible for God.”
text.DRB: He said to them: The things that are impossible with men, are possible with God.

text.NRSV: Then Peter said, “Look, we have left our homes and followed you.”
text.Rotherham: And Peter said—Lo! we, have left our own possessions, and followed thee!
text.KJV: Then Peter said, Loe, we haue left all, and followed thee.
text.NABRE: Then Peter said, “We have given up our possessions and followed you.”
text.DRB: And Peter said: Lo, we have left all things, and have followed thee.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Truly I tell you, there is no one who has left house or wife or brothers or parents or children, for the sake of the kingdom of God,
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Verily, I say unto you—No one, is there, who hath left—house, or wife, or brethren, or parents, or children,—for the sake of the kingdom of God;
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Uerily, I say vnto you, there is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children, for the kingdome of Gods sake,
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Amen, I say to you, there is no one who has given up house or wife or brothers or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of God
text.DRB: Who said to them: Amen I say to you, there is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children for the Kingdom of God,

text.NRSV: who will not get back very much more in this age, and in the age to come eternal life.”
text.Rotherham: who shall in anywise not receive manifold in this season, and, in the age that is coming, life age-abiding.
text.KJV: Who shall not receiue manifold more in this present time, and in the world to come life euerlasting.
text.NABRE: who will not receive [back] an overabundant return in this present age and eternal life in the age to come.” The Third Prediction of the Passion.
text.DRB: and shall not receive much more in this time, and in the world to come life everlasting.

text.NRSV: Then he took the twelve aside and said to them, “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written about the Son of Man by the prophets will be accomplished.
text.Rotherham: And, taking aside the twelve, he said unto them—Lo! we are going up unto Jerusalem, and all the things will be finished, which have been written through means of the prophets, respecting the Son of Man;
text.KJV: ¶ Then hee tooke vnto him the twelue, and said vnto them, Behold, we goe vp to Hierusalem, and al things that are written by the Prophets concerning the sonne of man, shall be accomplished.
text.NABRE: Then he took the Twelve aside and said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem and everything written by the prophets about the Son of Man will be fulfilled.
text.DRB: And Jesus took the Twelve, and said to them: Behold we go up to Jerusalem, and all things shall be consummate which were written by the Prophets of the Son of man.

text.NRSV: For he will be handed over to the Gentiles; and he will be mocked and insulted and spat upon.
text.Rotherham: For he will be delivered up unto the nations, and be mocked, and insulted, and spit upon,—
text.KJV: For he shall be deliuered vnto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and spitefully intreated, and spitted on:
text.NABRE: He will be handed over to the Gentiles and he will be mocked and insulted and spat upon;
text.DRB: For he shall be delivered to the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and scourged, and spit upon:

text.NRSV: After they have flogged him, they will kill him, and on the third day he will rise again.”
text.Rotherham: and, having scourged him, they will slay him, and, on the third day, will, he, arise.
text.KJV: And they shall scourge him, and put him to death, and the third day he shall rise againe.
text.NABRE: and after they have scourged him they will kill him, but on the third day he will rise.”
text.DRB: and after they have scourged him, they will kill him, and the third day he shall rise again.

text.NRSV: But they understood nothing about all these things; in fact, what he said was hidden from them, and they did not grasp what was said.
text.Rotherham: And, they, not one of these things, understood, and this saying was hidden from them, and the could not comprehend the things spoken.
text.KJV: And they vnderstood none of these things: and this saying was hid from them, neither knew they the things which were spoken.
text.NABRE: But they understood nothing of this; the word remained hidden from them and they failed to comprehend what he said. The Healing of the Blind Beggar.
text.DRB: And they understood none of these things, and this word was hid from them, and they understood not the things that were said.

text.NRSV: As he approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he was drawing near unto Jericho, a certain blind man, was sitting beside the road, begging.
text.KJV: ¶ And it came to passe, that as he was come nigh vnto Iericho, a certaine blinde man sate by the way side, begging,
text.NABRE: Now as he approached Jericho a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging,
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when he drew nigh to Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the way, begging.

text.NRSV: When he heard a crowd going by, he asked what was happening.
text.Rotherham: And. hearkening unto a multitude moving along, he enquired, what this, might be.
text.KJV: And hearing the multitude passe by, he asked what it meant.
text.NABRE: and hearing a crowd going by, he inquired what was happening.
text.DRB: And when he heard the multitude passing by, he asked what this should be.

text.NRSV: They told him, “Jesus of Nazareth is passing by.”
text.Rotherham: And they told him—Jesus of Nazareth, is passing by!
text.KJV: And they tolde him that Iesus of Nazareth passeth by.
text.NABRE: They told him, “Jesus of Nazareth is passing by.”
text.DRB: And they told him that Jesus of Nazareth passed by.

text.NRSV: Then he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And he cried aloud, saying—Jesus, son of David! have mercy upon me!
text.KJV: And he cried, saying, Iesus thou sonne of Dauid, haue mercie on me.
text.NABRE: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
text.DRB: And he cried saying: Jesus son of David, have mercy upon me.

text.NRSV: Those who were in front sternly ordered him to be quiet; but he shouted even more loudly, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And, they that were going before, began to rebuke him, that he might hold his peace. But, he, by so much the more, was crying out—O Son of David! have mercy upon me!
text.KJV: And they which went before, rebuked him, that hee should holde his peace: but hee cried so much the more, Thou Sonne of Dauid, haue mercie on mee.
text.NABRE: The people walking in front rebuked him, telling him to be silent, but he kept calling out all the more, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
text.DRB: And they that went before, rebuked him, that he should hold his peace. But he cried much more, Son of David have mercy upon me.

text.NRSV: Jesus stood still and ordered the man to be brought to him; and when he came near, he asked him,
text.Rotherham: And, standing still, Jesus commanded him to be led unto him; and, when he had drawn near, he questioned him—
text.KJV: And Iesus stood and commanded him to be brought vnto him: and when he was come neere, he asked him,
text.NABRE: Then Jesus stopped and ordered that he be brought to him; and when he came near, Jesus asked him,
text.DRB: And Jesus standing commanded him to be brought unto him. And when he was come near, he asked him,

text.NRSV: “What do you want me to do for you?” He said, “Lord, let me see again.”
text.Rotherham: What desirest thou, I should do unto thee? And, he, said—Lord! …that I may recover sight!
text.KJV: Saying, What wilt thou that I shall doe vnto thee? And he said, Lord, that I may receiue my sight.
text.NABRE: “What do you want me to do for you?” He replied, “Lord, please let me see.”
text.DRB: saying: What wilt thou that I do to thee? but he said: Lord, that I may see.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight; your faith has saved you.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Recover sight! Thy faith, hath saved thee.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto him, Receiue thy sight, thy faith hath saued thee.
text.NABRE: Jesus told him, “Have sight; your faith has saved you.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: Do thou see; thy faith hath made thee whole.

text.NRSV: Immediately he regained his sight and followed him, glorifying God; and all the people, when they saw it, praised God.
text.Rotherham: And, instantly, he recovered sight, and began to follow him, glorifying God. And, all the people, beholding, gave praise unto God.
text.KJV: And immediately he receiued his sight, and followed him, glorifying God: and all the people when they saw it, gaue praise vnto God.
text.NABRE: He immediately received his sight and followed him, giving glory to God. When they saw this, all the people gave praise to God.
text.DRB: And forthwith he saw, and followed him, magnifying God. And all the people as they saw it, gave praise to God.

text.NRSV: He entered Jericho and was passing through it.
text.Rotherham: And, having entered, he was passing through Jericho;
text.KJV: And Iesus entred, and passed thorow Iericho.
text.NABRE: Chapter 19 Zacchaeus the Tax Collector. He came to Jericho and intended to pass through the town.
text.DRB: And entering in, he walked through Jericho.

text.NRSV: A man was there named Zacchaeus; he was a chief tax collector and was rich.
text.Rotherham: and lo! a man, by name called Zacchaeus, and, he, was a chief tax-collector, and [he] was rich.
text.KJV: And behold, there was a man named Zacheus, which was the cheefe among the Publicanes, and he was rich.
text.NABRE: Now a man there named Zacchaeus, who was a chief tax collector and also a wealthy man,
text.DRB: And behold a man named Zachæus: and this was a Prince of the Publicans, and he rich.

text.NRSV: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but on account of the crowd he could not, because he was short in stature.
text.Rotherham: And he was seeking to see Jesus, what sort of man he was, and could not for the multitude, because, in stature, he was, small.
text.KJV: And he sought to see Iesus who he was, and could not for the prease, because he was litle of stature.
text.NABRE: was seeking to see who Jesus was; but he could not see him because of the crowd, for he was short in stature.
text.DRB: And he sought to see Jesus what he was, and he could not for the multitude, because he was little of stature.

text.NRSV: So he ran ahead and climbed a sycamore tree to see him, because he was going to pass that way.
text.Rotherham: And, running forward unto the front, he got up a sycamore-tree, that he might see him; for, by that way, was he about to pass.
text.KJV: And he ranne before, and climed vp into a sycomore tree to see him, for he was to passe that way.
text.NABRE: So he ran ahead and climbed a sycamore tree in order to see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
text.DRB: And running before, he went up into a sycamore tree that he might see him: because he was to pass by it.

text.NRSV: When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and said to him, “Zacchaeus, hurry and come down; for I must stay at your house today.”
text.Rotherham: And, as he came up to the place, looking up, Jesus said unto him—Zacchaeus! make haste, and come down; For, to-day, in thy house, I must needs abide.
text.KJV: And when Iesus came to the place, he looked vp and saw him, and said vnto him, Zacheus, make haste, & come downe, for to day I must abide at thy house.
text.NABRE: When he reached the place, Jesus looked up and said to him, “Zacchaeus, come down quickly, for today I must stay at your house.”
text.DRB: And when he was come to the place, Jesus looking up, saw him, and said to him: Zachæus come down in haste: because this day I must abide in thy house.

text.NRSV: So he hurried down and was happy to welcome him.
text.Rotherham: And he made haste, and came down, and received him joyfully.
text.KJV: And he made haste, and came downe, and receiued him ioyfully.
text.NABRE: And he came down quickly and received him with joy.
text.DRB: And he in haste came down, and received him rejoicing.

text.NRSV: All who saw it began to grumble and said, “He has gone to be the guest of one who is a sinner.”
text.Rotherham: And all, when they beheld, began to murmur, saying—With a sinful man, hath he gone in to lodge!
text.KJV: And when they saw it, they all murmured, saying, That he was gone to be guest with a man that is a sinner.
text.NABRE: When they all saw this, they began to grumble, saying, “He has gone to stay at the house of a sinner.”
text.DRB: And when all saw it, they murmured saying, that he turned in, to a man that was a sinner.

text.NRSV: Zacchaeus stood there and said to the Lord, “Look, half of my possessions, Lord, I will give to the poor; and if I have defrauded anyone of anything, I will pay back four times as much.”
text.Rotherham: But, taking his stand, Zacchaeus said unto the Lord—Lo! the half of my possessions, Lord, unto the destitute, I give; and, if, from anyone, I have taken aught by false accusation, I give back fourfold.
text.KJV: And Zacheus stood, and said vnto the Lord, Behold, Lord, the halfe of my goods I giue to the poore, & if I haue taken any thing from any man by false accusation, I restore him foure fold.
text.NABRE: But Zacchaeus stood there and said to the Lord, “Behold, half of my possessions, Lord, I shall give to the poor, and if I have extorted anything from anyone I shall repay it four times over.”
text.DRB: But Zachæus standing, said to our Lord: Behold the half of my goods, Lord, I give to the poor: and if I have defrauded any man of any thing, I restore fourfold.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “Today salvation has come to this house, because he too is a son of Abraham.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto him—This day, salvation, unto this house, hath come,—for that, he too, is, a son of Abraham;
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto him, This day is saluation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is the sonne of Abraham.
text.NABRE: And Jesus said to him, “Today salvation has come to this house because this man too is a descendant of Abraham.
text.DRB: Jesus said to him: That this day salvation is made to this house: because that he also is the son of Abraham.

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man came to seek out and to save the lost.”
text.Rotherham: For the Son of Man came, to seek and to save what was lost.
text.KJV: For the sonne of man is come to seeke, and to saue that which was lost.
text.NABRE: For the Son of Man has come to seek and to save what was lost.” The Parable of the Ten Gold Coins.
text.DRB: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.

text.NRSV: As they were listening to this, he went on to tell a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and because they supposed that the kingdom of God was to appear immediately.
text.Rotherham: And, because they were hearing these things, he added and spake a parable, because of his being near Jerusalem, and their supposing that, instantly, was the kingdom of God to shine forth.
text.KJV: And as they heard these things, he added, and spake a parable, because he was nigh to Hierusalem, and because they thought that the kingdome of God should immediately appeare.
text.NABRE: While they were listening to him speak, he proceeded to tell a parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought that the kingdom of God would appear there immediately.
text.DRB: They hearing these things, he added and spake a parable, for that he was nigh to Jerusalem, and because they thought that forthwith the Kingdom of God should be manifested.

text.NRSV: So he said, “A nobleman went to a distant country to get royal power for himself and then return.
text.Rotherham: He said, therefore—A certain man, of noble birth, went into a country far away, to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.
text.KJV: He said therefore, A certaine noble man went into a farre countrey, to receiue for himselfe a kingdome, and to returne.
text.NABRE: So he said, “A nobleman went off to a distant country to obtain the kingship for himself and then to return.
text.DRB: He said therefore: A certain noble man went into a far country to take to himself a kingdom, and to return.

text.NRSV: He summoned ten of his slaves, and gave them ten pounds, and said to them, ‘Do business with these until I come back.’
text.Rotherham: And, calling ten servants of his own, he gave unto them ten minas, and said unto them—Do business, till I come.
text.KJV: And hee called his ten seruants, and deliuered them ten pounds, and said vnto them, Occupy till I come.
text.NABRE: He called ten of his servants and gave them ten gold coins and told them, ‘Engage in trade with these until I return.’
text.DRB: And calling his ten servants, he gave them ten pounds, and said to them: Occupy till I come.

text.NRSV: But the citizens of his country hated him and sent a delegation after him, saying, ‘We do not want this man to rule over us.’
text.Rotherham: But, his citizens, hated him, and sent off an embassy after him, saying—We desire not, this, man, to be made king over us!
text.KJV: But his citizens hated him, and sent a message after him, saying, We wil not haue this man to reigne ouer vs.
text.NABRE: His fellow citizens, however, despised him and sent a delegation after him to announce, ‘We do not want this man to be our king.’
text.DRB: And his citizens hated him: and they sent a legacy after him, saying: We will not have this man reign over us.

text.NRSV: When he returned, having received royal power, he ordered these slaves, to whom he had given the money, to be summoned so that he might find out what they had gained by trading.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when he returned, having received the kingdom, that he bade be called unto him these servants, to whom he had given the silver, that he might take note, what business they had done.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that when he was returned, hauing receiued the kingdome, then hee commaunded these seruants to be called vnto him, to whom he had giuen the money, that hee might know how much euery man had gained by trading.
text.NABRE: But when he returned after obtaining the kingship, he had the servants called, to whom he had given the money, to learn what they had gained by trading.
text.DRB: And it came to pass after he returned, having received his kingdom: and he commanded his servants to be called, to whom he gave the money; that he might know how much every man had gained by occupying.

text.NRSV: The first came forward and said, ‘Lord, your pound has made ten more pounds.’
text.Rotherham: And the first came near, saying—Lord! thy mina, hath made, ten minas.
text.KJV: Then came the first, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained ten pounds.
text.NABRE: The first came forward and said, ‘Sir, your gold coin has earned ten additional ones.’
text.DRB: And the first came, saying: Lord thy pound hath gotten ten pounds.

text.NRSV: He said to him, ‘Well done, good slave! Because you have been trustworthy in a very small thing, take charge of ten cities.’
text.Rotherham: And he said to him—Well done! good servant. Because, in a very small thing, thou hast been, faithful, have thou authority over, ten cities.
text.KJV: And he said vnto him, Well, thou good seruant: because thou hast bene faithfull in a very little, haue thou authoritie ouer ten cities.
text.NABRE: He replied, ‘Well done, good servant! You have been faithful in this very small matter; take charge of ten cities.’
text.DRB: And he said to him: Well fare thee good servant, because thou hast been faithful in a little, thou shalt have power over ten cities.

text.NRSV: Then the second came, saying, ‘Lord, your pound has made five pounds.’
text.Rotherham: And the second came, saying—Thy mina, lord, hath made five minas.
text.KJV: And the second came, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained fiue pounds.
text.NABRE: Then the second came and reported, ‘Your gold coin, sir, has earned five more.’
text.DRB: And the second came saying: Lord, thy pound hath made five pounds.

text.NRSV: He said to him, ‘And you, rule over five cities.’
text.Rotherham: And he said, to him also, And, thou, be over five cities.
