text.KJV: And hee said likewise to him, Bee thou also ouer fiue cities.
text.NABRE: And to this servant too he said, ‘You, take charge of five cities.’
text.DRB: And he said to him: And be thou over five cities.

text.NRSV: Then the other came, saying, ‘Lord, here is your pound. I wrapped it up in a piece of cloth,
text.Rotherham: And, the other, came, saying—Lord, lo! thy mina, which I kept lying by in a napkin;
text.KJV: And another came, saying, Lord, behold, here is thy pound which I haue kept layd vp in a napkin:
text.NABRE: Then the other servant came and said, ‘Sir, here is your gold coin; I kept it stored away in a handkerchief,
text.DRB: And another came, saying: Lord, lo here thy pound, which I have had laid up in a napkin.

text.NRSV: for I was afraid of you, because you are a harsh man; you take what you did not deposit, and reap what you did not sow.’
text.Rotherham: For I was afraid of thee, because, a harsh man, thou art,—Thou takest up, what thou layedst not down, and reapest, what thou sowedst not!
text.KJV: For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest vp that thou layedst not downe, and reapest that thou didst not sow.
text.NABRE: for I was afraid of you, because you are a demanding person; you take up what you did not lay down and you harvest what you did not plant.’
text.DRB: For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou didst not set down, and thou reapest that which thou didst not sow.

text.NRSV: He said to him, ‘I will judge you by your own words, you wicked slave! You knew, did you, that I am a harsh man, taking what I did not deposit and reaping what I did not sow?
text.Rotherham: He saith to him—Out of thy mouth, do I judge thee, O wicked servant! Thou knewest that, I, a harsh man, am,—taking up, what I laid not down, and reaping, what I did not sow;
text.KJV: And hee saith vnto him, Out of thine owne mouth will I iudge thee, thou wicked seruant: Thou knewest that I was an austere man, taking vp that I layde not downe, and reaping that I did not sow.
text.NABRE: He said to him, ‘With your own words I shall condemn you, you wicked servant. You knew I was a demanding person, taking up what I did not lay down and harvesting what I did not plant;
text.DRB: He saith to him: By thine own mouth I judge thee, naughty servant. Thou didst know that I am an austere man, taking up that I set not down, and reaping that which I sowed not:

text.NRSV: Why then did you not put my money into the bank? Then when I returned, I could have collected it with interest.’
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, then, didst thou not place my silver upon a [money-changer’s] table, and, I, when I came, with interest might have exacted it?
text.KJV: Wherefore then gauest not thou my money into the bancke, that at my comming I might haue required mine owne with vsury?
text.NABRE: why did you not put my money in a bank? Then on my return I would have collected it with interest.’
text.DRB: and why didst thou not give my money to the bank, and I coming might certes with usury have exacted it?

text.NRSV: He said to the bystanders, ‘Take the pound from him and give it to the one who has ten pounds.’
text.Rotherham: And, unto the by-standers, he said—Take, from him, the mina, and give unto him that hath, the ten minas;—
text.KJV: And he said vnto them that stood by, Take from him the pound, and giue it to him that hath ten pounds.
text.NABRE: And to those standing by he said, ‘Take the gold coin from him and give it to the servant who has ten.’
text.DRB: And he said to them that stood by: Take the pound away from him, and give it to him that hath ten pounds

text.NRSV: (And they said to him, ‘Lord, he has ten pounds!’)
text.Rotherham: And they said to him, Lord! he hath ten minas;—
text.KJV: And they said vnto him, Lord, he hath ten pounds.
text.NABRE: But they said to him, ‘Sir, he has ten gold coins.’
text.DRB: And they said to him: Lord, he hath ten pounds.

text.NRSV: ‘I tell you, to all those who have, more will be given; but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
text.Rotherham: I tell you—Unto everyone that hath, shall be given, whereas, from him that hath not, even what he hath, shall be taken away.
text.KJV: For I say vnto you, That vnto euery one which hath, shalbe giuen, and from him that hath not, euen that hee hath shalbe taken away from him.
text.NABRE: ‘I tell you, to everyone who has, more will be given, but from the one who has not, even what he has will be taken away.
text.DRB: But I say to you, that to every one that hath shall be given: and from him that hath not, that also which he hath shall be taken from him.

text.NRSV: But as for these enemies of mine who did not want me to be king over them—bring them here and slaughter them in my presence.’”
text.Rotherham: But, these mine enemies who desired not that I should be made king over them, bring ye here, and slay them outright before me.
text.KJV: But those mine enemies which would not that I should reigne ouer them, bring hither, and slay them before mee.
text.NABRE: Now as for those enemies of mine who did not want me as their king, bring them here and slay them before me.’” VI. The Teaching Ministry in Jerusalem The Entry into Jerusalem.
text.DRB: But as for those mine enemies that would not have me reign over them, bring them hither; and kill them before me.

text.NRSV: After he had said this, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, having said these things, he was moving on in front, going up into Jerusalem.
text.KJV: ¶ And when he had thus spoken, he went before, ascending vp to Hierusalem.
text.NABRE: After he had said this, he proceeded on his journey up to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And having said these things, he went before ascending to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: When he had come near Bethphage and Bethany, at the place called the Mount of Olives, he sent two of the disciples,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he drew near unto Bethphage, and Bethany, unto the mount which is called the Mount of Olives, he sent off two of his disciples,
text.KJV: And it came to passe when he was come nigh to Bethphage and Bethanie, at the mount called the mount of Oliues, he sent two of his disciples,
text.NABRE: As he drew near to Bethphage and Bethany at the place called the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples.
text.DRB: And it came to pass when he was come nigh to Bethphage and Bethania unto the mount called Olivet, he sent two of his Disciples,

text.NRSV: saying, “Go into the village ahead of you, and as you enter it you will find tied there a colt that has never been ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
text.Rotherham: saying—Go your way unto the opposite village, in which, as ye are entering, ye shall find a colt, bound, whereon, no man, hath, ever sat,—and, having loosed him, bring him.
text.KJV: Saying, Goe ye into the village ouer against you, in the which at your entring ye shall find a Colt tied, whereon yet neuer man sate: loose him, and bring him hither.
text.NABRE: He said, “Go into the village opposite you, and as you enter it you will find a colt tethered on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it here.
text.DRB: saying: Go into the town which is over against, into the which as you enter, you shall find the colt of an ass tied, on which no man ever hath sitten: loose him, and bring him.

text.NRSV: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ just say this, ‘The Lord needs it.’”
text.Rotherham: And, if any one question you—Wherefore are ye loosing him? thus, shall ye say—His Lord, hath, need.
text.KJV: And if any man aske you, Why do ye loose him? Thus shall ye say vnto him, Because the Lord hath neede of him.
text.NABRE: And if anyone should ask you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ you will answer, ‘The Master has need of it.’”
text.DRB: And if any man ask you: Why loose you him? You shall say thus to him: Because our Lord needeth his service.

text.NRSV: So those who were sent departed and found it as he had told them.
text.Rotherham: And they who were sent, departing, found as he had said to them.
text.KJV: And they that were sent, went their way, and found euen as hee had said vnto them.
text.NABRE: So those who had been sent went off and found everything just as he had told them.
text.DRB: And they that were sent, went their ways, and found as he said to them, the colt standing.

text.NRSV: As they were untying the colt, its owners asked them, “Why are you untying the colt?”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were loosing the colt, his lords said unto them—Why are ye loosing the colt?
text.KJV: And as they were loosing the colt, the owners thereof said vnto them, Why loose ye the Colt?
text.NABRE: And as they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, “Why are you untying this colt?”
text.DRB: And when they loosed the colt, the owners thereof said to them: Why loose you the colt?

text.NRSV: They said, “The Lord needs it.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—His Lord, hath, need.
text.KJV: And they said, The Lord hath need of him.
text.NABRE: They answered, “The Master has need of it.”
text.DRB: But they said: Because our Lord hath need of him.

text.NRSV: Then they brought it to Jesus; and after throwing their cloaks on the colt, they set Jesus on it.
text.Rotherham: And they brought him unto Jesus; and, throwing their mantles over the colt, they seated Jesus thereon.
text.KJV: And they brought him to Iesus: and they cast their garments vpon the Colt, and they set Iesus thereon.
text.NABRE: So they brought it to Jesus, threw their cloaks over the colt, and helped Jesus to mount.
text.DRB: And they brought him to Jesus. And casting their garments upon the colt, they set Jesus thereupon.

text.NRSV: As he rode along, people kept spreading their cloaks on the road.
text.Rotherham: And, as he moved along, they were spreading their own mantles in the way.
text.KJV: And as he went, they spread their clothes in the way.
text.NABRE: As he rode along, the people were spreading their cloaks on the road;
text.DRB: And as he went, they spread their garments underneath in the way.

text.NRSV: As he was now approaching the path down from the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to praise God joyfully with a loud voice for all the deeds of power that they had seen,
text.Rotherham: And, when he was drawing near, already, unto the descent of the Mount of Olives, one-and-all the throng of the disciples began joyfully to be praising God with a loud voice, concerning all the mighty works which they had seen;
text.KJV: And when he was come nigh euen now at the descent of the mount of Oliues, the whole multitude of the disciples began to reioyce and praise God with a loud voice, for all the mighty workes that they had seene,
text.NABRE: and now as he was approaching the slope of the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of his disciples began to praise God aloud with joy for all the mighty deeds they had seen.
text.DRB: And when he approached now to the descent of mount-Olivet, all the multitudes of them that descended, began with joy to praise God with a loud voice, for all the miracles that they had seen,

text.NRSV: saying, “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest heaven!”
text.Rotherham: saying—Blessed is he that cometh… The king! In the name of the Lord! In heaven, peace! And glory in the highest!
text.KJV: Saying, Blessed bee the King that commeth in the Name of the Lord, peace in heauen, and glory in the Highest.
text.NABRE: They proclaimed: “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest.”
text.DRB: saying: Blessed is he that cometh King in the name of our Lord, peace in Heaven, and glory on high.

text.NRSV: Some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to him, “Teacher, order your disciples to stop.”
text.Rotherham: And, certain of the Pharisees from the multitude, said unto him—Teacher! rebuke thy disciples.
text.KJV: And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude saide vnto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.
text.NABRE: Some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to him, “Teacher, rebuke your disciples.”
text.DRB: And certain Pharisees of the multitudes said to him: Master, rebuke thy disciples.

text.NRSV: He answered, “I tell you, if these were silent, the stones would shout out.”
text.Rotherham: And he answered and said—I tell you—If, these, shall hold their peace, the stones, will cry out.
text.KJV: And he answered, and said vnto them, I tell you, that if these should holde their peace, the stones would immediatly cry out.
text.NABRE: He said in reply, “I tell you, if they keep silent, the stones will cry out!” The Lament for Jerusalem.
text.DRB: To whom he said: I say to you, that if these hold their peace, the stones shall cry.

text.NRSV: As he came near and saw the city, he wept over it,
text.Rotherham: And, when he drew near, beholding the city, he wept over it, saying—
text.KJV: ¶ And when he was come neere, he beheld the city and wept ouer it,
text.NABRE: As he drew near, he saw the city and wept over it,
text.DRB: And as he drew near, seeing the city, he wept upon it, saying,

text.NRSV: saying, “If you, even you, had only recognized on this day the things that make for peace! But now they are hidden from your eyes.
text.Rotherham: If thou hadst got to know, in this day, even thou, the conditions of peace… But, now, are they hid from thine eyes:
text.KJV: Saying, If thou hadst knowen, euen thou, at least in this thy day, the things which belong vnto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes.
text.NABRE: saying, “If this day you only knew what makes for peace—but now it is hidden from your eyes.
text.DRB: Because if thou also hadst known, and that in this thy day, the things that pertain to thy peace: but now they are hid from thine eyes.

text.NRSV: Indeed, the days will come upon you, when your enemies will set up ramparts around you and surround you, and hem you in on every side.
text.Rotherham: Because days will have come upon thee, that thine enemies will throw around a rampart against thee, and enclose thee; and hem thee in from every side,—
text.KJV: For the dayes shall come vpon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compasse thee round, and keepe thee in on euery side,
text.NABRE: For the days are coming upon you when your enemies will raise a palisade against you; they will encircle you and hem you in on all sides.
text.DRB: For the days shall come upon thee: and thy enemies shall compass thee with a trench, and inclose thee about, and straiten thee on every side,

text.NRSV: They will crush you to the ground, you and your children within you, and they will not leave within you one stone upon another; because you did not recognize the time of your visitation from God.”
text.Rotherham: And will level thee with the ground, and thy children within thee, and will not leave, stone on stone, within thee: because thou didst not get to know the season of thy visitation.
text.KJV: And shall lay thee euen with the ground, and thy children within thee: and they shall not leaue in thee one stone vpon another, because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.
text.NABRE: They will smash you to the ground and your children within you, and they will not leave one stone upon another within you because you did not recognize the time of your visitation.”
text.DRB: and beat thee flat to the ground, and thy children that are in thee: and they shall not leave in thee a stone upon a stone, because thou hast not known the time of thy visitation.

text.NRSV: Then he entered the temple and began to drive out those who were selling things there,
text.Rotherham: And, entering into the temple, he began to be casting out them who were selling;
text.KJV: And he went into the Temple, and began to cast out them that solde therein, and them that bought,
text.NABRE: The Cleansing of the Temple. Then Jesus entered the temple area and proceeded to drive out those who were selling things,
text.DRB: And entering into the Temple, he began to cast out the sellers therein and the buyers,

text.NRSV: and he said, “It is written, ‘My house shall be a house of prayer’; but you have made it a den of robbers.”
text.Rotherham: saying unto them—It is written, And, my house, shall be, a house of prayer; but, ye, have made, it, a den of robbers.
text.KJV: Saying vnto them, It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye haue made it a denne of theeues.
text.NABRE: saying to them, “It is written, ‘My house shall be a house of prayer, but you have made it a den of thieves.’”
text.DRB: saying to them: It is written, That my house is the house of prayer. But you have made it a den of thieves.

text.NRSV: Every day he was teaching in the temple. The chief priests, the scribes, and the leaders of the people kept looking for a way to kill him;
text.Rotherham: And he was teaching day by day in the temple; but, the High-priests and the Scribes, also the chiefs of the people, were seeking to destroy him,—
text.KJV: And he taught daily in the Temple. But the chiefe Priestes and the Scribes, and the chiefe of the people sought to destroy him,
text.NABRE: And every day he was teaching in the temple area. The chief priests, the scribes, and the leaders of the people, meanwhile, were seeking to put him to death,
text.DRB: And he was teaching daily in the Temple. And the chief Priests and the Scribes and the Princes of the people sought to destroy him:

text.NRSV: but they did not find anything they could do, for all the people were spellbound by what they heard.
text.Rotherham: and could not find what they might do, for, the people, one and all, were hanging upon him, as they hearkened.
text.KJV: And could not finde what they might doe: for all the people were very attentiue to heare him.
text.NABRE: but they could find no way to accomplish their purpose because all the people were hanging on his words.
text.DRB: and they found not what to do to him. For all the people was suspense, hearing him.

text.NRSV: One day, as he was teaching the people in the temple and telling the good news, the chief priests and the scribes came with the elders
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on one of the days, as he was teaching the people in the temple, and telling the good tidings, that the High-priests and the Scribes, with the Elders, came upon him,—
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that on one of those dayes, as he taught the people in the Temple, and preached the Gospel, the chiefe Priests and the Scribes came vpon him, with the Elders,
text.NABRE: Chapter 20 The Authority of Jesus Questioned. One day as he was teaching the people in the temple area and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests and scribes, together with the elders, approached him
text.DRB: And it came to pass in one of the days, when he was teaching the people in the Temple & evangelizing, the chief Priests & the Scribes with the Ancients assembled,

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Tell us, by what authority are you doing these things? Who is it who gave you this authority?”
text.Rotherham: and spake, saying, unto him—Tell us, by what authority, these things, thou art doing, and, who, is it, that gave thee this authority.
text.KJV: And spake vnto him, saying, Tell vs, by what authoritie doest thou these things? or who is hee that gaue thee this authoritie?
text.NABRE: and said to him, “Tell us, by what authority are you doing these things? Or who is the one who gave you this authority?”
text.DRB: and spake saying to him, Tell us, in what power doest thou these things? Or, who is he that hath given thee this power?

text.NRSV: He answered them, “I will also ask you a question, and you tell me:
text.Rotherham: And answering, he said unto them—I too, will question, you, as to a matter,—and tell me!
text.KJV: And hee answered, and said vnto them, I will also aske you one thing, and answere me.
text.NABRE: He said to them in reply, “I shall ask you a question. Tell me,
text.DRB: And Jesus answering, said to them: I also will ask you one word. Answer me,

text.NRSV: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin?”
text.Rotherham: The immersion of John, Of heaven, was it, or of men?
text.KJV: The Baptisme of Iohn, was it from heauen, or of men?
text.NABRE: was John’s baptism of heavenly or of human origin?”
text.DRB: The baptism of John was it from Heaven, or of men?

text.NRSV: They discussed it with one another, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why did you not believe him?’
text.Rotherham: And they deliberated together by themselves, saying—If we say, Of heaven, he will say, Wherefore did ye not believe him?
text.KJV: And they reasoned with themselues, saying, If wee shall say, From heauen, he will say, Why then beleeued yee him not?
text.NABRE: They discussed this among themselves, and said, “If we say, ‘Of heavenly origin,’ he will say, ‘Why did you not believe him?’
text.DRB: But they thought within themselves, saying: That if we say, From Heaven, he will say: Why then did you not believe him?

text.NRSV: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ all the people will stone us; for they are convinced that John was a prophet.”
text.Rotherham: But, if we say, Of men, the people, one and all, will stone us, for, persuaded, they are, that, John, was, a prophet.
text.KJV: But and if we say, Of men, all the people will stone vs: for they be perswaded that Iohn was a Prophet.
text.NABRE: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ then all the people will stone us, for they are convinced that John was a prophet.”
text.DRB: But if we say, of men, the whole people will stone us: for they are certain that John is a Prophet.

text.NRSV: So they answered that they did not know where it came from.
text.Rotherham: And they answered, that they knew not whence.
text.KJV: And they answered, that they could not tell whence it was.
text.NABRE: So they answered that they did not know from where it came.
text.DRB: And they answered that they knew not whence it was.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto them—Neither do, I, tell, you, by what authority, these things, I am doing.
text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto them, Neither tell I you by what authoritie I doe these things.
text.NABRE: Then Jesus said to them, “Neither shall I tell you by what authority I do these things.” The Parable of the Tenant Farmers.
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them: Neither do I tell you in what power I do these things.

text.NRSV: He began to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard, and leased it to tenants, and went to another country for a long time.
text.Rotherham: And he began, unto the people, to be speaking this parable:—A man, planted a vineyard, and let it out to husbandmen, and went from home for a long time.
text.KJV: Then began hee to speake to the people this parable: A certaine man planted a vineyard, and let it foorth to husbandmen, and went into a farre countrey for a long time.
text.NABRE: Then he proceeded to tell the people this parable. “[A] man planted a vineyard, leased it to tenant farmers, and then went on a journey for a long time.
text.DRB: And he began to say to the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, & let it out to husbandmen: and he was from home a long time.

text.NRSV: When the time came, he sent a slave to the tenants in order that they might give him his share of the produce of the vineyard; but the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: And, in due season, he sent unto the husbandmen, a servant, that, of the fruit of the vineyard, they might give him; but, the husbandmen, having beaten him, sent him away empty.
text.KJV: And at the season, hee sent a seruant to the husbandmen, that they should giue him of the fruit of the vineyard, but the husbandmen beat him, and sent him away emptie.
text.NABRE: At harvest time he sent a servant to the tenant farmers to receive some of the produce of the vineyard. But they beat the servant and sent him away empty-handed.
text.DRB: And in time he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard. Who beating him sent him away empty.

text.NRSV: Next he sent another slave; that one also they beat and insulted and sent away empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: And he, further, sent another servant; but, they, beating, that one also, and dishonouring him, sent him away, empty.
text.KJV: And againe hee sent another seruant, and they beat him also, and entreated him shamefully, and sent him away emptie.
text.NABRE: So he proceeded to send another servant, but him also they beat and insulted and sent away empty-handed.
text.DRB: And again he sent another servant. But they beating him also and reproachfully abusing him, sent him away empty.

text.NRSV: He sent still a third; this one also they wounded and threw out.
text.Rotherham: And he, further, sent, a third; but, they, bruising, this one also, thrust him out.
text.KJV: And againe he sent the third, and they wounded him also, & cast him out.
text.NABRE: Then he proceeded to send a third, but this one too they wounded and threw out.
text.DRB: And again he sent the third: who wounding him also, cast him out.

text.NRSV: Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.’
text.Rotherham: And the lord of the vineyard said—What shall I do? I will send my son, the beloved,—It may be, that, him, they will respect.
text.KJV: Then said the lord of the vineyard, What shall I doe? I will send my beloued sonne: it may be they will reuerence him when they see him.
text.NABRE: The owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I shall send my beloved son; maybe they will respect him.’
text.DRB: And the Lord of the vineyard said: What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: perhaps when they shall see him, they will reverence him.

text.NRSV: But when the tenants saw him, they discussed it among themselves, saying, ‘This is the heir; let us kill him so that the inheritance may be ours.’
text.Rotherham: But he husbandmen, seeing him, began to deliberate one with another, saying—This, is, the heir: Let us slay him, that, ours, may be, the inheritance.
text.KJV: But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselues, saying, This is þ heire, come, let vs kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.
text.NABRE: But when the tenant farmers saw him they said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Let us kill him that the inheritance may become ours.’
text.DRB: Whom when the husbandmen saw, they thought within themselves, saying: This is the heir, let us kill him, that the heritage may be ours.

text.NRSV: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
text.Rotherham: And, thrusting him forth outside the vineyard, they slew him. What, then, will the lord of the vineyard do unto them?
text.KJV: So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard doe vnto them?
text.NABRE: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
text.DRB: And casting him forth out of the vineyard, they killed him. What therefore will the Lord of the vineyard do to them?

text.NRSV: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.” When they heard this, they said, “Heaven forbid!”
text.Rotherham: He will come, and destroy these husbandmen, and give the vineyard unto others. But, when they heard it, they said—Far be it!
text.KJV: Hee shall come and destroy these husbandmen, and shall giue the vineyard to others. And when they heard it, they said, God forbid.
text.NABRE: He will come and put those tenant farmers to death and turn over the vineyard to others.” When the people heard this, they exclaimed, “Let it not be so!”
text.DRB: He will come, and will destroy these husbandmen, and will give the vineyard to others. Which they hearing, said to him: God forbid.

text.NRSV: But he looked at them and said, “What then does this text mean: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone’?
text.Rotherham: But, he, looking at them, said—What, then, is this that is written,—A stone which the builders’ rejected, The same, hath become, head of the corner?
text.KJV: And hee beheld them, and said, What is this then that is written, The stone which the builders reiected, the same is become the head of the corner?
text.NABRE: But he looked at them and asked, “What then does this scripture passage mean: ‘The stone which the builders rejected has become the cornerstone’?
text.DRB: But he beholding them said: What is this then that is written, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become into the head of the corner?

text.NRSV: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls.”
text.Rotherham: Every one who falleth on that stone, will be sorely bruised, but, on whomsoever it shall fall, it will utterly destroy him.
text.KJV: Whosoeuer shall fall vpon that stone, shalbe broken: but on whomsoeuer it shall fall, it will grinde him to powder.
text.NABRE: Everyone who falls on that stone will be dashed to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls.”
text.DRB: Every one that falleth upon this stone, shall be quashed: and upon whom it shall fall, it shall break him to powder.

text.NRSV: When the scribes and chief priests realized that he had told this parable against them, they wanted to lay hands on him at that very hour, but they feared the people.
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and the High-priests sought to thrust on him their hands, in that very hour; and feared the people; for they perceived that, against them, spake he this parable.
text.KJV: ¶ And the chiefe Priests and the Scribes the same houre sought to lay hands on him, and they feared the people: for they perceiued that he had spoken this parable against them.
text.NABRE: The scribes and chief priests sought to lay their hands on him at that very hour, but they feared the people, for they knew that he had addressed this parable to them. Paying Taxes to the Emperor.
text.DRB: And the chief Priests and Scribes sought to lay hands upon him that hour: and they feared the people, for they knew that he spake this similitude to them.

text.NRSV: So they watched him and sent spies who pretended to be honest, in order to trap him by what he said, so as to hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
text.Rotherham: And, watching narrowly, they sent forth suborned men, feigning themselves to be, righteous, that they might lay hold of a word of his, so as to deliver him up unto the rule and the authority of the governor.
text.KJV: And they watched him, and sent foorth spies, which should faine themselues iust men, that they might take holde of his words, that so they might deliuer him vnto the power and authoritie of the gouernour.
text.NABRE: They watched him closely and sent agents pretending to be righteous who were to trap him in speech, in order to hand him over to the authority and power of the governor.
text.DRB: And watching, they sent spies which should feign themselves just: that they might take him in his talk, and deliver him to the principality and power of the President.

text.NRSV: So they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you are right in what you say and teach, and you show deference to no one, but teach the way of God in accordance with truth.
text.Rotherham: And they questioned him, saying—Teacher! we know that, rightly, thou speakest and teachest, and respectest no person, but, in truth, the way of God, dost teach:
text.KJV: And they asked him, saying, Master, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, neither acceptest thou the person of any, but teachest the way of God truely.
text.NABRE: They posed this question to him, “Teacher, we know that what you say and teach is correct, and you show no partiality, but teach the way of God in accordance with the truth.
text.DRB: And they asked him, saying: Master, we know that thou speakest and teachest rightly; and thou doest not accept person, but teachest the way of God in truth.

text.NRSV: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?”
text.Rotherham: Is it allowable for us to give, unto Caesar, tribute, or not?
text.KJV: Is it lawfull for vs to giue tribute vnto Cesar, or no?
text.NABRE: Is it lawful for us to pay tribute to Caesar or not?”
text.DRB: Is it lawful for us to give tribute to Cæsar, or not?

text.NRSV: But he perceived their craftiness and said to them,
text.Rotherham: But, observing their villainy, he said unto them—
text.KJV: But he perceiued their craftines, and said vnto them, Why tempt ye me?
text.NABRE: Recognizing their craftiness he said to them,
text.DRB: But considering their guile, he said to them: Why tempt you me?

text.NRSV: “Show me a denarius. Whose head and whose title does it bear?” They said, “The emperor’s.”
text.Rotherham: Shew me a denary. Of whom, hath it an image and inscription? And, they, said—Of Caesar.
text.KJV: Shew me a peny: whose image and superscription hath it? They answered, and said, Cesars.
text.NABRE: “Show me a denarius; whose image and name does it bear?” They replied, “Caesar’s.”
text.DRB: Shew me a penny. Whose image hath it and inscription? They answering said: Cæsar's.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Then give to the emperor the things that are the emperor’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Well then! render the things of Caesar, unto Caesar, and the things of God, unto God.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Render therefore vnto Cesar the things which be Cesars, and vnto God the things which be Gods.
text.NABRE: So he said to them, “Then repay to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.”
text.DRB: And he said to them: Render therefore the things that are Cæsar's, to Cæsar: and the things that are God's, to God.

text.NRSV: And they were not able in the presence of the people to trap him by what he said; and being amazed by his answer, they became silent.
text.Rotherham: And they could not lay hold of the saying, before the people; and, marvelling at his answer, they held their peace.
text.KJV: And they could not take holde of his wordes before the people, and they marueiled at his answere, and helde their peace.
text.NABRE: They were unable to trap him by something he might say before the people, and so amazed were they at his reply that they fell silent. The Question About the Resurrection.
text.DRB: And they could not reprehend his word before the people: and marveling at his answer, they held their peace.

text.NRSV: Some Sadducees, those who say there is no resurrection, came to him
text.Rotherham: But there came near certain of the Sadducees, they who say, Resurrection, there is none! and questioned him,
text.KJV: ¶ Then came to him certaine of the Sadduces (which denie that there is any resurrection) and they asked him,
text.NABRE: Some Sadducees, those who deny that there is a resurrection, came forward and put this question to him,
text.DRB: And there came certain of the Sadducees, which deny that there is a resurrection, and they asked him,

text.NRSV: and asked him a question, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies, leaving a wife but no children, the man shall marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
text.Rotherham: saying—Teacher! Moses, wrote for us, If one’s brother die, having a wife, and, he, be, childless, that his brother shall take the wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
text.KJV: Saying, Master, Moses wrote vnto vs, If any mans brother die, hauing a wife, and hee die without children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise vp seede vnto his brother.
text.NABRE: saying, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us, ‘If someone’s brother dies leaving a wife but no child, his brother must take the wife and raise up descendants for his brother.’
text.DRB: saying: Master, Moyses gave us in writing: If a man's brother die having a wife, and he have no children, that his brother take her to wife, and raise up seed to his brother.

text.NRSV: Now there were seven brothers; the first married a woman and died childless;
text.Rotherham: Seven brethren, therefore, there were,—and, the first, taking a wife, died childless,
text.KJV: There were therefore seuen brethren, and the first tooke a wife, and died without children.
text.NABRE: Now there were seven brothers; the first married a woman but died childless.
text.DRB: There were therefore seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and died without children.

text.NRSV: then the second
text.Rotherham: And, the second,
text.KJV: And the second tooke her to wife, and he died childlesse.
text.NABRE: Then the second
text.DRB: And the next took her, & he died without child.

text.NRSV: and the third married her, and so in the same way all seven died childless.
text.Rotherham: and the third, took her,—yea, likewise, even the seven; They left no children, and died:
text.KJV: And the third tooke her, and in like maner the seuen also. And they left no children, and died.
text.NABRE: and the third married her, and likewise all the seven died childless.
text.DRB: And the third took her. In like manner also all the seven, and they left no seed, and died.

text.NRSV: Finally the woman also died.
text.Rotherham: Later on, the woman also, died.
text.KJV: Last of all the woman died also.
text.NABRE: Finally the woman also died.
text.DRB: Last of all the woman died also.

text.NRSV: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will the woman be? For the seven had married her.”
text.Rotherham: The woman, therefore, in the resurrection, Of which of them, doth she become wife? for, the seven, had her to wife.
text.KJV: Therefore in the resurrection, whose wife of them is she? for seuen had her to wife.
text.NABRE: Now at the resurrection whose wife will that woman be? For all seven had been married to her.”
text.DRB: In the resurrection therefore, whose wife shall she be of them? Sithens the seven had her to wife.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Those who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage;
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto them—The sons of this age, marry, and are given in marriage,—
text.KJV: And Iesus answering, said vnto them, The children of this world, marrie, and are giuen in marriage:
text.NABRE: Jesus said to them, “The children of this age marry and are given in marriage;
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them: The children of this world marry, and are given in marriage:

text.NRSV: but those who are considered worthy of a place in that age and in the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.
text.Rotherham: But, they who have been accounted worthy, that age, to obtain, and the resurrection that is from among the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage;
text.KJV: But they which shall be accompted worthy to obtaine that world, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marrie, nor are giuen in marriage.
text.NABRE: but those who are deemed worthy to attain to the coming age and to the resurrection of the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.
text.DRB: But they that shall be counted worthy of that world and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor take wives;

text.NRSV: Indeed they cannot die anymore, because they are like angels and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.
text.Rotherham: For they cannot, even die any more,—for, equal unto messengers, are they, and are, sons of God, Of the resurrection, being, sons.
text.KJV: Neither can they die any more; for they are equall vnto the Angels, and are the children of God, being the children of the resurrection.
text.NABRE: They can no longer die, for they are like angels; and they are the children of God because they are the ones who will rise.
text.DRB: neither can they die any more, for they are equal to Angels: and they are the sons of God, seeing they are the sons of the resurrection.

text.NRSV: And the fact that the dead are raised Moses himself showed, in the story about the bush, where he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: But, that the dead do rise, even Moses, disclosed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord—The God of Abraham and God of Isaac and God of Jacob:
text.KJV: Now that the dead are raised, euen Moses shewed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isahac, and the God of Iacob.
text.NABRE: That the dead will rise even Moses made known in the passage about the bush, when he called ‘Lord’ the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob;
text.DRB: But that the dead rise again, Moyses also shewed, beside the bush, as he calleth the Lord: The God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.

text.NRSV: Now he is God not of the dead, but of the living; for to him all of them are alive.”
text.Rotherham: Now, God, he is not, of the dead, but, of the living,—for, all, unto him, do live.
text.KJV: For he is not a God of the dead, but of the liuing for all liue vnto him.
text.NABRE: and he is not God of the dead, but of the living, for to him all are alive.”
text.DRB: For God is not of the dead, but of the living: for all live to him.

text.NRSV: Then some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you have spoken well.”
text.Rotherham: And certain of the Scribes, answering, said—Teacher! Well, hast thou spoken.
text.KJV: ¶ Then certaine of the Scribes answering, said, Master, Thou hast well said.
text.NABRE: Some of the scribes said in reply, “Teacher, you have answered well.”
text.DRB: And certain of the Scribes answering, said to him: Master, thou hast said well.

text.NRSV: For they no longer dared to ask him another question.
text.Rotherham: For, no longer, were they daring to ask him any questions.
text.KJV: And after that, they durst not aske him any question at all.
text.NABRE: And they no longer dared to ask him anything. The Question About David’s Son.
text.DRB: And further they durst not ask him anything.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “How can they say that the Messiah is David’s son?
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—How say they, that, the Christ, is, David’s Son?
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, How say they that Christ is Dauids sonne?
text.NABRE: Then he said to them, “How do they claim that the Messiah is the Son of David?
text.DRB: But he said to them: How say they that Christ is the son of David,

text.NRSV: For David himself says in the book of Psalms, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,
text.Rotherham: For, David himself, saith, in [the] book of Psalms: Said the Lord, unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand,
text.KJV: And Dauid himselfe saith in the booke of Psalmes, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
text.NABRE: For David himself in the Book of Psalms says: ‘The Lord said to my lord, “Sit at my right hand
text.DRB: and David himself saith in the book of Psalms: The Lord said to my Lord, sit on my right hand,

text.NRSV: until I make your enemies your footstool.”’
text.Rotherham: Until I make thy foes thy footstool.
text.KJV: Till I make thine enemies thy footestoole.
text.NABRE: till I make your enemies your footstool.”’
text.DRB: till I put thine enemies, the footstool of thy feet?

text.NRSV: David thus calls him Lord; so how can he be his son?”
text.Rotherham: David, therefore, calleth, him Lord: How, then, his son, is he?
text.KJV: Dauid therefore calleth him, Lord, how is he then his sonne?
text.NABRE: Now if David calls him ‘lord,’ how can he be his son?” Denunciation of the Scribes.
text.DRB: David then calleth him Lord: and how is he his son?

text.NRSV: In the hearing of all the people he said to the disciples,
text.Rotherham: And, all the people hearing, he said unto the disciples:
text.KJV: ¶ Then in the audience of all the people, he said vnto his disciples,
text.NABRE: Then, within the hearing of all the people, he said to [his] disciples,
text.DRB: And all the people hearing him, he said to his Disciples:

text.NRSV: “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces, and to have the best seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets.
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed of the Scribes—Who desire to walk about in long robes, and are fond of salutations in the markets, and first seats in the synagogues, and first couches in the chief meals,—
text.KJV: Beware of the Scribes, which desire to walke in long robes, and loue greetings in the markets, and the highest seates in the Synagogues, and the chiefe roumes at feasts:
text.NABRE: “Be on guard against the scribes, who like to go around in long robes and love greetings in marketplaces, seats of honor in synagogues, and places of honor at banquets.
text.DRB: Beware of the Scribes, that will walk in robes, and love salutations in the market-place, and the first chairs in the synagogues, and the chief rooms in feasts.

text.NRSV: They devour widows’ houses and for the sake of appearance say long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”
text.Rotherham: Who devour the houses of widows, and, for a show, are a long time at prayer. These, shall receive, a heavier sentence.
text.KJV: Which deuoure widowes houses and for a shew make long prayers: the same shall receiue greater damnation.
text.NABRE: They devour the houses of widows and, as a pretext, recite lengthy prayers. They will receive a very severe condemnation.”
text.DRB: Which devour widows' houses: feigning long prayer. These shall receive greater damnation.

text.NRSV: He looked up and saw rich people putting their gifts into the treasury;
text.Rotherham: And, looking up, he saw the, rich, who were casting their gifts into the treasury,—
text.KJV: And he looked vp, and saw the rich men casting their giftes into the treasurie.
text.NABRE: Chapter 21 The Poor Widow’s Contribution. When he looked up he saw some wealthy people putting their offerings into the treasury
text.DRB: And beholding, he saw them that did cast their gifts into the treasury, rich persons.

text.NRSV: he also saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins.
text.Rotherham: and he saw a certain poor widow, casting in thither two mites;
text.KJV: And hee saw also a certaine poore widow, casting in thither two mites.
text.NABRE: and he noticed a poor widow putting in two small coins.
text.DRB: And he saw also a certain poor widow casting two brass mites.

text.NRSV: He said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all of them;
text.Rotherham: and he said—Of a truth, I say unto you—This destitute widow, more than they all, hath cast in;
text.KJV: And he said, Of a truth, I say vnto you, that this poore widow hath cast in more then they all.
text.NABRE: He said, “I tell you truly, this poor widow put in more than all the rest;
text.DRB: And he said: Verily I say to you, that this poor widow hath cast more than all.

text.NRSV: for all of them have contributed out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on.”
text.Rotherham: For, all these, out of their superfluity, have cast in among the gifts, but, she, out of her deficiency, all the living that she had, hath cast in.
text.KJV: For all these haue of their abundance cast in vnto the offerings of God, but shee of her penurie hath cast in all the liuing that she had.
text.NABRE: for those others have all made offerings from their surplus wealth, but she, from her poverty, has offered her whole livelihood.” The Destruction of the Temple Foretold.
text.DRB: For all these of their aboundance have cast into the gifts of God: but she of her penury, hath cast in all her living that she had.

text.NRSV: When some were speaking about the temple, how it was adorned with beautiful stones and gifts dedicated to God, he said,
text.Rotherham: And, certain saying, of the temple—With beautiful stones and offerings, hath it been adorned! he said—
text.KJV: ¶ And as some spake of the Temple, how it was adorned with goodly stones, and gifts, he said,
text.NABRE: While some people were speaking about how the temple was adorned with costly stones and votive offerings, he said,
text.DRB: And certain saying of the temple that it was adorned with goodly stones and donaries, he said:

text.NRSV: “As for these things that you see, the days will come when not one stone will be left upon another; all will be thrown down.”
text.Rotherham: As to these things, which ye are looking upon, There will come days, in which there will not be left here, stone upon stone, which will not be taken down.
text.KJV: As for these things which yee behold, the dayes will come, in the which there shal not be left one stone vpon another, that shal not be throwen downe.
text.NABRE: “All that you see here—the days will come when there will not be left a stone upon another stone that will not be thrown down.”
text.DRB: These things which you see, the days will come wherein there shall not be left a stone upon a stone that shall not be destroyed.

text.NRSV: They asked him, “Teacher, when will this be, and what will be the sign that this is about to take place?”
text.Rotherham: And they questioned him, saying—Teacher! when, therefore, will these things be? And, what the sign, when these things shall be about to come to pass?
text.KJV: And they asked him, saying, Master, but when shall these things be? and what signe wil there be, when these things shall come to passe?
text.NABRE: The Signs of the End. Then they asked him, “Teacher, when will this happen? And what sign will there be when all these things are about to happen?”
text.DRB: And they asked him, saying: Master, when shall these things be: and what shall be the sign when they shall begin to come to pass?

text.NRSV: And he said, “Beware that you are not led astray; for many will come in my name and say, ‘I am he!’ and, ‘The time is near!’ Do not go after them.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Be taking heed ye be not deceived; for, many, will come upon my name, saying—I, am he,—and—The season, hath drawn near! Do not go after them.
text.KJV: And he said, Take heede that yee be not deceiued: for many shall come in my Name, saying, I am Christ, and the time draweth neere: goe yee not therefore after them.
text.NABRE: He answered, “See that you not be deceived, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and ‘The time has come.’ Do not follow them!
text.DRB: Who said: See you be not seduced. For many will come in my name, saying that I am he: and the time is at hand, go not therefore after them.

text.NRSV: “When you hear of wars and insurrections, do not be terrified; for these things must take place first, but the end will not follow immediately.”
text.Rotherham: But, whensoever ye shall hear of wars and revolutions, be not terrified,—for these things, must needs, come to pass first, but, not immediately, is the end.
text.KJV: But when ye shall heare of wars, and commotions, be not terrified: for these things must first come to passe, but the end is not by and by.
text.NABRE: When you hear of wars and insurrections, do not be terrified; for such things must happen first, but it will not immediately be the end.”
text.DRB: And when you shall hear of wars and seditions, be not terrified: these things must first come to pass, but the end is not yet by and by.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom;
text.Rotherham: Then, said he unto them—There will rise up, nation against nation, and kingdom against kingdom;
text.KJV: Then said he vnto them, Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdome against kingdome:
text.NABRE: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
text.DRB: Then he said to them: Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.

text.NRSV: there will be great earthquakes, and in various places famines and plagues; and there will be dreadful portents and great signs from heaven.
text.Rotherham: As well great earthquakes, as also, in places, pestilences and famines, will there be, as well objects of terror, as also, from heaven, great signs, will there be.
text.KJV: And great earthquakes shall be in diuers places, and famines, and pestilences: and fearefull sights and great signes shall there be from heauen.
text.NABRE: There will be powerful earthquakes, famines, and plagues from place to place; and awesome sights and mighty signs will come from the sky.
text.DRB: And there shall be great earth-quakes in places, and pestilences and famines, and terrors from Heaven, and there shall be great signs.

text.NRSV: “But before all this occurs, they will arrest you and persecute you; they will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
text.Rotherham: And before, all these things, they will thrust upon you their hands, and persecute you, delivering you up into the synagogues and prisons,—when ye have been led away before kings and governors, for the sake of my name;
text.KJV: But before all these, they shall lay their hands on you, and persecute you, deliuering you vp to the Synagogues, and into prisons, being brought before Kings and rulers for my Names sake.
text.NABRE: The Coming Persecution. “Before all this happens, however, they will seize and persecute you, they will hand you over to the synagogues and to prisons, and they will have you led before kings and governors because of my name.
text.DRB: But before all these things they will lay their hands upon you: and persecute you delivering you into Synagogues and prisons, drawing you to kings and Presidents for my name.

text.NRSV: This will give you an opportunity to testify.
text.Rotherham: But it shall turn out to you for a witness.
text.KJV: And it shall turne to you for a testimony.
text.NABRE: It will lead to your giving testimony.
text.DRB: and it shall happen unto you for testimony.

text.NRSV: So make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance;
text.Rotherham: Settle, then, in your hearts, not to be studying beforehand, how to make defense;
text.KJV: Settle it therfore in your hearts, not to meditate before what ye shall answere.
text.NABRE: Remember, you are not to prepare your defense beforehand,
text.DRB: Lay up this therefore in your hearts, not to premeditate how you shall answer.

text.NRSV: for I will give you words and a wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to withstand or contradict.
text.Rotherham: For, I, will give you a mouth and wisdom, which one-and-all who are setting themselves against, you shall be unable to withstand or gainsay.
text.KJV: For I will giue you a mouth and wisedome, which all your aduersaries shall not be able to gainsay, nor resist.
text.NABRE: for I myself shall give you a wisdom in speaking that all your adversaries will be powerless to resist or refute.
text.DRB: For I will give you mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to resist and gainsay.

text.NRSV: You will be betrayed even by parents and brothers, by relatives and friends; and they will put some of you to death.
text.Rotherham: But ye will be delivered up, even by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolk, and friends, and they will put to death some from among you;
text.KJV: And yee shall be betrayed both by parents and brethren, and kinsefolkes and friends, and some of you shall they cause to be put to death.
text.NABRE: You will even be handed over by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends, and they will put some of you to death.
text.DRB: And you shall be delivered up of your parents and brethren, and kinsmen and friends: and they will put to death of you.

text.NRSV: You will be hated by all because of my name.
text.Rotherham: And ye will be hated by all, because of my name:
text.KJV: And ye shalbe hated of all men for my Names sake.
text.NABRE: You will be hated by all because of my name,
text.DRB: And you shall be odious to all men for my name:

text.NRSV: But not a hair of your head will perish.
text.Rotherham: And, a hair of your head, in nowise shall perish,—
text.KJV: But there shall not a haire of your head perish.
text.NABRE: but not a hair on your head will be destroyed.
text.DRB: and a hair of your head shall not perish.

text.NRSV: By your endurance you will gain your souls.
text.Rotherham: By your endurance, shall ye gain your lives for a possession.
text.KJV: In your patience possesse ye your soules.
text.NABRE: By your perseverance you will secure your lives. The Great Tribulation.
text.DRB: In your patience you shall possess your souls.

text.NRSV: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation has come near.
text.Rotherham: But whensoever ye shall see Jerusalem, encompassed by armies, then, know, that her desolation hath drawn near.
text.KJV: And when yee shall see Hierusalem compassed with armies, then know that the desolation thereof is nigh.
text.NABRE: “When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, know that its desolation is at hand.
text.DRB: And when you shall see Jerusalem compassed about with an army, then know that the desolation thereof is at hand.

text.NRSV: Then those in Judea must flee to the mountains, and those inside the city must leave it, and those out in the country must not enter it;
text.Rotherham: Then they who are in Judaea, let them flee into the mountains, and they who are in her midst, let them go forth,—and they who are in the fields, let them not enter into her;
text.KJV: Then let them which are in Iudea, flee to the mountaines, and let them which are in the midst of it, depart out, and let not them that are in the countreys, enter thereinto.
text.NABRE: Then those in Judea must flee to the mountains. Let those within the city escape from it, and let those in the countryside not enter the city,
text.DRB: then they that are in Jewry, let them flee to the mountains: and they in the midst thereof, let them depart: and they in the countries, let them not enter into it.

text.NRSV: for these are days of vengeance, as a fulfillment of all that is written.
text.Rotherham: For, days of avenging, are, these, for all the things written to be fulfilled.
text.KJV: For these be the dayes of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.
text.NABRE: for these days are the time of punishment when all the scriptures are fulfilled.
text.DRB: For these are the days of vengeance, that all things may be fulfilled that are written.

text.NRSV: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days! For there will be great distress on the earth and wrath against this people;
text.Rotherham: Alas! for the women with child, and for them who are giving suck, in those days; for there will be great distress upon the land, and anger against this people.
text.KJV: But woe vnto them that are with childe, and to them that giue sucke in those dayes, for there shalbe great distresse in the land, and wrath vpon this people.
text.NABRE: Woe to pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days, for a terrible calamity will come upon the earth and a wrathful judgment upon this people.
text.DRB: But woe to them that are with child & that give suck in those days. For there shall be great affliction upon the land, and wrath on this people.

text.NRSV: they will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations; and Jerusalem will be trampled on by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
text.Rotherham: And they will fall by the edge of the sword, and be carried away captive into all the nations, and, Jerusalem, shall be trodden down by the nations, until the seasons of the nations shall be fulfilled [and shall be].
text.KJV: And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall bee led away captiue into all nations, and Hierusalem shall be troden downe of the Gentiles, vntill the times of the Gentiles bee fulfilled.
text.NABRE: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken as captives to all the Gentiles; and Jerusalem will be trampled underfoot by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled. The Coming of the Son of Man.
text.DRB: And they shall fall by the edge of the sword; and shall be led captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden of the Gentiles, till the times of nations be fulfilled.

text.NRSV: “There will be signs in the sun, the moon, and the stars, and on the earth distress among nations confused by the roaring of the sea and the waves.
text.Rotherham: And there will he signs in sun, and moon, and stars, and, on the earth, anguish of nations in embarrassment—sea and surge resounding,—
text.KJV: ¶ And there shalbe signes in the Sunne, and in the Moone, and in the Starres, and vpon the earth distresse of nations, with perplexity, the Sea and the waues roaring,
text.NABRE: “There will be signs in the sun, the moon, and the stars, and on earth nations will be in dismay, perplexed by the roaring of the sea and the waves.
text.DRB: And there shall be signs in the sun and the moon and the stars: and upon earth distress of nations, for the confusion of the sound of sea and waves,

text.NRSV: People will faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
text.Rotherham: Men fainting, from fear and expectation of the things overtaking the inhabited earth. For, the powers of the heavens, will be shaken.
text.KJV: Mens hearts failing them for feare, and for looking after those things which are comming on the earth; For the powers of heauen shall be shaken.
text.NABRE: People will die of fright in anticipation of what is coming upon the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
text.DRB: men withering for fear and expectation, what shall come upon the whole world. For the powers of Heaven shall be moved:

text.NRSV: Then they will see ‘the Son of Man coming in a cloud’ with power and great glory.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will they see the Son of Man—coming in a cloud, with great power and glory.
text.KJV: And then shall they see the sonne of man comming in a cloud with power and great glory.
text.NABRE: And then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
text.DRB: and then they shall see the Son of man coming in a cloud with great power and majesty.

text.NRSV: Now when these things begin to take place, stand up and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”
text.Rotherham: And, when these things are beginning to come to pass, unbend and lift up your heads, because that, your redemption, is drawing near.
text.KJV: And when these things begin to come to passe, then looke vp, and lift vp your heads, for your redemptiou draweth nigh.
text.NABRE: But when these signs begin to happen, stand erect and raise your heads because your redemption is at hand.” The Lesson of the Fig Tree.
text.DRB: But when these things begin to come to pass, look up and lift up your heads: because your redemption is at hand.

text.NRSV: Then he told them a parable: “Look at the fig tree and all the trees;
text.Rotherham: And he spake a parable unto them: See the fig-tree, and all the trees,—
text.KJV: And he spake to them a parable, Behold the figge tree, and all the trees,
text.NABRE: He taught them a lesson. “Consider the fig tree and all the other trees.
text.DRB: And he spake to them a similitude. See the figtree and all trees:

text.NRSV: as soon as they sprout leaves you can see for yourselves and know that summer is already near.
text.Rotherham: Whensoever they have already budded, seeing it, of yourselves, ye observe that, already near, is, the summer:
text.KJV: When they now shoot foorth, yee see and know of your owne selues, that summer is now nigh at hand.
text.NABRE: When their buds burst open, you see for yourselves and know that summer is now near;
text.DRB: when they now bud forth fruit out of themselves, you know that summer is nigh.

text.NRSV: So also, when you see these things taking place, you know that the kingdom of God is near.
text.Rotherham: Thus, ye also, whensoever ye shall see, these, things coming to pass, observe ye, that, near, is the kingdom of God!
text.KJV: So likewise yee, when yee see these things come to passe, know ye that the kingdome of God is nigh at hand.
text.NABRE: in the same way, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.
text.DRB: So you also when you shall see these things come to pass, know that the Kingdom of God is nigh.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things have taken place.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—In nowise shall this generation pass away, until, all things, shall happen:
text.KJV: Uerily I say vnto you, this generation shall not passe away, till all be fulfilled.
text.NABRE: Amen, I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.
text.DRB: Amen I say to you, that this generation shall not pass, till all be done.

text.NRSV: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
text.Rotherham: Heaven and earth, will pass away, but, my word, in nowise will pass away.
text.KJV: Heauen and earth shall passe away, but my words shall not passe away.
text.NABRE: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
text.DRB: Heaven and earth shall pass; but my words shall not pass.

text.NRSV: “Be on guard so that your hearts are not weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of this life, and that day catch you unexpectedly,
text.Rotherham: But be taking heed unto yourselves, lest once your hearts be made heavy—with debauch and drunkenness and anxieties about livelihood, and that day come upon you suddenly,
text.KJV: ¶ And take heed to your selues, least at any time your hearts be ouercharged with surfetting, and drunkennesse, and cares of this life, and so that day come vpon you vnawares.
text.NABRE: Exhortation to Be Vigilant. “Beware that your hearts do not become drowsy from carousing and drunkenness and the anxieties of daily life, and that day catch you by surprise
text.DRB: And look well to yourselves, lest perhaps your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting and drunkenness and cares of this life: and that day come upon you suddenly.

text.NRSV: like a trap. For it will come upon all who live on the face of the whole earth.
text.Rotherham: as a snare,—For it will come in by surprise, upon all them that are dwelling on the face of all the earth.
text.KJV: For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.
text.NABRE: like a trap. For that day will assault everyone who lives on the face of the earth.
text.DRB: For as a snare shall it come upon all that sit upon the face of all the earth.

text.NRSV: Be alert at all times, praying that you may have the strength to escape all these things that will take place, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
text.Rotherham: But be watching in every season, making supplication, that ye may gain full vigour, to escape all these things that are about to be coming to pass, and to stand before the Son of Man.
text.KJV: Watch ye therefore, and pray alwayes, that ye may be accompted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to passe, and to stand before the sonne of man.
text.NABRE: Be vigilant at all times and pray that you have the strength to escape the tribulations that are imminent and to stand before the Son of Man.”
text.DRB: Watch therefore, praying at all times, that you may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that are to come, and to stand before the Son of man.

text.NRSV: Every day he was teaching in the temple, and at night he would go out and spend the night on the Mount of Olives, as it was called.
text.Rotherham: And he was, by day, in the temple, teaching; but, by night, going forth, he was lodging in the mount which is called the Mount of Olives.
text.KJV: And in the day time he was teaching in the Temple, and at night hee went out, and abode in the mount that is called the mount of Oliues.
text.NABRE: Ministry in Jerusalem. During the day, Jesus was teaching in the temple area, but at night he would leave and stay at the place called the Mount of Olives.
text.DRB: And the days he was teaching in the temple: but the nights going forth, he abode in the mount that is called Olivet.

text.NRSV: And all the people would get up early in the morning to listen to him in the temple.
text.Rotherham: And, all the people, were coming at day-break unto him, in the temple, to be hearkening unto him.
text.KJV: And all the people came earely in the morning to him in the Temple, for to heare him.
text.NABRE: And all the people would get up early each morning to listen to him in the temple area.
text.DRB: And all the people in the morning went unto him in the temple to hear him.

text.NRSV: Now the festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was near.
text.Rotherham: And the feast of the unleavened bread, which is called a Passover, was drawing near.
text.KJV: Now þ feast of vnleuened bread drew nigh, which is called the Passeouer.
text.NABRE: VII. The Passion Narrative Chapter 22 The Conspiracy Against Jesus. Now the feast of Unleavened Bread, called the Passover, was drawing near,
text.DRB: And the festival day of the Azymes approached, which is called Pasch:

text.NRSV: The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to put Jesus to death, for they were afraid of the people.
text.Rotherham: And the High-priests and the Scribes were seeking, how they might get rid of him; for they feared the people.
text.KJV: And the chiefe Priests and Scribes sought how they might kill him; for they feared the people.
text.NABRE: and the chief priests and the scribes were seeking a way to put him to death, for they were afraid of the people.
text.DRB: & the chief Priests & the Scribes sought how they might kill him: but they feared the people.

text.NRSV: Then Satan entered into Judas called Iscariot, who was one of the twelve;
text.Rotherham: But Satan entered into Judas, him called Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve;
text.KJV: ¶ Then entred Satan into Iudas surnamed Iscariot, being of the number of the twelue.
text.NABRE: Then Satan entered into Judas, the one surnamed Iscariot, who was counted among the Twelve,
text.DRB: And Satan entered into Judas that was surnamed Iscariote, one of the Twelve.

text.NRSV: he went away and conferred with the chief priests and officers of the temple guard about how he might betray him to them.
text.Rotherham: and, departing, he conversed with the High-priests and Captains, as to how, unto them, he might deliver him up.
text.KJV: And he went his way, and communed with the chiefe Priests and captaines, how he might betray him vnto them.
text.NABRE: and he went to the chief priests and temple guards to discuss a plan for handing him over to them.
text.DRB: And he went, and talked with the chief Priests and the Magistrates, how he might betray him to them.

text.NRSV: They were greatly pleased and agreed to give him money.
text.Rotherham: And they rejoiced, and bargained with him to give him, silver;
text.KJV: And they were glad, and couenanted to giue him money.
text.NABRE: They were pleased and agreed to pay him money.
text.DRB: And they were glad, and bargained to give him money.

text.NRSV: So he consented and began to look for an opportunity to betray him to them when no crowd was present.
text.Rotherham: and he freely consented, and began seeking a good opportunity for delivering him up, in the absence of a multitude, unto them.
text.KJV: And he promised, and sought opportunitie to betray him vnto them in the absence of the multitude.
text.NABRE: He accepted their offer and sought a favorable opportunity to hand him over to them in the absence of a crowd. Preparations for the Passover.
text.DRB: And he promised. And he sought opportunity to betray him apart from the multitudes.

text.NRSV: Then came the day of Unleavened Bread, on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.
text.Rotherham: Now the day of the unleavened bread a came,—on which it was needful to be sacrificing the passover.
text.KJV: ¶ Then came the day of vnleauened bread, when the Passeouer must be killed.
text.NABRE: When the day of the feast of Unleavened Bread arrived, the day for sacrificing the Passover lamb,
text.DRB: And the day of the Azymes came, wherein it was necessary that the Pasch should be killed.

text.NRSV: So Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover meal for us that we may eat it.”
text.Rotherham: And he sent forth Peter and John, saying—Go, and make ready, for us, the passover, that we may eat.
text.KJV: And he sent Peter and Iohn, saying, Goe and prepare vs the Passeouer, that we may eate.
text.NABRE: he sent out Peter and John, instructing them, “Go and make preparations for us to eat the Passover.”
text.DRB: And he sent Peter and John, saying: Go and prepare us the Pasch, that we may eat.

text.NRSV: They asked him, “Where do you want us to make preparations?”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said unto him—Where wiliest thou, we should make ready?
text.KJV: And they said vnto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare?
text.NABRE: They asked him, “Where do you want us to make the preparations?”
text.DRB: But they said: Where wilt thou that we prepare it?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Listen: when you have entered the city, a man carrying a jar of water will meet you; follow him into the house he enters
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Lo! when ye have entered into the city, there will meet you a man, bearing, an earthen-jar of water: follow him into the house into which he is entering.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Behold, when ye are entred into the citie, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water, follow him into the house where he entreth in.
text.NABRE: And he answered them, “When you go into the city, a man will meet you carrying a jar of water. Follow him into the house that he enters
text.DRB: And he said to them: Behold, as you enter into the city, there shall meet you a man carrying a pitcher of water: follow him into the house into which he entereth,

text.NRSV: and say to the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher asks you, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
text.Rotherham: And ye shall say unto the master of the house—The teacher saith unto thee, where is the lodging, where, the passover, with my disciples, I may eat?
text.KJV: And yee shall say vnto the goodman of the house, The Master saith vnto thee, Where is the ghest-chamber where I shall eate the Passeouer with my disciples?
text.NABRE: and say to the master of the house, ‘The teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
text.DRB: and you shall say to the good-man of the house: The Master saith to thee, where is the inn where I may eat the Pasch with my Disciples?

text.NRSV: He will show you a large room upstairs, already furnished. Make preparations there.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, unto you, will shew, a large upper room, spread: there, make ready.
text.KJV: And he shall shew you a large vpper roume furnished, there make ready.
text.NABRE: He will show you a large upper room that is furnished. Make the preparations there.”
text.DRB: And he will shew you a great refectory adorned: and there prepare.

text.NRSV: So they went and found everything as he had told them; and they prepared the Passover meal.
text.Rotherham: And, departing, they found according as he had said unto them, and made ready the passover.
text.KJV: And they went, and found as hee had said vnto them, and they made readie the Passeouer.
text.NABRE: Then they went off and found everything exactly as he had told them, and there they prepared the Passover. The Last Supper.
text.DRB: And they going, found as he said to them, and prepared the Pasch.

text.NRSV: When the hour came, he took his place at the table, and the apostles with him.
text.Rotherham: And, when the hour arrived, he reclined, and the apostles with him.
text.KJV: And when the houre was come, he sate downe, and the twelue Apostles with him.
text.NABRE: When the hour came, he took his place at table with the apostles.
text.DRB: And when the hour was come, he sat down, and the twelve Apostles with him.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer;
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—I have greatly desired, to eat, this passover, with you before I suffer;
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, With desire I haue desired to eate this Passeouer with you before I suffer.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer,
text.DRB: And he said to them: With desire I have desired to eat this Pasch with you before I suffer.

text.NRSV: for I tell you, I will not eat it until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: For, I say unto you—In nowise shall I eat it, until it have been fulfilled in the kingdom of God.
text.KJV: For I say vnto you, I will not any more eate thereof, vntill it be fulfilled in the kingdome of God.
text.NABRE: for, I tell you, I shall not eat it [again] until there is fulfillment in the kingdom of God.”
text.DRB: For I say to you, that from this time I will not eat it, till it be fulfilled in the Kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he said, “Take this and divide it among yourselves;
text.Rotherham: And, accepting a cup, giving thanks, he said—Take this, and divide among yourselves;
text.KJV: And hee tooke the cup, and gaue thanks, and said, Take this, and diuide it among your selues.
text.NABRE: Then he took a cup, gave thanks, and said, “Take this and share it among yourselves;
text.DRB: And taking the chalice he gave thanks, and said: Take and divide among you.

text.NRSV: for I tell you that from now on I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.”
text.Rotherham: For, I say unto you—In nowise shall I drink henceforth of the fruit of the vine, until, the kingdom of God, shall come.
text.KJV: For I say vnto you, I will not drinke of the fruit of the Uine, vntill the kingdome of God shall come.
text.NABRE: for I tell you [that] from this time on I shall not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.”
text.DRB: For I say to you, that I will not drink of the generation of the vine, till the Kingdom of God do come.

text.NRSV: Then he took a loaf of bread, and after giving thanks he broke it and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
text.Rotherham: And, taking a loaf, he gave thanks, and brake, and gave unto them, saying—This is my body [[which in your behalf is being given: this, be ye doing, in remembrance, of me.
text.KJV: ¶ And hee tooke bread, and gaue thankes, and brake it, and gaue vnto them, saying, This is my body which is giuen for you, this doe in remembrance of me.
text.NABRE: Then he took the bread, said the blessing, broke it, and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body, which will be given for you; do this in memory of me.”
text.DRB: And taking bread, he gave thanks, and brake; and gave to them, saying: This is my Body Which is Given For You. Do this for a commemoration of me.
