text.NRSV: And he did the same with the cup after supper, saying, “This cup that is poured out for you is the new covenant in my blood.
text.Rotherham: And—the cup, in like manner, after the taking of supper, saying—This cup, is the new covenant in my blood, which, in your behalf, is to be poured out.]]
text.KJV: Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup is the New Testament in my blood, which is shed for you.
text.NABRE: And likewise the cup after they had eaten, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which will be shed for you.
text.DRB: In like manner the chalice also, after he had supped, saying: This is The Chalice The New Testament in my Blood, Which Shall be Shed For You.

text.NRSV: But see, the one who betrays me is with me, and his hand is on the table.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, lo! the hand of him who is delivering me up, is with me upon the table.
text.KJV: ¶ But beholde, the hand of him that betrayeth mee, is with mee on the table.
text.NABRE: The Betrayal Foretold. “And yet behold, the hand of the one who is to betray me is with me on the table;
text.DRB: But yet behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me, is with me on the table.

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man is going as it has been determined, but woe to that one by whom he is betrayed!”
text.Rotherham: Because, the Son of Man, indeed, according to what is marked out, goeth his way; nevertheless, alas! for that man through whom he is being delivered up.
text.KJV: And truly the Sonne of man goeth as it was determined, but woe vnto that man by whom he is betraied.
text.NABRE: for the Son of Man indeed goes as it has been determined; but woe to that man by whom he is betrayed.”
text.DRB: And the Son of man indeed goeth according to that which is determined: but yet woe to that man by whom he shall be betrayed.

text.NRSV: Then they began to ask one another which of them it could be who would do this.
text.Rotherham: And, they, began to be enquiring together, among themselves, as to which, then, it could be, from among them, who, this thing, was about to perpetrate!
text.KJV: And they began to enquire among themselues, which of them it was that should doe this thing.
text.NABRE: And they began to debate among themselves who among them would do such a deed. The Role of the Disciples.
text.DRB: And they began to question among themselves, which of them it should be that should do this.

text.NRSV: A dispute also arose among them as to which one of them was to be regarded as the greatest.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that there was, a rivalry also, among them, as to which of them seemed to be greater [than the rest] .
text.KJV: ¶ And there was also a strife among them, which of them should bee accompted the greatest.
text.NABRE: Then an argument broke out among them about which of them should be regarded as the greatest.
text.DRB: And there fell also a contention between them, which of them seemed to be greater.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them; and those in authority over them are called benefactors.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—The kings of the nations, lord it over them, and, they who wield authority over them, benefactors, are called.
text.KJV: And hee saide vnto them, The Kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship ouer them, & they that exercise authoritie vpon them, are called benefactors.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them and those in authority over them are addressed as ‘Benefactors’;
text.DRB: And he said to them: The Kings of the Gentiles overrule them; and they that have power upon them, are called beneficial.

text.NRSV: But not so with you; rather the greatest among you must become like the youngest, and the leader like one who serves.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, not so! On the contrary—the greater among you, Let him become as, the younger, and, he that governeth, as, he that ministereth,
text.KJV: But ye shall not be so; but he that is greatest among you, let him be as the yonger, and he that is chiefe, as he that doeth serue.
text.NABRE: but among you it shall not be so. Rather, let the greatest among you be as the youngest, and the leader as the servant.
text.DRB: But you not so: but he that is the greater among you, let him become as the younger: & he that is the leader, as the waiter.

text.NRSV: For who is greater, the one who is at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
text.Rotherham: For which is greater—he that reclineth? or he that ministereth? Is not he that reclineth? But, I, am, in your midst, as he that ministereth,
text.KJV: For whether is greater, hee that sitteth at meat, or hee that serueth? Is not he that sitteth at meat? But I am among you as he that serueth.
text.NABRE: For who is greater: the one seated at table or the one who serves? Is it not the one seated at table? I am among you as the one who serves.
text.DRB: For which is greater, he that sitteth at the table, or he that ministereth? Is not he that sitteth? But I am in the midst of you, as he that ministereth:

text.NRSV: “You are those who have stood by me in my trials;
text.Rotherham: Ye, however, are they who have remained throughout with me in my temptations;
text.KJV: Ye are they which haue continued with me in my temptations.
text.NABRE: It is you who have stood by me in my trials;
text.DRB: & you are they that have remained with me in my tentations.

text.NRSV: and I confer on you, just as my Father has conferred on me, a kingdom,
text.Rotherham: And, I, covenant unto you—as my Father hath covenanted unto me—a kingdom,
text.KJV: And I appoint vnto you a kingdome, as my Father hath appointed vnto me,
text.NABRE: and I confer a kingdom on you, just as my Father has conferred one on me,
text.DRB: And I dispose to you, as my Father disposed to me, a Kingdom:

text.NRSV: so that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: That ye may eat and drink, at my table, in my kingdom, and sit upon thrones, judging, the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.KJV: That yee may eate and drinke at my table in my kingdome, and sit on thrones iudging the twelue Tribes of Israel.
text.NABRE: that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom; and you will sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel. Peter’s Denial Foretold.
text.DRB: that you may eat & drink upon my table in my Kingdom, & may sit upon thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

text.NRSV: “Simon, Simon, listen! Satan has demanded to sift all of you like wheat,
text.Rotherham: Simon! Simon! lo! Satan, hath claimed you, to sift as wheat;
text.KJV: ¶ And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, beholde, Satan hath desired to haue you, that he may sift you as wheat:
text.NABRE: “Simon, Simon, behold Satan has demanded to sift all of you like wheat,
text.DRB: And our Lord said: Simon, Simon, behold Satan hath required to have you for to sift as wheat:

text.NRSV: but I have prayed for you that your own faith may not fail; and you, when once you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.”
text.Rotherham: But, I, have entreated for thee, that thy faith may not fail. And, thou, once thou hast turned, strengthen thy brethren.
text.KJV: But I haue prayed for thee, that thy faith faile not; and when thou art conuerted, strengthen thy brethren.
text.NABRE: but I have prayed that your own faith may not fail; and once you have turned back, you must strengthen your brothers.”
text.DRB: But I have Prayed For Thee, that thy faith fail not: and thou once converted, confirm thy brethren.

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you to prison and to death!”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto him—Lord! with thee, am I, ready, both into prison and unto death, to be going.
text.KJV: And hee said vnto him, Lord, I am ready to goe with thee both into prison, and to death.
text.NABRE: He said to him, “Lord, I am prepared to go to prison and to die with you.”
text.DRB: Who said to him: Lord, with thee I am ready to go both into prison and unto death.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “I tell you, Peter, the cock will not crow this day, until you have denied three times that you know me.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said: I tell thee, Peter—A cock will not crow this day, until, thrice, thou deny that thou knowest me.
text.KJV: And hee said, I tell thee Peter, the cocke shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt thrise denie that thou knowest me.
text.NABRE: But he replied, “I tell you, Peter, before the cock crows this day, you will deny three times that you know me.”
text.DRB: And he said: I say to thee Peter, the cock shall not crow today, till thou deny thrice that thou knowest me.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “When I sent you out without a purse, bag, or sandals, did you lack anything?” They said, “No, nothing.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—When I sent you forth, without purse or satchel or sandals, of anything, came ye short? And, they, said—Of nothing!
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shooes, lacked ye any thing? And they said, Nothing.
text.NABRE: Instructions for the Time of Crisis. He said to them, “When I sent you forth without a money bag or a sack or sandals, were you in need of anything?” “No, nothing,” they replied.
text.DRB: And he said to them: When I sent you without purse and scrip and shoes, did you lack any thing? But they said: Nothing.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “But now, the one who has a purse must take it, and likewise a bag. And the one who has no sword must sell his cloak and buy one.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—But, now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, in like manner also, a satchel; and, he that hath nothing, let him sell his mantle, and buy a sword!
text.KJV: Then saide hee vnto them, But now he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and hee that hath no sword, let him sel his garment, and buy one.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “But now one who has a money bag should take it, and likewise a sack, and one who does not have a sword should sell his cloak and buy one.
text.DRB: He said therefore unto them: But now he that hath a purse, let him take it, likewise also a scrip: and he that hath not, let him sell his coat, and buy a sword.

text.NRSV: For I tell you, this scripture must be fulfilled in me, ‘And he was counted among the lawless’; and indeed what is written about me is being fulfilled.”
text.Rotherham: For I say unto you—This that is written, must needs be completed in me,—And, with lawless ones, was he reckoned; for, even that which concerneth me, hath, completion.
text.KJV: For I say vnto you, that this that is written, must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the transgressors: For the things concerning me haue an end.
text.NABRE: For I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in me, namely, ‘He was counted among the wicked’; and indeed what is written about me is coming to fulfillment.”
text.DRB: For I say to you, that yet this that is written must be fulfilled in me: And with the wicked was he reputed. For those things that are concerning me, have an end.

text.NRSV: They said, “Lord, look, here are two swords.” He replied, “It is enough.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—Lord, lo! two swords, here! And, he, said unto them—’Tis, enough!
text.KJV: And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And hee saide vnto them, It is ynough.
text.NABRE: Then they said, “Lord, look, there are two swords here.” But he replied, “It is enough!” The Agony in the Garden.
text.DRB: But they said: Lord, lo two swords here. But he said to them: It is enough.

text.NRSV: He came out and went, as was his custom, to the Mount of Olives; and the disciples followed him.
text.Rotherham: And going out, he went, according to his custom, unto the Mount of Olives; and the disciples [also] followed him.
text.KJV: ¶ And he came out, and went, as hee was wont, to the mount of Oliues, and his disciples also followed him.
text.NABRE: Then going out he went, as was his custom, to the Mount of Olives, and the disciples followed him.
text.DRB: And going forth he went according to his custom into mount-Olivet. And his Disciples also followed him.

text.NRSV: When he reached the place, he said to them, “Pray that you may not come into the time of trial.”
text.Rotherham: And, coming up to the place, he said unto them—Be praying, lest ye enter into temptation.
text.KJV: And when he was at the place, he said vnto them, Pray, that yee enter not into temptation.
text.NABRE: When he arrived at the place he said to them, “Pray that you may not undergo the test.”
text.DRB: And when he was come to the place, he said to them: Pray, lest ye enter into tentation.

text.NRSV: Then he withdrew from them about a stone’s throw, knelt down, and prayed,
text.Rotherham: And, he, was torn from them about a stone’s throw; and, bending his knees, he began to pray,
text.KJV: And he was withdrawen from them about a stones cast, and kneeled downe, and prayed,
text.NABRE: After withdrawing about a stone’s throw from them and kneeling, he prayed,
text.DRB: And he was pulled away from them a stone's cast: and kneeling he prayed,

text.NRSV: “Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me; yet, not my will but yours be done.”
text.Rotherham: saying—Father! if thou please, bear aside this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but thine, be accomplished.
text.KJV: Saying, Father, if thou be willing, remooue this cup from me: neuerthelesse, not my will, but thine be done.
text.NABRE: saying, “Father, if you are willing, take this cup away from me; still, not my will but yours be done.”
text.DRB: saying: Father, if thou wilt, transfer this chalice from me. But yet not my will, but thine be done.

text.NRSV: Then an angel from heaven appeared to him and gave him strength.
text.Rotherham: [[And there appeared unto him a messenger from heaven, strengthening him,—
text.KJV: And there appeared an Angel vnto him from heauen, strengthening him.
text.NABRE: [ And to strengthen him an angel from heaven appeared to him.
text.DRB: And there appeared to him an Angel from Heaven, strengthening him. And being in an agony, he prayed the longer.

text.NRSV: In his anguish he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.
text.Rotherham: and, coming to be in an agony, more intensely, was he praying; and his perspiration became, as if great drops of blood, falling upon the ground.]]
text.KJV: And being in an agonie, he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat was as it were great drops of blood falling downe to the ground.
text.NABRE: He was in such agony and he prayed so fervently that his sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.]
text.DRB: And his sweat became as drops of blood trickling down upon the earth.

text.NRSV: When he got up from prayer, he came to the disciples and found them sleeping because of grief,
text.Rotherham: And, arising from prayer, he came unto his disciples, and found them falling asleep by reason of their grief;
text.KJV: And when he rose vp from prayer, and was come to his disciples, hee found them sleeping for sorrow,
text.NABRE: When he rose from prayer and returned to his disciples, he found them sleeping from grief.
text.DRB: And when he was risen up from prayer, and was come to his Disciples, he found them sleeping for pensiveness.

text.NRSV: and he said to them, “Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray that you may not come into the time of trial.”
text.Rotherham: and he said unto them—Why are ye slumbering? Arise! and be praying, lest ye enter into temptation.
text.KJV: And said vnto them, Why sleepe yee? Rise, and pray, lest yee enter into temptation.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray that you may not undergo the test.” The Betrayal and Arrest of Jesus.
text.DRB: And he said to them: Why sleep you? Arise, pray, lest you enter into tentation.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking, suddenly a crowd came, and the one called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He drew near to Jesus to kiss him;
text.Rotherham: While yet he was speaking, lo! a multitude; and, he that was called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them on,—and he drew near unto Jesus, to kiss him.
text.KJV: ¶ And while he yet spake, behold, a multitude, and hee that was called Iudas, one of the twelue, went before them, and drewe neere vnto Iesus, to kisse him.
text.NABRE: While he was still speaking, a crowd approached and in front was one of the Twelve, a man named Judas. He went up to Jesus to kiss him.
text.DRB: As he was yet speaking, behold a multitude: and he that was called Judas, one of the Twelve, went before them, and approached to Jesus, for to kiss him.

text.NRSV: but Jesus said to him, “Judas, is it with a kiss that you are betraying the Son of Man?”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said to him—Judas! with a kiss, the Son of Man, dost thou deliver up?
text.KJV: But Iesus said vnto him, Iudas, betrayest thou the sonne of man with a kisse?
text.NABRE: Jesus said to him, “Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: Judas with a kiss doest thou betray the Son of man?

text.NRSV: When those who were around him saw what was coming, they asked, “Lord, should we strike with the sword?”
text.Rotherham: And they who were about him, seeing what would be, said—Lord! shall we smite with the sword?
text.KJV: When they which were about him, saw what would follow, they said vnto him, Lord, shall wee smite with the sword?
text.NABRE: His disciples realized what was about to happen, and they asked, “Lord, shall we strike with a sword?”
text.DRB: And they that were about him, seeing what would be, said to him: Lord, shall we strike with the sword?

text.NRSV: Then one of them struck the slave of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
text.Rotherham: And a certain one from among them smote, the High-priest’s, servant, and took off his right ear.
text.KJV: ¶ And one of them smote the seruant of the high Priest, and cut off his right eare.
text.NABRE: And one of them struck the high priest’s servant and cut off his right ear.
text.DRB: And one of them smote the servant of the high Priest: and cut off his right ear.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said, “No more of this!” And he touched his ear and healed him.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, answering, said—Let be—as long as this! …And, touching the ear, he healed him.
text.KJV: And Iesus answered, and said, Suffer ye thus farre. And he touched his eare, and healed him.
text.NABRE: But Jesus said in reply, “Stop, no more of this!” Then he touched the servant’s ear and healed him.
text.DRB: But Jesus answering, said: Suffer ye thus far. And when he had touched his ear, he healed him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come for him, “Have you come out with swords and clubs as if I were a robber?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto the High-priests, and Captains of the temple, and Elders, who had come against him—As against a robber, have ye come out, with swords and clubs?
text.KJV: Then Iesus said vnto the chiefe Priests, and captaines of the Temple, and the Elders which were come to him, Be ye come out as against a thiefe, with swords and staues?
text.NABRE: And Jesus said to the chief priests and temple guards and elders who had come for him, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?
text.DRB: And Jesus said to them that were come unto him, the chief Priests, and magistrates of the temple, & ancients: As it were to a thief are you come forth with swords and clubs?

text.NRSV: When I was with you day after day in the temple, you did not lay hands on me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness!”
text.Rotherham: Daily, was I with you, in the temple, and yet ye stretched not forth your hands against me! …But, this, is your hour, and the authority of darkness.
text.KJV: When I was daily with you in the Temple, yee stretched foorth no hands against mee: but this is your houre, and the power of darkenesse.
text.NABRE: Day after day I was with you in the temple area, and you did not seize me; but this is your hour, the time for the power of darkness.”
text.DRB: When I was daily with you in the temple, you did not lay hands upon me, but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.

text.NRSV: Then they seized him and led him away, bringing him into the high priest’s house. But Peter was following at a distance.
text.Rotherham: And, having arrested him, they led him away, and took him into the house of the High-priest; and, Peter, was following afar.
text.KJV: ¶ Then tooke they him, and led him, and brought him into the high Priests house, and Peter followed afarre off.
text.NABRE: Peter’s Denial of Jesus. After arresting him they led him away and took him into the house of the high priest; Peter was following at a distance.
text.DRB: And apprehending him, they led him to the high Priest's house: but Peter followed a far off.

text.NRSV: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
text.Rotherham: And, they having kindled up a fire in the midst of the court, and taken seats together, Peter was for sitting among them.
text.KJV: And when they had kindled a fire in the middes of the hall, and were set downe together, Peter sate downe among them.
text.NABRE: They lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat around it, and Peter sat down with them.
text.DRB: And a fire being kindled in the midst of the court, & they sitting about it, Peter was in the midst of them.

text.NRSV: Then a servant-girl, seeing him in the firelight, stared at him and said, “This man also was with him.”
text.Rotherham: But, a certain maid-servant, seeing him seating himself towards the light, and looking steadfastly at him, said—This one also, was, with him.
text.KJV: But a certaine maide beheld him as he sate by the fire, and earnestly looked vpon him, and said, This man was also with him.
text.NABRE: When a maid saw him seated in the light, she looked intently at him and said, “This man too was with him.”
text.DRB: Whom when a certain wench saw sitting at the light, and had beheld him, she said: This fellow also was with him.

text.NRSV: But he denied it, saying, “Woman, I do not know him.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, denied, saying—I know him not, woman!
text.KJV: And he denied him, saying, Woman, I know him not.
text.NABRE: But he denied it saying, “Woman, I do not know him.”
text.DRB: But he denied him, saying: Woman, I know him not.

text.NRSV: A little later someone else, on seeing him, said, “You also are one of them.” But Peter said, “Man, I am not!”
text.Rotherham: And, after a little, another, beholding him, said—Thou also, art, from among them. But, Peter, said—Man! I am not.
text.KJV: And after a little while another saw him, & said, Thou art also of them. And Peter said, Man, I am not.
text.NABRE: A short while later someone else saw him and said, “You too are one of them”; but Peter answered, “My friend, I am not.”
text.DRB: And after a while another man seeing him, said: And thou art of them. But Peter said: O man I am not.

text.NRSV: Then about an hour later still another kept insisting, “Surely this man also was with him; for he is a Galilean.”
text.Rotherham: And, about one hour having passed, a certain other, began stoutly to insist, saying—Of a truth, this one also, was with him; for indeed he is, a Galilaean.
text.KJV: And about the space of one houre after, another confidently affirmed, saying, Of a trueth this fellow also was with him; for he is a Galilean.
text.NABRE: About an hour later, still another insisted, “Assuredly, this man too was with him, for he also is a Galilean.”
text.DRB: And after the space as it were of one hour, a certain other man affirmed, saying: Verily this fellow also was with him: for he is also a Galilæan.

text.NRSV: But Peter said, “Man, I do not know what you are talking about!” At that moment, while he was still speaking, the cock crowed.
text.Rotherham: But Peter said—Man! I know not what thou art saying! And, instantly while yet he was speaking, a cock crew.
text.KJV: And Peter said, Man, I know not what thou sayest. And immediately while he yet spake, the cocke crew.
text.NABRE: But Peter said, “My friend, I do not know what you are talking about.” Just as he was saying this, the cock crowed,
text.DRB: And Peter said: Man I know not what thou sayest. And incontinent as he was yet speaking, the cock crew.

text.NRSV: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, “Before the cock crows today, you will deny me three times.”
text.Rotherham: And the Lord turned, and looked at Peter; and Peter was put in mind of the declaration of the Lord, how he had said to him—Before, a cock, crow this day, thou wilt deny me thrice!
text.KJV: And the Lord turned, and looked vpon Peter; and Peter remembred the word of the Lord, how he had said vnto him, Before the cocke crow, thou shalt deny me thrise.
text.NABRE: and the Lord turned and looked at Peter; and Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, “Before the cock crows today, you will deny me three times.”
text.DRB: And our Lord turning looked on Peter. And Peter remembered the word of our Lord, as he had said: That before the cock crow, thou shalt thrice deny me.

text.NRSV: And he went out and wept bitterly.
text.Rotherham: [And, going forth outside, he wept bitterly.]
text.KJV: And Peter went out, and wept bitterly.
text.NABRE: He went out and began to weep bitterly.
text.DRB: And Peter going forth a doors, wept bitterly.

text.NRSV: Now the men who were holding Jesus began to mock him and beat him;
text.Rotherham: And, the men who held him bound, kept mocking him, with blows;
text.KJV: ¶ And the men that helde Iesus, mocked him, and smote him.
text.NABRE: The men who held Jesus in custody were ridiculing and beating him.
text.DRB: And the men that held him, mocked him, beating him.

text.NRSV: they also blindfolded him and kept asking him, “Prophesy! Who is it that struck you?”
text.Rotherham: and, blindfolding him, questioned him, saying—Prophesy! which is he that smote thee?
text.KJV: And when they had blindfolded him, they stroke him on the face, and asked him, saying, Prophesie, who is it that smote thee?
text.NABRE: They blindfolded him and questioned him, saying, “Prophesy! Who is it that struck you?”
text.DRB: And they did blind-fold him, and smote his face. And they asked him saying: Prophesy, who is it that smote thee?

text.NRSV: They kept heaping many other insults on him.
text.Rotherham: and, many other things, with profane speech, were they saying unto him.
text.KJV: And many other things blasphemously spake they against him.
text.NABRE: And they reviled him in saying many other things against him. Jesus Before the Sanhedrin.
text.DRB: And blaspheming many other things they said against him.

text.NRSV: When day came, the assembly of the elders of the people, both chief priests and scribes, gathered together, and they brought him to their council.
text.Rotherham: And, when it became day, the Eldership of the people was gathered together—both High-priests and Scribes,—and they led him away into their high-council, saying—If, thou, art the Christ, tell us!
text.KJV: ¶ And assoone as it was day, the Elders of the people, & the chiefe Priests and the Scribes came together, and led him into their Councell, saying,
text.NABRE: When day came the council of elders of the people met, both chief priests and scribes, and they brought him before their Sanhedrin.
text.DRB: And when it was day, there assembled the Ancients of the people and chief Priests and Scribes, and they brought him into their Council, saying:

text.NRSV: They said, “If you are the Messiah, tell us.” He replied, “If I tell you, you will not believe;
text.Rotherham: But he said unto them—If I should tell, you, in nowise would ye believe,
text.KJV: Art thou the Christ? Tell vs. And hee said vnto them, If I tell you, you will not beleeue.
text.NABRE: They said, “If you are the Messiah, tell us,” but he replied to them, “If I tell you, you will not believe,
text.DRB: If thou be Christ tell us. And he said to them: If I tell you, you will not believe me:

text.NRSV: and if I question you, you will not answer.
text.Rotherham: And, if I should put questions, in nowise would ye answer;
text.KJV: And if I also aske you, you will not answere me, nor let me goe.
text.NABRE: and if I question, you will not respond.
text.DRB: if also I ask, you will not answer me, nor dismiss me.

text.NRSV: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”
text.Rotherham: But, henceforth, shall the Son of Man be seated on the right hand of the power of God.
text.KJV: Hereafter shal the sonne of man sit on the right hand of the power of God.
text.NABRE: But from this time on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”
text.DRB: But from henceforth the Son of man shall be sitting on the right hand of the power of God.

text.NRSV: All of them asked, “Are you, then, the Son of God?” He said to them, “You say that I am.”
text.Rotherham: And they all said—Thou, then, art the Son of God? And, he, unto them said—Ye, say [it],—because, I, am.
text.KJV: Then said they all, Art thou then the Sonne of God? And hee said vnto them, Ye say that I am.
text.NABRE: They all asked, “Are you then the Son of God?” He replied to them, “You say that I am.”
text.DRB: And they all said: Art thou then the Son of God? Who said: You say that I am.

text.NRSV: Then they said, “What further testimony do we need? We have heard it ourselves from his own lips!”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—What, further, need have we, of witness? We ourselves, have heard it from his mouth.
text.KJV: And they said, What need we any further witnesse? For wee our selues haue heard of his owne mouth.
text.NABRE: Then they said, “What further need have we for testimony? We have heard it from his own mouth.”
text.DRB: But they said: What need we testimony any further? For ourselves have heard of his own mouth.

text.KJV: And the whole multitude of them arose, and led him vnto Pilate.
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 Jesus Before Pilate. Then the whole assembly of them arose and brought him before Pilate.
text.NRSV: Then the assembly rose as a body and brought Jesus before Pilate.
text.DRB: And all the multitude of them rising up, led him to Pilate.

text.KJV: And they began to accuse him, saying, We found this fellow peruerting the nation, and forbidding to giue tribute to Cesar, saying, that he himselfe is Christ a king.
text.NABRE: They brought charges against him, saying, “We found this man misleading our people; he opposes the payment of taxes to Caesar and maintains that he is the Messiah, a king.”
text.NRSV: They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man perverting our nation, forbidding us to pay taxes to the emperor, and saying that he himself is the Messiah, a king.”
text.DRB: And they began to accuse him, saying: We have found this man subverting our nation, & prohibiting to give tributes to Cæsar, and saying that he is Christ the King.

text.KJV: And Pilate asked him, saying, Art thou the king of the Iewes? And he answered him, & said, Thou sayest it.
text.NABRE: Pilate asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” He said to him in reply, “You say so.”
text.NRSV: Then Pilate asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” He answered, “You say so.”
text.DRB: And Pilate asked him, saying: Art thou the King of the Jews? But he answering said: Thou sayest.

text.KJV: Then saide Pilate to the chiefe Priests, and to the people, I finde no fault in this man.
text.NABRE: Pilate then addressed the chief priests and the crowds, “I find this man not guilty.”
text.NRSV: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, “I find no basis for an accusation against this man.”
text.DRB: And Pilate said to the chief Priests and multitudes: I find no cause in this man.

text.KJV: And they were the more fierce, saying, He stirreth vp the people, teaching thorowout all Iurie, beginning from Galilee to this place.
text.NABRE: But they were adamant and said, “He is inciting the people with his teaching throughout all Judea, from Galilee where he began even to here.”
text.NRSV: But they were insistent and said, “He stirs up the people by teaching throughout all Judea, from Galilee where he began even to this place.”
text.DRB: But they were more earnest, saying: He stirreth the people teaching throughout all Jewry, beginning from Galilee even hither.

text.KJV: When Pilate heard of Galilee, he asked whether the man were a Galilean.
text.NABRE: Jesus Before Herod. On hearing this Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean;
text.NRSV: When Pilate heard this, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.
text.DRB: But Pilate hearing Galilee, asked if the man were of Galilee.

text.KJV: And assoone as he knew that hee belonged vnto Herods iurisdiction, hee sent him to Herode, who himselfe also was at Hierusalem at that time.
text.NABRE: and upon learning that he was under Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod who was in Jerusalem at that time.
text.NRSV: And when he learned that he was under Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him off to Herod, who was himself in Jerusalem at that time.
text.DRB: And when he understood that he was of Herod's jurisdiction, he sent him back to Herod, who was also himself at Jerusalem in those days.

text.KJV: ¶ And when Herode saw Iesus, he was exceeding glad, for hee was desirous to see him of a long season, because he had heard many things of him, and hee hoped to haue seene some miracle done by him.
text.NABRE: Herod was very glad to see Jesus; he had been wanting to see him for a long time, for he had heard about him and had been hoping to see him perform some sign.
text.NRSV: When Herod saw Jesus, he was very glad, for he had been wanting to see him for a long time, because he had heard about him and was hoping to see him perform some sign.
text.DRB: And Herod seeing Jesus, was very glad, for he was desirous of a long time to see him, for because he heard many things of him: and he hoped to see some sign wrought by him.

text.KJV: Then he questioned with him in many words, but he answered him nothing.
text.NABRE: He questioned him at length, but he gave him no answer.
text.NRSV: He questioned him at some length, but Jesus gave him no answer.
text.DRB: And he asked him in many words. But he answered him nothing.

text.KJV: And the chiefe Priests and Scribes stood, and vehemently accused him.
text.NABRE: The chief priests and scribes, meanwhile, stood by accusing him harshly.
text.NRSV: The chief priests and the scribes stood by, vehemently accusing him.
text.DRB: And there stood the chief Priests and the Scribes constantly accusing him.

text.KJV: And Herod with his men of warre set him at naught, and mocked him, and arayed him in a gorgeous robe, and sent him againe to Pilate.
text.NABRE: [Even] Herod and his soldiers treated him contemptuously and mocked him, and after clothing him in resplendent garb, he sent him back to Pilate.
text.NRSV: Even Herod with his soldiers treated him with contempt and mocked him; then he put an elegant robe on him, and sent him back to Pilate.
text.DRB: And Herod with his army set him at naught: and he mocked him, putting on him a white garment, and sent him back to Pilate.

text.KJV: ¶ And the same day Pilate and Herod were made friends together; for before, they were at enmitie betweene themselues.
text.NABRE: Herod and Pilate became friends that very day, even though they had been enemies formerly.
text.NRSV: That same day Herod and Pilate became friends with each other; before this they had been enemies.
text.DRB: And Herod and Pilate were made friends that day. For before they were enemies one to another.

text.KJV: ¶ And Pilate, when hee had called together the chiefe Priests, and the rulers, and the people,
text.NABRE: Pilate then summoned the chief priests, the rulers, and the people
text.NRSV: Pilate then called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people,
text.DRB: And Pilate calling together the chief Priests and Magistrates, and the people,

text.KJV: Said vnto them, Ye haue brought this man vnto me, as one that peruerteth the people, and behold, I hauing examined him before you, haue found no fault in this man, touching those things whereof ye accuse him.
text.NABRE: and said to them, “You brought this man to me and accused him of inciting the people to revolt. I have conducted my investigation in your presence and have not found this man guilty of the charges you have brought against him,
text.NRSV: and said to them, “You brought me this man as one who was perverting the people; and here I have examined him in your presence and have not found this man guilty of any of your charges against him.
text.DRB: said to them: You have presented unto me this man, as averting the people, and behold I examining him before you, have found no cause in this man of those things, wherein you accuse him.

text.KJV: No, nor yet Herod: for I sent you to him, and loe, nothing worthy of death is done vnto him.
text.NABRE: nor did Herod, for he sent him back to us. So no capital crime has been committed by him.
text.NRSV: Neither has Herod, for he sent him back to us. Indeed, he has done nothing to deserve death.
text.DRB: No, nor Herod neither. For I sent you to him, and behold, nothing worthy of death is done to him.

text.KJV: I will therefore chastise him, and release him.
text.NABRE: Therefore I shall have him flogged and then release him.”
text.NRSV: I will therefore have him flogged and release him.”
text.DRB: I will chasten him therefore and dismiss him.

text.NABRE: [ ] The Sentence of Death.
text.KJV: For of necessitie hee must release one vnto them at the Feast.
text.DRB: And he of necessity had to release unto them upon the feast day, one.

text.KJV: And they cried out all at once, saying, Away with this man, and release vnto vs Barabbas,
text.NABRE: But all together they shouted out, “Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us.”
text.NRSV: Then they all shouted out together, “Away with this fellow! Release Barabbas for us!”
text.DRB: But the whole multitude together cried out, saying: Dispatch him, and release us Barabbas.

text.KJV: Who for a certaine sedition made in the citie, and for murder, was cast in prison.
text.NABRE: (Now Barabbas had been imprisoned for a rebellion that had taken place in the city and for murder.)
text.NRSV: (This was a man who had been put in prison for an insurrection that had taken place in the city, and for murder.)
text.DRB: Who was for a certain sedition made in the city and murder, cast into prison.

text.KJV: Pilate therefore willing to release Iesus, spake againe to them:
text.NABRE: Again Pilate addressed them, still wishing to release Jesus,
text.NRSV: Pilate, wanting to release Jesus, addressed them again;
text.DRB: And Pilate again spake to them, desirous to release Jesus.

text.KJV: But they cried, saying, Crucifie him, crucifie him.
text.NABRE: but they continued their shouting, “Crucify him! Crucify him!”
text.NRSV: but they kept shouting, “Crucify, crucify him!”
text.DRB: But they cried again, saying: Crucify, crucify him.

text.KJV: And hee said vnto them the third time, Why, what euill hath he done? I haue found no cause of death in him, I will therefore chastise him, & let him goe.
text.NABRE: Pilate addressed them a third time, “What evil has this man done? I found him guilty of no capital crime. Therefore I shall have him flogged and then release him.”
text.NRSV: A third time he said to them, “Why, what evil has he done? I have found in him no ground for the sentence of death; I will therefore have him flogged and then release him.”
text.DRB: And he the third time said to them: Why, what evil hath this man done? I find no cause of death in him. I will correct him therefore & let him go.

text.KJV: And they were instant with loud voyces, requiring that he might be crucified: and the voyces of them, and of the chiefe Priests preuailed.
text.NABRE: With loud shouts, however, they persisted in calling for his crucifixion, and their voices prevailed.
text.NRSV: But they kept urgently demanding with loud shouts that he should be crucified; and their voices prevailed.
text.DRB: But they were instant with loud voices requiring that he might be crucified. And their voices prevailed.

text.KJV: And Pilate gaue sentence that it should be as they required.
text.NABRE: The verdict of Pilate was that their demand should be granted.
text.NRSV: So Pilate gave his verdict that their demand should be granted.
text.DRB: And Pilate adjudged their petition to be done.

text.KJV: And he released vnto them, him that for sedition and murder was cast into prison, whom they had desired, but he deliuered Iesus to their will.
text.NABRE: So he released the man who had been imprisoned for rebellion and murder, for whom they asked, and he handed Jesus over to them to deal with as they wished. The Way of the Cross.
text.NRSV: He released the man they asked for, the one who had been put in prison for insurrection and murder, and he handed Jesus over as they wished.
text.DRB: And he released unto them him that for murder and sedition had been cast into prison, whom they demanded: but Jesus he delivered to their pleasure.

text.KJV: And as they led him away, they laid hold vpon one Simon a Cyrenian, comming out of the countrey, and on him they laid the crosse, that hee might beare it after Iesus.
text.NABRE: As they led him away they took hold of a certain Simon, a Cyrenian, who was coming in from the country; and after laying the cross on him, they made him carry it behind Jesus.
text.NRSV: As they led him away, they seized a man, Simon of Cyrene, who was coming from the country, and they laid the cross on him, and made him carry it behind Jesus.
text.DRB: And when they led him, they took one Simon of Cyrene coming from the country: and they laid the Cross upon him to carry after Jesus.

text.KJV: ¶ And there followed him a great company of people, and of women, which also bewailed & lamented him.
text.NABRE: A large crowd of people followed Jesus, including many women who mourned and lamented him.
text.NRSV: A great number of the people followed him, and among them were women who were beating their breasts and wailing for him.
text.DRB: And there followed him a great multitude of people, and of women which bewailed and lamented him.

text.KJV: But Iesus turning vnto them, said, Daughters of Hierusalem, weepe not for me, but weepe for your selues, and for your children.
text.NABRE: Jesus turned to them and said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me; weep instead for yourselves and for your children,
text.NRSV: But Jesus turned to them and said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
text.DRB: But Jesus turning to them, said: Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not upon me, but weep upon yourselves, and upon your children.

text.KJV: For beholde, the dayes are comming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that neuer bare, and the paps which neuer gaue sucke.
text.NABRE: for indeed, the days are coming when people will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, the wombs that never bore and the breasts that never nursed.’
text.NRSV: For the days are surely coming when they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.’
text.DRB: For behold the days shall come, wherein they will say: Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that have not born, & the paps that have not given suck.

text.KJV: Then shall they begin to say to the mountaines, Fall on vs, and to the hils, Couer vs.
text.NABRE: At that time people will say to the mountains, ‘Fall upon us!’ and to the hills, ‘Cover us!’
text.NRSV: Then they will begin to say to the mountains, ‘Fall on us’; and to the hills, ‘Cover us.’
text.DRB: Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall upon us: and to the hills, Cover us.

text.KJV: For if they doe these things in a green tree, what shalbe done in the drie?
text.NABRE: for if these things are done when the wood is green what will happen when it is dry?”
text.NRSV: For if they do this when the wood is green, what will happen when it is dry?”
text.DRB: For if in the green wood they do these things, in the dry what shall be done?

text.KJV: And there were also two other malefactors led with him, to bee put to death.
text.NABRE: Now two others, both criminals, were led away with him to be executed. The Crucifixion.
text.NRSV: Two others also, who were criminals, were led away to be put to death with him.
text.DRB: And there were led also other two malefactors with him, to be executed.

text.KJV: And when they were come to the place which is called Caluarie, there they crucified him, and the malefactors, one on the right hand, and the other on the left.
text.NABRE: When they came to the place called the Skull, they crucified him and the criminals there, one on his right, the other on his left.
text.NRSV: When they came to the place that is called The Skull, they crucified Jesus there with the criminals, one on his right and one on his left.
text.DRB: And after they came to the place which is called Calvary, there they crucified him: and the thieves, one on the right hand and the other on the left.

text.KJV: ¶ Then said Iesus, Father, forgiue them, for they know not what they doe: And they parted his raiment, and cast lots.
text.NABRE: [Then Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, they know not what they do.”] They divided his garments by casting lots.
text.NRSV: Then Jesus said, “Father, forgive them; for they do not know what they are doing.” And they cast lots to divide his clothing.
text.DRB: And Jesus said: Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do. But they dividing his garments, did cast lots.

text.KJV: And the people stood beholding, & the rulers also with them derided him, saying, Hee saued others, let him saue himselfe, if he be Christ, þ chosen of God.
text.NABRE: The people stood by and watched; the rulers, meanwhile, sneered at him and said, “He saved others, let him save himself if he is the chosen one, the Messiah of God.”
text.NRSV: And the people stood by, watching; but the leaders scoffed at him, saying, “He saved others; let him save himself if he is the Messiah of God, his chosen one!”
text.DRB: And the people stood beholding, and the Princes with them derided him, saying: Others he hath saved, let him save himself, if this be Christ, the elect of God.

text.KJV: And the souldiers also mocked him, comming to him, and offering him vineger,
text.NABRE: Even the soldiers jeered at him. As they approached to offer him wine
text.NRSV: The soldiers also mocked him, coming up and offering him sour wine,
text.DRB: And the soldiers also mocked him coming to him, and offering him vinegar,

text.KJV: And saying, If thou be the king of the Iewes, saue thy selfe.
text.NABRE: they called out, “If you are King of the Jews, save yourself.”
text.NRSV: and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”
text.DRB: saying: If thou be the King of the Jews, save thyself.

text.KJV: And a superscription also was written ouer him in letters of Greeke, and Latin, & Hebrew, THIS IS THE KING OF THE IEWES.
text.NABRE: Above him there was an inscription that read, “This is the King of the Jews.”
text.NRSV: There was also an inscription over him, “This is the King of the Jews.”
text.DRB: And there was also a superscription written over him in Greek, and Latin, and Hebrew letters: This is The King of The Jews.

text.KJV: ¶ And one of þ malefactors, which were hanged, railed on him, saying, If thou be Christ, saue thy selfe and vs.
text.NABRE: Now one of the criminals hanging there reviled Jesus, saying, “Are you not the Messiah? Save yourself and us.”
text.NRSV: One of the criminals who were hanged there kept deriding him and saying, “Are you not the Messiah? Save yourself and us!”
text.DRB: And one of those thieves that were hanged, blasphemed him, saying: If thou be Christ, save thyself, and us.

text.KJV: But the other answering, rebuked him, saying, Doest not thou feare God, seeing thou art in the same condemnation?
text.NABRE: The other, however, rebuking him, said in reply, “Have you no fear of God, for you are subject to the same condemnation?
text.NRSV: But the other rebuked him, saying, “Do you not fear God, since you are under the same sentence of condemnation?
text.DRB: But the other answering, rebuked him, saying: Neither doest thou fear God, where as thou art in the same damnation?

text.KJV: And we indeed iustly; for we receiue the due reward of our deeds, but this man hath done nothing amisse.
text.NABRE: And indeed, we have been condemned justly, for the sentence we received corresponds to our crimes, but this man has done nothing criminal.”
text.NRSV: And we indeed have been condemned justly, for we are getting what we deserve for our deeds, but this man has done nothing wrong.”
text.DRB: And we indeed justly, for we receive worthy of our doings: but this man hath done no evil.

text.KJV: And he said vnto Iesus, Lord, remember me when thou commest into thy kingdome.
text.NABRE: Then he said, “Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.”
text.NRSV: Then he said, “Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.”
text.DRB: And he said to Jesus: Lord, remember me when thou shalt come into thy Kingdom.

text.KJV: And Iesus said vnto him, Uerily, I say vnto thee, to day shalt thou be with me in Paradise.
text.NABRE: He replied to him, “Amen, I say to you, today you will be with me in Paradise.” The Death of Jesus.
text.NRSV: He replied, “Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.”
text.DRB: And Jesus said to him: Amen I say to thee; this day thou shalt be with me in Paradise.

text.KJV: And it was about the sixt houre, and there was a darkenesse ouer all the earth, vntill the ninth houre.
text.NABRE: It was now about noon and darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon
text.NRSV: It was now about noon, and darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon,
text.DRB: And it was almost the sixth hour: and there was made darkness upon the whole earth until the ninth hour.

text.KJV: And the Sunne was darkened, and the vaile of the temple was rent in the mids.
text.NABRE: because of an eclipse of the sun. Then the veil of the temple was torn down the middle.
text.NRSV: while the sun’s light failed; and the curtain of the temple was torn in two.
text.DRB: And the sun was darkened; and the veil of the temple was rent in the midst.

text.KJV: ¶ And when Iesus had cried with a loud voice, he said, Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit: And hauing said thus, he gaue vp the ghost.
text.NABRE: Jesus cried out in a loud voice, “Father, into your hands I commend my spirit”; and when he had said this he breathed his last.
text.NRSV: Then Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commend my spirit.” Having said this, he breathed his last.
text.DRB: And Jesus crying with a loud voice, said: Father, into thy hands I commend my Spirit. And saying this, he gave up the ghost.

text.KJV: Now when the Centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, Certainly this was a righteous man.
text.NABRE: The centurion who witnessed what had happened glorified God and said, “This man was innocent beyond doubt.”
text.NRSV: When the centurion saw what had taken place, he praised God and said, “Certainly this man was innocent.”
text.DRB: And the Centurion seeing that which was done, glorified God, saying: Indeed this man was just.

text.KJV: And all the people that came together to that sight, beholding the things which were done, smote their breasts, and returned.
text.NABRE: When all the people who had gathered for this spectacle saw what had happened, they returned home beating their breasts;
text.NRSV: And when all the crowds who had gathered there for this spectacle saw what had taken place, they returned home, beating their breasts.
text.DRB: And all the multitude of them that were present together at that sight, and saw the things that were done, returned knocking their breasts.

text.KJV: And all his acquaintance, and the women that followed him from Galilee, stood a farre off, beholding these things.
text.NABRE: but all his acquaintances stood at a distance, including the women who had followed him from Galilee and saw these events. The Burial of Jesus.
text.NRSV: But all his acquaintances, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.
text.DRB: And all his acquaintance stood a far off, and the women that had followed him from Galilee, seeing these things.

text.KJV: ¶ And behold, there was a man named Ioseph, a counseller, and hee was a good man, and a iust.
text.NABRE: Now there was a virtuous and righteous man named Joseph who, though he was a member of the council,
text.NRSV: Now there was a good and righteous man named Joseph, who, though a member of the council,
text.DRB: And behold a man named Joseph, which was a Senator, a good man and a just.

text.KJV: (The same had not consented to the counsell and deede of them) he was of Arimathea, a city of the Iewes (who also himselfe waited for the kingdome of God.)
text.NABRE: had not consented to their plan of action. He came from the Jewish town of Arimathea and was awaiting the kingdom of God.
text.NRSV: had not agreed to their plan and action. He came from the Jewish town of Arimathea, and he was waiting expectantly for the kingdom of God.
text.DRB: He had not consented to their counsel and doings, of Arimathæa a city of Jewry, who also himself expected the Kingdom of God.

text.KJV: This man went vnto Pilate, and begged the body of Iesus.
text.NABRE: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
text.NRSV: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
text.DRB: This man came to Pilate, and asked the body of Jesus.

text.KJV: And he tooke it downe, and wrapped it in linnen, and layd it in a Sepulchre that was hewen in stone, wherein neuer man before was layd.
text.NABRE: After he had taken the body down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid him in a rock-hewn tomb in which no one had yet been buried.
text.NRSV: Then he took it down, wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid it in a rock-hewn tomb where no one had ever been laid.
text.DRB: And taking it down, wrapped it in sindon, and laid him in a monument hewed of stone, wherein never yet any man had been laid.

text.KJV: And that day was the Preparation, and the Sabbath drew on.
text.NABRE: It was the day of preparation, and the sabbath was about to begin.
text.NRSV: It was the day of Preparation, and the sabbath was beginning.
text.DRB: And it was the day of Parasceve, and the Sabboth drew near.

text.KJV: And the women also which came with him from Galilee, followed after, and beheld the Sepulchre, and how his body was layd.
text.NABRE: The women who had come from Galilee with him followed behind, and when they had seen the tomb and the way in which his body was laid in it,
text.NRSV: The women who had come with him from Galilee followed, and they saw the tomb and how his body was laid.
text.DRB: And the women that were come with him from Galilee, following after, saw the monument, and how his body was laid.

text.KJV: And they returned, and prepared spices and ointments, and rested the Sabbath day, according to the commandement.
text.NABRE: they returned and prepared spices and perfumed oils. Then they rested on the sabbath according to the commandment.
text.NRSV: Then they returned, and prepared spices and ointments. On the sabbath they rested according to the commandment.
text.DRB: And returning they prepared spices and ointments: and on the Sabboth they rested according to the commandment.

text.NRSV: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they came to the tomb, taking the spices that they had prepared.
text.Rotherham: but, on the first day of the week, at early dawn, unto the tomb, came they, bringing the spices which they had prepared.
text.KJV: Now vpon the first day of the weeke, very earely in the morning, they came vnto the Sepulchre, bringing the spices which they had prepared, and certaine others with them.
text.NABRE: VIII. The Resurrection Narrative Chapter 24 The Resurrection of Jesus. But at daybreak on the first day of the week they took the spices they had prepared and went to the tomb.
text.DRB: And in the first of the Sabboth very early they came to the monument, carrying the spices which they had prepared.

text.NRSV: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
text.Rotherham: And they found the stone, rolled away from the tomb;
text.KJV: And they found the stone rolled away from the Sepulchre.
text.NABRE: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb;
text.DRB: And they found the stone rolled back from the monument.

text.NRSV: but when they went in, they did not find the body.
text.Rotherham: but, when they entered, they found not the body [[of the Lord Jesus]].
text.KJV: And they entred in, and found not the body of the Lord Iesus.
text.NABRE: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
text.DRB: And going in, they found not the body of our Lord Jesus.

text.NRSV: While they were perplexed about this, suddenly two men in dazzling clothes stood beside them.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while they were perplexing themselves concerning this, that lo! two men, stood near them, in dazzling raiment.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, as they were much perplexed thereabout, behold, two men stood by them in shining garments.
text.NABRE: While they were puzzling over this, behold, two men in dazzling garments appeared to them.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, as they were astonished in their mind at this, behold two men stood beside them in glistering apparel.

text.NRSV: The women were terrified and bowed their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, “Why do you look for the living among the dead? He is not here, but has risen.
text.Rotherham: And, they becoming afraid, and bending their faces unto the ground, they said unto them—Why seek ye the Living One with the dead?
text.KJV: And as they were afraid, and bowed downe their faces to the earth, they said vnto them, Why seek ye the liuing among the dead?
text.NABRE: They were terrified and bowed their faces to the ground. They said to them, “Why do you seek the living one among the dead?
text.DRB: And when they feared and cast down their countenance toward the ground, they said unto them: Why seek you the living with the dead?

text.NRSV: Remember how he told you, while he was still in Galilee,
text.Rotherham: [[He is not here, but hath arisen:]] Remember how he spake unto you while yet he was in Galilee:
text.KJV: He is not heere, but is risen: Remember how he spake vnto you when he was yet in Galilee,
text.NABRE: He is not here, but he has been raised. Remember what he said to you while he was still in Galilee,
text.DRB: he is not here, but is risen. Remember how he spake to you, when he yet was in Galilee,

text.NRSV: that the Son of Man must be handed over to sinners, and be crucified, and on the third day rise again.”
text.Rotherham: Saying, as to the Son of Man, that he must needs be delivered up into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified,—and, on the third day, arise.
text.KJV: Saying, The Sonne of man must be deliuered into the hands of sinfull men, and be crucified, and the third day rise againe.
text.NABRE: that the Son of Man must be handed over to sinners and be crucified, and rise on the third day.”
text.DRB: saying: That the Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinners and be crucified, and the third day rise again.

text.NRSV: Then they remembered his words,
text.Rotherham: And they remembered his sayings;
text.KJV: And they remembred his words,
text.NABRE: And they remembered his words.
text.DRB: And they remembered his words.

text.NRSV: and returning from the tomb, they told all this to the eleven and to all the rest.
text.Rotherham: and, returning [from the tomb], reported all these things unto the eleven, and unto all the rest.
text.KJV: And returned from the Sepulchre, and told all these things vnto the eleuen, and to all the rest.
text.NABRE: Then they returned from the tomb and announced all these things to the eleven and to all the others.
text.DRB: And going back from the monument, they told all these things to those eleven, and to all the rest.

text.NRSV: Now it was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told this to the apostles.
text.Rotherham: Now they were the Magdalene Mary, and Joana, and Mary the mother of James; and, the other women with them, were telling, unto the apostles, these things.
text.KJV: It was Marie Magdalene, & Ioanna, & Mary the mother of Iames, and other women that were with them, which tolde these things vnto the Apostles.
text.NABRE: The women were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, and Mary the mother of James; the others who accompanied them also told this to the apostles,
text.DRB: And it was Mary Magdalene, and Joane, and Mary of James, and the rest that were with them, which said these things to the Apostles.

text.NRSV: But these words seemed to them an idle tale, and they did not believe them.
text.Rotherham: And these sayings appeared before them, as if idle talk, and they were minded to disbelieve them.
text.KJV: And their words seemed to them as idle tales, and they beleeued them not.
text.NABRE: but their story seemed like nonsense and they did not believe them.
text.DRB: And these words seemed before them as dotage, and they did not believe them.

text.NRSV: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb; stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths by themselves; then he went home, amazed at what had happened.
text.Rotherham: [[But, Peter, arising, ran unto the tomb,—and, bending near, beholdeth the linen bandages, alone; and departed, unto himself, marvelling at the thing that had happened.]]
text.KJV: Then arose Peter, and ranne vnto the Sepulchre, and stowping downe, hee behelde the linnen clothes layd by themselues, and departed, wondering in himselfe at that which was come to passe.
text.NABRE: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb, bent down, and saw the burial cloths alone; then he went home amazed at what had happened. The Appearance on the Road to Emmaus.
text.DRB: But Peter rising up ran to the monument; and stooping down he saw the linen cloths lying alone, and went away marveling with himself at that which was done.

text.NRSV: Now on that same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem,
text.Rotherham: And lo! two from among them, on the selfsame day, were journeying unto a village, distant sixty furlongs from Jerusalem, the name of which, was Emmaus;
text.KJV: ¶ And behold, two of them went that same day to a village called Emaus, which was from Hierusalem about threescore furlongs.
text.NABRE: Now that very day two of them were going to a village seven miles from Jerusalem called Emmaus,
text.DRB: And behold, two of them went the same day into a town which was the space of sixty furlongs from Jerusalem, named Emmaus.

text.NRSV: and talking with each other about all these things that had happened.
text.Rotherham: and, they, were conversing one with another, about all these things which had occurred.
text.KJV: And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
text.NABRE: and they were conversing about all the things that had occurred.
text.DRB: And they talked betwixt themselves of all those things that had chanced.

text.NRSV: While they were talking and discussing, Jesus himself came near and went with them,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as they were conversing and reasoning together, that, Jesus himself, drawing near, was journeying with them;
text.KJV: And it came to passe, that while they communed together, and reasoned, Iesus himselfe drew neere, and went with them.
text.NABRE: And it happened that while they were conversing and debating, Jesus himself drew near and walked with them,
text.DRB: And it came to pass, while they talked and reasoned with themselves, Jesus also himself approaching went with them.

text.NRSV: but their eyes were kept from recognizing him.
text.Rotherham: but, their eyes, were held, so as not to know him.
text.KJV: But their eyes were holden, that they should not know him.
text.NABRE: but their eyes were prevented from recognizing him.
text.DRB: But their eyes were held that they might not know him.

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “What are you discussing with each other while you walk along?” They stood still, looking sad.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—What are these things which ye are debating one with another, as ye walk along? And they came to a stand, sad in countenance.
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, What maner of communications are these that yee haue one to another as yee walke, and are sad?
text.NABRE: He asked them, “What are you discussing as you walk along?” They stopped, looking downcast.
text.DRB: And he said to them: What are these communications that you confer one with another walking, and are sad?

text.NRSV: Then one of them, whose name was Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only stranger in Jerusalem who does not know the things that have taken place there in these days?”
text.Rotherham: But one, by name Cleopas, answering, said unto him—Art, thou, lodging, alone, in Jerusalem, and knowest not the things which have come to pass therein in these days?
text.KJV: And the one of them, whose name was Cleophas, answering, saide vnto him, Art thou onely a stranger in Hierusalem, and hast not knowen the things which are come to passe there in these dayes?
text.NABRE: One of them, named Cleopas, said to him in reply, “Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who does not know of the things that have taken place there in these days?”
text.DRB: And one whose name was Cleophas, answering, said to him: Art thou only a stranger in Jerusalem, and hast not known the things that have been done in it, these days?

text.NRSV: He asked them, “What things?” They replied, “The things about Jesus of Nazareth, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people,
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—What things? And, they, said unto him—The things concerning Jesus the Nazarene, who became a prophet, mighty in work and word, before God and all the people:
text.KJV: And hee saide vnto them, What things? And they said vnto him, Concerning Iesus of Nazareth, which was a Prophet, mighty in deede and word before God, and all the people.
text.NABRE: And he replied to them, “What sort of things?” They said to him, “The things that happened to Jesus the Nazarene, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people,
text.DRB: To whom he said: What things? And they said, concerning Jesus of Nazareth, who was a man a Prophet, mighty in work and word before God and all the people.

text.NRSV: and how our chief priests and leaders handed him over to be condemned to death and crucified him.
text.Rotherham: In what way also our High-priests and Rulers delivered him up unto a sentence of death, and crucified him.
text.KJV: And how the chiefe Priests and our rulers deliuered him to be condemned to death, and haue crucified him.
text.NABRE: how our chief priests and rulers both handed him over to a sentence of death and crucified him.
text.DRB: And how our chief Priests and Princes delivered him into condemnation of death, and crucified him.

text.NRSV: But we had hoped that he was the one to redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things took place.
text.Rotherham: We, however, were hoping that, he, was the one destined to redeem Israel! But indeed, even with all these things, this brings on, the third, day, since these things happened:—
text.KJV: But wee trusted that it had bene hee, which should haue redeemed Israel: and beside all this, to day is the third day since these things were done.
text.NABRE: But we were hoping that he would be the one to redeem Israel; and besides all this, it is now the third day since this took place.
text.DRB: But we hoped that it was he that should redeem Israel: and now besides all this, today is the third day since these things were done.

text.NRSV: Moreover, some women of our group astounded us. They were at the tomb early this morning,
text.Rotherham: Nay! certain women also, from amongst us, have made us beside ourselves, in that they went early unto the tomb;
text.KJV: Yea, and certaine women also of our company made vs astonished, which were early at the Sepulchre:
text.NABRE: Some women from our group, however, have astounded us: they were at the tomb early in the morning
text.DRB: But certain women also of ours, made us afraid: who before it was light, were at the monument,

text.NRSV: and when they did not find his body, they came back and told us that they had indeed seen a vision of angels who said that he was alive.
text.Rotherham: and, not finding his body, came, saying—that, a vision of messengers, they had seen, who were affirming him to be alive.
text.KJV: And when they found not his bodie, they came, saying, that they had also seene a vision of Angels, which saide that he was aliue.
text.NABRE: and did not find his body; they came back and reported that they had indeed seen a vision of angels who announced that he was alive.
text.DRB: and not finding his body, came, saying that they saw a vision also of Angels, who say that he is alive.

text.NRSV: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said; but they did not see him.”
text.Rotherham: And certain of them who were with us departed unto the tomb,—and found so, according as, the women, had said; but, him, they saw not.
text.KJV: And certaine of them which were with vs, went to the Sepulchre, and found it euen so as the women had said, but him they saw not.
text.NABRE: Then some of those with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women had described, but him they did not see.”
text.DRB: And certain men of ours went to the monument: and they found it so as the women said, but him they found not.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Oh, how foolish you are, and how slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have declared!
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—O thoughtless ones! and slow in heart to rest your faith upon all things which the prophets have spoken:—
text.KJV: Then hee saide vnto them, O fooles, and slow of heart to beleeue all that the Prophets haue spoken:
text.NABRE: And he said to them, “Oh, how foolish you are! How slow of heart to believe all that the prophets spoke!
text.DRB: And he said to them: O foolish, and slow of heart to believe in all things which the Prophets have spoken.

text.NRSV: Was it not necessary that the Messiah should suffer these things and then enter into his glory?”
text.Rotherham: Was it not needful for the Christ, these very things, to suffer, and to enter into his glory?
text.KJV: Ought not Christ to haue suffered these things, and to enter into his glorie?
text.NABRE: Was it not necessary that the Messiah should suffer these things and enter into his glory?”
text.DRB: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and so to enter into his glory?

text.NRSV: Then beginning with Moses and all the prophets, he interpreted to them the things about himself in all the scriptures.
text.Rotherham: And, beginning from Moses, and from all the prophets, he thoroughly explained to them, in all the Scriptures, the things concerning himself.
text.KJV: And beginning at Moses, and all the Prophets, hee expounded vnto them in all the Scriptures, the things concerning himselfe.
text.NABRE: Then beginning with Moses and all the prophets, he interpreted to them what referred to him in all the scriptures.
text.DRB: And beginning from Moyses and all the Prophets, he did interpret to them in all the scriptures the things that were concerning him.

text.NRSV: As they came near the village to which they were going, he walked ahead as if he were going on.
text.Rotherham: And they drew near unto the village, whither they were journeying; and, he, made for journeying, further.
text.KJV: And they drew nigh vnto the village, whither they went, and hee made as though hee would haue gone further.
text.NABRE: As they approached the village to which they were going, he gave the impression that he was going on farther.
text.DRB: And they drew nigh to the town whither they went: and he made semblance to go further.

text.NRSV: But they urged him strongly, saying, “Stay with us, because it is almost evening and the day is now nearly over.” So he went in to stay with them.
text.Rotherham: And they constrained him, saying—Abide with us; because it is, towards evening, and the day hath already declined. And he went in to abide with them.
text.KJV: But they constrained him, saying, Abide with vs, for it is towards euening, and the day is farre spent: And he went in, to tarrie with them.
text.NABRE: But they urged him, “Stay with us, for it is nearly evening and the day is almost over.” So he went in to stay with them.
text.DRB: And they forced him, saying: Tarry with us, because it is toward night, and the day is now far spent. And he went in with them.

text.NRSV: When he was at the table with them, he took bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when he reclined with them, taking the loaf, he blessed, and, breaking it, went on to give unto them.
text.KJV: And it came to passe, as hee sate at meate with them, hee tooke bread, and blessed it, and brake, and gaue to them.
text.NABRE: And it happened that, while he was with them at table, he took bread, said the blessing, broke it, and gave it to them.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, whiles he sat at the table with them, he took bread, and blessed and brake, and did reach to them.

text.NRSV: Then their eyes were opened, and they recognized him; and he vanished from their sight.
text.Rotherham: And, their, eyes were opened, and they knew him; and, he, vanished from them.
text.KJV: And their eyes were opened, and they knew him, and he vanished out of their sight.
text.NABRE: With that their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he vanished from their sight.
text.DRB: And their eyes were opened, and they knew him: and he vanished out of their sight.

text.NRSV: They said to each other, “Were not our hearts burning within us while he was talking to us on the road, while he was opening the scriptures to us?”
text.Rotherham: And they said one to another—Was not, our heart, burning, as he was speaking to us in the way, as he was opening to us the Scriptures?
text.KJV: And they said one vnto another, Did not our heart burne within vs, while hee talked with vs by the way, and while hee opened to vs the Scriptures?
text.NABRE: Then they said to each other, “Were not our hearts burning [within us] while he spoke to us on the way and opened the scriptures to us?”
text.DRB: And they said one to the other: Was not our heart burning in us, whiles he spake in the way, and opened unto us the scriptures?

text.NRSV: That same hour they got up and returned to Jerusalem; and they found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
text.Rotherham: And, arising in that very hour, they returned unto Jerusalem,—and found, gathered together, the eleven, and them who were with them,
text.KJV: And they rose vp the same houre, and returned to Hierusalem, and found the eleuen gathered together, and them that were with them,
text.NABRE: So they set out at once and returned to Jerusalem where they found gathered together the eleven and those with them
text.DRB: And rising up the same hour they went back into Jerusalem: and they found the eleven gathered together, and those that were with them,

text.NRSV: They were saying, “The Lord has risen indeed, and he has appeared to Simon!”
text.Rotherham: saying—In truth, the Lord hath arisen, and hath appeared unto Simon!
text.KJV: Saying, The Lord is risen indeed, and hath appeared to Simon.
text.NABRE: who were saying, “The Lord has truly been raised and has appeared to Simon!”
text.DRB: saying: That our Lord is risen indeed, and hath appeared to Simon.

text.NRSV: Then they told what had happened on the road, and how he had been made known to them in the breaking of the bread.
text.Rotherham: And, they, went on to relate the things, [that had passed] on the journey, and how he was made known unto them in the breaking of the loaf.
text.KJV: And they told what things were done in the way, & how he was knowen of them in breaking of bread.
text.NABRE: Then the two recounted what had taken place on the way and how he was made known to them in the breaking of the bread.
text.DRB: And they told the things that were done in the way: and how they knew him in the breaking of bread.

text.NRSV: While they were talking about this, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
text.Rotherham: Now, as, these very things, they were telling, he himself, stood in their midst [[and saith unto them—Peace to you!]]
text.KJV: ¶ And as they thus spake, Iesus himselfe stood in the midst of them, and sayeth vnto them, Peace bee vnto you.
text.NABRE: The Appearance to the Disciples in Jerusalem. While they were still speaking about this, he stood in their midst and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
text.DRB: And whiles they speak these things, Jesus stood in the midst of them, and he saith to them: Peace be to you; it is I, fear not.

text.NRSV: They were startled and terrified, and thought that they were seeing a ghost.
text.Rotherham: But, being agitated and becoming, afraid, they began to imagine, that, upon a spirit, they were looking.
text.KJV: But they were terrified, and afrighted, and supposed that they had seene a spirit.
text.NABRE: But they were startled and terrified and thought that they were seeing a ghost.
text.DRB: But they being troubled and frighted, imagined that they saw a Spirit.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Why are you frightened, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Why are ye troubled? and for what cause do reasonings arise in your hearts?
text.KJV: And he said vnto them, Why are yee troubled, and why doe thoughts arise in your hearts?
text.NABRE: Then he said to them, “Why are you troubled? And why do questions arise in your hearts?
text.DRB: And he said to them: Why are you troubled, and cogitations arise into your hearts?

text.NRSV: Look at my hands and my feet; see that it is I myself. Touch me and see; for a ghost does not have flesh and bones as you see that I have.”
text.Rotherham: See my hands, and my feet,—that it is, I, myself: Handle me, and see! because, a spirit, hath not, flesh and bones, as ye perceive, I, have.
text.KJV: Beholde my hands and my feete, that it is I my selfe: handle me, and see, for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me haue.
text.NABRE: Look at my hands and my feet, that it is I myself. Touch me and see, because a ghost does not have flesh and bones as you can see I have.”
